## 1. Core30 Agent not working for me
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/core30-agent-not-working-for-me
Autor: wolfgang-dreher-8549  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  5 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
So, I have my Claude API Key set and want to start generating, but there are credits required?
I could not find anything to acquire those credits and thought they are a \(future\) additional pay and usage method.
I thought Claude API is sufficient, as the other tools \(for image generation e.g.\) are optional or for heavy users.

What am I missind here? What other API Key is required? 🤔

RESPUESTAS:
• kendrick-mayer-8407: I can't say for sure, but I *think* the C30 Agent is having issues at the moment. Railway \(upon which it is built\), was down for a while yesterday. It's back, but the stuff I had in process is currently not moving. Hopefully just a temporary glitch that they'll get worked out soon.
• caleb-ulku-6571 ★: you start with 25 credits, visible in the top right of the build queue
  
  there are a lot of keys you can add if you run out of credits, but the 25 should be enough to get started with some content, especiallly as you have the most important key entered already
• caleb-ulku-6571 ★: From the screencap you have 0 remaining, so you used them all. they'll refresh each month but there's no way to buy more - the intent is for credits to let users see how it works, then rather than my marking up ai tokens 10x to make money off users, you get your own api keys for the services you want
  • chiara-burns-7791: [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) I have all my API keys in and it’s still charging me to generate content
• wolfgang-dreher-8549: I did not generate any content yet. I only had some fields magically filled out. So I guess this burned the credits. But I did not notice I had credits at all.
  
  So adding a key for an image generation service will make it work then? The "included" made me think I could start without one.

## 2. Plumbing Ad Feedback
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/plumbing-ad-feedback
Autor: chris-white-1808  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  4 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hey [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) I know you've done a lot of work with plumbers. My plumbing client asked me to create an ad for him. Can you give me your feedback on this ad/let me know if I can make a stronger offer based on what you know? Also open to feedback from others who have worked with Plumbers successfully. Thanks in advance!! FYI. This is going out as direct mail to homeowners in the area.

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: honestly ad looks solid visually clean and trust signals are on point, only thing id tweak is make the offer stronger like "$50 off any service over $200" or bundle a free water heater inspection with any plumbing call cause $50 flat off isnt super compelling for direct mail compared to a value bundle
• chris-white-1808: Thanks for the insight Miles!
• caleb-ulku-6571 ★: I haven't ever run ads for a plumber before. In my experience, the best way to see if it's a good ad or not is to split test it against a variety of ads and see which ones perform =\) otherwise we're just guessing
  • chris-white-1808: [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) thanks brother! Make sense. And I thought you were the guru of everything. LOL. My wife doesn't laugh at my jokes so if that wasn't funny don't feel bad for not laughing.

## 4. Category/Service help
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/categoryservice-help
Autor: tom-church-4353  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hi guys,
I have a secondary category "Gas engineer"
Services provided are:
[ul][li]Gas Boiler Installation[li]Gas Boiler Repair[li]Gas Boiler Replacement[li]Gas Boiler Servicing
Each of these have been listed under the secondary category in the GBP.
The search results in the area for each of these services shows the same three GBP's does mean I can merge all into one service page or because they are listed separately do I need to build a page for each service? [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) TIA.

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: build dedicated pages for each one cause they are listed as separate services in GBP and each targets a different search intent, even if the same 3 GBPs rank for all of them the dedicated pages strengthen your topical authority for the whole gas boiler category and let you capture variations like "boiler repair" vs "boiler replacement" which have different user intents :\)
  • tom-church-4353: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) thanks very much for clarifying

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 5. H1 & Page Title for website homepage for various locations.
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/h1-page-title-for-website-homepage-for-various-locations
Autor: chris-beazley-9965  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  8 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
The different GBPs for different towns all go to the same home page.
What should the Title Tags and H1 be if the home page is the landing page for multiple GBPs ? i.e What Town do you use \(head Office\) or do you leave the Town name out?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: if multiple GBPs all point to the same homepage thats actually a structural problem cause each GBP should ideally point to its own dedicated location page with matching H1 and title tag for that specific town, the homepage should use your head office town as the anchor but build dedicated location pages for the other GBPs so each profile has its own properly aligned landing page :\)
  • chris-beazley-9965: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Thank you for your comments. I spoke about this in the live call today and Caleb said that if the GBPs point to the home page don't change it. So what should I do?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Chris Beazley](obj://user/5d93490bb0c8499d9e7754083384e141) if Caleb said then leave it that way and trust the guidance, in that case keep the homepage H1 and title tag anchored to your head office town since thats the primary location, and use the location pages for content depth on other towns without changing the GBP routing
  • chris-beazley-9965: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Thank you
• chris-beazley-9965: I ran additional heatmaps today and the results confirm that both the website and the Google Business Profile for the main location are performing poorly across all of their categories and services.
  
  The core problem is that the site is trying to represent too many different services at once. That lack of focus is diluting its ability to rank competitively for any single one of them.
  
  One division in particular stands out as an opportunity. It operates differently to the rest of the business, serves a different type of customer and already has its own dedicated phone number on the main site. Does this separation means it meets Google's requirements for its own standalone Google Business Profile?
  
  My thoughts are to build a separate GBP and a dedicated website for that division. Doing so would allow it to rank independently, target its own search terms properly and compete in a space where the current setup is giving it no visibility at all.
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Chris Beazley](obj://user/5d93490bb0c8499d9e7754083384e141) if that division operates independently with its own phone and serves a different customer type it can potentially need for its own GBP and dedicated website, but it needs to be a real standalone division or department that google would recognize as eligible like its own legal entity DBA or clearly separate operating unit, not just a service category within the same business. if it meets that bar separating them is the cleaner option cause it gives you focused signals for google and lets you build a tight core 30 site around just that division's services without diluting the main brand. before moving confirm the division genuinely operates as separate from the parent business so the second GBP doesnt get flagged as a duplicate or violation
  • chris-beazley-9965: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Thank you Miles. I will confirm with the client.
• chris-beazley-9965: This division operates independently, serves a different customer base and has its own dedicated phone number — which together meet Google's criteria for a separate Google Business Profile?

## 6. Welcome to AI SEO Mastery Pro - Your Fast Track to SEO Success!
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/welcome-to-ai-seo-mastery-pro-your-fast-track-to-seo-success
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles  ·  36 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Welcome to the AI SEO Mastery Pro community! Thank you for joining us. As a Pro member, you now have full access to the complete AI SEO Mastery course and all the premium resources designed to help you master AI-powered SEO and grow your business.

Your Pro Member Benefits:
✅ Full AI SEO Mastery Course - All modules unlocked and ready
✅ Weekly Tuesday Zoom Huddles - Live Q&A and strategy sessions
✅ Priority Support - Get your questions answered faster
✅ Pro Resources - Templates, SOPs, and advanced guides
✅ Pro Member Channel - Connect with other serious SEO professionals
✅ Lifetime Updates - Stay current as AI and SEO evolve

This community is here to help you develop your AI SEO skills, attract quality clients, scale your services, and build the business you want.

Reporting Issues
[ul][li]Received a spammy DM? Let an admin know[li]See a rule violation? Use the three-dot menu to report[li]We work hard to keep this a valuable space for everyone

Getting Started
📚 Classroom Tab: Find the complete AI SEO Mastery course here. All modules are available - start wherever makes sense for you.
💬 Community Tab: Share insights, ask questions, and connect with other members.
🎥 Friday Zoom Huddles: Join us each Friday for live discussions and Q&A. Check the Events tab for schedule.
💎 Pro Member Channel: Your space to connect with other Pro members.

Next Steps:
[ol:1][li]Introduce yourself in the Pro Member Channel[li]Explore the course in the Classroom tab[li]Join us this Friday for the live huddle[li]Check out the Pro resources in the Classroom

Community Guidelines:
>>> Be Respectful: Treat fellow members with kindness and professionalism. We're all here to learn and grow together.
>>> Stay On Topic: Keep discussions focused on SEO, AI, client work, and business growth. This helps everyone get the most value from our conversations.
>>> No Advertising: Please refrain from self-promotional content in posts or DMs. This is a space for learning and collaboration.
>>> No Spam: Help us maintain quality discussions. No spam or engagement farming allowed.

Stay Connected
Don't forget to subscribe for additional content: \[Caleb Ulku YouTube Channel\]
We're excited to have you here and look forward to seeing your success with AI SEO.

Welcome aboard,
Caleb & The AI SEO Mastery Team

## 7. BETA: Core30 AI Agent Tool Bugs & Features Wish List
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/beta-core30-ai-agent-tool-bugs-features-wish-list
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles + comentó  ·  59 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hello!

The Core30 AI Agent tool is very much still in beta! Thank you for your help in testing it. So far we've fixed several dozen bugs.

Here's the link straight to the tool:
[https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/492fd5bc?md=e7d75255384a492a865ca01132509fe9](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/492fd5bc?md=e7d75255384a492a865ca01132509fe9)

The reason for this thread is so you can post any bugs that affected you, as well as request any features.

If you've just joined, Zapier should push your email address to the tool and allow you access. If it's been ~10 minutes and that hasn't happened yet, send me a message so I can add you manually. The Zapier has been surprisingly inconsistent unfortunately.

A few big ones that are in development on the staging site:
[ul][li]Internal linking[li]Integration with "not AI slop" link building[li]Integration with the local quality link finder[li]Integration with GSC data to parse adjectives[li]Password on login[li]Credit system: Each API will have an option for you to either provide your key or pay for credits

  Overall really solid though.

• shareef-huddle-7505: By the way, love the tool
• fenwick-snowdon-5296: Is there a way of exporting articles? If not? That would be very helpful.
  • caleb-ulku-6571 ★: [@Fenwick Snowdon](obj://user/e1f31836c8f448409796856820a85032) Added. Under the "Publish" tab all the previously generated content lives. You can now export from that tab
• daniel-tenney-7382: Entity Research is Phase 1, GBP Audit it Phase 2, and Site Crawl & Analysis is Phase 3, but they are out of order on the nav bar.

Research is after the gbp categories and crawl because it needs those inputs before it can generate the gap analysis
• daniel-tenney-7382: Service Entity Overlap Research doesn't save after generation. I generated it twice and the results don't stay after refresh. Console does throw an error.
  • caleb-ulku-6571 ★: [@Daniel Tenney](obj://user/8f635359dd4d4b0f8d7556758cef6fb9) adding this =\)
• daniel-tenney-7382: Bulk Generate tab throws "deleteSelected is not defined". It throws this error on a project where I have all the phases built out. I stood up a brand new client to test it and it works with nothing filled out.
  • daniel-tenney-7382: [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) Added another client, started filling things out. Once I got to the Bulk generate step, same thing happened.
  • daniel-tenney-7382: I added an empty "deleteSelected" function to the console and it threw a "clearQueue" error. This is the code I added to the console below and the bulk generate now renders.
    
    window.deleteSelected = function \(...args\) {
    console.warn\('temporary stub deleteSelected called with:', args\);
    return false;
    };
    
    window.clearQueue = function \(...args\) {
    console.warn\('temporary stub clearQueue called with:', args\);
    return false;
    };
  • caleb-ulku-6571 ★: [@Daniel Tenney](obj://user/8f635359dd4d4b0f8d7556758cef6fb9) Thank you! helpful - pushing an update
  • caleb-ulku-6571 ★: [@Daniel Tenney](obj://user/8f635359dd4d4b0f8d7556758cef6fb9) this is now fixed for me
• daniel-tenney-7382: On the Site Crawler, there are metrics under the site scan. On click, those should show/filter which pages fall under that category. Ex. No H1 metric. Cool, but which one is it?
  • caleb-ulku-6571 ★: [@Daniel Tenney](obj://user/8f635359dd4d4b0f8d7556758cef6fb9) adding this =\)
• daniel-tenney-7382: On Settings tab, put an option to pull info directly from GBP. \(Leadsnap has a version of this as "Sync Values From GBP Profile"\).

  • daniel-tenney-7382: [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) yeah, I’m just not sure the point of the emoji. In the list of clients if I was going to easily pick through to find my client, instead of having to look through emojis, just display their business logo url.

## 8. Core30Agent Content Writing Major Update
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/core30agent-content-writing-major-update
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles + comentó  ·  16 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I'm excited about this one!

You've heard me talk about this. March '26 Google rolled out a core update that resulted in mass de-indexing of content that doesn't add information gain to the content already published on the SERPS.

So of course we checked the search console for our clients

Category, service, and supporting content pages all good. No significant issues.

Location pages though... Honestly a bit of a bloodbath.

Kind of makes sense? "Lasik eye surgery chicago" vs "lasik eye surgery in bucktown chicago" how different is that content really?

But we have years of evidence the hyper geo pages work.

So we ran tests \(as we often do\)

And the results of those tests are that we can get geo relevance content indexed, but it required content with information gain. That required research

Today I pushed an update to the Core30Agent with those results in mind.

Category, service, and supporting content research have all been improved with additional LLM and DataForSEO calls to give better, more interesting FAQs. But that wasn't the big change.

Location pages were the big change.

I added multiple new APIs, including the US Census Bureau and Google Maps Distance Matrix, so the location pages could talk about why lasik eye surgery impacted 20-somethings in bucktown differently than office workers near the Willis Tower.

The changes are live now. No new API keys needed from anyone. Try out the location page generator and let me know what you think!

Check your Search Consoles to see if you have any "crawled not indexed" pages that started popping up recently. If you do, regenerate the content using the newest version of the tool.

RESPUESTAS:
• mark-farrow-6630: Looking forward to seeing these pages!
• joe-susana-7208: Are you planning on explaining deeper for those of us that don't use the agent?
  • caleb-ulku-6571 ★: [@Joe S](obj://user/42157838a3914ce59aed205a5f7c4b1b) in what way? The work to do the research manually makes it very difficult to do at scale. Claude can’t get the data from the us census bureau api. The message is the same if you aren’t using the core30agent - the content needs to add meaningful information gain over the existing results or Google is unlikely to index it
• allen-wagner-3902: This is super cool Caleb! I was just studying this very thing yesterday.
• james-omara-7067: Awesome! Can’t wait.
• chris-beazley-9965: I hope this is not a silly question, but will it work in the UK or any other non USA markets?
• marina-n-7893: would u mind sharing which APIs, besides the US census one, you're using for the location pages content enhancements ? 👉👈
• stephan-franklyn-4962: Amazing
• mark-aburn-7618: [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) Thanks for all of your work on the Core30 Agent, it looks great and ai will be jumping in and trying it this weekend.
  My question is similar to [@Chris Beazley](obj://user/5d93490bb0c8499d9e7754083384e141), I have Australian clients, The US census bureau would not be relevant to Australia, but we don’t the Australian Bureau of Statistics which collects our census data.
  • caleb-ulku-6571 ★: [@Mark Aburn](obj://user/48e97edfbcbb42a59511d45f52399e5e) Great question - here's the honest answer: the location enrichment uses US-specific data sources \(US Census Bureau + FCC for tract lookups\). For AU clients, those calls quietly return no results, and your location pages will still generate - they just won't have the demographic context that US pages get. The drive-time component \(Google Maps\) works fine globally, so you'll still see distance and travel time on your pages
  • mark-aburn-7618: [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) thank you, it’s ok, we will enhance the pages with the missing elements.
• kendrick-mayer-8407: [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) FYI, C30Agent seems to be down at the moment. I was in the middle of some content generation and it seemed stalled, so I tried refreshing the process first with no luck, then tried refreshing the page and ended up getting a "Not Found" page from Railway
  • caleb-ulku-6571 ★: [@Kendrick Mayer](obj://user/7ef8ec6039174d62b888271d7aae84ac) Yeah railway is down right now and core30agent uses railway
  • kendrick-mayer-8407: [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) It seems that processing is 'stuck'. I saw that railway came back online last night and it seemed that things were 'working' - under the outlines section there was a spinning timer in front of 'Full Auto — writing content as outlines complete'. However, this morning nothing has progressed from where it was. Could it be that since I was in the middle of the process when it went down that something got corrupted? Do I need to cancel everything and start over?
  • caleb-ulku-6571 ★: [@Kendrick Mayer](obj://user/7ef8ec6039174d62b888271d7aae84ac) with railway going down, yes you'll need to - the app can recover for small bips but railway was down for a while
  • kendrick-mayer-8407: [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) got it, thank you. All taken care of now. 👍

## 9. 52 GBP Posting
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/52-gbp-posting
Autor: patrick-leon-6173  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  3 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Does anyone has information on "how to" post Caleb's 52 generated posts using Leadsnap? Thank you in advance.

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: the 52 generated posts prompt from Caleb gives you the content but leadsnap has its own built in post generation tool that you cant upload your manually generaetd posts, so for using Calebs 52 posts you'll need to either manually posts or schedule them in a third party tool like publer for bulk scheduling cause leadsnap doesnt accept bulk imports of pre written posts directly

## 10. Trust badges

Autor: mark-fazio-5761  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  5 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
The brokerage firm I work for has a lot of trust badges from awards they have received. For my website, should I use those badges? They are not personal awards but awards as a company.

RESPUESTAS:
• chiara-burns-7791: Yes, important from a UX perspective for proof/trust-building. Just be honest in the framing that they were awarded to so-and-so brokerage of which you're a part.
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: yes you should use the company trust badges cause they boost credibility and conversion rates significantly, just place them strategically on the homepage above the fold and on service pages near the CTAs, they're powerful trust signals that help close prospects
• terri-sassone-7507: Is the website representing the company you work for or you? If it is you, I would not use awards that belong to a company, not you.

## 12. Is there a best practice for linking pages internally aside from the anchor text to use?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/is-there-a-best-practice-for-linking-pages-internally-aside-from-the-anchor-text-to-use
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  8 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Like linking geo pages to category pages or the home page, or to other geo articles, etc?

If this is covered in one of the trainings, please let me know so I can rewatch. Thank you.

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: the core 30 internal linking hierarchy is homepage links to category pages, category pages link down to service pages, service pages link back up to their parent category page. geo pages follow the same logic, each geo page links to the relevant service pages for that location and back to the homepage, and your main service pages can contextually link to geo pages where it makes sense naturally in the content. avoid linking every page to every other page cause that flattens the hierarchy, keep it directional with pages linking up to parents and down to children. Caleb covers internal linking structure in this [https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=892b004d3b5d46448803e50528ca0536](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=892b004d3b5d46448803e50528ca0536) module so rewatch :\)
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Thanks again, I will definitely rewatch! Planning to go back through the course in general now that I have more perspective & experience on everything.
    
    Is there any sort of limit to how many internal links are too many? Because a lot of the content will talk about chiropractic but also how it helps with back pain, neck pain, and AI will often have a sentence or two talking about another service or condition in it's content - wasn't sure if I should always try to link those to a page if we have it.
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) no hard limit on internal links but quality and relevance matter more than quantity, link contextually when it genuinely helps the reader find related information not just to hit a number. if the content naturally mentions back pain treatment and you have a dedicated back pain page then yes link it, but dont force links into every mention of every service cause that starts to look spammy and dilutes the value of each individual link. a good rule of thumb is one contextual link per topic mention maximum, and prioritize linking to your most important category and service pages over linking to every possible related page :\)
• stephan-franklyn-4962: 
• stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) coming back to this. I had a question about the main service hub page \(the one with all the services and categories, typically in the nav bar\). I understand we link to the services from that page, do we also link back to that service hub page? Would it be a parent page or a sister page?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) yes link back to the main services hub page from each individual service page, treat it as a parent page in the hierarchy so service pages link up to it the same way they would to a category hub, that creates a clean two way relationship google can follow
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) gotcha, so on a service page we should have an internal link back up to the category hub page \(Tire Repair service linking to Tire Shop category page\) as well as to the service hub page.
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) exactly, service page links back up to both its parent category hub page like tire shop and the main services hub page in your nav, that two way linking reinforces the hierarchy and helps google follow the silo structure cleanly

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 13. Google Business Profile Products - worth filling out for service businesses?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/google-business-profile-products-worth-filling-out-for-service-businesses
Autor: karan-holland-4673  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  1 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Anyone using the Products section on GBP for service businesses? Worth filling out for trust or does it actually help with SEO? Keen to hear if anyone's noticed a difference.

People talk about it for more trust as it does look nice if its filled out with photos etc

[@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d)

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: yes it can work if you actually have products not just services like cleaning supplies or branded merchandise, but for pure service businesses its lower priority cause google rewards proper categories and services way more than the products section, fill it out with your top services as "products" if you have time but its trust signal more than ranking factor

## 14. GBP Landing Pages
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/gbp-landing-pages
Autor: jeffrey-sanders-5546  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  3 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
So some of my clients use a proprietary tool for their website and their is no way to extract the HTML. I will need to rebuild their website. Based on the training i understand we should try not to change the landing page much especially if it is ranking well. I assume if the site is not ranking well then its fine to change it more. But I also wanted to confirm if i change the landing page slightly due to moving to a different platform, i assume Google does not care and will not reduce the ranking at all. The last think i want is to change the platform and have my clients ranking be penalized.

RESPUESTAS:
• caleb-ulku-6571 ★: moving to a different platform has a very high likelihood of negatively impacting rank position - google hates changes
  
  I've seen it happen with the same images, same design, same content. just moving platforms and rank drops
• jeffrey-sanders-5546: How to handle this? I guess if my prospect is ranking well then I should not take them on. I guess I should only take on prospects where I can access the html, or migrate the platform to me or else if they are ranking poorly anyway.
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Jeffrey Sanders](obj://user/81aea6d4e2c04b4bbf01be934715ff1a) yeah thats a smart filter, if a client ranks well and you cant access the HTML the risk isnt worth it, focus on prospects ranking poorly where you have room to work or where they agree to platform migration as part of the engagement!

## 16. Purchased PBN Links....Rankings Dropped
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/purchased-pbn-linksrankings-dropped
Autor: colm-fitzgerald-6027  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  17 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I'm working with a chiropractor and we were seeing rankings increase. I purchased PBN links from Ice Cream Truck. Now our rankings have dropped :\(

I know PBN links can be a risk. Has anyone else experienced this?

RESPUESTAS:
• caleb-ulku-6571 ★: unlikely pbn links had that impact - rankings honestly fluctuate all the time. if it were that easy to drop rankings, it would be easier to just get low quality links to all your competitors. google is known to largely ignore bad link attacks like that for that exact reason
  
  something else is likely the cause of what you're seeing, including the possibillity that it's normal fluctuation
  • colm-fitzgerald-6027: [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) Got it. I have avoided PBN links until they were recommended in the course. Hopefully it's just normal fluctuation.
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: hey [@Colm Fitzgerald](obj://user/6ccdc587cb4a426cbb014eec0dd805ed) yeah Caleb is right, pbn links wouldnt tank your rankings that fast, more likely just normal fluctuation or something else changed on site or gbp. check if any technical issues popped up or if competitors got review surges cause that impacts rankings way more than backlinks in local seo :\)
  • colm-fitzgerald-6027: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) typically once I get the momentum going I don't see drops like that unless we do something major.
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Colm Fitzgerald](obj://user/6ccdc587cb4a426cbb014eec0dd805ed) yeah that tracks, once a site has established authority and momentum minor fluctuations smooth out and you really only see significant drops when something meaningful changes like GBP edits or technical issues. the fact that your stable until something major happens is actually a sign the foundation is solid, just keep the consistency going with GBP posts reviews and content and that momentum compounds over time
• anne-gerszewski-7814: [@Colm Fitzgerald](obj://user/6ccdc587cb4a426cbb014eec0dd805ed) I just had a huge drop on a clients account \(my lovely little cousin\). Turns our he changed his GBP location and he did not tell me... oh well :D
• neal-hopkin-3283: Please keep us updated on this [@Colm Fitzgerald](obj://user/6ccdc587cb4a426cbb014eec0dd805ed) because I have always understood PBNs to be a against Google's guidelines even if hey are only used as "validation links."
• stephan-franklyn-4962: Keep an eye on it and keep us updated - I work exclusively with chiropractors and I think just about all of mine have had rankings drop in the last couple weeks. It's been agitating and worrisome.
• hipster-marketing-3708: following this also
• douglas-page-2404: There was a major Google update the last part of March into April. The effects have been rolling out for the past 3-4 weeks as re-indexing catches up.
  • colm-fitzgerald-6027: [@Doug Page](obj://user/2667d9a03c934d32ad8bed6e5f7041e9) Yes I saw some hits to other accounts but they have recovered. This one just keeps getting worse despite new reviews, added service pages, faqs etc on site. We had serious momentum in the first month and now it's reversing :\(
  • douglas-page-2404: [@Colm Fitzgerald](obj://user/6ccdc587cb4a426cbb014eec0dd805ed) I would consider that adding the PBN links were just a coincidence of timing. It could be something else on this site that was flagged with the recent G update that is not allowing the rebound.
• chiara-burns-7791: [@Colm Fitzgerald](obj://user/6ccdc587cb4a426cbb014eec0dd805ed) [@Hipster Marketing](obj://user/35c73c3ba7b44aab8bc8b70214093d94) [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) [@Neal Hopkin](obj://user/b66f5c01d835485ba9ce7bd85d3904f5)
  
  I was today years old when I learned that Google actually considers PBNs a black hat tactic: [https://www.semrush.com/blog/private-blog-network/](https://www.semrush.com/blog/private-blog-network/)
  
  Have held off on PBNs for a long while now because had a gut feeling about it, but didn't have any backing for it until now. It does actively violate Google's webmaster guidelines. if they catch you, they heavily penalize the site.
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c) Yes, I've heard this before. I think that's why it's important that if you're going to use PBNs to use a very trusted source \(assuming you're comfortable with it\). I would be interested to know if [@Colm Fitzgerald](obj://user/6ccdc587cb4a426cbb014eec0dd805ed) ever saw improvement?
    
    Our clients' rank maps went down for a couple weeks mid-month but have since recovered and approved \(mostly\)
  • jeffrey-sanders-5546: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) was this after using pbns
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Jeffrey Sanders](obj://user/81aea6d4e2c04b4bbf01be934715ff1a) yes - although some clients went down who we did not use PBNs for
• garitey-cyril-5167: Hello, what are your anchor link ? % ? Optimized ? Generic ?
  And what are pbn health ? indexed pages ? trafic ?

## 17. Do service pages for primary category services go back to Service hub or home page or a main service page?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/do-service-pages-for-primary-category-services-go-back-to-service-hub-or-home-page-or-a-main-service-page
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Few questions here:
1\) Would a service page for Back Pain Treatment internally link back to the service hub page since there's no category page for it? Or back to the home page? Or should there be one of the main service pages that act like a category page where Back Pain Treatment would link to the service page and vise versa?

2\) Chiro adjustments are the main service but nobody really searches for that, they'd just search "Chiropractor." Should I dtill put it on the home page anyways like a typical main service?

3\) In the GBP Services for the prim cat, should it be structured where there's a main service \(chiro adjustments\) and then services underneath that like back pain treatment, neck pain treatment etc? And then there might be another service for Animal Chiro with services related to dogs and horses \(there's no secondary cat for Animal Chiro in GBP\)?

Context: part of my confusion is that "Sciatica treatment" is much more likely to get searched and I'd see that as more of a main service but Neck Pain treatment or Scoliosis Treatment or Herniated Disc Treatment seems awkward to be iwithin a main service page about Sciatica treatment.

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: for question 1 back pain treatment as a primary category service links directly back to the homepage since the homepage IS the primary category hub, no separate service hub page needed.
  
  for question 2 yes still include chiropractic adjustments on the homepage as an H2 cause google needs that signal even if people search the broader term "chiropractor", you can word it naturally like "chiropractic care and adjustments" without overthinking.
  
  for question 3 dont nest services under other services in GBP, list them all as flat parallel services under the chiropractor category cause GBP doesnt support hierarchical service structures, and each major servic all get their own dedicated pages linking back to homepage as siblings not parent child relationships
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) deleted previous reply bc question was answered in a previous comment. Thanks for this!

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 18. GBP Work
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/gbp-work
Autor: jeffrey-sanders-5546  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  3 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
In the Local SEO foundations it says for some clients we only need to do GBP work. How to handle that for pricing. Let’s say you charge 2000$ a month and the first month does GBP and Landing page work. If the client ranks as Caleb mentioned then what do we do next?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: then your monthly retainer goes toward maintenance like GBP posts review management citation monitoring rank map reporting and continuing to publish core 30 content to lock in those rankings and push for geographic expansion, theres always more work to do cause maintaining and growing rankings requires ongoing effort especially as competitors react :\)
• jeffrey-sanders-5546: Gotcha so even when they rank it’s not over :\)
• caleb-ulku-6571 ★: yep and if they're ranked everywhere they want to rank for all the keywords they want to rank for, it's time to start the conversation about adding more gbp locations =\)

## 19. GBP landing page question
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/gbp-landing-page-question
Autor: ryan-carlin-4538  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
The core30 site structure graphic doesn't show the primary category linking out to any services - only to secondary category pages.

Does the primary category page link out to secondary categories AND primary category services?

RESPUESTAS:
• mark-farrow-6630: Yes it links down to 3-4 of the highest valued services and each of the secondary categories.
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: yes the homepage which is your primary category page links out to both secondary category hub pages AND your top main primary category services directly. so structure is homepage links to all secondary category pages as H2s, plus links directly to your top primary category services the graphic simplifies it but in practice the homepage hubs everything related to your primary category services plus connects to secondary categories

• jeffrey-sanders-5546: Thanks

## 21. Opening up a second location!!!!
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/opening-up-a-second-location
Autor: aiden-hardy-8619  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  4 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
So a couple months ago I onboarded a plumber in a county of 100k we ranked him number 1 in 45 days
then we hit a page limit on GoDaddy's website builder so we have currently been working on revamping his site and migrating it to a platform that can hold more pages

We had a conversation yesterday and was saying he wanted more work in a county that neighbors his with a population of 800k. So i expressed the idea of buying another google profile and expanding them out into that other county more opportunity for everyone!

Basically now I have to learn how to bridge 2 GBPS to the same website multi location GBP Ive seen the pictures on how it should be linked and what not but is there anything I'm missing in terms of setting up the google profile? should it be the exact same? just linking the site properly

If anyone has anything to give me pointers or tips or anything about multi location sites please im all ears!!!

RESPUESTAS:
• aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) big one for ya today
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: congrats on the expansion! for the second GBP make sure it has a unique phone number different from the first profile, completely different physical address in the new county, then link it to a dedicated location subpage on the main site that has its own core 30 service pages structure under it, same categories and services are fine but each location needs its own dedicated landing page and unique local content
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) so redo all all core 30 pages for the area on the same site?
    
    So for example for the one gbp
    Plumber Putnam county ny
    
    Second gbp
    
    Plumber westchester county ny
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) yes all the core 30 pages for the new area on the same site under a westchester subfolder like [yoursite.com/westchester](http://yoursite.com/westchester/) with its own targeting plumber westchester county ny + dedicated service and category pages for that location, same structure as putnam but unique content referencing westchester specific neighborhoods cities and landmarks, this way both locations get their full core 30 silo while keeping all the domain authority consolidated on one site

## 22. updating GBP address
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/updating-gbp-address
Autor: nat-couropmitree-1767  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  4 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I advised a client to get an office because his competition all have addresses. Once he secures the office, what needs to happen before the GBP address is updated, assuming the address will trigger reverification?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: yes reverification will be required, before updating the GBP address make sure you have clear business signage at the new office, or utility bills etc showing the business name at the new address, and update the website footer and schema to match the new address so all signals align when google does the video verification
  • nat-couropmitree-1767: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) thank you
• mark-farrow-6630: Add that address to his incorporation with the state, use that for the verification with an invoice with that address on it to any vendor, get business cards made with that address on it. Show those with permanent signage = win.
  • nat-couropmitree-1767: [@Mark Farrow](obj://user/5d7f726c110a43dc973a719104a5e4b4) thank you

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 23. What does everyone do for KPIs?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/what-does-everyone-do-for-kpis
Autor: karan-holland-4673  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  4 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Keen to hear how you're all tracking performance. What KPIs do you actually use day to day, month to month? As clients always love KPIs

RESPUESTAS:
• karan-holland-4673: [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d)
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: the main KPIs that matter for local SEO clients are rank map green coverage percentage tracked monthly in leadsnap, GBP insights showing calls direction requests and website clicks, search impressions and clicks from GSC, total review count and review velocity, and number of new core 30 pages published and indexed. visual rank map comparisons month over month are the most powerful KPI cause clients can literally see the red turning to green, and combine that with call volume increases from GBP insights and you have the perfect monthly report that justifies the retainer and shows real business impact :\)
• mark-farrow-6630: The more communication the better especially early on. The comparison maps sometimes even 7-15 days movement is nice to show early on from work provided.
• caleb-ulku-6571 ★: yep just rank map green coverage
  
  as Mark said, we heavily front load client communication - check out our first 60 day plan for details

## 24. Potentially taking on client who's keyword stuffed in their GBP...change it or leave it?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/potentially-taking-on-client-whos-keyword-stuffed-in-their-gbpchange-it-or-leave-it
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  4 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
The client's GBP is essentially ABC Auto Repair | Chicago Mechanic. I know that's keyword stuffing so I'm assuming to take that down. I'd like to know:

[ol:1][li]will their rankings drop \(already not the best but avg around 5, not 15-20\)[li]If so, is it worth getting a DBA and using that? And then changing all citations to that?[li]Is there any situation where you'd leave it?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: dont change the GBP name yet cause changing the business name can trigger reverification or suspension, if the rankings are decent at avg 5 leave it for now and focus on the other ranking core 30 site structure review velocity ,citations and validation links, those will improve rankings without touching the risky GBP name change. only consider the DBA route if you can legally register the brand name then update GBP citations and website consistently but honestly if its working leave it alone cause cleaning up a keyword stuffed name on an established profile carries real suspension risk that isnt worth the slight cleanup benefit
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) got it - and then for citations especially in LeadSnap, would we have it do the same name at the GBP across everything? Also, would we do that for the NAP on the GBP LP & Footer? Or in the title of the GBP is the only place it'll be?
  • mark-farrow-6630: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) make it consistent. Then if Google hits you with the change, go back thru and update site and citations. Pain for sure.
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Mark Farrow](obj://user/5d7f726c110a43dc973a719104a5e4b4) makes sense, thanks!

## 25. Next steps here
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/next-steps-here
Autor: aiden-hardy-8619  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  32 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Core 30 built out

Haven't purchased a chamber link yet

Wondering if we pivot to location pages or FAQs

im curious as what the play is here because basically the entire right side of the map is red

RESPUESTAS:
• aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d)
• mark-farrow-6630: Dont rush into location pages, build out topical relevance first - looks like a great start.
• douglas-page-2404: What does the top player in this market look like? Are your pins too far apart?
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Doug Page](obj://user/2667d9a03c934d32ad8bed6e5f7041e9)
  • douglas-page-2404: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) Then you definitely need to keep building topical relevance as Mark said above. Maybe to give you some ideas, carefully study the GPB's and sites of the top 1 or 2 competitors to see if you notice any gaps.
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Doug Page](obj://user/2667d9a03c934d32ad8bed6e5f7041e9) word great advice
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Doug Page](obj://user/2667d9a03c934d32ad8bed6e5f7041e9) what kind of gaps should I be looking out for honestly competitor analysis and what not is definitely our weakest part of our game we know the work it takes we understand how to get clients we have great client relations but understanding competition definitely don’t know much about
  • douglas-page-2404: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) I collect competitive data during my initial audit. It helps steer me in the right direction to know what I'm up against. If you're not doing this at the beginning, I highly suggest doing so. Even in quoting prices for an offer, how would you know the difficulty of getting the client to the top 3? You need to know this in order to lay the proper expectations out for the client as well as to the amount of work it will take to move the needle.
    Analyzing the GBPs is pretty easy to do with GMB Everywhere. Gap analysis of the site is lot more dedicated work but necessary. You're basically looking at their site to evaluate what information you might be missing on yours. You need to analyze the other sites from a position of authority as well. Older sites that are well structured and have been receiving great interaction signals for a long time are going to have a high reputation score with Google. There is a part in the course where Caleb goes over identifying low competition. He talked about other things to check. You might want to look over that section as a refresh.
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: based on your rank map you still need topical authority before moving into geographical man, your only at like 15-20% green coverage and core 30 says you gotta hit 30-40% before expanding geographically. the entire right side being red means topical depth hasnt registered yet across your full service area
  
  id suggest finish topical authority first, double check every core 30 page is indexed in gsc cause unindexed pages dont contribute to topical signals, then check rank map again after 1 week. if still stuck at same coverage add faq supp content to each service page with internal links back to category and homepage, and start validation links one per published page like Caleb teaches
  
  dont pivot to location pages yet cause your foundation isnt strong enough, geographical expansion only works after topical authority is solid in your immediate area :\)
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) yeah so where am I gonna find these validation links I ranked a plumber in a county without one single link other then chambers to the home page
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) you can use [https://www.qgp.com/](https://www.qgp.com/) for validation links, and yeah you can rank without them but to stay ranked consistent you need one per page cause its so easy to build pages in bulk now with AI that google needs that external signal to know your site is real authority not just ai slop content
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) alright word what’s average price normally?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) you can check their pricing page
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) and the $25 ones do well?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) yes for each page cuase you know you need to build authory for each page in google eyes
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) alright man fuck I gotta lot of back links to buy
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) in terms of pricing how should we break up these back links to not get fucked with like $500 a month on them yk like maybe if it’s indexing pages slower to expand that $500 longer and upping the retainer slightly
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) If the backlink cost is too high for your budget, you can look at more affordable providers on Legiit
    Just make sure you’re still focusing on quality over quantity, even at a lower price point
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) okay it’s called legiit?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) yeah legiit.com
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) they’re all quality enough?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) Hard to say without seeing them
    Best move is to check who had good reviews, and ask for sample work they’ve done so you can verify quality before buying
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) so why can’t we just index the pages using Google? For now atleast while the budget expands are PBNs really that great? It’s hard to tell my profit margins right now because of performance marketing
    
    Honestly we can still do every single core 30 step just the doubling down of PBNs kind of fucks our profit margins slightly
    
    I know that someone does $1 pbn links I don’t know how to really validate them or what would make it invalid
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) It’s not just for indexing like I mentioned earlier.
    Yeah you can rank without them, but the issue is longterm stability with how easy it is to generate pages in bulk using AI now, Google relies more on external signals to confirm authority and trust.
    Backlinks or PBNs when used properly help reinforce that the site is actually legit and not just mass produced content
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) understood, so taking the extra POP steps and doing all the SEO perfectly isn't enough? We just need more trust signals? also what might be detrimental with a $1 pbn link for example I know [indexmenow.com](http://indexmenow.com) does it for $1 and gets the page indexed the next day,
    
    im at the point where if the risk isnt too high I might just rip those if the highest risk is that theyll be taken down I rather that be a later problem when I have a more consistent cash flow, if it s a problem of yk it fucking shit up thats a entire different convo
    
    also why cant you just make your own PBN links overtime? and just house them to yourself
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) $1 PBN links are risky cause they're usually mass produced low quality sites that can hurt your site by associating it with spammy neighborhoods, building your own PBN is possible but expensive and time consuming with expired domains hosting and content costs adding up fast. if budget is tight skip PBNs for now and focus on chamber memberships citations and reviews cause those move the needle without the cost
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) okay and we can still drive jobs and shit, and bring value over time? Reinforce later?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) yes you can still drive real jobs and revenue with just GBP optimization core 30 pages citations reviews and chamber links, those fundamentals alone get clients ranking and generating calls without needing PBN links at all, but make sure to reinforce with paid links later once cash flow allows
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) I understand totally how likely is it calls and jobs get driven without a GBP getting ranked 1? Because our one dude we’ve bumped his impressions up 100 overnight he’s ranking extremely well for other cats on like day 3 man
    
    I just want to break ROI for these guys early so we can work together for a extended amount of time
    
    Because my clients all 5 of them have all expressed how they can refer me to everyone and everything I just want to make sure we’re executing the steps properly
    
    I care more about the results we bring rather then the money we collect man
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) with that mindset you'll do great, for your question about generating calls without ranking 1 yes possible, businesses ranking 2-5 still generate calls especially with strong reviews and with proper GBP setup but not too much calls but good percentage, focus on getting them solidly into top 3 in 60+ days with core 30 and let the referrals do the rest

## 26. Multiple Locations, Multiple GBPs & One Website
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/multiple-locations-multiple-gbps-one-website
Autor: chris-beazley-9965  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  5 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I would be really interested in your thoughts on a proposal I'm putting together a proposal for a well-established business that has been trading for 50+ years.

They operate from 5 locations across England and run 2–3 distinct service brands under the same parent company.

Each location has its own Google Business Profile, all pointing to one main website. They already appear in some map packs but the profiles are underleveraged — sparse descriptions, inconsistent categories, weak review velocity, no posting activity.

The strategic question:
They have roughly 7 GBPs across the business including a separate brand that has its own dedicated website. Should each GBP link to a dedicated location landing page on the main site, or should the separate brand GBP link exclusively to its own website? My instinct is location-specific landing pages per depot on the main site, with the standalone brand GBP pointing to its own site as it operates as a genuinely separate brand. Does that hold up?

The website structure problem:
The site has no location silos, no embedded Maps, no links to or from the GBPs, and virtually no local schema. A [Schema.org](http://Schema.org) test returns only two detected types — WebPage and hatom — nothing local, nothing service-specific, nothing that connects the site meaningfully to any of the 7 GBPs.

My plan is to build:
[ul][li]Dedicated location landing pages per depot[li]LocalBusiness schema per location matching each GBP exactly \(NAP, hours, coordinates, service area\)[li]Organisation and Service schema across the site[li]Embedded Google Maps per location page[li]GBP links and review CTAs integrated into the site[li]A proper internal linking structure creating location silos

The goal is to get the website and the GBPs speaking the same language so Google has no ambiguity about which page belongs to which physical location.

The pricing question:
I'm proposing two separate fees:
One-off setup — £6,000 total
[ul][li]GBP and strategy setup \(full audit, competitor analysis, keyword mapping, NAP audit, location page briefs, schema specification, baseline rankings\): £2,500[li]Website implementation \(7 location pages, full schema build, Maps integration, silo structure, GBP integration\): £3,500

Monthly retainer — £3,500/month Covering 7 GBP profiles across 3 service brands and 5 geographic markets — active management, posting, review strategy, citation management, competitor monitoring, website strategy oversight and monthly reporting.

This feels right given the scope but I'd welcome a gut check from anyone managing multi-location clients at this scale.

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: hey [@Chris Beazley](obj://user/5d93490bb0c8499d9e7754083384e141) your strategy is solid and your instinct on the GBP routing is correct, each of the 5 location GBPs on the parent brand should point to dedicated location landing pages on the main site only, and the standalone brand GBP should keep pointing to its own separate website since it operates as a distinct brand entity. honestly pricing depends a lot on your experienc, the 6k setup and 3500 monthly is reasonable but if you have a strong portfolio of multi location wins you can justify higher easily cause 7 GBPs across 3 brands and 5 markets is genuinely complex work. if your earlier in your journey that pricing might be more aligned with where you can confidently deliver, dont overprice yourself out of the deal but also dont underprice the scope cause they have 50 years of business equity at stake. either way the strategy is solid, GBPs to dedicated location pages on main site with the separate brand pointing to its own site is exactly right :\)
  • chris-beazley-9965: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Thank you once again.
  • chris-beazley-9965: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) If I am using LeadSnap to manage this account do I require multiple citations for each GBP location even though the all link to one website, or just one citation account for the head office? Thanks
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Chris Beazley](obj://user/5d93490bb0c8499d9e7754083384e141) yes you need separate citation in leadsnap for each GBP cause each one has its own unique NAP that needs to be cited consistently across directories, one citation account per GBP location not per website
  • chris-beazley-9965: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Thank you

## 28. Landing Page Redirect
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/landing-page-redirect
Autor: mark-fazio-5761  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
My homepage is my landing page. I have it set up to whenever you go to my domain it redirects it to the URL format DOMAIN/\[primary-category-slug\]-\[city-slug\]-\[state-abbrev\]. Do I really need that long URL as a landing page or can I just use my regular domain url? Its easier to just put my domain instead of that long URL when Im doing backlinks or whatever

RESPUESTAS:
• caleb-ulku-6571 ★: yeah the straight domain is much better assuming it isn't ranking - changing the gbp landing page url will often have a negative impact on rank if it's doing well but we wouldn't do a 301 redirect off of the homepage
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: just use your regular domain URL as the homepage and landing page, the redirect to a long slug version is unnecessary and actually weakens your link equity cause every backlink to your domain has to pass through a 301 redirect, simplify by making the homepage itself target your primary category + city through H1 title tag and content without any redirect

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 29. GBP Rankings Not Moving Despite Core 30 + — What Are We Missing?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/gbp-rankings-not-moving-despite-core-30-what-are-we-missing
Autor: tomas-affranchino-9734  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  6 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hi team,
Would love some feedback on this one.
I have a building inspector client whose GBP/maps rankings are basically not moving despite us following the Core 30 process pretty closely for their primary keyword building inspector melbourne. However, what I do notice is that they are getting some traction for the suburb the coworking is listed but still not optimal.

So far we’ve:
[ul][li]Rolled out Core 30[li]Completed the main service pages[li]Built internal links[li]Connected citations via LeadSnap[li]Been focusing on topical relevance/content clusters \(still more to grow here\)[li]Started getting some traction in traditional search + even some visibility in AI models after publishing a listicle
Site: [https://inscopeinspections.com.au/](https://inscopeinspections.com.au/)

The challenge is specifically the Maps/GBP side — rankings are still stagnant.
One thing to note is that the GBP was only recently verified as it was a SAB business and recently used a coworking address, so I’m wondering whether this is more of a trust/age issue vs something missing in execution. I still need to get them into the Chamber of Commerce and continue authority building.
At this stage, would you:
[ul][li]Re-run a full audit?[li]Double down on authority/citations/reviews?[li]Push behavioural signals?[li]Focus more on proximity/service area optimisation?[li]Or is there another lever you’d prioritise first to move the needle faster?
Curious what you’d do next in this situation. Do we still just focus on blogs and maybe tweaking the service page to reflect the hierarchy better or what would you recommend?

THx!

RESPUESTAS:
• mark-farrow-6630: How long ago was the SAB converted to the address? A few comments from what I see.
  Address City on the GBP is Moonee Ponds, Homepage is optimized for Melbourne. I realize its close, they should match up. I've done this and ranked but should have them in alignment.
  Primary Category is Home Inspector on the GBP, homepage is optimized for Building Inspections.
  Im not seeing Category pages for Building Consultant or Pest Control Service
  Minimize links in main menu and footer, there are a lot.
  phone number is different on GBP than site - tracking number?
  Did you backlink an external link to all pages created?
  Create / Join any local authority links yet?
  I don't see any links from your FAQ's yet - have you started creating SUPP content to grow topical authority?
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: hey [@Tomas Affranchino](obj://user/5a215abd0bff47028103c902b023920c) Mark is spot on with every point , these alignment issues are actually whats blocking your maps rankings more than gbp age
  
  fix this first before anything else, change homepage h1 and title to match gbp primary category actually is + the gbp city. then build the missing category pages. also sort the phone number mismatch cause nap inconsistency hurts trust signals basically you need full topical authority to outrank, looking at your rank maps your basically invisible across the whole grid 0% top 3, but on "building inspector moonee ponds" your at avg 8.28% top 3 which proves core 30 is working its just the geographic scope is too big you can see the difference that its baiscally address issues of site thats mismatched with gbp and next moves in priority, double down on review velocity push, build location pages for surrounding melbourne suburbs cause your already ranking moonee ponds so expand from there one suburb at a time, get chamber of commerce backlinks done they hit different than citations
  
  on validation links and supp content Marks 100% right, every published page needs one external link per Calebs framework and faq with internal links to service pages builds topical authority. fix the alignment issues first cause those are foundational, then layer in link building and supp content after. mark caught the real problem here
• tomas-affranchino-9734: Thanks [@Mark Farrow](obj://user/5d7f726c110a43dc973a719104a5e4b4) and [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) really solid inputs.
  
  Some clarifications below:
  [ol:1][li]Yes moone poonds is a very small suburb \(8km\) to melbourne cbd approx hence why we are targeting melbourne which I've done with other industries and has always work.[li]Primary category is an interesting one: In the backend its actually building inspector but Google is changing it- what do we do in that case and is there a tweak around it?[li]Building Consultant/Pest Control Service- there is a bit of an overlap with the combined building and inspection service page and timber pest inspection- the building consultant the client didn't want to build a page- do we absolutely need it? Pest control service- it also reads strange- do we do category re theme or what would you recommend?[li]Footer links- great insight- is that something you always recommend keeping minimal- e.g. just 'Services' page and then link to all services there?[li]Phone number- its actually a call tracking number- do we need to change it?[li]85% of them had a PBN link as per the training. In terms of chamber of commerce not yet- its coming up this sprint.[li]Just published some blogs and was going to internally link to them from FAQ and then get an external link as well- Once we hit the supporting content for the main category we move to the next and so on or whats the best approach?[li]Shall I focus on topical relevance in this case or geographical relevance?
  
  • mark-farrow-6630: [@Tomas Affranchino](obj://user/5a215abd0bff47028103c902b023920c) 4. No links in footer, is ok!
    5. make sure your real business phone number is the secondary number on the GBP then.
    7. On those blogs also link them up to the Service page they are supporting.
    8. 100% topical relevance focus.
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: 
  [ol:1][li]moonee ponds 8km from cbd is fine for melbourne targeting, just add moonee ponds as h2 or subtitle so both signals are present.[li]if google keeps changing to home inspector their algorithm thinks thats what you are or the 2nd thing is on your location google dont suggest that category cause google show category based on region[li]you need dedicated pages for every gbp category period, retheme pest control to timber pest inspection if it fits better, and push the client on building consultant cause your leaving topical authority on the table without it[li]yeah keep footer minimal, just services about contact and main category pages, more links dilutes internal authority[li]tracking number is fine just make sure gbp and website show the exact same number, no mismatches[li]push pbn to 100% on every published page before chamber, chamber is bonus not foundational[li]build out supp content for one category, internal link to parent and service pages, get external links, then move to next category. dont spread thin[li]topical first 100%, your already ranking moonee ponds so geographic is fine, the gap is topical depth across full melbourne grid
• tomas-affranchino-9734: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Thx for jumping. Amazing, we actually already have a timber pest inspection so will look to create the business consultant as a top priority. Thx for the nuggets!

## 30. site map
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/site-map
Autor: brandon-mac-6006  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  1 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I submitted my site to google search console and wanted to know if it is okay that is says google has indexed 18 pages and didn't index 11 pages? any advice?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: yes thats normal but check each page manually with site:yourdomain.com to confirm what's actually indexed cause sometimes GSC reports lag behind reality, also check the not indexed reasons in GSC under indexing report to see if those 11 are page with redirect duplicate canonical or actually problem pages, normal pages with redirects or canonicals showing as not indexed isnt an issue but if youre seeing crawled not indexed or discovered not indexed thats worth investigating

## 31. Early GBP seeing first movement, normal timeline?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/early-gbp-seeing-first-movement-normal-timeline
Autor: karan-holland-4673  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  1 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Cleaning client in Christchurch NZ, GBP rebuilt from scratch about 4 months ago. Core 30 is done and indexed, still got 2 pages to go but everything is live. Finally seeing some movement on the heatmap, got a number 1 in central Christchurch and a cluster of oranges around the business location. Avg ranking 19.53, top 3 at 0.59%. Competitors are sitting at avg rank 7-11 with 38-46% top 3.

Is this normal progress for an early GBP? Also thinking of moving into FAQ content now for authority since there's not much on the site. Good next step or should I be doing something else first?

[@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d)

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: hey [@Karan Holland](obj://user/98eb442ef7f44e1bae025edeee895522) yeah this is exactly where you should be seeing first movement, that #1 in central christchurch and orange cluster around the business means topical relevance is registering with google
  
  the gap with competitors at avg rank 7-11 is mostly reviews and GBP age not core 30 structure, your foundation is solid. only 9 reviews is your biggest gap, push hard on review velocity cause competitors prob have 100+ each. finish the remaining 2 pages first then add validation links per page, push reviews and ensure schema is on every page. FAQ can wait till core 30 is fully indexed then move to geographically
