## 32. Ranking an SAB
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/ranking-an-sab
Autor: aiden-hardy-8619  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  14 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
So I have an SAB plumber who doesn’t have an official offense but would be fine with his home address is just afraid of going through verification because the GBP gets him business

Is there any different approach to ranking an SAB or is it the same shit just a little harder

Because I was honestly thinking we push rankings as an SAB for awhile 170+ 5 star reviews, domain and GBP 5 years old since covid lol

We can def still rank him, it’s just a competitive county in terms of population

Just terms of is the approach any different

RESPUESTAS:
• aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) herewe go again son
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: the approach is the same just harder cause you lose the proximity advantage that physical address listings have, but with 5 years of GBP age + 170 reviews and a 5 year domain hes actually in a really strong position to rank as SAB if you push the other levers hard. focus on review velocity stay, full core 30 site build with location pages for each city in his service area, strong validation links per page, monthly PR for entity signals, and aggressive citation building. the established age and review base gives you a massive head start that newer SAB clients dont have, you can improve as SAB it just takes 6-9 months of consistent work vs the 3-4 months a physical address profile might take in the same market
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) is there a reason to take it slow with content uploading early? And ramp it up over time
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) cause google needs time to crawl index and trust new pages, dumping 30+ pages at once on a fresh site signals automated content generation and risks pages getting filtered. start with 8-12 pages week 1 then 4-6 per week after that until all pages are live, this paced publishing looks like natural growth and lets google properly evaluate each batc
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) when do chamber links come in?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) chamber links come in early phase 1 right after the initial site launch cause thats high authority foundational link that establish local credibility, ideally join the chamber of commerce in your clients primary service area within the first 2-4 weeks of the engagement so the link is indexed and passing authority while you're still building out the core 30 pages
• mark-farrow-6630: Go early on the chambers as much as you can afford, and grab him an address. 🤷🏼
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Mark Farrow](obj://user/5d7f726c110a43dc973a719104a5e4b4) where would we find an address you think? And how much would an office cost? Like he has a home address he just doesn’t want to run the risk of verification litterally after fixing his website and getting his main cats indexed he’s ranking top 3 for emergency plumber and drainage service
  • mark-farrow-6630: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) virtual, it can be done. Just did another one this week.
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Mark Farrow](obj://user/5d7f726c110a43dc973a719104a5e4b4) what’s the process of finding him an office and what’s cost ?
  • mark-farrow-6630: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) search for office, call office, get quote, get quote approved, get owner to do DBA with that address, wait for that to come in, google print shop, call print shop, ask the if they do polycarbonate signs, order 2 signs with business name and that address, once you have the business docs and sign go to office, rent it for two hours, hang signs one in front one on office door, film V video, wait an see. 🙌
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Mark Farrow](obj://user/5d7f726c110a43dc973a719104a5e4b4) so you rent the office for 2 hours do the video and never rent it again😂 that’s genius
  • mark-farrow-6630: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) sorry rent it on a monthly virtual, get it verified by renting the physical for 2 hours. Once verified if you have city name in the business name, keep it for a couple months because you’ll likely get suspended. Go back get reverified then you can cancel the monthly. Explain it all to the owner. Have them understand the cost. Charge them for all this work.
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Mark Farrow](obj://user/5d7f726c110a43dc973a719104a5e4b4) I thought my deliverables were good man, that’s some serious game I’m gonna have to try it

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 33. Wix doesn't seem to render HTML code to Google - can Google still see the review embed?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/wix-doesnt-seem-to-render-html-code-to-google-can-google-still-see-the-review-embed
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  5 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I recently learned Wix does something to HTML embedded codes that prevents Google from seeing everything. Does that effect the Review Embed code & Schema?

I'd imagine if Google can't see it, it could be harmful to put the Review Schema on the page because Google doesn't see the Review Embed but wanted to double check? I still do them for Goal Completion.

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: Wix review embeds can be fine for users, but Google may not fully read JS embed content the same way it reads normal HTML. I’d only use review schema if the reviews are actually visible on the page and the structured data matches the visible content
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) yes, the review embed will show to the user. Just wanted to make sure it wouldn't hurt anything to still have the review schema on there even if it doesn't render to Google.
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) ok then if the embed is JS skip the review schema to avoid visible/markup mismatch
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) okay that makes sense, will do. I'm not sure if it's JS but anything I do that's HTML on a wix website doesn't seem to show for Google, unfortunately. I believe Wix has an add-on for Trustindex but I haven't been able to figure it out yet. Thanks!
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) you can chek page source and give that to claude it can help you in finding what is that html or not :\)

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 34. Having issues separating additional category services for Family law Attorney and Divorce Lawyer
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/having-issues-separating-additional-category-services-for-family-law-attorney-and-divorce-lawyer
Autor: bayley-cook-8120  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  4 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
What I add something like "Child support litigation" as a service under one it automatically adds it to both additional category sections. Is this going to cause bad vibes with google since I am double listing services?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: yeah you can manually remove the duplicate service from one of the categories if google auto adds it to both, just delete it from whichever category fits less and keep it under the more relevant one, that prevents the double listing without causing any issues
  • bayley-cook-8120: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) deleting from one seems to delete from both
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Bayley Cook](obj://user/eb5369650be94a3c943eb5ae2121fb36) honestly it's weird then you should just add your services and ignore google adding for 1-2 weeks then try to remove duplicate services
• mark-farrow-6630: Hi Bailey, I’ve had this happen, and I left them in both, then on the website only built out the Service page under one of them. Has not seemed to make a difference in my instance..

I'm also not clear on whether it's implemented for existing projects or just new ones

## 36. Looking for some input on a dual GBP situation — what would you do?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/looking-for-some-input-on-a-dual-gbp-situation-what-would-you-do
Autor: chris-beazley-9965  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  3 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Picked up a prospect this week in the trades space — plumbing and heating contractor, been going a while, decent reputation locally but completely invisible on Maps. Both heatmaps are 0% market share across the board.

When I dug in I found this:

They have two GBPs at the same address. One categorised as Plumber, one as Central Heating Service. Same phone number listed on Profile 1 and the website. Different number on Profile 2. Both profiles link to the same website.

The website domain has no brand name in it whatsoever.

Their email domain is completely different again from the website domain.

Nine reviews split across both profiles.

My read is that Google is just confused. Two listings, same address, overlapping services, fragmented brand signals everywhere — neither profile has enough clean authority to rank for anything.

My instinct is to consolidate into one profile rather than try to make two work.

Primary category Plumber, then add Heating Contractor, Boiler Installation Service and Gas Engineer as secondaries. Carefully handle the second profile so no reviews are lost in the process.

A few things I'd love input on:

[ol:1][li]Anyone successfully merged two profiles at the same address without losing reviews? What was your approach — support ticket, mark as closed, something else?[li]Is there ever a genuine case for keeping two profiles at the same address in this kind of trade setup, or is consolidation always the cleaner answer?[li]With only 9 reviews on each profile, does a merge realistically combine that authority or does it depend on which profile Google treats as the "survivor"?[li]The website is doing no heavy lifting at all — no dedicated service pages, broken internal links, homepage hero talks about the business rather than the customer's problem. Would you tackle the GBP first and website second, or do both simultaneously to get the on-page signals working straight away?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: your thinking is right, 2 GBPs at same address with overlapping services is whats keeping them at 0% market share. for consolidating mark profile 2 as permanently closed which preserves its reviews while removing it from active competition, you cant actually merge GBPs without losing reviews so the closed status is the cleanest play. its rare that two profiles at the same address makes sense unless theyre genuinely separate legal entities like distinct LLCs with different stuff,
  
  the 9 reviews on the closed profile dont transfer to the surviving 1 technically but they stay visible on closed listing for brand credibility. tackle both GBP and website simultaneously cause they feed each other, consolidate the GBP and update the surviving profile to the website then rebuild the website with proper core 30 structure including service pages for plumbing heating boiler installation and gas engineering doing them in parallel gets you ranking faster than sequential cleanup
  • chris-beazley-9965: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Thank you. What would you say to the client about a website url being different to the email address?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Chris Beazley](obj://user/5d93490bb0c8499d9e7754083384e141) definitely flag it to the client as a trust signal issue, mismatched website and email domains look unprofessional to customes, recommend they get a matching email domain set up like info@theirwebsitedomain.com which is usually free with their hosting

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 39. Godaddy to hostinger
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/godaddy-to-hostinger
Autor: aiden-hardy-8619  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  5 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I got my website ready to transfer as soon as I get everything in place what needs to be done

Just got a couple questions in terms of how to make this as smooth as possible

RESPUESTAS:
• aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) 😔😔😔 sorry again
  • aiden-hardy-8619: Alright I’m gonna need the process of how we do this
    
    I matched every page name up correctly home page everything
    
    We have the domain ready to transfer so in terms of sight maps and indexing and ranking drops what should I expect
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: migration process is normal, 1 export godaddy content as backup, 2 set up hostinger with matching URLs, 3 QA on staging first, 4 transfer DNS only when ready, 5 submit new sitemap to GSC and request indexing, 6 set up 301 redirects from old URLs even if slugs match. yes after migration expect ranking drops for 2-4 weeks which is normal, then recovery and usually improvement once google fully crawls the new site, keep NAP consistent and dont stack other changes during this window
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) how important is the social icon? Having it on every page
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) it's just a icon it doesn't actually affect on SEO

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 40. Small question
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/small-question
Autor: aiden-hardy-8619  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
So I have weekly gbp posts setup for a plumber of mine

he wants to know if he should keep doing his daily ones and if they’re that important in terms of SEO

I didn’t wanna give him false information

RESPUESTAS:
• aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) here we go again….
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: GBP posts have minimal direct SEO impact but they do signal active business management which is a also ranking factor and help with conversion when people land on the GBP, weekly posts are plenty for SEO benefit, daily posts give diminishing returns so if hes doing them for engagement and conversion thats good

## 41. New GBP or updating an old GBP
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/new-gbp-or-updating-an-old-gbp
Autor: igor-bubis-6307  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I have a client who operates his business from his home. After a lengthy approval process, Google allowed him to verify and claim a Google Business Profile using his home address, which we will call Address A. This was completed about nine months ago.
The client has now moved approximately 10 miles away to a new residence, which we will call Address B, and has asked me to establish a business presence there as well.
From my perspective, there appear to be two possible approaches:
[ol:1][li]Create a new GBP using Address B, resulting in two active GBPs.[li]Update the existing GBP by changing the address from Address A to Address B.
What would you recommend as the best approach in this situation?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: id go with option 2 update the existing GBP to address B not create a new one cause running 2 GBPs for the same business at different home addresses will absolutely trigger duplicate detection just update the existing profile to the new address and keep the 9 months of established history reviews and ranking signals intact, prepare for reverification cause address changes typically trigger it especially for home based businesses
  • igor-bubis-6307: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Great, that is the direction I was leaning toward as well. Now I just need to find a simple way to explain it to the client.

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 42. Category pages for personal injury
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/category-pages-for-personal-injury
Autor: kevin-tully-4285  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  4 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I’m new to the core 30 concept and trying to wrap my head around how this would apply for my personal injury law firm website. The GBP categories that I see my competitors ranking with are things like personal injury, Lawyer, Law firm, legal service, lawyer. My understanding is that I want to have a category page for each of these however they seem very redundant. How should I think about the content that goes on each of these pages and which pages link from each?

The structure that I’m familiar with is to build location hubs with service area pages linking off of the location hub. For example, personal injury, lawyer Denver, which includes the category, personal injury lawyer. I’m not used to seeing an additional page called Law Firm Denver.

I also understand that we’re prioritizing the GBP over everything else and that I’m here because when I was doing previously wasn’t working. So I’m OK with this. I just wanna make sure I’m going about it the right way.

Thanks

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: those categories actually do represent different search intents and each deserves its own dedicated hub page in core 30. personal injury lawyer targets accident victims looking for compensation, lawyer is broader for anyone seeking legal representation, law firm targets people researching established legal practices, and legal service is for general legal assistance searches. build each as its own category hub page with unique content targeting that specific intent, personal injury lawyer denver covers injury cases, lawyer denver covers broader legal services overview, law firm denver covers the firms practice areas and credentials, legal service denver covers general legal help. each hub links down to specific service pages underneath and they cross link to each other that gives you full coverage of all the GBP category search intents
• kevin-tully-4285: Thanks [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) What if we only do personal injury law? Do we still want to use those broader topics?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Kevin Tully](obj://user/3874991bbae44ce19f8f42dd6129c9c0) yes still use the broader categories as secondary GBP categories and build hub pages for each cause that captures broader search intent and entity signals google rewards, even if PI is your only practice area those broader categories help establish authority and capture searchers who use general legal terms when looking for help :\)
• mark-farrow-6630: Setting the sail to capture a wider swath - Entity

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 43. Question about structuring GBP Landing Page > Category Pages > Services Pages
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/question-about-structuring-gbp-landing-page-category-pages-services-pages
Autor: ryan-carlin-4538  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  1 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I have two questions

First- I've watched all of the site structure videos relating to Core30.

The graphic of the site structure shows:
[ul][li]GBP landing page[li]GBP category 1[li]GBP category 2[li]GBP category 3

Are Category 1, 2, and 3 all secondary categories? Or is Category 1 the primary GBP category? It it seems to make sense that the GBP landing page is the primary category page, and the categories 1, 2, and 3 are all secondary categories.

Can you confirm?

Second,

We have fairly poor ranking in Rank Maps and we only have one GBP profile. It is ranking well in the immediate area where the address is listed, and it is ranking in some other areas for some other keywords that we find valuable.

I know that Caleb suggests not replacing a GBP landing page URL, but my client and I are discussing the pros and cons. Yes, we will lose ranking if we remove it. We understand that, but in the long term, is it better to change the GBP landing page?

This specific scenario:

[ol:1][li]For context: planning to do multi-location GBPs [li]Right now, the GBP landing page url is a location page that has built up a lot of authority over time. It's a solid page with a good profile. [li]The training suggests having the GBP landing page location number one be the home page. 

The nuances for us are: the GBP landing page is a "sell my house fast Long Island" landing page. It's not a specific city - rather a region. Additionally, Sell my house fast is not a primary category, so we would need to retheme that page \(doing it anyways\)

I know the course and Caleb strongly recommend against this, but given that my client brought it up and is open to it - we're wrestling with the idea: if the current URL/architecture isn't ideal, is it worth biting the bullet and changing it now \(and eating the temporary ranking hit\) to get clean architecture permanently?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: hey [@Ryan Carlin](obj://user/e09047f8464844058cf81ab78843cc43) for question 1 yes GBP landing page is the primary categry hub and categories 1, 2, 3 in the graphic are the secondary cats that each get their own dedicated hub page, the homepage IS the primary categry page in the core 30 structure not a separate page
  
  for question 2 honestly id still side with Caleb here, dont change the GBP landing page URL even though youre open to the short term hit. the issue is your current page has built up authority signals google associates with that specific URL and changing it means starting from zero on the new URL while losing what works now. better play is keep the existing GBP landing page where it is, but retheme that pages content to better align with your primary category, you can rebuild the content structure without changing the URL. for multi location each new GBP gets its own dedicated location page so the existing page stays as long island primary while new GBPs point to new dedicated URLs, you get clean architecture going forward without sacrificing the existing authority

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 45. How to integrate a service page hub with client's existing service page?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/how-to-integrate-a-service-page-hub-with-clients-existing-service-page
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  12 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
So they have this design where the service page has a list of their services and an image. It's stock photos.

It seems a little odd to have that setup and then having the Hub page similar to the Core 30 example site's service page: [https://ai-seo-pro-gary.lovable.app/services](https://ai-seo-pro-gary.lovable.app/services)

What would you recommend?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: id suggest replace their existing services page with the core 30 hub style cause having both creates confusion keep the same URL but rebuild it as the proper core 30 hub format and change stock photos for real work photos where possible otherwise you can use AI images
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Ah gotcha okay. Additionally, I was thinking of making my AI images look more like stock photos rather than that AI-realistic design...do you think there's any harm in that? I'm assuming the images are more about goal completion vs ranking?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) nah no harm at all, images dont directly impact rankings just conversion and trust, so make them look however converts best for your audience
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Another Q: Should I avoid using the exact same service descriptions in the GBP on the hub page? Does it matter?
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Also, another Q 😅: I have a client who previously created a hub page for Auto Repair Services and then a separate one for Tire Services. Would it be a problem to break them apart like that or should I just go ahead and combine them into one page?
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) So many questions! I saw on that service hub page, I'm assuming each service links to the category/main service page except for the primary cat section; I'm also assuming those are H2s. Idk if the order matters at all \(noticed primary cat was at the bottom\) And then I noticed there was service schema for each service on the page, is that recommended as well?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) no worries, for GBP service descriptions vs hub page yes avoid using the exact same descriptions cause google will see them as duplicate content, rewrite the hub page versions to be more detailed conversion focused while keeping the GBP versions concise
    
    for auto repair services and tire services its actually fine to keep them as separate hub pages they target different search intent and each maps to a distinct GBP category, dont combine them cause that dilutes the topical focus. on the hub page structure yes each service is an H2 with a brief description linking down to its dedicated category or service page, the primary cat section can be at top makes more sense for user experience
    
    and yes adding Service schema for each service listed on the hub page is recommended cause that strengthens the entity signals google reads from the page, makes the hub work harder for both rankings and rich result eligibility!relationships and what each service is so definitely keep it
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) okay awesome - so for auto repair and tire services, should we then maybe do different pages for semantically different categories? For example:
    Page 1 - Auto Repair Services, Transmission Shop, Mechanic;
    Page 2 - Tire Shop, Wheel Alignment Services, Brake Shop;
    Page 3 - Oil Change Service \(or put this in with Tire Shop\)
    
    For schema, Claude recommended "a single ItemList containing one ListItem per service, each with a nested Service type. This avoids 50 separate JSON-LD blocks while still giving search engines structured data for every offering." does that seem okay?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) yes that semantic clustering works, page 1 auto repair + mechanic + transmission together cause general repair intent, page 2 tire shop + wheel alignment + brake shop together cause wheel and brake related, page 3 oil change can go either as its own page or grouped with tire shop. group by what customers think of as similar service types not just GBP categories. for the schema yes the single ItemList with nested ListItem and Service types is the cleaner approach
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) excellent, thank you so much, I understand that! So if it's multiple pages would I use a Service dropdown in the nav bar with something like "Auto Repair" and "Tire, Wheel, and Oil" or something to that effect?
    
    Thinking this through for a chiro client who may have something similar.
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) yes exactly, services dropdown in the nav works great cause it lets users find what they need quickly while keeping the nav clean, same approach works perfectly for your chiro client with grouping :\)
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d)

## 47. Multiple Locations
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/multiple-locations-3
Autor: brian-clark-4989  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I'm doing the GMBs and site for a roofing company with multiple locations. Do I build out service pages for each location? \(ie: brand.com/location-1/location-1-service-page/service-type-location-1 and brand.com/location-2/location-2-service-page/service-type-location-2 etc\), or do I just create one service page with a single set of service type pages that don't mention the specific location?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: hey [@Brian Clark](obj://user/afb58354c08241b9a822264b8bfd07dc) for multi location dont use nested URLs like /location-1/services/service-name, keep flat like /roof-repair-city-1 each at root level. each location gets its own location page plus dedicated location specific service pages, hierarchy comes through internal linking not URL structure. dont use one generic service page for all locations cause that loses the local ranking signal, separate pages per location with flat URLs is the right play :\)
• brian-clark-4989: Do I consider the location page the root for that location \(ie: brand.com/location-1/roof-repair-location-1\) or directly to the general root \(ie: brand.com/roof-repair-location-1\)?
  I appreciate you answering, Miles. You've been super responsive!

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 48. Videos to website pages
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/videos-to-website-pages
Autor: jon-burden-1856  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I’m a Realtor with a YouTube channel all about living in my town. [https://youtube.com/@livingindurangocolorado?si=a9RWyWBkekg9f7pq](https://youtube.com/@livingindurangocolorado?si=a9RWyWBkekg9f7pq)

What do you think about taking my videos on my YouTube channel and create a page with AI based off of what I talked about in each one then also have the video on the page and have several external links? [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c)

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: yes you can do that but make sure each page is properly optimized with core30 back to your real estate services. also make sure each video topic actually relates to your services, dont just dump every video into pages cause unrelated lifestyle content wont feed authority back to your money pages. focus on videos that support buyer or seller intent and link each page back to your relevant service pages, that turns your youtube channel into a topical authority engine for your real estate SEO instead of just isolated lifestyle content
  • jon-burden-1856: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) ah ok that really makes sense! Thank you!

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 49. Gmb services
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/gmb-services-2
Autor: ram-mizrahi-3549  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
[ol:1][li]I do have gmb for a garage door and electric gate repair and installation business. Im using categories: garage door supplier, fence contractor, door supplier, welder,repair service. 
Claude found for me over 200 services when the maximum you can add its 167 so my question if its good to use all the services and creating a page each or its to match and will be better to use a smaller number?

2 . Question the category page link from the home page so i need to setup the homepage as a parent in worldspress? And do the same with the service pages from the category ones?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: hey [@Ram Mizrahi](obj://user/2c9b6574cc0546a181735d03cd0e9010) dont add all 200 services, thats way too many and most will be duplicate variations or services the business doesnt actually offer. consolidate down to 50-60 actual unique services across your 5 categories and only build dedicated pages for the highest value 15-25 of those that have real search demand. for the parent child page structure dont nest pages in wordpress parent settings cause that creates ugly nested URLs, keep all pages flat at root level. the hierarchy comes through internal linking from homepage to category pages then category pages to service pages, all sitting at service-name format. flat URL structure with proper internal linking is the right play not parent child nesting :\)
• ram-mizrahi-3549: Thank you very much

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 51. Core 30 for restaurants
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/core-30-for-restaurants
Autor: rafael-juarez-4209  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  6 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Quick question for anyone doing Core 30 sites for restaurants. For service-based businesses, the “service hub” page usually acts as the main page that links out to all services. But for restaurants, we already have a “Menu” page \(food, drinks, etc.\), which is different from a service hub in structure and intent.
Would you recommend:
[ol:1][li]Using the Menu page as the main hub \(even though it’s more for browsing items\), or[li]Creating a separate “Food & Drink” or “Our Offerings” hub page that links out to subpages \(tacos, drinks, catering, events, etc.\), while keeping the actual menu as its own page?
Curious what’s working best from an SEO + conversion standpoint.

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: option 2 is the fine core 30 approach, keep the menu page as a user facing browsing experience and create a separate services hub that targets SEO keywords as dedicated pages. the menu page is great for conversion but terrible for ranking specific service keywords cause google sees it as a product list not a service page. your GBP secondary categories each get their own hub page and the menu page stays clean for customers :\)
  • rafael-juarez-4209: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Thanks for the clarification. I was struggling with trying to make the Menu page function as both the customer browsing experience and the SEO/service hub, but separating those intents seems way cleaner strategically.
    The point about Google viewing menu pages more like product lists than service pages is huge too. I think using GBP secondary categories as supporting hub/service pages while keeping the actual menu conversion-focused is probably the best move for restaurants.
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Rafael Juarez](obj://user/1caec46e34e542c583b131d0fa05d6c2) yeah exactly, separating intent between SEO ranking pages and conversion focused menu page gives you the best of both worlds, your customers get the clean menu experience while google gets the structured service hub pages it needs to rank you for those specific category and service keywords :\)
• caleb-ulku-6571 ★: yeah I'd say option 2 to avoid cluttering the menu page with links as that's one of few pages on a restaurant's site that users will actually view =\)
  
  also, add things like recipes or background history on iconic menu items that people would specifically be searching for on google \(i.e. best gumbo in new orleans\)
  • rafael-juarez-4209: [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) That makes a lot of sense honestly. I was thinking the same thing regarding keeping the actual Menu page clean since it’s one of the highest-intent conversion pages on a restaurant site. I like the idea of separating the SEO structure from the customer browsing experience instead of trying to force both into one page. The recipe/history angle is interesting too because that opens the door for informational intent + long-tail searches without cluttering the main menu. So instead of just “Tacos” on a menu, you could have supporting content around authentic birria tacos, margarita variations, Mexican street corn, etc. tied back into the restaurant experience/pages.
• mark-farrow-6630: I have one of these and doing it the same, not thru the menu page, just creating content around the CAT and SERV - Core 30

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 52. Multi location services hub page
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/multi-location-services-hub-page
Autor: ryan-carlin-4538  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  1 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hi again! what does the services hub page look like when you have multiple locations? Would the GBP services for the non-primary location be listed in the hub page? how would that look?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: for multi location the services hub page lists all your services brand level not location specific, then each service item links to the appropriate location specific service pages, so users see "drain cleaning" once on the hub but the link goes to the city specific service page for the nearest location based on context and you can watch this lesson hope after watching this lesson you will get better understanding [https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=8c266b3b783246908270d88672365071](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=8c266b3b783246908270d88672365071)

## 54. Advise on changing GBP title
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/advise-on-changing-gbp-title
Autor: neal-hopkin-3283  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  1 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
My client has a GBP where the title is "Company Name | Salt Lake City"
However, they are now located in American Fork, Utah. Should I change their GBP name/title to "Company name | | American Fork"?

Will that have negative consequences? Currently they don't rank on the maps much in Salt Lake City.

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: yes you can keep a city in the GBP name but only if the business documents like business license registration certificate or DBA filing actually show that exact name with the city included. google can ask for verification documentation at any time and if the docs dont match the GBP name exactly thats a suspension trigger. if his official business docs say just "Company Name" without the city then remove the city from the GBP cause having a city that isnt on official documents is keyword stuffing risk. only keep "Company Name | American Fork" if his business registration legitimately shows that exact name with american fork in it

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 55. Content for service pages
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/content-for-service-pages
Autor: ryan-carlin-4538  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  1 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Where can I find an outline of what should go on a service page?

Is there a formula I can follow? I couldn't find any documentation besides the lovable prompts

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: hey [@Ryan Carlin](obj://user/e09047f8464844058cf81ab78843cc43) check out this module [https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=e64641a74ba146208b675d47f569c09b](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=e64641a74ba146208b675d47f569c09b) Caleb walks through on each type of page in the core 30 structure including service pages, the formula and required sections are all covered there. for a service page typically you need H1 with service + city, intro paragraph explaining the service, sections covering common problems or use cases, why choose us trust signals, process or what to expect, pricing transparency if applicable, FAQ section, internal links to related services and category page, GBP map embed, and clear CTA throughout

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 56. Service area business gbp creation
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/service-area-business-gbp-creation
Autor: idan-bubnovsky-4121  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  5 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hey guys, I need to create a service area business gbp and for that I would have to find a physical office to rent out for an address right? How important it is for that location to have signage and staff in it? I was thinking of just offering the client to rent a physical space for the verification as I've understood virtual addresses get flagged and suspended.

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: for SAB you actually dont need a physical address that's visible to the public, SABs are specifically designed to hide the address so even a home address works as long as its where the business is legitimately operated from. just make sure whatever address you use is somewhere the business actually operates from cause google does spot check during verification, but coworking spaces home offices all work fine for SAB setup. the key is dont try to make it look like a storefront if its actually SAB, set it up correctly from the start with address hidden :\)
  • idan-bubnovsky-4121: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) So inserting a virtual office/coworking space like regus is fine and not against their guidelines? Does the client actually has to pay for renting or can he just input the address and do a video verification from there?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Idan Bubnovsky](obj://user/9d0ce896f25a4839865d592b143d8f82) based on Calebs comment SAB isnt recommended cause its much harder to rank, you want a visible address GBP for proper ranking. for coworking spaces like regus the client actually needs to pay and rent the space, you cant just input the address without genuinely having access cause google checks during verification and you need to display signage there. budget for a real rental upfront and frame it to the client as the cost of getting properly verified for ranking advantage
• caleb-ulku-6571 ★: we generally wouldn't recommned an SAB - much harder to rank
  
  because of that, all my team's verification experience is in visible address gbp's. we actually don't take clients on who don't have a visible address because they're working at such a massive seo disadvantage
  
  and yeah, getting a visible address verified means you need to have some way to display signage at that address. virtual addresses are rarely successful these days
  • idan-bubnovsky-4121: [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) Thank Caleb.

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 58. Does Google Business Profile strength affect ranking?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/does-google-business-profile-strength-affect-ranking
Autor: karan-holland-4673  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  3 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
GBP profile strength isn't fully green even though I've filled out most things, Like everything. Does the profile strength indicator actually affect ranking or is it just Google's way of getting you to fill out more stuff? Anyone noticed a difference when they got it to full green?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: no dont worry about the profile score, its just googles way of pushing you to fill out more sections including their paid services. it doesnt directly affect rankings, what matters is having all the meaningful sections completed properly like categories services hours photos posts reviews, which you can have green or not green from google's perspective and still rank perfectly. focus on the core 30 fundamentals and the actual completion of all relevant sections instead of chasing that color indicator :\)
• mark-farrow-6630: seems to always come down to adding storefront images to get it across the line.
• caleb-ulku-6571 ★: fill out absolutely everything - it takes like no time at all compared to an ongoing seo campaign

## 60. For high-authority external links - do they need to go to the actual organizations website or is PubMed fine?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/for-high-authority-external-links-do-they-need-to-go-to-the-actual-organizations-website-or-is-pubmed-fine
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  6 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
for example: "According to the American Journal of Sports Medicine , blah blah blah" does the anchor text need to go to a url directly from AJSM or does a PubMed article work \([https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/17307892/](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/17307892/)\)?

The reason I ask is because I can find the PubMed link, but not a link to AJSM to that specific study.

RESPUESTAS:
• ted-tibbetts-5295: I think it's better if you can find the AJSM link, but if you can't, you can't.
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Ted Tibbetts](obj://user/b2238bbb591249e48db6fd6a63dc0d7c) I do too but if we can't I'm wondering if it's better to find an alternative org that we can or if the pubmed will be fine
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: Yes PubMed is fine if it clearly points to the specific study, especially when you can’t find a direct article page from the journal
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) okay perfect!
• caleb-ulku-6571 ★: the goal is to have external links to authority domains. pubmed is a high authority domain so you're fine linking to it
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) okay got it that makes sense!

## 61. Getting GBP verified
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/getting-gbp-verified
Autor: aiden-hardy-8619  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  15 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Going through all of Caleb’s steps on GBP verification

Can someone confirm all of this with me

Start Recording Your Video Outside
1 Start at truck / van with magnet on side
2 Hold up key fob showing it and the truck / van
3 Hit lock and unlock so truck / van headlights blink, honks, etc to show you control vehicle
4 Open the passenger door and show business card and driver’s license with matching name
5 Show tools in the back of the truck / van / car or show tools in garage
6 Close passenger door to truck / van / car and lock the door
7 Show a street sign if near a corner or intersection \(if possible\). Show left and right neighbors
8 Walk to house / office
9 Show house numbers
10 Demonstrate door is locked by trying to open it
11 Unlock and open door
12 Walk inside
13 Show the website edit access on computer screen
14 Switch to other window / tab showing the website
15 End video

Can we use a tablet instead of computer with the website?

Also does the truck have to have a magnet? With the logo? We have a signboard to throw up front with the logo and I’m sure we can find something for the door

What else am I missing? I sent him all the reminders?

RESPUESTAS:
• aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d)
• caleb-ulku-6571 ★: we've had the most success doign it as described. I haven't tried with a tablet and we haven't tried without a vehicle logo. Those are generally pretty easy to get and work with so we haven't split tested without them. if they're difficult for you, my guess is that it wouldn't make a huge difference, but yeah I'm guessing
  
  the more important thing we found was getting the citations and press release done a month or so in advance
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) okay interesting he’s got the sign board he said last time for his old address he just threw that shit on the wall and was fine without the truck
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) yeah a sign board on the wall has worked for him before so it should work again, just make sure its visible and clearly shows the business name. follow Caleb's full process otherwise with the citations and PR done a month in advance cause thats the more critical piece, the verification video itself is just confirming what google has already been seeing through web signals. document everything carefully and keep the verification process clean :\)
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) we might just have to run it without the citations for now we’re on crunch time
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) then run it without citations but at minimum get the basic ones done before the video like bing, apple maps, yelp and BBB cause those take less than a day to set up and significantly strengthen your verification case even if you cant do the full citation push
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) okay we do it through lead snap but how are we supposed to link the gbp if we can’t update the address on their first
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) you can update the address in the GBP first to the new location, that change itself triggers the reverification process and during that window leadsnap will sync the new address across citations as you push them through, so update GBP address first then run citations through leadsnap simultaneously
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) okay and we shouldn’t do any of this until the website is up?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) correct dont do GBP or citations until the new website is live and indexed cause if google does manual verification during this period they'll check the website and you want them seeing the new site not the old one. proper sequence is 1 build and launch new drainage themed website,
    
    2 wait for it to index,
    3 then update GBP address and primary cat simultaneously,
    4 run citation updates right after,
    5 verification video ready when google asks. doing it in that order means everything aligns cleanly during reverification
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) wordddd, I’ve had a situation when we did the white gbp audit added a home address and it all got approved because we had her change it from her account
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) yeah thats a smart move having the owner make the actual changes from her own account, that approach generally has a higher approval rate cause google sees the changes coming from the verified owner not an agency manager which looks more legit
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) alright cool, he’s asking me this you got any answer for this?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) for the google ads issue tell him contact google ads support directly they can transfer ownership or close the account if he provides business verification documentation. for checking GBP ownership go to [business.google.com](http://business.google.com) he need to sign in with each google account he might have used, whichever one shows the GBP listed under businesses is the owner
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) you’re the goat

═══════════════════════════════════════

Any advice or tips would be great

  creatives that work best are screen recordings of the actual audit process showing real results, and before and after rank map screenshots. for client acquisition specifically check the FB ads module [https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/7a28366e?md=2cfa65058fa948aa8d78d1dbbcd03527](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/7a28366e?md=2cfa65058fa948aa8d78d1dbbcd03527) which has Caleb's specific strategies :\)

    Is there any update to the is content?
    
    I believe that module is 2 years old and there has been a lot of changes on meta since then
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Connor Doyle](obj://user/b0650d3939db40068fade25d6f700e17) yes the module is older but the core strategy is still solid, fundamentals of what converts service business owners havent changed, what changed on meta is targeting options campaign objectives and creative formats, but the offer angles still work. check the weekly live recorded call [https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/52e05dc7?md=38bc3e5191d5486384ce1036f1896690](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/52e05dc7?md=38bc3e5191d5486384ce1036f1896690) you will get better understanding how things works with ads or if you have questions you can ask to Caleb in next weekly cohort :\)

## 63. Geo Page Approach
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/geo-page-approach
Autor: chiara-burns-7791  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
[@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) , Keeper of Knowledge, question for you 🙏

Real Estate client has location pages established -- but in that industry those pages are intended to get people excited about the area. School systems, restaurants, lifestyle, etc. Demonstrating the agent is an expert in the community/neighborhood.

The output from Core30 Agent + what I've been able to glean from the classroom has the location pages structured so that it's like:

Plumber in Oak Park, CA
What a Plumber Does for You In Oak Park, CA
How to Choose a Plumber in Oak Park, CA

etc.

With Real Estate it's a bit different because the expectation that they're going to display expertise in the area. So my question is, how best to proceed? I can see a couple of options:

\(a\) try to fuse "Real Estate Agent in Oak Park, CA" with the existing location pages \([domainname.com/explore-areas/oak-park](http://domainname.com/explore-areas/oak-park)\)

\(b\) have 2 distinct pages, with the location page remaining local color, and the Real Estate Agent in \[City\] as a distinct page, almost like a landing page \([domainname.com/oak-park](http://domainname.com/oak-park)\)

I also have the opportunity to name & describe specific landmarks \(school, restaurant, lake\) in depth which seems like a good way of tackling those yellow --> red pins that fall within a specific city/neighborhood. Just trying to decide on the most optimal container for it.

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c) for real estate id keep the area guide and the agent service page separate, the /explore-areas/oak-park page focuses on schools restaurants and neighborhood lifestyle for buyer research while a separate /real-estate-oak-park-ca page targets people ready to hire an agent. and do link them both ways so the guide supports discovery and the service page captures leads
  • chiara-burns-7791: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) thank youuuu 🙏🫡

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 64. PBN links to GBP Landing Pages?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/pbn-links-to-gbp-landing-pages
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Would I do a PBN link to a home page that's already indexed? Specifically, the page is already indexed and then I add on the categories, do I get a PBN for it?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: its not just about indexing cause with so many AI built pages on the web now indexing alone doesnt automatically pass authority to a page. Caleb specifically teaches one validation link per every published page including homepage and category pages cause each page needs its own authority signal to actually rank well. so yes get validation links to your homepage even if its already indexed especially after adding new categories cause those category H2 sections are essentially new content sections that benefit from the boost :\)
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) got it thank you!

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 65. Existing service pages
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/existing-service-pages
Autor: ryan-carlin-4538  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hi question about service pages - i have all my services pages that were previously created. They are set up like traditional sales pages, not blog posts. Form up top with H1, value props etc.

Is the recommendation to replace the content with what you suggest in the core 30? or can I add the content to the bottom of the existing page? Whats the protocol for editing existing content to match core 30 principles?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: dont replace just optimize them by adding the core 30 content below your existing form and value props section. keep your H1 and other elements at the top cause those are working for leads, then add the optimized service description with proper internal linking schema and faq below
  
  main thing is making sure your H1 follows "service + city" format, gbp category alignment is exact, and internal linking back to homepage and related services is solid. the sales page structure on top stays for conversions, the content underneath handles google rankings :\)
• ryan-carlin-4538: thank you!

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 66. Change primary category?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/change-primary-category-3
Autor: brian-kiselstein-6058  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I was thinking of changing my clients primary category \(hvac contractors to air conditioning contractor\). However, he operates out of his home \(not as a SAB\).

Based off everything I’ve read, changing the primary category can trigger a reverification.

Google states you MUST function as an SAB if you operate out of a residential home. Obviously I’m not doing that.

My question is have any of you successfully gotten a gbp verified with an address listed at residential home?

I’m weighing the risk vs reward. [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d)

Thanks guys

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: changing primary cat is risky cause it can trigger reverification but going SAB with hidden address isnt the right move either cause SABs with no visible address are really tough to rank. if the business genuinely operates from a home and wants to rank well, the better play is keep the physical address setup since thats what gives you the proximity advantage for map pack rankings, just be aware home addresses for HVAC carry some suspension risk and prepare verification documentation in advance. dont stack changes, get the address situation settled first then handle the category change as a separate step!
• ted-tibbetts-5295: Yes, I have had businesses verify a GBP as SABs from a home address.

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 67. Big Question
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/big-question
Autor: aiden-hardy-8619  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  4 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
So about to onboard another person

They have a list of services they do

Residential Stormwater Drainage, Basement Waterproofing, Tree Service \(Tree removal, trimming, planting, etc.\), and Landscape Construction \(New Lawns, New Flower Beds, Retaining Walls, Patios, Decks, Sheds, Gazebos, etc.\).

How are these jobs in terms of SEO? Are dudes looking this shit up or what😂

Obviously not expecting the volume of a plumber but before I write up this contract I want to understand the market a lil better

RESPUESTAS:
• aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d)
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: Yep, those niches absolutely have search demand tree service and basement waterproofing especially are strong local leadgen servics, and landscape construction can be great too if you target the right city/service terms. I’d treat them as solid SEO verticals, just not as broad as plumbing
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) lfg!
• ted-tibbetts-5295: Try using Ahrefs or another SEO tool to get a sense of search volume for particular phrases. It won't be local, but you can get an idea of what people are actually searching for. You can also try Google autofill; type in related keywords and see what Google fills in. you could also try using DataforSEO to scrape local search volume data. Based on that, you can see what to prioritize.

RESPUESTAS:
• aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d)
• aiden-hardy-8619: [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c)
• aiden-hardy-8619: His main service is drainage service tho but does alot of the tree removal stuff and what not
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: if drainage service is genuinely his main service yes make that the primary category cause google rewards alignment between primary category and actual business focus.
  
  property maintenance as primary is too broad and waters down the ranking signal for what he actually wants to rank for. switch primary to drainage service and add property maintenance landscaper tree service waterproofing service as secondaries, that focuses google on drainage as the core business while still covering the supporting service categories he offers.
  
  just be aware switching primary cat can triggers reverification so make sure his documentation is ready and his website content align with drainage as the primary focus before making the change
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) recertification or suspension
  • aiden-hardy-8619: Because we can deal with suspensions all day it’s just the reverificaiton process that’s a pain in the ass
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) cant give tell you exactly but reverification in most cases
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) okay so he has an address up basically that isn’t his but he can use it but has another address
    
    We decided to keep the old one up because of this reverification process
    
    So is it even worth the risk reward now? He wants to mainly rank for drainage badly like that’s his bread and butter
    
    I’m just afraid if recertification happens we’re gonna have to go through the entire process of getting a new address live
  • aiden-hardy-8619: Also with the website we’re gonna redo his entire website and just domain swap point it to the new site
    
    So should I get the new site up that has the drainage theme? and live before we change the GBP
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) honestly if hes using an address that isnt his thats a bigger risk than reverification, that situation could trigger a suspension if google catches it during manual review. in this situation id suggest get the address situation sorted first either through a legitimate location or proper SAB setup with hidden address, then change primary cat after thats solid. doing it in that order means each step has clean documentation, vs stacking category changes on top of a questionable address setup
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) we have full permission with the new address anyways if we had to do a switch we could do it
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) yes definitely get the new drainage website live first before touching the GBP, that way when google reverifies after the category change everything aligns perfectly, drainage focused homepage drainage with full core 30 site structure matching the new primary category.
    
    switching the GBP first while the old site is still live creates a mismatch between GBP cat and site content which raises red flags. proper sequence is new site live first let it index, then switch GBP primary cat and address simultaneously cause everything will align cleanly and you'll have a stronger case during reverification
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) How we go about this:
    
    [ol:1][li]Retheme the website to match drainage service and the other services as well  [li]Get new website live and indexed and what not [li]We gotta change the address regardless \(have him do it\)[li]Retheme the website to match drainage service and the other services as well [li]Make sure we have everything for reverification video just incase [li]get over this hump asap so we can start bringing him jobs 
    let me know if this makes sense in terms of everything?
    
    we cant fuck around with this guy his old marketing agency screwed him out of 30k and we got to make shit happen for him
    
    I just wanna confirm the steps before we hit the ground running let me know if these are the correct steps please
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611)
    
    order is mostly right but step 1 and 4 are duplicates
    
    [ol:1][li]retheme and rebuild website to match drainage service with proper core 30 structure[li]get new site live let it index[li]prep verification docs \(business license, utility bill etc, mail at new address\)[li]change GBP address and primary category at the same time so google does one reverification not two[li]have verification video ready showing the new address and operations[li]keep posting and responding to reviews through the verification process
    that minimizes risk and gets him ranking for drainage fast after the dust settles
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) What if we changed the citations as well? with the new address before the verification video?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) yes you can update citations to the new address before the verification video cause google checks NAP consistency across the web during reverification
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) so we can keep the secondary categories and what not about the tree shit and what not he does do them for real, we’re just wondering if it can work with the entire retheme of the fbp
  • aiden-hardy-8619: PRIMARY: Drainage Service
    [ol:1][li]French Drain Installation[li]Catch Basin Installation[li]Sump Pump Installation[li]Yard Drainage Solutions[li]Stormwater Drainage[li]Surface Drain Installation[li]Downspout Drainage Extension[li]Erosion Control[li]Channel Drain Installation[li]Driveway Drainage Installation
    Why these 10: French drain, catch basin, sump pump, and yard drainage are the highest volume searches. Stormwater and surface drain are confirmed high intent. Downspout drainage extension is a frequent homeowner search. Erosion control and channel drain are mid-tier but confirmed and relevant. Driveway drainage beats out dry creek bed, swale, curtain drain, pooling water solutions, and yard flooding solutions on search specificity and ticket size.
    
    SECONDARY 1: Basement Waterproofing Company
    [ol:1][li]Basement Waterproofing[li]Foundation Waterproofing[li]Foundation Crack Repair[li]Crawl Space Waterproofing[li]Interior Drainage System Installation[li]Exterior Waterproofing[li]Sump Pump Installation[li]Basement Sealing[li]Wet Basement Solutions[li]Foundation Drainage
    All 9 confirmed checked. Nothing added, nothing removed.
    
    SECONDARY 2: Landscaper
    [ol:1][li]Retaining Wall Installation[li]Patio Installation[li]Walkway Installation[li]Paver Installation[li]Hardscape Design & Installation[li]Landscape Construction[li]Sod Installation[li]Fence Installation[li]Lawn Seeding[li]Landscape Grading
    Dropped from top 10: Mulch Installation, Garden Bed Installation, Grading & Leveling, Outdoor Staircase Installation, Land Clearing — all confirmed but lower ticket and intent than the 10 above.
    
    SECONDARY 3: Tree Service
    [ol:1][li]Tree Removal[li]Tree Trimming & Pruning[li]Stump Grinding[li]Stump Removal[li]Dead Tree Removal[li]Storm Damage Tree Removal[li]Hazardous Tree Removal[li]Tree Planting[li]Tree & Brush Clearing
    All 9 confirmed. Nothing added.
    
    SECONDARY 4: Property Maintenance
    [ol:1][li]Seasonal Cleanup[li]Spring Cleanup[li]Fall Cleanup[li]Debris Removal[li]Property Cleanup[li]Yard Waste Removal 
    
    heres the list of everything he does with categories and services would this work or should we ditch some of the categories for now and add them later on

## 69. 2 GBP to merge into 1 GBP
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/2-gbp-to-be-merged-into-1-gbp
Autor: chris-beazley-9965  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  12 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Very excited to say that I have my first paying client - a Heating Engineer.

He currently has 2 GBPs but we need to merge into one. It's the same company and website etc.

The older one was set up as a SAB with 70 5star reviews, but does not rank as well as the local area business with 30 reviews.

He agrees that we should merge them. My question is how do I do this and are Google OK to do this and would we loose any reviews etc?

RESPUESTAS:
• kurt-trew-4932: I am in exactly the same boat as you with a local plumber, who wants to merge reviews from his 'heating only' business, into the main business profile. Any suggestions welcomed!
  • chris-beazley-9965: [@Kurt Trew](obj://user/76be3a23b4304ed988bcf7f5895c67ae) Mine has a service area business as well as local business trading under the same name in the same are.
    
    I am wondering if its worth keeping separate as one is a SAB. Help!
• chris-beazley-9965: can anyone explain this to me?
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: hey [@Chris Beazley](obj://user/5d93490bb0c8499d9e7754083384e141) congrats on the first paying client! merging GBPs is tricky but doable, the safest approach is mark the older SAB profile as permanently closed which preserves its existence and reviews, then make the local area business with the address your primary GBP going forward. you cant directly transfer reviews between profiles cause google ties reviews to specific business profile entities, but the closed profile keeps showing reviews to anyone who finds it through direct search which still helps with brand trust. dont try to actually merge them or duplicate the listings cause google will likely suspend both, the cleanest play is closing the SAB and consolidating all future activity onto the local area profile that already has the address and ranking advantage
  • chris-beazley-9965: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Hi Miles,
    
    Thank you for your comments.
    
    This is now getting confusing.
    
    The original business was set up as a SAB. The SAB has been marked as a duplicate by Google and has 70 5* reviews. \(See first heat map.\) - no activity for 2 years on GBP. - Position
    
    The SAB is ranking better \(#5\) than the storefront \(#20\) that has 29 5* reviews.
    
    There may be other factors including working hours and different primary categories \(the SAB is Gas Engineer and storefront - Central Heating Service\).
    
    As a SAB is normally harder to rank than a storefront should I get Google to mark the store front as a duplicate and keep the SAB as it is currently ranking higher?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: ok this changes things, thisis more nuanced then I thought the position difference is likely driven by the much stronger review count on the SAB + the better category fit gas engineer vs central heating service, those signals matter more than physical address advantage in this case. id keep the SAB as the primary cause its already ranking better and has the established review history and category alignment, then mark the storefront as the duplicate or permanently closed instead. before doing anything submit an appeal to remove the duplicate flag on the SAB cause that flag is suppressing it from ranking even higher. once the duplicate flag is cleared you'll likely see the SAB jump significantly, then transition all activity going forward to that profile
  • chris-beazley-9965: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Thanks Miles. Just to make it even more confusing if i add the town name to the search "boiler install" the the storefront beats the SAB by 2 places. Could this be due to the suppressing of the SAB?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Chris Beazley](obj://user/5d93490bb0c8499d9e7754083384e141) yeah thats exactly whats happening, the duplicate flag is suppressing the SAB so the storefront wins on geo modified searches cause of proximity signals just submit the duplicate appeal first, give it 2-3 weeks to clear, then youll see the SAB jump on town specific searches too cause it has stronger reviews and better category alignment
  • chris-beazley-9965: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Thank you - I have now done the request.
• caleb-ulku-6571 ★: You'll want to reach out to Google support and ask for the merge. Take screen caps of every single existing review so you can verify they were all properly migrated. Any missing reviews, you'll need proof to get them readded \(which google can do\)
  
  ask them to be merged into whichever one is ranking higher. if it's the SAB that's ranking higher, once the merge is complete add the visible address to it.
• caleb-ulku-6571 ★: overall which one you keep isn't an end all be all decision - clearly better to get it right but they're both ranking okay. merging to one and adding an address will get better ranking once it settles out - could take 4-8 weeks for it to settle after the merge
  • chris-beazley-9965: [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) Thank you - I have now done the request.

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 70. Triple Checking
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/triple-checking
Autor: wendy-craven-3180  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  5 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
My Client has 1 Primary and 9 Additional Categories. All 10 Category GBP "Names" get listed on the HOMEPAGE? Correct?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: not exactly, only the primary category and 4-6 top secondary categories should be prominently featured on the homepage as H2 sections, not all 10 categories. listing all 10 categories on the homepage can dilutes the focus and the homepage cant rank well for that many topics simultaneously. the remaining secondary categories each get their own dedicated category hub page where they can be the primary focus and rank for their specific keywords. homepage features primary plus top 4-6 secondaries, the rest get linked through the other relevant pages
• wendy-craven-3180: AHHHHH ---- THank you! I made that mistake, once!
• wendy-craven-3180: one more question --- can you add TWO GBP categories to the H1 Page? "Medical Spa - Primary, Wellness Spa -- 2ndary. Or should we DELETE "wellness Spa".
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Wendy Craven](obj://user/af817df91af246aca05b6f436bf5a55a) the H1 has to be focused on one primary keyword cause google looks for the strongest primary signal there, mixing two dilutes the ranking strength. medical spa as H1, wellness spa gets H2 treatment plus its own dedicated hub page!
• jeremy-bengtson-6101: [@Wendy Craven](obj://user/af817df91af246aca05b6f436bf5a55a) 1. The H1 Strategy: Brand & Entity Check
  Your current H1—"Be Still Wellness & Aesthetics: Medical Spa & Wellness Center in Jacksonville"—successfully follows the "semantic triplet" format: Subject \(Brand\), Object \(Service\), and Predicate \(Location\). It identifies who the business is, what they do, and where they do it.
  
  [ul][li][li]The Risk: There is significant redundancy between your SEO Title and your H1. Your documentation warns that having the exact same terms in the URL, Title, and H1 can be seen as "points of crawl resistance" or over-optimization. Currently, your H1 nearly repeats your SEO Title word-for-word.
  2. H2 & H3 Hierarchy: Marketing vs. SEO
  The H2 and H3 structure is quite dense \(31 headings total\). While informative for users, many of these headings lean toward marketing copy rather than semantic reinforcement.
  [ul][li]Marketing Focus: Headings like "One Body. One Life. Optimize Both." \(H2\) and "NOW OPEN: Southside..." \(H3\) are great for conversion but provide little semantic value to search engines regarding your core entities.[li][li]Location Entities: You mention "Southside" and "Riverside" in the description, but the homepage focuses on "Jacksonville" as the primary location entity. This is correct for a homepage, as it represents the broadest relevant location.
  Actionable Improvements
  1. Diversify the H1 with Entity Variants
  To avoid over-optimization, you should restate the information in your H1 rather than repeating your SEO Title. Use "entity variants"—synonyms or related terms found on Wikipedia—to help search models understand the context better.
  
  [ul][li]Current H1: Be Still Wellness & Aesthetics: Medical Spa & Wellness Center in Jacksonville[li]Recommended H1: Be Still Wellness & Aesthetics: Premier Aesthetic Clinic & Holistic Health Hub in Duval County
  2. Optimize H2s for Semantic Reinforcement
  Your H2s should be subtopics that support the main ideas defined in the H1. Replace generic marketing phrases with headers that include your "trigger words" or business categories.
  
  [ul][li]
  3. Standardize FAQ Headings \(AI SOP Implementation\)
  If you have an FAQ section \(or plan to add one\), utilize the provided "Standard Two-Line FAQ Heading" formula to ensure consistency and high semantic value.
  
  [ul][li][li]
  4. Leverage Location Variance for Multi-Clinic Support
  Since you mention "Additional Locations" \(H2\), ensure the homepage remains broad while the "Inner Pages" \(location-specific pages for Southside or Riverside\) handle the specific location keywords.
  
  [ul][li]Tip: Do not use the homepage as the landing page for your Google Business Profile \(GBP\). Instead, link the GBP to a specific location landing page optimized for that exact city/neighborhood to avoid conflicting with the broad homepage data.
  5. Check for "Crawl Resistance"
  Audit your URL, SEO Title, and H1 one last time. If all three use the exact same primary keyword \(e.g., "Medical Spa Jacksonville"\), you are likely being suppressed for over-optimization. Ensure each of these three elements uses a different variant of your primary service and location entities.

## 71. New Plumber

Autor: aiden-hardy-8619  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  8 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Just onboarded a new plumber!!!

He don’t have an address listed and has 170+ 5 star reviews but like zero ranking whatsoever

He doesn’t actually have an office at his house but he has everything to pass a verification video if needed…

Idk if it’s worth tossing the address on their just to rank him better or just thug it out he seemed kind of skeptical about it I told him we’ll handle it

RESPUESTAS:
• aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d)
• aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) or should we just have him throw the address up from his account
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: 170 reviews with weak rankings isn’t always just an address issue, because gogle still uses relevance, distance, and prominence that said if he’s a true service area plumer, hiding address is the correct setup and I’d only use a public address if it’s a real legitimate location where the business is actually operated from. best play is to keep the SAB setup and strengthen service area pages, local content, and review velocity
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) okay he has zero website with a 5 year old domain we’re gonna rip everything ground up no address
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) then it is actually a goldmine, that aged domain authority combined with fresh core 30 build will rank way faster than starting from scratch
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) alright perfect county size of 1 million also, he’s got endless opportunities for work, his gbp is 4+ years old also just nobody ever optimized shit
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) that's great once you implement core 30 the rankings will move fast cause google already trusts both entities. with a 1 million county and great work, build core 30 first then aggressive geo expansion to capture surrounding cities
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) I’m not done with questions for the day lol
