## 72. Do you make the Citations section an H2, H3, or it doesn't matter?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/do-you-make-the-citations-section-an-h2-h3-or-it-doesnt-matter
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  6 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
just curious.

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: if you mean the citations section on your homepage where you display logos of directories like yelp BBB facebook etc, that should be H2 cause its a major section of the page, not H3. the heading hierarchy is H1 for the page title then H2 for major sections like services categories trust signals etc, H3 only for subsections within those H2 areas.
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) I meant citations to external high authority domains for the category/service/geo pages or general topical relevance \(not home page\)
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) oh got it, for citations to external high authority domains within thats not actually a heading cause its just contextual links inside paragraphs not a labeled section. so dont add an H2 or H3 for those external citation links, just naturally add them into the body content
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) I believe we add the naked url at the bottom under a Citations section, that was what I was trying to figure out how that's formatted
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) yeah you can add make sure its linked not just a text :\)
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Yessir! Thank you

## 74. What is a good budget for a web developer to add pages to websites?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/what-is-a-good-budget-for-a-web-developer-to-add-pages-to-websites
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Never hired one so I don't even know where to begin. I did plan on getting someone overseas to do it, what is a good starting budget per hour for a moderately skilled developer? Since AI makes the HTML most of the time, I don't think it will be a ton of work.

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: honestly cant give you exact numbers cause it varies a lot, but you can expect something around $10-15 per hour for moderately skilled developers from upwork. for simple page additions where AI generates the HTML and they just need to publish format and check responsiveness budget around $10 per page give them a clear test task first to see speed and quality before committing to ongoing work, and document your SOPs upfront cause clear instructions makes the difference between great and frustrating outsourcing experience
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) great, thank you! This is a good place to start!

## 76. GBP --- why are the SERVICES NOT showing up?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/gbp-why-are-the-services-not-showing-up
Autor: wendy-craven-3180  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  3 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Why are the "services" no showing up on this GBP?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: hey [@Wendy Craven](obj://user/af817df91af246aca05b6f436bf5a55a) services dont show up on the desktop version of google maps, theyre only visible in the mobile app. if you mean services arent showing in the GMB everywhere extension you need to click the audit button in the extension which then pulls all the categories and services for that GBP. the desktop maps view shows limited info compared to the mobile app, so if you're checking from a computer thats why you dont see them, use GMB everywhere audit feature to see the full services list and more details :\)
• wendy-craven-3180: WOW, I did NOT know this about the mobile versus desktop. I was watching the video and scratching my head like omg, what have I done.,..... Anyways, Im learning so much. TY! :\)
• mark-farrow-6630: Click Basic, it will go in and show you the services that any business has on its profile.

## 78. Heat Map
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/heat-map
Autor: dillon-smith-9488  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  4 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Im looking at trying to rank a local Restaurant that I have been working with for a couple years and now really starting to dive into SEO and LLM for them. When I run the heat map it shows the same 3 Restaurants at the top no matter how big or small i make the circle and shows 2 of them with 100% marketshare. Not sure what to do from here to even start competing.

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Dillon Smith](obj://user/2f771c1fab4e465a859be97c58121851) when 2 competitors show 100% market share its usually a sign theyve got a combination of GBP age strong reviews and proper optimization, but you can still dig in and find their weaknesses. check from GMB everywhere on those top 3 to see exactly what categories services and trust signals they have, then focus on differentiating your client through stronger review velocity, secondary categries and core 30 site structure. for restaurants specifically reviews are massive weighted heavier than most niches, push hard on getting 3-5 new reviews per week consistently and respond to every single one mentioning the cuisine type city and neighborhood naturally, that review velocity combined with proper core 30 can close the gap over 3 months with proper strategy
• dillon-smith-9488: Okay that makes sense they're consistently getting reviews every week, they are at 690 on Google and 964 on Yelp and this year was ranked #22 on Yelps top 100 list. The top competitors in the area though have 4.1k and 2.7k reviews on google alone. Someone in the top 3 just showed 19 reviews though with 4.2 stars so its just weird
  
  most of them though do have over 2k reviews on google alone so long ways to go
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Dillon Smith](obj://user/2f771c1fab4e465a859be97c58121851) ok but the one with only 19 reviews ranking top 3 is a huge clue cause that tells you they have something else strong like GBP age proximity or category fit. dig into that 19 review competitor with GMB everywhere and figure out what theyre doing differently, that is your roadmap. for the high review competitors you cant out review them in 3 months so focus on review velocity and recency over total count google weighs recent reviews heavier than ancient ones, plus ramp up GBP posts to twice a week and dial in core 30 structure cause those signals can outweigh raw review count when reviews are old and stagnant on competitor profiles
  • dillon-smith-9488: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Awesome thank you!!

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 79. Core Service Pages
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/core-service-pages
Autor: mark-fazio-5761  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  1 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
How many core service pages is recommended for the website?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: honestly theres no fixed number, build core service pages for every distinct service you offer that people actually search for, typically 8-15 core service pages depending on your niche and area/comptetion.the rule is one page per service that has its own search intent and meaningful SV, dont split into too many micro variations cause that creates duplicate content issues. use GMB everywhere to see what services your top competitors have pages for and mirror their structure as a baseline

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 80. Linking to internal pages outside of Core30?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/linking-to-internal-pages-outside-of-core30
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
AI put in some internal links to a couple pages that were on the website before I got involved.

One was for signs and symptoms, another for treatment expectations.

Would it be worthwhile to keep those links in there? if so, should I link them back to this service page? And would these be considered sister pages vs parent/child \(just for my own understanding\)?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: yes keep those links in if the pages are actually related to the service and provide value to users, these seems like technically supporting content not parent or child pages cause theyre topically related but not directly hierarchical, and yes link them back to the service page bidirectionally for that proper internal linking flow. the rule is if a link helps the user understand more about your services or makes them more likely to convert keep it, the only links to remove are to outdated unrelated or low quality pages that dont serve the visitor
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) gotcha, thank you!

• miles-hayat-9206 ★: hey [@Karter Maki](obj://user/528e712e1ed846acbd86d8b34ca1b911) [icecreamtruck.shop](http://icecreamtruck.shop) is the recommended source for validation links one per page, thats what most people in the community use, for 15 pages just order 15 validation links and use branded or naked URL anchors not exact match keyword anchors. also look into chamber of commerce if your client doesnt have it yet cause that gives a high authority local backlink, plus PR release for monthly press releases which build brand entity signals over time and leadsnap for citations, those sources cover the full link building strategy Caleb teaches

## 82. LeadSnap heatmaps = Places API output, not what users actually see
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/leadsnap-heatmaps-places-api-output-not-what-users-actually-see
Autor: chiara-burns-7791  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  6 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hey all, I've been playing around with 4 different local seo softwares and noticed that 3 of them \(Local Falcon, GridMyBusiness, Paige\) are pretty identical in their heatmaps, with LeadSnap being a far less flattering outlier.

Asked the rep where their data comes from. Got "the Official Google API which has most realistic numbers."

There's no official Google API that returns local pack or Maps rank positions at coordinates. GBP API doesn't expose position data. Places API returns its own RELEVANCE/DISTANCE order. According 2 Google's own docs, that ain't what users actually see in Maps or the Local Pack...

***it literally says they are not designed to simulate user-centric search results***

Story shifted through course of convo to be like: "Official Google API" → "simulating searches from each coordinate" \(which is SERP scraping, not an API\) → "our proprietary API talks to the Google API" \(= a software wrapper lol\).

I would imagine for local SEO client reporting, you would want geolocated SERP-scraped data \(i.e., WHAT USERS ACTUALLY SEE\) not Places API output.

Open to alternative points of view, but I was a bit disappointed to discover this because I definitely prefer their functionality and UI/UX.

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: Good catch, if LeadSnap is using Places API data thats useful for proximity and relevance analysis, but it’s not the same as real user-facing Local Pack or Maps rankings. for client reporting, I’d prefer geolocated SERP-based rank data when the goal is to show what users actually see that said according to Caleb leadsnap is part of his core workflow for tracking rank maps and managing citations, so its consistent enough for tracking progress over time even if its not a perfect user simulation, just worth knowing what the data actually represents when you present it to clients!
• caleb-ulku-6571 ★: all results for leadsnap are done by running searches in specific areas using geo spoofed proxies and then scraping google maps for the real time results
  
  the google api doesn't give search results or search placement - no version of the api even gives that info
  
  the leadsnap support person has it wrong
• hipster-marketing-3708: I’ve seen very different results across lead snap, Viking and Local falcon. Used local falcon for a long time and switched over to lead snap. Might still hold on to it for comparison though. Lead snap was a bit more flattering for me.
  • chiara-burns-7791: [@Hipster Marketing](obj://user/35c73c3ba7b44aab8bc8b70214093d94) Interesting! LeadSnap was less flattering for me.
  • hipster-marketing-3708: [@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c) so one thing I found out which makes total sense but I didn’t think about it was the time that you pull the maps. I was working late nights and running maps and the business wasn’t open. So I started scheduling my maps for noon and they looked a lot better than 2am haha
  • chiara-burns-7791: [@Hipster Marketing](obj://user/35c73c3ba7b44aab8bc8b70214093d94) Yeah I noticed that about the time of day, as well! Ty for sharing

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 83. Core30 Agent Credits are live for Pro members 💳
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/core30-agent-credits-are-live-for-pro-members
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles + comentó  ·  15 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Quick heads-up on something we just rolled out: a credit system

First, the most important thing: if you're already using Core 30 with your own API keys, nothing changes for you. Keep doing what you're doing. Your BYOK setup still works exactly the same way

What credits are: an alternative way to run Core 30 without plugging in your own API keys for Anthropic, OpenAI, DataForSEO, etc. Instead of managing a dozen vendor accounts, you can just use credits and we handle the vendor side

Why we built this: the BYOK setup is great if you're already comfortable wrangling API keys, but it's also the #1 thing that slows new Pro members down on day one. Credits remove that friction. Set up your account, start generating content. That's it

What this means for Pro members:
[ul][li]Credits are included in your Pro subscription. No additional charge. 25 per month[li]You can switch between BYOK and credits per-vendor in your settings. Use your own Anthropic key but credits for DataForSEO? That works[li]Your existing usage, queues, clients, and Core 30 setups are untouched

What to do: nothing, unless you want to. The new options are visible in your settings if you want to explore. If you've been on the fence about onboarding because of the API key setup, this is the unlock

If anything looks off or you've got questions, drop them below or DM me

RESPUESTAS:
• stefan-girndt-6973: nice, [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) been waiting for that!
  And very generous to add some credits for everybody as well 👏
• miles-harding-9169: Thank you!
• andrew-lin-7557: Thanks [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c)
• stephan-franklyn-4962: nice, I'm still having issues with Pictory so hopefully I can just use the credits for that!
  • kendrick-mayer-8407: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) I'm wondering about this as well - can credits be applied to specific access points, such as Pictory? [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c)
    
    \[edit\] - I just watched the recording from yesterday where it's explained that yes, the credits can be used "a la carte", which is awesome! 👍

• kevin-tully-4285: Love that
• matthew-clark-6696: Thank you, Caleb. It’s a great time to be doing SEO!

  • caleb-ulku-6571 ★: [@Austin Delaney](obj://user/c61e510a6883428db5bc4d4d3d1c212e) yes - a pro subscription is required to log into it

And even if I go to "buy credits" the message tells me that feature isn't available yet.

## 84. Spam calls on tracking numbers - is this normal?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/spam-calls-on-tracking-numbers-is-this-normal
Autor: connor-doyle-8725  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  6 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Almost all of my clients are getting 5-20 spam calls per day on their tracking numbers. We're using GHL for tracking, and I'm wondering if that's contributing to the issue.

The calls don't even seem to be from humans, just robots/automated systems. Clients are getting frustrated.

Has anyone else run into this? Is it just par for the course with tracking numbers, or are there ways to filter/block this stuff at the GHL level \(or elsewhere\)?

Any tips appreciated.

RESPUESTAS:
• chiara-burns-7791: Any time I set up numbers through GHL I get a handful of those, but not 5-20/day. Are your clients in a major metro? Typically an easy way to weed out robocalls is to have a basic IVR like "press 1 to continue." They can't navigate a menu. If I remember correctly, you can build that in as a workflow.
  • connor-doyle-8725: [@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c) thanks heaps!! not in a major metro, but that is a great idea
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: spam calls on tracking numbers is super common across all platforms not just GHL cause you know now everywhere Ai agnets are setup, any new business number gets hammered by automated spammers scraping directories. you can add a quick IVR menu that asks "press 1 if youre a customer" which filters out bots since they cant respond to prompts
  • connor-doyle-8725: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) thank you sir, that is helpful!
• douglas-page-2404: As Twilio has been the source of numbers for many spammers, I will always do a search on any phone number I am looking to order to see what its history is like. Doesn't completely eliminate the issue but usually a lot better than most.
• aiden-hardy-8619: Yeah, one of my plumbers got 30 calls in one day from them they’re ruthless I just tell my plumbers to press 1 to opt in take a convo with them and break the 4th wall and get off their list

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 85. I made the mistake...
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/i-made-the-mistake
Autor: dillon-smith-9488  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  8 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
So my wife and I own a couple hair salons. I had our first location ranking really well on google and google maps. Soon as we opened the second location I created location pages and made the mistake of pointing the first location to the new location page and the new location to its own landing page and obviously our rankings have tanked now and now I am having a hard time getting them back to the top and ranking and its becoming quiet frustrating.

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: yeah cause you basically removed all the authority signals google had built up on the homepage for that GBP and started fresh on a new URL. the fix is to either update the first location GBP to point back to the homepage which was working before, or aggressively build authority signals to the new location page through internal links from your homepage, citations updated to the new URL, and any backlinks pointing to the homepage updated to point to the new location page. give it sometimes after making the changes for rankings to recover, and going forward never change the GBP website URL on a profile thats already ranking well unless absolutely necessary cause that move resets a lot of the ranking signals
  • dillon-smith-9488: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Ya totally get that I learned for sure. I think in the long run this structure will be better for us as we plan on opening more locations but just hard hit in the meantime and now trying to figure out how to structure it the right way for multiple locations and still outrank everyone who has 1 location in 1 city
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Dillon Smith](obj://user/2f771c1fab4e465a859be97c58121851) for long term dedicated location pages is the right move for multi location growth. to outrank single location competitors target hyper local terms per location like "hair salon massapequa" not broader "hair salon long island" so each office owns its tight radius. push hard on review velocity per GBP location and keep building location specific content cause that geographic depth eventually outranks single location guys through proximity to more searchers across more areas
  • dillon-smith-9488: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Understood! Can I have a brand category page as well as services page thats not city specific that could live in the menu, but then create designated category and service pages per city that link to location pages?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Dillon Smith](obj://user/2f771c1fab4e465a859be97c58121851) yes thats works well for multi location, you can have brand level service and category pages that explain what your business does overall which live in the main nav, then location specific target the city specific keywords and link from the location pages. just make sure the brand level pages dont compete with the location specific ones for the same keywords, brand level targets unbranded informational searches while location specific ones target the local keyword plus city searches.
    internal linking flows from location page down to location specific service pages, and brand level service pages link to all location variations of that service so users can find the closest location to them
  • dillon-smith-9488: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Great thank you!
• caleb-ulku-6571 ★: have you tried changing the landing page back to what it was?
  • dillon-smith-9488: [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) No I haven't we have been working hard to just move everything over and have already changed a lot of stuff to these new location pages. I believe in the long term its better we have separate location pages as we plan to continue to grow I would rather us have a landing page for each location and homepage be the brands homepage in general no specific location. It's just the rebuilding of everything but we will get tthere and long term should pay off better

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 86. For choosing services, would you have 2 similarly named services under different categories?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/for-choosing-services-would-you-have-2-similarly-named-services-under-different-categories
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  4 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
For example, 2 cats are Auto Repair Shop and Mechanic. Under Auto Repair Shop \(primary\) we have engine tune-up, under Mechanic we've been recommended to have Car Engine Tune-Up.

Will that dilute or is that acceptable?

RESPUESTAS:
• chiara-burns-7791: There's an awesome functionality built into the Core30 agent that will show you how different/similar Google views services. It's on the Research tab. Best to drop overlapping terms.
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c) ohhh I see, I wasn't sure how to use that part of the tool
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: dont add the same service under 2 categories, just pick 1 category for each service and keep the GBP services list clean cause google shows all services in a flat list anyway so duplicating across cats doesnt help and just looks like clutter, assign engine tune up to whichever category fits best and leave it at that
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) excellent thank you!

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 87. Exporting to Wordpress
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/exporting-to-wordpress
Autor: ted-tibbetts-5295  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Forgive the basic question...but can someone give me a quick rundown on the export to Wordpress function with the tool? I see that it can generate images and content, but does it design the whole category/service page too? Or do we create templates and somehow the tool knows where to drop content? \(Which seems unlikely\). Haven't tried yet and my instinct is that I should probable test this out before doing it on a client site :\).

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: the core 30 agent generates the content structure and publishes pages directly to wordpress but it doesnt design pages to match your specific styling preferences, you get functional pages with the right SEO structure not visually polished designs according to your preference. it can build solid category and service pages but if you want them designed your way you can grab the page code and ask claude to redesign according to your preferences while keeping the SEO material intact. id suggest testing it on a staging site first not a live client site so you can see exactly what the output looks like and decide if you need to adjust or apply additional design polish before going live with real clients
  • ted-tibbetts-5295: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Great, thanks, Miles!

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 88. Advice For Creating Webpages Faster?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/advice-for-creating-webpages-faster
Autor: brian-mills-8533  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  8 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I have to be honest, I love local SEO but I do not enjoy making webpages, organizing layouts, color schemes etc. Overall, I like Wordpress - its pretty much a staples and very versatile.

Any advice or tools on how to create pages faster without shelling out a lot of extra money or juggling monthly credits?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: for you id suggest build a single solid wordpress template with your standard layout color scheme and design elements, then duplicate that template for every new page and just swap out the content cause this kills 80% of the time wasted on design decisions. use a page builder like elementor, starter templates which give you pre made page layouts you can customize once and reuse forever. the core 30 AI agent in pro also handles content generation and publishing directly to wordpress which solves the layout repetition issue entirely cause it auto applies consistent structure, definitely worth using
• chiara-burns-7791: If you really want to stay in Wordpress, I'd hire a VA from the Phillipines or Colombia to do that part for you. Personally, I prefer building with AI because you can build a gorgeous website in a very short period of time. Core30 Agent + Replit = a level of painlessness I never thought was going to be possible with webdev.
  
  Also I've said this before on here, but I really think that if Wordpress doesn't make it easier for folks to edit \(i.e., vibecode-able\), and they stick with their Elementor and Guttenbuerg whatever the hell, they're going to be like the Blockbuster of CMSs by 2028.
  • connor-doyle-8725: [@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c) what tech stack are you using for building websites with AI?
  • chiara-burns-7791: [@Connor Doyle](obj://user/b0650d3939db40068fade25d6f700e17) Replit is my favorite. Whatever platform you build on, just make sure it doesn't default to an SPA \(single page application\) where all the code is rendered in such a way that Google can't see it as easily.
    
    So with Replit, I just designate the stack from the outset as astro + SSG and it's a clean build :-\)
  • connor-doyle-8725: [@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c) amazing!
  • douglas-page-2404: [@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c) Are you just copying the Core30 content into your prompt with Replit to create the page or doing something else?
  • chiara-burns-7791: [@Doug Page](obj://user/2667d9a03c934d32ad8bed6e5f7041e9) Varies a bit depending on the client but generally speaking, Core30 Agent does a great job of outputting the content esp if you run through a Humanizer. I design a template for the Parent pages and a template for the Child pages in Replit. Upload the ZIP file of docxs. Replit fits it to the template.
• garitey-cyril-5167: Hello here is my setup :
  . Install Wamp or Laragon on Mac or PC.
  . Install a local wordpress
  . Open Wordpress directory in VS CODE or in your Terminal.
  . Install Claude Code
  . Install Skills Claude Code \(Superpowers, Frontend Design, Code review, Security\)
  . Launch Claude Code
  . Tell Claude what you want. I'll create a page and a theme for your WordPress site.
  . Install CLI command
  . Start to play :\).
  . Install Tailwind ou ShadCDN UI ou Lucid UI
  . Expot when finished with All in one migration or other
  
  By doing it this way, you won't need a builder anymore.
  You'll have a custom-built site, with your theme hard-coded.
  
  You'll need to specify that the static pages should be hard-coded and the blog pages should use Gutenberg.
  
  For the static pages, you'll need to inject PHP into HTML.
  You can also use a custom post type plugin if you need one.
  
  Claude Code will handle everything from A to Z.
  Tailwind, ShadCDN UI, or Lucid UI will let you create cool designs with custom CSS.
  
  In 10 minutes, you can have a static page ready with the design you want.
  
  You should also know that CLI commands manage your WordPress via the REST API.
  So you’ll be able to manage themes or plugins if you wish.
  
  But you can also manage the creation of Core 30 within the page hierarchy :\).
  
  So it will essentially be all about prompting. Enjoy :\)

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 89. Backlink Not Working?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/backlink-not-working
Autor: justin-mckay-7670  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  3 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hey everyone, quick SEO question.
I got a backlink/profile page from my Chamber of Commerce, but it is not showing up in Semrush or Google Search Console yet.
This is the page:[https://members.centralyorkchamber.com/?c=automotive&category=2023101810580062611111111111#293075_JMcKays-Auto-Detailing](https://members.centralyorkchamber.com/?c=automotive&category=2023101810580062611111111111#293075_JMcKays-Auto-Detailing)
Did I do something wrong setting this up, or is this type of page maybe not indexable by Google?
If it is not indexable, what is the best way to get it indexed or at least make sure the backlink gets picked up?
I already made sure my website link is correct, so now I am trying to figure out if:
[ol:1][li]the page is not crawlable[li]the link is nofollow/sponsored[li]it just needs more time[li]the page itself is not actually indexed
Any help would be appreciated.

RESPUESTAS:
• justin-mckay-7670: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d)
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Justin McKay](obj://user/22e840bd8dca43658eb61b7460a560c7) probably nothing is broken yet chamber profile pages can take time to get crawled, and some are treated as duplicates or alternate canonicals. i’d inspect the exact URL in GSC first to check crawlability, indexing, and canonical status, then decide if it just needs time or if the page is being canonicalized elsewhere
• garitey-cyril-5167: Don't worry, Google sees all the links, and it's still an authoritative site, so sooner or later, it will be taken into account.
  
  Even if you don’t see it showing up in SEMrush or Google Search Console, keep in mind that SEMrush isn’t a guarantee of confirmation, let alone indexing. And you should also know that Search Console has been broken for years. If your backlink doesn’t show up there, it’s not a big deal. If it’s on the Chamber of Commerce website, that’s something at least.
  
  Another important point, honestly, is that the site was coded really poorly. When you look at the source code, it’s just a complete mess.

## 90. Business category
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/business-category
Autor: mark-fazio-5761  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  1 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
For a mortgage loan officer, can bank and credit union be used as a business category? Though not employed by a bank or CU, but they use them to run their mortgage loans through. Not sure if that will help or make it worse since bank or CU offers services a loan officer doesnt such as checking, savings, etc.

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: id suggest dont use bank or credit union as categories cause i think those are misleading to customers, you'd be claiming services the business doesnt actually offer like checking and savings accounts which can risks suspension. stick to relevant categories depending on what google offers and what fits the actual business. use GMB everywhere to see what top ranking competitors in your market use and mirror those, the goal is accurate categorization not stuffing in adjacent categories that dont apply :\)

## 91. Prospecting Tool
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/prospecting-tool
Autor: ted-tibbetts-5295  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  3 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
So I'm trying to build a prospecting tool. I enter the niche and city. The tool scrapes google map data and returns businesses who meet this criteria: 1. Ranking 4-10 in GBP, 2. has \[X} amount of reviews \(I'm playing with the specific number 3. has a website. The idea is that these businesses aren't ranking, but have decent reviews and a website, so they have assets to work with.

I'm wondering if there's any value in targeting businesses without websites. With the system that I've been using we can build sites fairly quickly and cheaply. Maybe we can fill a need with these businesses. On the other hand, if they don't even have a website, would they even be a good client.

Any thoughts on ideal client parameters?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: yes businesses without websites can actually be great clients cause theyre easier to close with a clear tangible deliverable but they tend to have lower budgets but offers like "complete online presence package" convert well with them, while existing website prospects respond better to audit focused offers. include both as separate segments with different messaging rather than excluding the no website ones, test both and see which gives better LTV in your market
• douglas-page-2404: There are many small niches, especially in smaller cities, where the owner feels their FB page is good enough without need of a website. I just explain that they are lucky the competition is so weak in their area and that should their competition start doing the right things their GBP will drop. I still offer to optimize their GBP and provide other services but remind them if their ranking drops off below #3, then we will need to address setting up the website for them to reclaim the business they will be losing. Also, as the authority of the website also helps with building out their ranking further, they will decide to do it along with the rest of the work.
  • ted-tibbetts-5295: [@Doug Page](obj://user/2667d9a03c934d32ad8bed6e5f7041e9) Good thoughts, [@Doug Page](obj://user/2667d9a03c934d32ad8bed6e5f7041e9), thanks!

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 92. Smaller clients not trusting the process
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/smaller-clients-not-trusting-the-process
Autor: karan-holland-4673  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  4 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Curious if anyone else has dealt with this. Client's Core 30 is done, pages are still indexing, rank map hasn't shown green yet. Client wants to cancel because they're not seeing leads and doesn't trust the process. It's a fresh GBP so it was always going to take longer. How do you handle it when smaller clients want out before the work has had a chance to kick in? [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) Ive been honest with them telling them how long but they just want now now now

RESPUESTAS:
• karan-holland-4673: They want me to change there business name too which i told them to wait for core 30 indexed but they want now now now
• caleb-ulku-6571 ★: yeah it's about setting expectations - brand new stie and gbp take time to age, really not much can be done about it. if I'm working with a brand new client, I'll usually try to lower the monthly rate, or waive a few months while we sit there and let it age hoenstly
• garitey-cyril-5167: Hello, if a customer doesn’t want to wait, it’s because they haven’t understood and don’t want to make the effort to understand. Later on, they’ll become a problem customer. Generally, when a customer is like that, even if I lose money and it hurts, I cut them loose. These days, toxic customers are no longer acceptable.
  
  Your client is someone who’s probably already tried 400 consultants, and they’ll always be like that. After 20 years in the business, I’ve learned to deal with it, so I tell them, “Thanks, goodbye—I don’t have time to waste explaining things to you.” Especially for €200 a month.
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: hey [@Karan Holland](obj://user/98eb442ef7f44e1bae025edeee895522) Caleb nailed it, brand new GBPs and sites just take time to age and theres no shortcut for that. you cant control the time factor but you can control the relationship by managing expectations upfront and offering payment flexibility when it makes sense, like dropping the monthly rate temporarily or waiving a few months during the aging period to keep the relationship alive. for this specific client try a frank conversation showing them rank map progress over the last 30 days even if its not green yet, growth direction matters more than current state, and offer a reduced rate for the next 2-3 months until they start seeing results. if they still want out let them go gracefully cause forced relationships rarely end well, document the work delivered and use it as a case study going forward

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 93. External Linking For New Pages (Core 30 Process)
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/external-linking-for-new-pages-core-30-process
Autor: karter-maki-3673  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  4 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Caleb suggests getting an external link to your new pages you create. It doesn't have to be a dofollow link if I'm remembering correctly. If I were to make a post on my Facebook page or LinkedIn and link to the page I created on our website would that suffice as an external link to that page?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Karter Maki](obj://user/528e712e1ed846acbd86d8b34ca1b911) facebook and linkedin posts give you nofollow links from social platforms which do count as external links and provide some value as social signals, but theyre weaker than the validation links or chamber of commerce links which carry actual link equity from authoritative domains. for the core 30 validation link strategy Caleb specifically recommend one per page cause those pass real ranking authority not just social signals. social posts are worth doing as supplementary signals especially for indexation and brand entity get the validation links first then use social as additional reinforcement :\)
  • karter-maki-3673: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Thank you! You're always on top of these questions haha!
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Karter Maki](obj://user/528e712e1ed846acbd86d8b34ca1b911) Haha no worries 🙌
    Just trying to help where I can :\)
• caleb-ulku-6571 ★: As Miles said. Different domains is also important so you could do fb or linkedin, but only once with even a small amount of value

## 94. Wix to WordPress
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/wix-to-wordpress
Autor: tom-perry-3623  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  3 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hi everyone, I am in the process of moving a client's \(local Melbourne electrician\) website from Wix to WordPress. I have recreated the site in WordPress with some different pages and simpler navigation structure. The previous pages, 'How We Help' and "Why Choose Us' which I have merged into "About Us' and "Services" pages don't seem to have any traffic or backlinks. Is it worth adding 301 redirects to these pages, despite their poor rankings? Thanks in advance!

RESPUESTAS:
• ron-gerber-2624: [@Tom Perry](obj://user/fee2107861cd4960bd7b16234b892d8d) if the site is indexed and getting traffic, I would add the 301s. It also helps Google understand your site changes.
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: yes still add 301 redirects even for low traffic pages cause any URL that exists in google's index needs a proper redirect to avoid 404 errors which hurt your overall site quality signals. the cost of adding redirects is essentially zero so theres no downside, just point those old URLs to the most relevant new pages for any pages with literally zero traffic and zero backlinks they technically dont need redirects but adding them is the safest catch all approach during a migration
• tom-perry-3623: Thank you Ron and Miles for your helpful advice!

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 95. My wife's website was hacked, what should I do?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/my-wifes-website-was-hacked-what-should-i-do
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  6 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
There's a bunch of pages indexed for some auto parts in spanish and we didn't create this \(obviously\). The home page was also showing the page with auto parts but we got that fixed...now I'm not sure what to do about de-indexing these other pages.

RESPUESTAS:
• stephan-franklyn-4962: Update: I turned on HackGuardian and some Firewall things \(can't believe I had them off!\) and so now all the pages are returning 404 errors. Is there a way to get these pages de-indexed? They're not in my WP.
  • chiara-burns-7791: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) Well...one of the first things attackers typically do is turn off your firewalls and protections. So I doubt that was on you.
• chiara-burns-7791: Guessing this is Wordpress? It's notorious for getting hacked through third-party plugins. I'm sorry that happened to you and your family.
  
  Before you start thinking about deindexing you need to boot the attackers and immediately contain the situation.
  
  Would recommend putting the site in maintenance mode first, then doing a full backup of the site in its current form
  
  Steps from there:
  
  [ol:1][li]You need to change all the passwords immediately. [li]Rotate the WP salts \(wp-config.php\) to boot anyone who's currently logged in. [li]See if there are any new users who don't look like they should be in there. [li]Sometimes your host \(e.g., WPEngine\) has an incident response option -- basically something they deploy to help you during a security breach. 
  
  They may be able to identify where the breach came from.
  
  Still might be good to run all your plugins through something like [wpscan.com](http://wpscan.com) to see if any are known vulnerabilities.
  
  Beyond that, it gets very technical so I'd check with your host first to see if they can assist you.
  
  EDIT: To be honest, if your host isn't able to help, you probably want to find someone on Upwork who specializes in this. Hunting down any injected code or web shells is pretty technical \(and I say this as a fairly technical marketer who wouldn't want to touch that\). Even if you manage to boot the attackers, they can get back in unless all of that is found and fixed.
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c) thank you so much for your detailed reply!
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: sorry to hear that, hacked WP sites have been increasingly common lately with auto parts spam pages being a popular spam pattern. first contact your hosting support immediately and ask them to scan for malware and identify the vulnerability, then change all passwords for wordpress hosting and FTP, update wordpress core and all plugins, and remove any suspicious user accounts. for the indexed spam pages submit a removal request through GSC use the URL removal tool, then add a malware notification or work with hosting to ensure those URLs return 404 errors so google deindexes them naturally install wordfence plugin going forward to prevent future hacks
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Thank you for your in-depth answer!

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 99. Does it matter if new links open in new tab/window or stay on same page?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/does-it-matter-if-new-links-open-in-new-tabwindow-or-stay-on-same-page
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I noticed all mine defaulted in wix to new window - wondering if I need to change that.

RESPUESTAS:
• ted-tibbetts-5295: I usually do "new window"
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: from a SEO perspective it doesnt matter, google treats both the same for ranking purposes. but for user experience internal links should open in the same tab so users dont end up with 20 tabs open while browsing your site, and external links should open in new tabs so users dont leave your site entirely

## 101. Data from Google Search Console
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/data-from-google-search-console
Autor: andrew-lin-7557  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  1 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
What information can I derive from Google Search Console when reviewing my Performance data?

I’m currently looking at the Performance report, specifically the top queries with the highest number of impressions. I noticed that some queries have high impressions but low clicks.

My assumption is that this may be because my average position for those queries is still relatively low..

I’m trying to better understand which KPIs I should focus on and what the next plan of action should be to increase clicks.

Should my main priority be improving the average position for those high-impression queries? Or should I also be looking at other factors, such as CTR, title tags, meta descriptions, search intent, and whether the ranking page matches the query?

Ultimately, I want to understand how to use Google Search Console data to decide which pages and queries to optimize first, and what specific actions I should take to drive more organic traffic.

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: yeah high impressions low clicks usually means youre ranking page 2+ so people see your snippet but dont click. priority is queries ranking 4-10 with quick wins like better title tags meta descriptions and internal links to push them into top 3, those are your fastest movers. for queries ranking 8+ build supporting content to break through, and ignore queries 15+ unless theyre high volume cause those need more authority. also check CTR against expected position benchmarks, if your CTR is way below average for your position then title and meta is the issue not ranking. low hanging fruit is queries 4-10 first

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 102. GBP service price ?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/gbp-service-price
Autor: karlan-miller-9646  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  5 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hey guys, I couldn't find anything in the community about putting pricing on a GBP service. Is this something you do or don't do?

This service is for weed control for a lawn care company.
I would not have known this, but it is actually the most profitable service of a lawn care company.

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: id suggest dont add pricing its not recommended don't do add to GBP services unless you have to, leaving prices off lets you have the pricing conversation when prospects call which gives you a chance to upsell and build value. and for weed control where pricing varies just keep it open and let your sales process handle the pricing conversation
• karlan-miller-9646: I assumed the same thing about pricing. Thanks. 😊
• james-ruffer-3463: If you leave prices off then AI will not use your profile for comparison results.
  • karlan-miller-9646: [@James Ruffer](obj://user/0e1dff4c279546d2bf7353da1aede1da) Do you normally do a price range, let's say a GBP service costs between $1,000 and $1,500.
• james-ruffer-3463: Depends on the industry. Coupons/specials and normal pricing usually gets it done

Autor: steve-mizer-1007  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  1 comentarios

In the Core 30 AI Agent course you mention the Locations Hub and a Supporting Content Hub. I'm familiar with the Locations Hub but is the Supporting Content Hub new?

I was under the impression we avoided "archive" pages or a general blog page and just used internal linking for all the supporting content. Could you kindly expound or point me to the course module that explains it?

Thanks Caleb!

• miles-hayat-9206 ★: hey [@Steve Mizer](obj://user/c4fa1f5aebd049fd8fd6997850197295) check out the topical relevance lesson at this link, Caleb covers exactly how supporting content connects through internal linking rather than needing a traditional blog archive page. the supporting content hub concept in the core 30 agent is basically a structured way to organize PAA based articles that link to relevant service pages, its not a traditional blog hub or archive page just a logical grouping of supporting content tied to specific service topics. caleb walks through the linking structure in detail in that module so worth rewatching for clarity

## 104. Core 30 homepage structure question
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/core-30-homepage-structure-question
Autor: austin-delaney-1000  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Looking at the Gary IN demo site I understand the homepage is structured to rank the GBP, acting as a hub.

In the niche I'm working on, the primary GBP category IS the main service searched. The searcher arrives at the homepage and it needs to convert them for that main keyword.

I've got:

Primary category \(main keyword\) with 10 services

One secondary category \(also a strong keyword\) with 12 services

Am I right in thinking that my homepage needs to be deeper and denser on conversion factors for my keyword than the Gary IN site is on "plumber"?

Plus include the H2s for the secondary category and other preferred services.

Thanks.

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: yes exactly, when your primary GBP category is also your main moneymaker keyword the homepage has to do double duty as both the ranking page and the converting page. so add the H2s for secondary category and top services like normal, but also pack in more conversion elements like detailed service breakdowns, strong social proof, trust signals, and clear CTAs throughout. think of it as a sales page with proper SEO structure layered on top, same core 30 framework just deeper content and stronger conversion focus
• austin-delaney-1000: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Thank you!

## 106. Human Editing of AI-produced content
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/human-editing-of-ai-produced-content
Autor: kevin-tully-4285  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  5 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
What are you all doing, and more specifically what are you looking for, when you are editing AI-written content? Are you only looking to fix errors? Are you replacing words that don't feel natural? What other things are you looking for when asking a human to review your AI content? If AI-produced content looks good and otherwise doesn't need any changes, are you still making human edits to satisfy Google?

Lastly how do the humanizer tools work in this workflow?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: the main things to look for are verifying all business specific details are accurate like services offered, pricing, areas served and any claims made, replacing generic phrases that sound robotic or templated with more natural conversational language, adding any local specific details the business has that claude wouldnt know, and making sure the tone matches how the business actually communicates. even if the content looks good make at least light edits cause adding genuine local knowledge and personality is what separates rankable content from AI slop that google is getting better at detecting. humanizer tools work by rewriting sentence structure and word choice to reduce AI pattern signatures but human edits adding real local knowledge are always more valuable than running through a humanizer tool alone cause you know thats also AI that do humanize
• ted-tibbetts-5295: I come at this from a copywriting perspective. While I value human writing, it's not cost effective these days to human write all the pages. Especially since I suspect that very few humans actually get to the services pages. I think they generally make decisions at the home page or category page. \(If anyone has GSC data on this I would love to see it...what's the drop off at service pages?\)
  
  That being said, I generally do a quick scan of service page copy...remove "You're not alone" and em-dashes which are huge AI flags. I use AI to write the home, about, and contact page, but more just to get the structure. I revise it heavily trying to get the specific voice of the business owner.
• chiara-burns-7791: I fix factual errors and don't worry about the writing other than running it through a humanizer \(which is in-built with Caleb's Core30 Agent as long as you provide the API\). Reason being, most people aren't even going to read this. They'll skim \(if that\).
• reece-rainer-3922: I have a SEO copywriting skill in Claude and then I send it to zeroGPT and POP API in addition to using the brand voice as an input. It's working OK so far but if the zeroGPT threshold is too low it creates absolute swap so I'm still in the process of refining it
• sean-young-9702: Also, if you not entirely up on the GBP's industry jargon, you can send the content to the owner to see if they flag something that is not quite 'right' to an insider's eye. Then fold their feedback into your content creation so future content has the information \(when needed\).

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 107. Cracking tough LA County market w/ geo expansion
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/cracking-tough-la-county-market-w-geo-expansion
Autor: chiara-burns-7791  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  3 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
1. Is it optimal to publish geo content as a blog/article or a webpage? \([https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=043327fc50e34ae0834cf601fdac960f](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=043327fc50e34ae0834cf601fdac960f)\)

2. Real estate is interesting from a content creation perspective because they're expected to commentate on school systems, lifestyle, things to do, neighborhoods, restaurants, etc. I think that might be an opportunity...

e.g., This client has over a THOUSAND blogs covering various cities and topics -- is it worthwhile to take the time to link back to relevant locations pages? Or is that neither here nor there?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: hey [@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c) crushing it again, that progression from march 17th to may 2nd shows real geographic expansion happening with more green/yellow coverage spreading outward
  
  for the 1000+ blogs yes do internal linking but before you do that check gsc to see which blogs are actually getting traffic vs just wasting crawl budget or deindexed, if theyre dead weight just delete them dont waste time linking from pages that dont rank or get impressions! once you filter down to the blogs actually getting traffic and indexed, then add internal links from those to your relevant locations pages cause those will pass real authority. real estate is gold for geo content with neighborhood lifestyle school commentary naturally building local relevance, leverage that hard but only with the blogs that are actually performing :\)
  • chiara-burns-7791: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Thanks Miles! Yeah I think real estate is unique in that regard because it's almost exepcted that they're going to talk about certain landmarks.
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c) yeah exactly real estate is one of the easiest niches for geo content cause its built into the industry, leverage that advantage and youll dominate

## 108. How do you determine what services to use in GBP?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/how-do-you-determine-what-services-to-use-in-gbp
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  8 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I've had Claude help me come up with services and it's better than nothing...however, it can't really look at search volume.

I noticed some of the Claude services it used were like New Tire Installation, Auto Tire Replacement....which were fine at first, but then I looked them up in ahrefs and found that while those exact terms had under 200 sv, Tire Replacement had 21K and tire installation had about 2K.

Any thoughts on how I can be sure I'm using the best, most searched services in the GBP?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: GMB everywhere is the most reliable tool for finding the right GBP services cause it shows you exactly what top ranking competitors are using which tells you what google actually accepts. combine that with a quick ahrefs or google keyword planner check on the highest priority services like you did to confirm SV, and you have a data driven service list that is both google approved and SV confirmed
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) d'oh! I've been using GMB Everywhere for cats, not services. Will do this moving forward! Thank you!
• reece-rainer-3922: Hey dude, are you using Claude or Claude code? Claude can get search data if you give it an API key to something like SerpApi or DataforSEO. I now validate my GBP services against actual local keyword search data and it's cheap to do too. I get Claude code to find the highest search volume relevant keywords for my client and build the GBP services around that
  • ted-tibbetts-5295: [@Reece Rainer](obj://user/dd7f5e98c32541d8b25db016fb63fd38) Sounds efficient! Would you mind sharing your process in a bit more detail? I have Claude pro and DataforSEO.
  • reece-rainer-3922: [@Ted Tibbetts](obj://user/b2238bbb591249e48db6fd6a63dc0d7c) DM me bro
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Reece Rainer](obj://user/dd7f5e98c32541d8b25db016fb63fd38) I'm going to DM you as well, I do have DataforSEO
  • sean-young-9702: [@Reece Rainer](obj://user/dd7f5e98c32541d8b25db016fb63fd38) I'd like to DM you to see your process, but your Chat is turned off.
• mark-farrow-6630: I use GMB everywhere, on the CAT's I manually check them in my GBP as I've made the mistake trusting GMB everywhere CAT's suggestion, building out the Core 30 then going to the GBP and finding the CAT offered is different or does not exist! Yes very frustrating mistake. So now I verify them by hand to make sure they exist. :\)\) I also run the services suggested thru Claude to get improved suggestions. It will rank them high to low based on intent and actual service terms people are using in search.

## 109. Mirroring GBP services into GBP products? Bad practice?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/mirroring-gbp-services-into-gbp-products-bad-practice
Autor: hery-makna-2140  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  3 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hi [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c)
So, I'm handling a dental clinic GBP.
Previously, the dental clinic used an SEO agency, but it didn't yield any results.
The previous agency listed products on GBP, which were identical to the service listings.
Does this practice provide benefits or is it detrimental from an SEO perspective?
Should I remove all of these products?
Thank you.

RESPUESTAS:
• caleb-ulku-6571 ★: Duplicating services as products is unnecessary and can confuse Google's algorithm.
  
  Products and services serve different purposes. Services describe what you do, products are items you sell.
  
  For dental clinic, keep services \(cleanings, fillings, crowns\) and remove duplicate products unless you actually sell physical items.
  
  Clean up the GBP structure to match what you actually offer =\)
  • hery-makna-2140: [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) Thankssssss
• ryan-t-5170: [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) For auto repair, we sell alternator replacement services but technically sell the alternator. Would you do products and services or just toss products because its implied?

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 110. Opening secondary locations
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/opening-secondary-locations
Autor: connor-doyle-8725  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  19 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
How are other agencies handling clients who want to rank in areas where they don’t have a physical location? \(Storefront businesses, not SABs.\)

A few things I’m trying to figure out:
[ul][li]Are agencies having clients use a friend or family member’s address in the target city? What works when the client has no connection to the area?[li]How do you frame this conversation to keep clients long-term?[li]Best practices for opening a second location?[li]How do you avoid duplicate listing flags and losing both GBPs?

The situation I keep hitting: a client ranks well in their main location but isn’t generating enough leads to justify the retainer. What’s the actual play here, and how does it work operationally?

Clients get noticeably more excited when we mention a second location. Trying to understand what people are really doing, not the textbook answer. Real experience appreciated.

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: the real approach is coworking space in the target city clients are usually willing to pay that when you frame it as the cost of a second location that generates leads and trying to rank with no address at all. friends and family addresses work but carry suspension risk especially if google does a manual review and theres no real business presence there. to avoid duplicate listing flags make sure each GBP has completely different phone numbers different landing pages and no category overlap, and never let both GBPs point to the same homepage. the conversation with clients is simple, show them the rank map coverage from their current location and the gap where a second location would fill in, the visual makes the investment case for them :\)
  • connor-doyle-8725: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Thanks for that, that is helpful.
    
    When you say no Category overlap, do you mean the primary category must be different or even the secondary categories must not overlap?
    
    If there a minimum distance between two locations recommended?
    
    Do you mean actually purchasing an office at a coworking location? That seems like a big ongoing expense for the client. Also how would we get past signage requirements in a verification video at a coworking office?
    
    Thanks
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Connor Doyle](obj://user/b0650d3939db40068fade25d6f700e17) for categories i means ideally no overlap on secondary overlap , for distance no official minimum but generally the further apart the better, even 5-10 miles helps significantly. for coworking its just a mailbox or hot desk membership not a full office, and for signage most coworking spaces have building directories where you can add your business name which counts as signage in the verification video
  • connor-doyle-8725: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) awesome, thanks! The category requirement seems a bit difficult to get around. For example for a roofer it would not make sense to have another category other than roofing contractor
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Connor Doyle](obj://user/b0650d3939db40068fade25d6f700e17) yeah but google does allow it as long as the business genuinely operates from both addresses. the key differentiators in that case become the number being completely different, each GBP pointing to its own unique location page with distinct NAP, and the two profiles being far enough apart geographically that google sees them as serving different markets
  • reece-rainer-3922: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) hey Miles when you say different landing pages do you mean completely different domains , or could you use the same domain with different city subdirectory on the same domain?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Reece Rainer](obj://user/dd7f5e98c32541d8b25db016fb63fd38) i mean same domain with different city pages is perfectly fine and actually preferred, in fact keeping everything in one domain consolidates your authority rather than splitting it. the key is each landing page has completely unique content unique NAP matching that specific GBP and unique local signals for that city
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) \(For clarity\) So in the case of a chiropractor that offers Acupuncture and Massage Therapy \(Both additional cats\) would the 1st location have all 3 and then the 2nd location would only have Chiropractor, but not Acu or MT?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) no both locations can have all 3 cat if the business offers all those services at both locations. the no overlap advice is for situations where you're trying to differentiate 2 GBPs to avoid the possum filter, but in your case if both locations legitimately offer all 3 services then both GBPs should reflect that accurately. the differentiation comes from different numbers different addresses and different dedicated landing pages
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) I see what you're saying, that's helpful to know actually. I was more referring to getting a 2nd location that's like a coworking office to expand the GBP.
    
    A better example might be a remodeling company \(SAB\) that offers Remodeling, roofing, and plumbing as primary cats in the primary location, then when they buy the coworking space, they would only put Remodeling and not the other 2?
    
    If that's correct, and they were a remodeling company focusing on Roofing customers, could they make the primary cat of the 2nd location Roofing? \(Company name ABC Remodeling\)
    
    I have a client we're about to expand into a 2nd location for in this situation.
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) yes for a SAB expanding into a coworking space second location to avoid possum filter you do want to differentiate the categories, so the 2nd GBP could have remodeler as primary with different secondary cats than the first location. but be careful with making the second locations primary cat roofing if the registered business name is ABC Remodeling cause google does check business name to category alignment.
    
    you can do in safe way that keeping remodeler as primary at both locations but loading up the second location with completely different secondary categories, services, dedicated landing page, and unique phone number. the differentiation in NAP unique website pages and secondary cat combined is usually enough to avoid possum filter without needing to change the primary category which can trigger reverification
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) okay got it thank you!
• mark-farrow-6630: Once you get them ranking in the primary location, pitch leaving 50% of the retainer applied to that location, then apply the full budget they were spending on the new location. Just a thought. Grows your income incrementally and assists them in growing their business.
  • arielle-gamble-3898: [@Mark Farrow](obj://user/5d7f726c110a43dc973a719104a5e4b4) my brain is not computing this for some reason, would you mind providing an example, please? Maybe I'll get it with some hypothetical numbers plugged in. I'd really appreciate it!
  • mark-farrow-6630: [@Arielle Gamble](obj://user/d1e5b6dc291f4d6fb68ecbb6f8d1a0b5) Regarding secondary locations, i'm just mentioning pricing, that has worked for me.
  • arielle-gamble-3898: [@Mark Farrow](obj://user/5d7f726c110a43dc973a719104a5e4b4) correct, I was wondering if you'd give an example with some hypothetical numbers plugged in so I can better understand the structure you laid out.
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Arielle Gamble](obj://user/d1e5b6dc291f4d6fb68ecbb6f8d1a0b5) I'm assuming [@Mark Farrow](obj://user/5d7f726c110a43dc973a719104a5e4b4) means that if they're paying you $1,000/mo that you'd offer to put $500/mo towards the 2nd location. Whatever is left over, you would put all of that towards ranking the 2nd location.
• brian-dunn-7776: What documentation needs to be provided in the GBP verification video for a coworking office space to get these approved?
  • mark-farrow-6630: [@Brian Dunn](obj://user/ff3dc62068ed4bd98d732f47df5a136a) state license / DBA with that address, video verification with signage, invoice with that address on it, business card with that address on it.

## 111. 1 company is dominating GPB
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/1-company-is-dominating-gpb
Autor: ryan-carlin-4538  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  13 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hi everyone - new to the community, excited to be here.

I have a challenge that I would love some advice on.

I'm running SEO in a fairly competitive region \(all Long Island, NY\). I only have 1 GBP listing currently.

There is a competitor that has essentially taken over the top 5 spots across all rankmaps on Long Island. There are gaps that we can potentially exploit, but generally they have taken position everywhere.

It looks like the competitor is running the same GBP strategy that you guys teach.

The nature of our business is is multi-location - probably 15-20 high level cities \(and then the subcities/hamlets across LI would be additional.

My questions:
[ol:1][li]how can I set up GBP at scale?[li]What should the strategy be to beat out his competitor? 

Thanks!
Ryan

RESPUESTAS:
• chiara-burns-7791: Answers to your two questions:
  
  [ol:1][li]Setting up GBP for multiple locations: [https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=8c266b3b783246908270d88672365071](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=8c266b3b783246908270d88672365071)
  
  2.If they're also running Caleb's Core30 and you want to outgun them, it'll be a matter of nailing the topical relevance pages/FAQs, geolocation pages, and highquality backlinks. Do more of all of that than they are ;p Another thing that isn't talked about a lot in this Skool Group but *does* impact rankings \(even moreso with recent updates\) is the number of reviews on your GBP, and the actual words customers use in those reviews. [@Clay Lawrence](obj://user/1df3449b0e0245279d8880c8ebf695b1) has the best content & teachings on how to run review reactivation campaigns imho, especially if you operate out of GHL \(I've no affiliation btw, just spend way too much time in Skool lol\)
• ryan-carlin-4538: [@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c) how do i add more GBP's? Is google still strict about adding as many GBP as you want?
  • chiara-burns-7791: [@Ryan Carlin](obj://user/e09047f8464844058cf81ab78843cc43) If you have a physical address it shouldn't be an issue. The vid linked above will show you how to set up the GBPs <--> landing pages
  • ryan-carlin-4538: [@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c) What if i don't have access to more locations. What do you do then? Any way to get more?
  • chiara-burns-7791: [@Ryan Carlin](obj://user/e09047f8464844058cf81ab78843cc43) Miles covered this below -- coworking spaces for proximity signal.
• ryan-carlin-4538: Also, my GBP doesn't list the 'perfect' category in my industry. Companies use "real estate consultant" as the category when its commonly used with a differetn keyword phrase or name.
  
  The competitor that is ranking is not using the GBP category as their h1 and title tag
  
  What should I do?
  • chiara-burns-7791: [@Ryan Carlin](obj://user/e09047f8464844058cf81ab78843cc43) See if re-theming applies to your situation: [https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=2bd7a9995606430eaa63c344c3edd9c4](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=2bd7a9995606430eaa63c344c3edd9c4)
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: hey [@Ryan Carlin](obj://user/e09047f8464844058cf81ab78843cc43) welcome! 🙌 to compete against a competitor dominating all of long island with one GBP you need multiple physical locations strategically placed across the island, coworking spaces so you have proximity signals competing directly against them in different parts of the market. for beating a well optimized competitor the edge comes from review velocity, more reviews coming in faster than them, plus deeper core 30 topical coverage with more service pages and stronger internal linking. check their GBP with GMB everywhere to see exactly what categories and services they have and make sure you match or exceed every signal they have while adding any gaps you find :\) and for further you can watch this lecture: [https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=8c266b3b783246908270d88672365071&utm_campaign=skool_link_post&utm_content=15482c0c7dec4b84a29a295bc7678f2f](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=8c266b3b783246908270d88672365071&utm_campaign=skool_link_post&utm_content=15482c0c7dec4b84a29a295bc7678f2f)
  • ryan-carlin-4538: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Thanks. I've heard that google sometimes does video verification and they require proper signage. is that true?
  • ryan-carlin-4538: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) or is it pretty easy?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Ryan Carlin](obj://user/e09047f8464844058cf81ab78843cc43) yes google require video verification for new GBP listings and signage is one of the things they look for in the video. for coworking spaces most have building directories where you can add your business name which counts as signage, the video also needs to show you walking around the space with your business documents like a business license or invoice showing that address :\)
• ryan-carlin-4538: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) got it thanks. Are there any services out there that can do this for you?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Ryan Carlin](obj://user/e09047f8464844058cf81ab78843cc43) yes people offer these kind of services but its not recommended cause thats temporary verification but in future google may ask for documentation etc at that time thats huge issues you can face

## 112. Press Release Questions
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/press-release-questions
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  1 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
If they have something newsworthy, do you still tie it to the silly day?

Client is running a promo deal in general on FB, should we still tie that to the silly day?

For PRunderground.com
I'm assuming for the boilerplate About section, we can just copy/paste it from website or GBP?

Any promo codes available? 😁

RESPUESTAS:
• caleb-ulku-6571 ★: no if it's newsworthy go with with =\) the silly day stuff is just for ideas because most don't have something newsworthy every month
  
  usually we just have claude write it based on website / gbp info
