## 113. [Keyword] + [City] in H1, does the order matter for ranking?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/keyword-city-in-h1-does-the-order-matter-for-ranking
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  5 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
a client's most searched term according to GBP Searches Breakdown is Chiropractor \[city state abbrev\].

So does it have a big impact if the H1 is something like "Your \[City State\] Chiropractor for the whole family" vs "Your Chiropractor in \[City State\] for the whole family"?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: Yes the order does matter slightly with primary keyword earlier in the H1, but honestly the difference between those two versions is minimal and google is smart enough to understand both. what matters way more is that both the primary category and city are mention in the H1 and match the title tag, so either version works fine :\)
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) okay that makes sense. thank you.
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) for the h2s on a cat page, should they ust be the service name or service + city?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) you can use service + city that helps local relevance, but just keep it natural and don’t overdo it
• reece-rainer-3922: Not really sure, I have competitors in Sydney where I live ranking #1 for 'SEO agency' in a city of 5 million and their H1 does not match their primary category at all. Some of them the H1 doesn't even mention the keyword.

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 114. Blog Commenting
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/blog-commenting
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
IF we find a blog and want to comment to boost a client...what do we say exactly in the comment? Is there a general length it should be, and do we target them towards the home page or specific service/cat pages?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: honestly the easiest way to do this at scale is just copy the blog post URL and paste it into claude with a prompt like "write a genuine helpful comment for this blog post that adds value to the discussion, keep it 3-4 sentences and make it sound natural with my website mention" and use the business name or naked URL as your name/website field rather than keyword stuffed anchor text. link to the homepage or most relevant category page depending on the blog topic, and never use exact match keyword anchors in the comment itself cause that looks manipulative and most blog owners will reject or delete it
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) excellent, thank you!

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 115. Topical Relevance Query
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/topical-relevance-query
Autor: tomas-affranchino-9734  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  1 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hi Team,

Quick question on the execution of the topical relevance strategy. In terms of the prompts we are supposed to do the following:
1. Target keyword \(supporting article keyword here?\)
2. Target keyword for page it's supporting \(main service/category we want to improve?\)
3. People Also Ask \(PAA\) + competitor H2/H3s \(what are we supposed to include here? Is this the PAA that appears for item 1 above and the competitors top ranking articles H2/H3s?

Also, my understanding is that once we create the FAQs, then we start building out these supporting articles so that we are linking back to that home page/service page and also linking from the FAQs to the supporting article. From there, we need to source an external link to the article. Anything else I am missing?

We are executing this primarily on what leadsnap shows on the grid for that keyword right? So if its the home page, we follow the strategy there, if its a category page or service we replicate it there and so on?

Thanks for the clarifications.

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Tomas Affranchino](obj://user/5a215abd0bff47028103c902b023920c) your understanding is mostly correct. for the prompt inputs, target keyword is the specific article topic, the supporting page target is the main service page you want to rank higher like water heater replacement houston, and the PAA/Reddit plus competitor H2s come from googling that article keyword and pulling the people also ask questions plus checking what H2s top ranking articles use for that specific topic. your workflow of FAQ on service page linking to supporting article plus external validation link to the article is exactly right. and yes execute this strategy based on what leadsnap shows needs topical support, start with your homepage primary keyword then work through category and service pages that need more topical depth to break into top 3

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 117. Facebook ads comments
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/facebook-ads-comments
Autor: idan-bubnovsky-4121  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hey guys, I started running meta ads today and got a couple comments from interested people on my ad \(they didnt fill the form\). Does anyone have any SOP for answering such comments?

RESPUESTAS:
• ted-tibbetts-5295: From a copywriting perspective, try and keep the conversation going. Definitely respond to them, ask a question, make a statement that arouses curiosity, something that sparks engagement. The more people engage, the more Facebook will push it to the top.
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: first make sure reply quickly within the first hour cause speed matters a lot for conversion, keep it short and friendly then immediately send them a direct message with your offer and a clear CTA. the goal is to move the conversation from public comments to private DM as fast as possible where you can have a real conversation and close them, never try to pitch in the comments cause it looks desperate and other prospects can see it

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 118. Is Social Signals just a 1-time thing?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/is-social-signals-just-a-1-time-thing
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  6 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Or do you do it every month, quarter, year, etc?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: social signals are an ongoing thing, consistent activity over time is what builds the entity signals google looks for. posting regularly on the business social profiles even just once or twice a week keeps the brand active and visible across platforms, and each new page or article you publish is worth sharing on social to create fresh signals pointing to that URL
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Okay so to clarify: if I'm going to buy the social signals from ice cream shop, would you recommend we do that 1-2x/wk, and then every time we make a new page or article do it again? Or are you referring to social media posting in general \(like manually on their own FB/IG, etc?\)
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) icecreamtruck social signals are a one time purchase per page to establish the initial signal, while the ongoing weekly posting is the organic social media activity on their actual FB and IG profiles, those are two separate things and both are worth doing
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Totally understand everything now, thank you Miles!
  • reece-rainer-3922: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) is the social signals the same thing that Caleb refers to as PBN links to our core 30 pages?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Reece Rainer](obj://user/dd7f5e98c32541d8b25db016fb63fd38) no they are different things, PBN links are backlinks that pass authority to your pages while social signals are shares and engagement on social media platforms, both help but they serve different purposes and work differently as ranking signals

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 119. Purchasing A Domain With SEO In Mind
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/purchasing-a-domain-with-seo-in-mind
Autor: dominic-trejo-3180  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I have to purchase a domain for a business in Ohio I am helping. Their business name domain is taken so I'm thinking of adding "Ohio" in the name, which is available. So it would be [businessnameohio.com](http://businessnameohio.com).
They live in a small rural area called Amherst, which is in Lorain County.
The business address is in Amherst but they serve ALL of Lorain County which encompasses 5 other areas besides Amherst.
For SEO purposes would it be better to get a domain with their state name like I said above.
Or county name, like [businessnameLorainCounty.com](http://businessnameLorainCounty.com)?
Or city name like [businessnameAmherst.com](http://businessnameAmherst.com)?
Or does it even matter?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: id suggest go with the business name plus amherst since thats the verified GBP city and keeps NAP alignment clean. avoid putting ohio or lorain county in the domain cause that limits you if they ever expand beyond that area. the geographic expansion to all of lorain county is handled through location pages on the site not through the domain name, so just get the cleanest most brandable version available and focus on the core 30 structure instead :\)
  • dominic-trejo-3180: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Ah ok. I was wondering if Amherst in the domain would be limiting since they service other areas but it makes sense since their address is in Amherst. Thank you!

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 120. Help with pre-existing Massage Therapy service page (new category page, service pages, etc)
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/help-with-pre-existing-massage-therapy-service-page-new-category-page-service-pages-etc
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  6 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I have a chiro client who also offers Massage Therapy. I created a Massage Therapist category page and now working on the service pages for all the different types of massages.

They have a service page for massage therapy already, I'm assuming I'd link the cat page to that service page but then would I need to add more content to the service page or should I see if it's indexed first or how to handle that? I'm assuming I would not take away content, similar to a GBP LP.

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: yes link the massage therapist cat page down to the existing massage therapy service page and dont remove any content from it. check if its already indexed in GSC first, if it is then just add the internal link from the category page and optimize the existing service page title tag and H1 to match the GBP service name format with city. if its thin on content add more but dont rebuild it from scratch, treat it like any other service page in the silo and let the category page above it pass authority down through the new internal link you just created :\)
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) ty! How do I determine if something is considered thin content?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) you can use screaming frog tool it will tell you about thin pages
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) ah got it thank you
• ted-tibbetts-5295: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) You can also do quick competitor research to compare content. I have a custom bot that I built for analyzing content, but I think there's a prompt in here somewhere \(can't find it at the moment\) where AI searches the different components on competitors' pages and lets you know what you really need to have to rank...not necessarily in word count but in problems, tool/material references, trust, etc..
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Ted Tibbetts](obj://user/b2238bbb591249e48db6fd6a63dc0d7c) good idea! thanks, I'll need to search for that!

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 121. Any tips on hiring a proofreader/copywriter?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/any-tips-on-hiring-a-proofreadercopywriter
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  4 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I've gotten to a point that I need extra help. My mom is helping out sporadically but I need to hire someone to 1\) proofread/fact check articles, and possibly 2\) create the articles and post them on the site.

Any insight on where to hire, how much to pay for a fact-checker, and possibly an actual copywriter, and how to determine who's the right candidate?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: you can use upwork to find proofreaders and content editors for local SEO work. but first id suggest build your SOPs first documenting exactly what good looks like for your content including the core 30 structure etc and what to check for, then give them a test task using one of your actual articles before committing to ongoing work :\)
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Thank you! What would you recommend paying for these kinds of positions?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) honestly cant tell you exact pricing cause everyone charge based on their experience but u can expect form 5-10$ per hour
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) fair enough ty!

## 123. GoDaddy to WP🔜
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/godaddy-to-wp
Autor: aiden-hardy-8619  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  9 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
We’re transferring our clients website to Wordpress after extending him for another 4 months

What’s the best way of doing this so we don’t lose any ranking power???

I know we have to do it manually and re index all the pages but what’s the plan for something like this

Do we keep the other site live while building another one?

Need help here

RESPUESTAS:
• aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d)
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: yes expect a temporary ranking drop during the migration cause google has to reprocess everything but it recovers within 3+ weeks if done right. build the wordpress site on a staging environment first while keeping godaddy live, match every URL exactly from the old site to new or set up 301 redirects for any that change, submit new sitemap in GSC immediately after launch and manually request indexing for all important pages for recrawling. the key is never having both sites live simultaneously pointing to the same GBP, switch everything over at once and do the redirects properly and you'll recover rankings faster
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) alright you’re gonna have to dumb this down lmao
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d)
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) haha ok simplified version. build the new wordpress site in staging first, keep godaddy live the whole time. once wordpress is ready, point your domain to WP and turn godaddy off on the same day. thats it, expect rankings to wobble for a few weeks then recover
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) okay and match all like the domains they just won’t really have optimized content yet?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) yes exactly, match all the URLs so 301 redirects work properly and rankings transfer, then optimize the content on each page after the migration is stable
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) every page at once?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) yes do redirect at once just make sure all pages are proper aligned

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 124. Current site
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/current-site
Autor: ryan-carlin-4538  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  1 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I inherited a site to do their SEO. They have 100+ location pages \[target keyword\] + location.

They also have 8 service pages for two locations \(16 total\)

And about 250 blog posts

Where do I start with services?

Do I keep the current service pages \(16 total\) and make those my services in GBP? They are accurate in the eyes of me and the client

Should i cluster up the blog posts and have those link up to their appropriate services?

What do I do with the location pages? We only have 1 GBP listing \(looking to get more\).

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: first run screaming frog on the whole site and audit all existing service pages against core 30 standards, check every title tag has service plus city format proper H1 and internal links back to parent category pages cause fixing existing pages is faster than building new ones. for the 250 blog posts go through each one and check if its supporting a specific service or just a generic guide, if its a generic guide thats not driving traffic or linking to a service page deindex and delete it cause thin guide content dilutes topical authority and google doesnt reward it for local businesses and waste crawl budget. for the 100 location pages make sure every single one has genuinely unique local content not just city name swapped templates cause google will filter out the thin ones. fix the foundation first before adding anything new

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 125. Easy Youth Links
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/easy-youth-links
Autor: david-dutton-6463  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  18 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I made two different Facebook posts in local groups about sponsoring and I got probably 30 leads and I’ve already done two sponsorships for youth sports! Super easy. Hoping to do like $50-75.

RESPUESTAS:
• tristin-wurzbach-1741: When you get the sponsorship, do you have them put a back link on their page?
  • david-dutton-6463: [@Tristin Wurzbach](obj://user/05e3e2d09bb841dc896a8f2d6501f7ec) what I’m telling them is when they put the back link on their page I’ll send a payment.
  • david-dutton-6463: [@Tristin Wurzbach](obj://user/05e3e2d09bb841dc896a8f2d6501f7ec) This is what I sent them and said they can modify just don’t change anchor text or link.
    
    Thank you to Franklin estate planning attorney Sara Thomas of August Law PLLC for making a donation.
    Here's the HTML code if you need to embed it directly:
    Thank you to <a href="[https://www.augustlawpllc.com">Franklin](https://www.augustlawpllc.com">Franklin) estate planning attorney Sara Thomas of August Law PLLC</a> for making a donation.
• caleb-ulku-6571 ★: [@David Dutton](obj://user/c1e80e98442242d7b5bad3d467f94bee) I saw those posts! Great idea if your local clients are local to you =\)
  • david-dutton-6463: [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) Thanks man. Yep! Thank are. What I think I’m gonna do is make that post once a month and maybe try to pick up two local sports teams a month.
• christy-schlothauer-6194: Thanks for posting! I did a search for local teams and the ones I found want $500 per season. I’ll give your technique a try.
  [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c)
  Do you refresh sponsorships each season for sports teams? Are you finding these for your clients or do you ask them to find them?
  You also said you sponsor a local event. What happens when the event is over? That seems temporary. If they take down the page after the event, you would lose that link.
  Thanks!
  • caleb-ulku-6571 ★: [@Christy Schlothauer](obj://user/f0010ccd41c84fecad6b658b24d7f21d) they rarely take down pages after the event in my experience. you can always ask them if they do or not
    
    But yes you’re definitely right that you want to sponsor teams / etc that will keep the page up long term
  • david-dutton-6463: [@Christy Schlothauer](obj://user/f0010ccd41c84fecad6b658b24d7f21d) also here’s what I found because they just don’t know any better.
    
    They’ll come back to you and give you like a sponsor sheet for their uniforms or a banner or something like that.
    
    Just tell them that you’re not interested in that at the time. Just let them know that you’re happy to Venmo or send them a check once the link is on their page.
    
    I think I’m gonna make a monthly announcement on my Facebook and do like two a month maybe.
  • david-dutton-6463: [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) do you do the anchor text or do you really care that much on these if they do the name of the business or the anchor text?
  • caleb-ulku-6571 ★: [@David Dutton](obj://user/c1e80e98442242d7b5bad3d467f94bee) Business name anchor text is fine.
    
    Link source matters more than perfect anchors. Don’t overthink it.​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​
• matt-hirsch-2389: [@David Dutton](obj://user/c1e80e98442242d7b5bad3d467f94bee) smart move. How are you making posts in your local community? Are you posting the $ sponsor or are you asking if anyone is interested and asking for people to reach out to you?
  • david-dutton-6463: [@Matt Hirsch](obj://user/c849657f7c16430bbea81c3a339d38b5) Post: If anyone has a local sports team or local charity and would like a small sponsorship, please comment below or send me a message. You must have a website to be considered.
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@David Dutton](obj://user/c1e80e98442242d7b5bad3d467f94bee) Smart, I might have to give this a shot!
• david-dutton-6463: OK, here’s what I keep running into that aggravating. I just wanted everybody to know if you start doing this.
  
  1. People do not understand and so they’ll send you a form to fill out for sponsorship to put a banner on the field or on the T-shirts and all that.
  
  2. People are also sending me their Facebook page even after I tell them that I just want their website. So if you post the message, put it very, very clear that you’re only interested in organizations that have a website.
• david-dutton-6463: All right, well that whole easy link thing is not easy.
  
  I’ve made multiple post. Have yet to get one youth link. I just got turned down by a homeschool travel team that wouldn’t even put a link on their website for 100 bucks.
  
  I just looked back at one of my posts and I had 135 comments.
  
  All other people posting links are just moms or parents tagging a school.
  
  [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) any secrets? It’s crazy these people need money, but they rather sell you a T-shirt or a banner.

## 126. High Authority Domain Links, do we use typical anchor text or is the 1 the anchor text?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/high-authority-domain-links-do-we-use-typical-anchor-text-or-is-the-1-the-anchor-text
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  4 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
When we cite it at the end, will that be a link to the article as well so it gets hit twice or will that just be plain text url?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: for high authority domai links use branded or naked URL anchors not exact match keyword anchors, cause exact match anchors on external links look manipulative to google. for the citation at the end of supporting articles it should be a clickable link not plain text so the target page gets the link equity, plain text URLs dont pass any ranking value so always make it an actual hyperlink even if the anchor is just the naked URL format :\)
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) okay let me see if I understand properly: so I would do something like "according to the CDC, blah blah blah. \[1\]" and then make the anchor text "CDC" in that sentence, then at the bottom would be the citation, and it would have the naked URL \([www.cdc.gov/study](www.cdc.gov/study)\) which would also be a clickable link to that specific article?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) yes exactly right, the inline citation anchor like CDC or the source name is the clickable link in the body text pointing to the external source. the numbered citation at the bottom should also be a clickable hyperlink to the same URL not just plain text, so the reader can click either the inline mention or the footnote citation to reach the source
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) sweet, thanks!!

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 127. Is it worth creating content in different languages?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/is-it-worth-creating-content-in-different-languages
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  5 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I have a client who's best CTR and most impressions have come from a Spanish-language search \("quiropráctico cerca de mi"\).

Would it make sense to make some content using Spanish or wouldn't worry about it?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: hey [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) before building spanish content worth asking what percentage of his actual patients are spanish speaking cause if its significant then yes a spanish version of key pages or a spanish language service description in GBP is worth doing. if the impressions are coming from spanish searches but converting to english speaking patients then the effort might not justify the return, but if the local market has a meaningful spanish speaking population that chiro wants to serve then dedicated spanish content with proper hreflang tags is a genuine ranking opportunity competitors probably arent pursuing
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) excellent, thank you! I know he's got a few spanish speaking patients so will keep this in mind.
  • ted-tibbetts-5295: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) I worked on a dual language Spanish site for an MSP in Chicago. It was a Wix site and I said lot's of bad words trying to get Hreflang in Wix to behave. Perhaps it's easier on other platforms. Maybe provide translations on the most viewed pages?
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Ted Tibbetts](obj://user/b2238bbb591249e48db6fd6a63dc0d7c) caleb answered on yday call - they use a spanish speaking copywriter to edit. That's most likely what I would do.
  • ted-tibbetts-5295: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) Correct. Even then, consider whether you have a full translated "mirror of the page in Spanish...then I would think you would need an hreflang set up for SEO. If it's just a few sections of the main page that includes a bit of Spanish translation, then you wouldn't. \(And again, I don't claim to be an expert on it, I just know that I struggled with it in Wix and kept getting Screaming Frog errors which made me scream profanity....\)

Autor: allen-wagner-3902  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  1 comentarios

This is for tree service companies in the Dallas are. Check out these metrics. The top two have 5 pages or less indexed. LOL And many of those are pretty thin. WTH? :\)

RANK #1: Live Oak Tree Service

Reviews: 1,031

RANK #2: Dallas Tree Trimming and Removal Service

Reviews: 76

RANK #3: Texas Tree Surgeons
[https://texastreesurgeons.com](https://texastreesurgeons.com)

Google Index: 1,000+ pages
Reviews: 1,303

RANK #4: Texas Tree Transformations

Google Index: 59 pages
Reviews: 163

RESPUESTAS:

## 129. Next steps
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/next-steps-2
Autor: aiden-hardy-8619  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
We completed a mini core 20 matching the GBP cats and services will all 20 pages indexed this is what the rank map looks like, wanted to know if the next step would be adding a category and some more services or to start FAQ pages and or geographical relevance

Also looking to buy our first chamber of commerce link!

RESPUESTAS:
• aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c)
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: looking at that rank map you got solid green cluster at business location but coverage drops off quick outside proximity which means you need more topical depth before geographic expansion
  
  next step is finish full core 30 by adding gbp category or service pages you havent built yet to max out topical relevance, THEN once you hit 30-40% overall green coverage you can start building location pages for geographic expansion. dont jump to geo pages yet when your core topical foundation isnt complete, finish category and service pages first then do supportig articles to existing pages then chamber link then location pages. youre at like 15-20% green right now so keep building topical before going geographic :\)

## 130. How to structure the locations section on GBP LP?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/how-to-structure-the-locations-section-on-gbp-lp
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Is this just like a blurb of "We serve the following areas" and then a list of locations? For a fixed location \(chiro clinic\) do I use the locations in the GBP? A lot of mine are the city itself, and then a bunch of neighborhoods, zip codes, counties, etc. I'm assuming I'd put all of those in that section like in a footer or something?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: yes a simple service areas section works well, something like a paragraph mentioning the main cities served plus a clean list of neighborhoods, for a fixed location chiro keep it focused on the realistic service radius not every single GBP service area, so main city + 5-8 surrounding neighborhoods and nearby cities that patients actually come from. put it naturally within the page content or in a dedicated section and each city or area name becomes an opportunity to link to a dedicated location page if you have one or plan to build one for geographic expansion later
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) ty!

## 131. Does Adding More GBP Categories Hurt Your Rankings? Case Study
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/does-adding-more-gbp-categories-hurt-your-rankings-case-study
Autor: geo-bel-8803  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  4 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
There's a common fear in local SEO that adding more categories to your Google Business Profile will "dilute" your primary category and hurt your rankings.
I came across a case study that actually tested this with real businesses — a law firm in California and a medical spa in Washington. They tracked rankings daily across a 25-point grid and only changed categories \(nothing else\).
Here's what they found:
→ Adding categories did NOT hurt existing rankings. In fact, most original categories either stayed stable or improved.
→ The law firm saw a 29% increase in search appearances after adding 3 categories.
→ The medical spa saw a 34% increase in visibility and a 49% jump in top-5 placements after adding 4 categories.
→ They even stress-tested by stripping a profile down to 1 category — rankings tanked, but recovered almost immediately once categories were restored.
The takeaway: category dilution appears to be a myth, as long as you're adding categories that genuinely match your services. Don't stuff irrelevant ones — but don't be afraid to list everything you actually do.
Full case study here: [https://www.gmbeverywhere.com/case-studies/does-adding-categories-hurt-rankings.html](https://www.gmbeverywhere.com/case-studies/does-adding-categories-hurt-rankings.html)
What's been your experience? Have you held back on adding categories because of dilution fears? Or have you gone all-in and seen results? Drop your experience below 👇

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: hey [@Geo Bel](obj://user/9c80dda056664ff381efd74970438fee) thanks for sharing this, what Caleb teaches about adding relevant secondary categories being a net positive not a dilution risk. the core 30 methodology is built on this exact principle, each secondary category is a new ranking opportunity that gets its own hub page and the data here backs that up with real numbers :\)
  • geo-bel-8803: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Ive always worried that I would get diluted, happy to have found this data and have it reconfirmed by Calebs teaching. Thank you .
• ted-tibbetts-5295: Great info, thanks for the share, Geo!
• caleb-ulku-6571 ★: I actually had never heard that concern before of it getting diluted but good to see it isn't real? I mean we knew it wasn't a real concern since that's how we rank local businesses but nice to have even more verification I suppose

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 134. Help: Just noticed a client has services for a different category that's not in GBP
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/help-just-noticed-a-client-has-services-for-a-different-category-thats-not-in-gbp
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
A chiro client has "physiotherapy services" on their website, and I've noticed some chiros in different areas will commonly use "Physiotherapist" as a secondary category \(they can't use Physical Therapist, but they can use Physiotherapist\)

I'm about 30 days into the campaign...should I add that category to the GBP with the services from the website and then make a cat page, and service pages?

Some of those services on the website are currently listed as services under different categories in the GBP, should I leave them as-is or move them \(if moving them, would I also change the cat page content those service are listed under by removing those H2 sections?\)

I think I remember in the training we didn't want to make a ton of changes too often on the GBP LP so I wanted to make sure this shouldn't be a problem.

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: if the business actually offers physiotherapy services and competitors are using physiotherapist as a secondary category then add it, core 30 is about matching the GBP to what the business actually. just add it as a secondary category and build the corresponding hub page and service pages to back it up properly. for the services that currently sit under other categories leave them where they are unless moving them creates a cleaner more logical silo structure, the key is every service in GBP has a matching page on the website and the internal linking hierarchy is clean regardless of which category hub they fall under :\)
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) nice, got it! Thanks again!

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 135. Bilingual Website
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/bilingual-website
Autor: luca-budgen-4222  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  3 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hi, I'm gonna re-do a website for an HVAC company in Montreal. About 50% of that area speaks French so we're gonna do a bilingual website. How do I go about this?

We did their website a few years back too and back then we just did KW research in French and wrote content according to that research. But obviously this core 30 structure works in a different way. What is the best way to go about this?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: you can build separate URL versions for each language like [domain.com/en/](http://domain.com/en/) for english and [domain.com/fr/](http://domain.com/fr/) for french, with proper hreflang tags telling google which version to serve to which searcher. each language version gets its own full core 30 structure with dedicated category and service pages targeting the relevant keywords in that language :\)
  • luca-budgen-4222: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Ok yes thanks! 1 Question:
    [ul][li]Is KW research still necessary? Or can I just create the french core 30 structure with AI and let a french person proof read it? This would make it way easier, hiring a french copy writer and kw researcher was horrible last time
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Luca Budgen](obj://user/4b1d9b441bad42e099240aa0504eb9dc) for the french version keyword research is much simpler than traditional SEO cause core 30 uses your GBP categories and services as the keywords not random keyword tool research. just translate your GBP category and service names into natural french equivalents, use google autocomplete to verify how french speakers actually phrase those searches, and claude can generate the french content and a native french speaker just needs to proofread for naturalness, no need for a dedicated french copywriter or keyword researcher like last time :\)

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 137. Client acquisition
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/client-acquisition-2
Autor: idan-bubnovsky-4121  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  3 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hey everyone, revcently joined this community, I've spent the last week going over all the material here and in the regular community and I feel like I got the gist of what needs to be done.

I am having trouble with approaching client acquisition, not sure if ads are worth the spend when I have no prior experience \(both SEO and sales calls\) and a tight daily budget.

What are some ways you guys have managed to land your first and second client and what do you recommend from your learnings?

I've considered - ads, fb groups, upwork, cold email

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: Welcome [@Idan Bubnovsky](obj://user/9d0ce896f25a4839865d592b143d8f82) glad to have you here😊, for your situation skip ads for now and start with outreach and upwork. You can go to people you already know, friends family local business owners you frequent, pull up their rank map show them the red dots and let the visual do the selling, cold email and facebook groups work too but take longer to convert, the fastest path to first client is always someone who already knows and trusts you so exhaust that network before spending money on ads
  • idan-bubnovsky-4121: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) I dont know anyone with a business that needs SEO and also im not from the US and in my country it doesn't seem like people are using a lot of google maps near me searches to find businesses its a lot of word of mouth.
    
    About upwork, is the strategy Caleb teaches on his old youtube videos still valid? It seems like sending 5 proposals a day costs 10-15$ which is kind of pricey
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Idan Bubnovsky](obj://user/9d0ce896f25a4839865d592b143d8f82) then target businesses that serve international markets consider targeting businesses in US UK or Australian markets remotely through upwork since local SEO is location agnostic and you can serve clients anywhere. for upwork the proposal cost is real but think of it as a customer acquisition cost, landing one $500 setup fee client from $50 in connects is a 10x return or you can apporch US businesses and offer them in low budget in starting

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 138. What to do when everybody (or nobody?) is doing well?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/what-to-do-when-everybody-or-nobody-is-doing-well
Autor: stefan-girndt-6973  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  1 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I analyzed a lawyer in my city and I run into the following issue:

First, I ran the rank map with 0.5mi distance between the spots \(screenshot 1\) and based on the training, that's too wide because the top ranking guy only has 30% top3.
Then I ran a rank map with 0.25mi distance \(screenshot 2\) and now the company I'm interested in is in the first position with almost 75% top 3.

My "problem" is that none of these businesses are really optimized!
The company I'm interested in \(#1 in 2nd screen\) has a few services on their GMB but not really corresponding service pages on their website and no H1 on their homepage.

The #1 in the first image has a more optimized website \(meta title and H1\) but 0 services on his GMB.
It seems he only has lawyer as his category and that's it.

So I wonder if the #1 is really the benchmark here, or if the situation can be improved much more than his top3 would let you think.

Based on the few businesses here in Germany, that's often the case, really not a lot of businesses know what they are doing...

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: yes this is actually a perfect opportunity cause when nobody is properly optimized core 30 implementation will give you a massive advantage. the #1 guy at 73% top 3 coverage could be winning for any combination of reasons including relevance signals GBP age or reviews, but the key insight is that his ceiling isnt your ceiling. in a market where competitors have zero services on GBP no H1 tags and no service pages you can build the full relevance structure that nobody else has, proper GBP category alignment dedicated service pages correct H1s and internal linking hierarchy will push you well past what anyone is currently achieving. build core 30 properly and youll dominate this market faster than you would in a properly contested one :\)

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 139. I'm curious how important having multiple cats are?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/im-curious-how-important-having-multiple-cats-are
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
A new chiro client is a very straightforward chiro. I could add some related cats like doctor, holistic medicine practitioner, wellness center - those are fairly common, but the vast majority are just using Chiropractor.

There's other related cats I'll see them use like Physiotherapist, Specialized Clinic, but they feel like they're a stretch and I don't think any other ranking chiros in their area use those cats unless they're multi-disciplinary.

Would you put in some of these other cats just to have multiple cats for the GBP or just stick to Chiropractor in this situation?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: id suggets just use the categories that top competitors in that specific local market are actually using, dont add categories just to have more if they dont represent real services offered. if wellness center and holistic medicine practitioner are genuinely relevant to what this chiro does then add them cause each becomes a ranking opportunity and hub page, but if competitors arent using physiotherapist or specialized clinic in that market then skip those cause adding irrelevant categories can dilute your primary category signal. use GMB everywhere to see what the top 3 ranking chiros in that exact market are using and mirror that as your starting point
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) got it, okay thank you!

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 141. Would something like "Women Chiropractors in Leesburg" as a Title Tag be worse than Chiropractor in Leesburg?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/would-something-like-women-chiropractors-in-leesburg-as-a-title-tag-be-worse-than-chiropractor-in-leesburg
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  1 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Wondering how important the title tag be exact match to the category or if we can get away with something more like this?

Also - is it recommended to put a picture up next to the SERP link? Example Screenshot attached \(and if so, how do we do that?\)

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: id suggest keep chiropractor in leesburg as your primary title tag since that exact match to your GBP category is what drives the core ranking signal, then use the women chiropractor angle naturally in your subheadings or first paragraph where it reads more naturally for the target audience. for the image thats google automatically pulling a page image, you cant reliably force it but having a properly tagged images with good schema gives google better signals to work with

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 142. Can someone explain to me a little about GSC and making decisions from it?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/can-someone-explain-to-me-a-little-about-gsc-and-making-decisions-from-it
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  3 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Screenshots attached - I'm noticing a popular query that they're getting impressions for \(but no clicks\) is around chiropractor and scoliosis.

I have deduced that it would be a good idea to target scoliosis in my content, maybe put a service in the GBP primary category and put it on the homepage + service page.

[ol:1][li]If so, I'm assuming it would be wise to look at some of the questions people are asking in GSC to address in FAQs \(as well as pulling from PAA\)[li]Are the impressions from the SERP + GBP? If they're mostly the SERP does that change the strategy at all?[li]I'm generally trying to figure out how to capitalize on some of this information I never thought to look at before 😅

RESPUESTAS:
• stephan-franklyn-4962: In relation to this, another client is seeing quries for "chiropractor for xyz" and I'm wondering if that's something that I should target, but I'm not sure how? Like "Sleep treatment" or "Sleep therapy" or "anxiety treatment" doesn't sound like it would fit as services for a chiropractor...
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) yes target scoliosis, GSC showing impressions with zero clicks means google is already associating that chiro with scoliosis searches but the page isnt optimized enough to get the click. create a dedicated scoliosis treatment page, add it as a GBP service, and mention it on the homepage as that data is telling you exactly what to build. for impressions GSC shows organic web search not GBP so this is purely website visibility data which means the strategy is on page optimization and a dedicated service page to convert those impressions into clicks.
    
    for the sleep and anxiety queries those are legitimate chiropractic service just make sure the service page content accurately describes how chiropractic care addresses those conditions if that make sense and related to your business
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) got it okay thank you!

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 143. Do all heatmap tools work for the Core 30, or just Lead Snap?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/do-all-heatmap-tools-work-for-the-core-30-or-just-lead-snap
Autor: emily-gregg-4779  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  3 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hi all,
Quick question regarding heatmaps: do other tools like Local Dominator work for this section \( for example, Local Dominator \), or is it best to use Lead Snap?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: hey [@Emily Gregg](obj://user/1c3fecd2371f441e87781c8630faa8b5) yes other heatmap tools work fine and the methodology decisions are the same regardless of which tool you use. however the course prompts and core 30 gap analysis workflows are specifically built around leadsnap so if you want to use the automated gap analysis prompts you need leadsnap for those specific exports, but for just tracking rank map progress and making strategic decisions about topical vs geographic expansion any quality heatmap tool works :\)
• caleb-ulku-6571 ★: only leadsnaps rank maps work with the tool at the moment because that's the one we use at the agency so I don't have examples of what the others tools output
  
  I've had a few requests to add local dominator sot hat's on the list, but I can't add every single rank map generating tool =\)
  
  and I just realized you're probably asking after the local seo methodology - which yes, any rank map will work for that
• emily-gregg-4779: Got it. Thank you!

## 144. Youtube videos
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/youtube-videos
Autor: andrew-lin-7557  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  3 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
[@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d)
In the huddle videos, it was mentioned to use pictory to generate youtube videos to embed in the pages to help with indexing and authority.

Are these youtube videos only on Geo pages? Or are they supposed to be on all pages? Whats the best practices? Thanks!

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: You can put the YT videos on all Core 30 pages \(service + city\), but best practice is to prioritize the most important ones first especially your main category pages and high-intent service pages. Geo/neighborhood pages are also great for them
  
  Don’t overdo it on every single page though 1 good video per important page is enough :\)
  • andrew-lin-7557: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) is there a lesson that goes through this in the training? I don't recall this being in the training
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Andrew Lin](obj://user/a049778615d640539f8149e823998da7) honestly there isn’t a specific lesson just on that it’s more of a best practice you can test yourself try it on a few key pages first and see how it impacts performance

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 145. AI Built Front End Local Sites Ranking?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/ai-built-front-end-local-sites-ranking
Autor: josh-delaney-6388  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  9 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Is anyone building AI built front end websites with wordpress systems on the back end and ranking with them? Anyone made the full transition to doing this with every client?

I am seeing multiple AI sites front end and WP back end ranking great and fast and so much time being cut from development and bottle necks and page edits, etc.

Anyone doing this already or have any insights or suggestions as we think about making the full transition?

RESPUESTAS:
• chiara-burns-7791: Not sure why they'd use Wordpress for the backend, unless the idea is to create a headless CMS/WYSIWIG editor for the client. I use Notion if clients need to be able to update content. Or just hand the reigns to them after the build; it's like giving them their own in-house dev/designer.
  
  The big thing with AI websites is you want to make sure the code is server side and therefore crawlable by Google. Most AI builders are going to default to SPA \(single page application\), and only some of them are going to let you designate an SEO-friendly stack. I know people do the html plugin or whatever with Lovable but I'd rather just build astro + SSG.
  
  It's way faster to make site-wide changes with AI. I think everything is going to pivot that direction in the next 3-5 years. Tech sector has already adopted it, other sectors lag \(as usual\).
  
  Unpopular opinion, but I think if Wordpress doesn't adapt, and they stick with their clunky Elementor and Guttenberg options, they'll be like the Blockbuster of CMSs by 2028 lol.
  • josh-delaney-6388: [@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c) I agree with you. The backend being on WP still is really just my 50/50 caution of clients wanting more familiar platform with their sites because the mass of devs will remain in that knowledge base to your point for another 2-3 years. So take the jump and just do what you're saying now and only sell that and specialize in that. Or still with some of the familiar to clients. We are going to do both and see how it plays out.
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: yes this is great workflow and makes sense, using AI to generate the design and content then deploying on wordpress backend gives you the best of both worlds, the core 30 agent already does something similar by generating page content and publishing directly to wordpress. if your seeing good ranking results with that approach in your market then absolutely systematize it cause anything that cuts development time without sacrificing SEO fundamentals is a win at scale :\)
  • chiara-burns-7791: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Question: When deployed this way, what is the editing process like for the client? Are they reliant on the agency or a developer to make changes?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c) most clients in this setup are reliant on the agency for changes which is actually a feature not a bug since it keeps them on retainer, but if you want to give clients self edit ability just build the wordpress backend with elementor or a simple page builder so they can make basic text and image changes without touching code
  • chiara-burns-7791: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) I know it's intentional to get them on a retainer; That's why I asked. I just don't feel right about creating a dependency.
• ted-tibbetts-5295: Following. I'm working with a partner to go the Lovable to Supabase route. I'm doing all the front end work, he's handling the Supabase backend. Interesting to see how it works out.
  • josh-delaney-6388: [@Ted Tibbetts](obj://user/b2238bbb591249e48db6fd6a63dc0d7c) Ah I'd be super curious.
  • ted-tibbetts-5295: [@Josh Delaney](obj://user/97608e63ffe04f82b6a62ce394fd3d6f) I'll keep this thread updated...

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 147. Local Phone Number Concerns
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/local-phone-number-concerns
Autor: shawn-jones-2347  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  4 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Doing SEO for an urgent care. Town of about 100k. They are using a google voice number as their main number with an area code that is local but the phone number is specific to a smaller town 60 minutes away. When they call from their main number it shows up on the phone as this smaller town which they do not serve. Instead of their town of their service area. Any awareness of this same area code prefix but from a different town affecting rankings?

RESPUESTAS:
• caleb-ulku-6571 ★: [@Shawn Jones](obj://user/f4e0631243e940e58a602d9b01d3fd12) NAP consistency matters, but honestly don’t know of a time where Google Voice location display affected rankings specifically. =\)
• anil-agrawal-2726: Not sure but Claude tells me that Google policy does not allow Google Voice number to be used for business use. I haven't checked the policy myself... in transition of switching from Google Voice to a Twilio number in a few weeks.
  • douglas-page-2404: [@Anil Agrawal](obj://user/cedd2eb6ed1446bc9dcf33515c7cca9b) I am guessing that the response from Claude is about not verifying a GBP with a G Voice number and not using it on the site for clients to call. I also wouldn't have much worry about the phone number being from a nearby city. In my area, they are allowing you to take your number with you when you move. A few years ago, I was able to keep my number despite being in a completely different city now albeit only 30 minutes away. So I'm guessing that exchanges are now a thing of the past or rapidly becoming such.
  • anil-agrawal-2726: [@Doug Page](obj://user/2667d9a03c934d32ad8bed6e5f7041e9) yeah that would make sense. Just being a bit over cautious here 😉😆

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 148. Just gotta give a shotout for all the support! (again)
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/just-gotta-give-a-shotout-for-all-the-support
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  3 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
The last few weeks, I've been very worried as rank maps for my clients were getting pretty bad. I know there was an update recently with Google, but I also knew I wasn't doing things properly.

Through a lot of questions, a GBP LP review on the huddle, and general support from the group...I'm happy to say that rank maps are returning to normal \(and improving\).

Caleb pointed out that I wasn't using Exact Match KW for a client's H1 \(Chiropractic Care for the family vs Chiropractor in City\), and then I realized I wasn't doing the Core30 properly.

I was skipping to geography pages before I even created the Category pages...then my category pages didn't even have all the services from the GBP on them, or had services that weren't on the GBP at all.

[@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c) gave me SOLID advice, which was to weave the services into the page more naturally vs remaking the entire page \(which saved me countless hours\) and of course [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) has been an absolute godsend for answering my \(sometimes repetitive\) questions.

I really feel like this helped me stay confident and gain traction in my agency. I can't stop shouting out to the support in this group because it's just SO damned good and helpful! There's no reason to be stuck with this - just ask the group and somebody will help out.

Thanks again everyone!

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: Love this, [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) 🙌
  That’s a perfect example of how small Core30 fixes = big results. Once the structure and alignment is dialed in, things start moving again :\)
• caleb-ulku-6571 ★: love hearing it, Stephan! great to see progress!
• chiara-burns-7791: Love the tenacity, and glad to hear your rank maps are improving!

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 149. Hiring a Content Manager – What Should I Look For to Fully Step Out of Content Ops?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/hiring-a-content-manager-what-should-i-look-for-to-fully-step-out-of-content-ops
Autor: tomas-affranchino-9734  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  10 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I’m currently looking to expand my team and my next key hire is a Content Manager.

At the moment, I have content editors producing most of the content, but I’m still heavily involved in assigning tasks, reviewing drafts, and managing the overall workflow — which is becoming a bottleneck.

From Caleb’s calls, my understanding is that this role should manage writers, handle quality control, coordinate with developers, and ensure proper implementation \(e.g. internal linking, schema, publishing\).

I’d love to validate if I’m thinking about this correctly, and learn from those who have already hired this role successfully:

[ul][li]What are the key traits you look for in a strong Content Manager?[li]What skills and prior experience are non-negotiable vs. nice-to-have?[li]What are the core responsibilities this role should fully own in practice?[li]What kind of hourly rate / salary range have you found works for a solid hire?[li]Where have you successfully sourced this role \(e.g. agencies, Upwork, OnlineJobs, etc.\)?[li]In your experience, does this role need to be local \(e.g. Australian\), or can a strong offshore hire \(e.g. Philippines with content experience\) perform just as well?

Context: this role would ideally take ownership of the content production workflow end-to-end, so I can step out of day-to-day management and focus more on strategy and growth.

Thx!

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: hey [@Tomas Affranchino](obj://user/5a215abd0bff47028103c902b023920c) before hiring a content manager id suggest first documenting your current workflow as a proper SOP covering exactly how you assign tasks review drafts and manage quality, cause if you cant write it down clearly a new hire wont be able to follow it either. for sourcing you can use upwork the go to find there content managers who can handle everything you described, just look for someone with local SEO experience who understands internal linking and schema not just general writing skills cause thats the non negotiable for core 30 content ops :\)
• tomas-affranchino-9734: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) - Thx for that. In terms of their main role is what I covered above their main responsibilities or what am I looking for? I guess I am trying to fully understand their scope of work so I can start documenting the SOPs and review process so I can successfully remove myself from that role. Thx.
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Tomas Affranchino](obj://user/5a215abd0bff47028103c902b023920c) the core responsibilities could be managing the content calendar and assigning pages to writers, reviewing AI generated content for quality accuracy and local relevance before publishing, ensuring every page has proper title tags H1s internal links and schema before it goes live, coordinating with your developer or VA for technical implementation, and tracking indexation in GSC after publishing. essentially they own everything between the content prompt being run and the page being live and properly indexed, your job becomes strategy client relationships and oversight not day to day content ops
• tomas-affranchino-9734: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) - Got it and do you have a recommended hourly rate range to consider so as to find A players for this role? My main focus this quarter is to build up the operations team so as to step out of it and want to bring quality members onboard only.
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Tomas Affranchino](obj://user/5a215abd0bff47028103c902b023920c) you can expect to pay $5-10 per hour for asian based guys on upwork, just make sure you give them a test task first using one of the core 30 prompts and review the output before hiring cause you want someone who can follow the methodology not just write generic content :\)
• tomas-affranchino-9734: Amazing thx Miles!
• mark-farrow-6630: Attention to detail!
• tomas-affranchino-9734: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) [@Mark Farrow](obj://user/5d7f726c110a43dc973a719104a5e4b4) One final follow up question. The SEO content brief should be created by the Content Manager right? The SEO manager decides the keyword/strategy and the content manager is responsible for creating the brief with the title tag, meta, etc etc?
  • mark-farrow-6630: [@Tomas Affranchino](obj://user/5a215abd0bff47028103c902b023920c) that sounds correct its all depending on your structure, RN i am all of those things in my agency :\)\)
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Tomas Affranchino](obj://user/5a215abd0bff47028103c902b023920c) yes exactly 👍

## 152. Multi-Location Clinic - Should they put any location-verbiage in their title?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/multi-location-clinic-should-they-put-any-location-verbiage-in-their-title
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I'm assuming not but I have seen some that will put like ABC Chiropractic - East Chicago or something to that effect but I'm assuming that might dilute the GBP itself?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: id say no dont put location verbiage in the GBP business name cause adding east chicago or any city modifier to the business name is considered keyword stuffing which can get the profile flagged or suspended. the location differentiation for multiple offices is handled through each GBPs separate verified address not through the business name, google displays the address automatically so customers can tell locations apart. keep all GBPs under the exact registered business name cause if some time google ask for documents verfication then your business name would be different from GBP so and let the address do the geographic differentiation
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) makes perfect sense, thank you for the explanation!

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 153. Opportunity with a Custom Home builder prospect
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/opportunity-with-a-custom-home-builder-prospect
Autor: jean-almonte-2448  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  1 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I have a prospect in the custom home builder space and I started building out his website just to get ahead a show it to him. I was using the prompts [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) has in the lovable website build he showed but realized custom home builder niche is a bit different than the home services. I started adjusting some of the prompts but wanted to see if people in this group have experience ranking in this niche and willing to share how they approached it or examples of customers they’ve ranked if they can share.

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: hey [@Jean Almonte](obj://user/dbbc42147ce141d8ac752fb1194a835f) the methodology is the same just the categories and services are different. primary category is custom home builder and secondary categories depending on what google has available in that market you should use GMB everywhere. service pages target specific things people search like custom home builder city, luxury home builder city, new home construction city etc the longer sales cycle means conversion focused content matters more than typical home services so make sure each page has strong trust signals like portfolio photos project case studies and testimonials, that E-E-A-T depth is what closes high ticket

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 154. Ongoing services
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/ongoing-services
Autor: josh-mckennon-2093  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  4 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hello everyone. I just joined and have been diving into the lessons. I just started my agency and have a couple interested clients that do not have websites , a paving company and a tree service,, My plan is to build core 30 website on lovable\(use lovablehtml\) and manage their gbp monthly, I have no clue on pricing and what to charge ongoing after the website build. Also I’m a little confused about what the monthly Seo service would be after initial website build. . Any help on this would be appreciated.

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: hey [@Josh McKennon](obj://user/65557b48b0a84157a44fe86e4ebb6032) welcome to the community! check out this [https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=7ecaadb4be6a4ab19a54f38a0ee616a1](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=7ecaadb4be6a4ab19a54f38a0ee616a1) module in the classroom cause Caleb walks through exactly how to price both setup and ongoing retainers based on deliverables. for ongoing monthly work after the initial build it covers continued page publishing 4-6 pages per week, GBP posts twice weekly, review management, rank map reporting and citation maintenance, theres always more work to do as you expand topically then geographically so the retainer justifies itself easily once clients see the rank map turning green :\)
  • josh-mckennon-2093: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) thank you
• reece-rainer-3922: If it were me, I'd be trying to charge him an affordable price so that you can learn on the job and build up case studies as I'm doing for my current clients now. However, building websites from scratch is incredibly time consuming, even with vibe coding tools, and in future in my company I won't be charging anything less than probably about $3,000 per website just because some of them are taking like 10 even 20 hours to get exactly right. Which is yeah extremely time consuming. In regards to retainer I believe a lot of people charge between $500-2000/month . My question is do you think you're worth that much ? Do you have the conviction that you can make that a profitable choice for them ? If not then maybe charge the amount you think you're worth currently . Just my two cents
  • josh-mckennon-2093: 100%. I’m new at this I do look at these clients as learning/case studies. Fortunately they are in lower competition areas . I believe it will be a win win for both all of us .. appreciate the reply.

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 155. Just Joined. Where To Start First?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/just-joined-where-to-start-first
Autor: dominic-trejo-3180  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  6 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hello! Looking for some insight on where exactly to start. I have a landscaping company that I will be working with that needs everything. They have been in business for 4-5 years but they have never had a a website or their GBP set up. I have been playing around in this space for a few years here and there but still consider myself pretty green since this is the first time I'm actually taking action and have a client i'm helping from the ground up.

I watched one of Calebs YT videos where he talks about the \[Beta\] Core 30 AI Agent Website Builder which sounds amazing. Is this where I should start in regards to getting this business set up?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: hey [@Dominic Trejo](obj://user/78ada2220e5846f9a6aa1a81c07751a2) welcome to pro 😊! dont start with the core 30 agent yet, start with the GBP setup and verification first cause that is the foundation everything else builds on. watch the classroom to understand the GBP optimization and landing page alignment before touching the website builder, once GBP is verified and optimized with the right categories and services then you build the website to mirror that structure. After use the core 30 agent for now use this time to nail the GBP and learn the methodology so when you do get access you know exactly what to build 🙌
  • dominic-trejo-3180: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) I appreciate your response and thanks so much for the advice. Would you say I start under the The Google Business Profile tab in the AI SEO Mastery Pro program? Or the Advanced: Verifying a GBP program in the classroom?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Dominic Trejo](obj://user/78ada2220e5846f9a6aa1a81c07751a2) Yes 👍
    
    For verification, start with the verification module first. Once your GBP is verified, then move on to the main GBP section and continue from there.
  • dominic-trejo-3180: I'm sorry [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) , I'm still a little confused on which one is the verification module. Which program is that module under?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Dominic Trejo](obj://user/78ada2220e5846f9a6aa1a81c07751a2) no problem let me give you links here's GBP verification module; [https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/293caaf6](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/293caaf6)
    
    And here's the GBP lectures module: [https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=7a06ff4df2bb44f28f8f2b2aeba3a1a7](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=7a06ff4df2bb44f28f8f2b2aeba3a1a7)
    
    So first watch the verification module and after verification watch the GBP setup lectures :\)
