═══════════════════════════════════════
## 156. Should we do it's own GBP LP for a multi-location clinic that's ranking for micro-niche?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/should-we-do-its-own-gbp-lp-for-a-multi-location-clinic-thats-ranking-for-micro-niche
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
They are a chiropractic clinic that specializes in prenatal/pediatric care. They rank well for prenatal chiro, decent for pediatric chiro, but hardly at all for chiro. \(Screenshots attached\)

I'm not in their GBP yet, we're still in convo, but I'm going to venture to say general chiro terms get searched more often than the preg/ped chiro search terms.

Any feedback/insight would be helpful...my thoughts at this point is to focus on the general chiro search terms as it is their primary category; put preg/ped chiro as services on the home page, create service pages around preg/ped and chiro in general.

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: hey [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) first issue is youre tracking "near me" keywords which you shouldnt be doing in leadsnap, leadsnap automatically geotags so just track "prenatal chiropractor", "pediatric chiropractor", and "chiropractor"
  
  once you fix that and rerun the maps youll get accurate data, but yeah for multi location each location needs its own gbp landing page then homepage targets primary category "chiropractor + city" with prenatal and pediatric as category pages underneath. this builds topical foundation for general chiro while keeping their specialty niches strong, and make sure not to rank for everything on homepage alone you need the full core 30 silo structure to compete for broader chiropractor keyword :\)
• stephan-franklyn-4962: ahh okay I will remember that. These scans were not in leadsnap but I will keep that in mind for leadsnap.
  
  I'm just saying this for clarity, confirmation, and reinforcement to myself: Prenatal and pediatric chiros are not categories in the GBP. Therefore, they will be main services on the home page and then will interlink to their own service + city pages. That structure will be copied to the other locations as well \(primary cat on gbp LP, main services on GBP LP, interlink to their individual Service + city page\)

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 157. Rank Map
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/rank-map-3
Autor: lalita-sundari-2382  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
How is it possible that I look like this right now?

Two weeks ago the same keyword was all green.

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: hey [@Lalita Sundari](obj://user/6685057634644cd5b45935b0e6b6a045) what time of day did you run both scans and were they on the same time day cause rankings fluctuate heavily based on time and day especially for competitive keywords
  
  also check if any competitors got review surges cause going from all green to mixed red/yellow in 2 weeks usually means either competitor activity spiked or you got hit with some kind of penalty/filter. run another scan at the exact same time as your original baseline to comparing
• stephan-franklyn-4962: Just my humble opinion, but when I see it was ranking pretty well and then all of a sudden it's showing a bunch of red 20s mixed in between the 1s I consider that a bad scan. I will typically rerun it but it will usually still be like that, so I think it could be on google's end \(I've even tried with multiple tools before and got the same result\)
  
  So monitor it, scan it the next day and the next day if it isn't changing. More often than not, it will show a more reasonable scan within 1-3 days.

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 158. Local Dominator compatibility with Core 30 AI Agent?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/local-dominator-compatibility-with-core-30-ai-agent
Autor: ted-tibbetts-5295  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I've been using local dominator instead of Lead Snap, but I get error messages from the tool when uploading CSV files. Is the export from Lead Snap and Local Dominator that different?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: yes the CSV export format from local dominator is different from leadsnap and the core 30 agent is specifically built around leadsnap export format so that's why you're getting errors. the column headers and data structure differ enough between the two tools the cleanest solution is to use leadsnap for the gap analysis exports even if you prefer local dominator for rank tracking, or use claude to reformat the local dominator CSV columns to match what leadsnap outputs
  • ted-tibbetts-5295: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) thanks for the info, Miles!

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 159. For main services, do we create the page like a category hub page, or as a regular service page?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/for-main-services-do-we-create-the-page-like-a-category-hub-page-or-as-a-regular-service-page
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  8 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
In our GBP Services for an animal chiro we have several services related to being an animal chiro under the Primary "Chiropractor" category. Would we make it like a hub page where Animal Chiropractor is the target kw and then list those other services as H2s?

Or should we make it like a regular service page with statement H2s from the PAAs?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: since animal chiropractor is a main service under the primary category not a secondary GBP category it gets a regular service page not a full hub page with child services underneath. so build it like a standard service page with H1 targeting animal chiropractor + city, use PAA based H2s to cover the topic and link back up to the homepage since primary category services report directly to homepage in the core 30 hierarchy. no need to create a mini hub structure for it unless you have so many animal specific sub services that they each their own dedicated pages
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) got it, okay! Since the home page is considered the category page for the primary cat, I'm assuming I make service pages for the services under the primary cat, but then how do they fit into the Core30 from parent/sister/child structure? Is there a service hub page for them still?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) no separate hub page needed for primary cat services, they link directly to and from the homepage. so the structure is homepage is the primary cat hub, and all the services under the primary cat, each get their own dedicated service pages that link back up to homepage directly. homepage links down to those service pages within the content and H2 sections, and each service page links back up to homepage. secondary cat services follow the same logic but link to their secondary cat hub page instead of homepage
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) I think this is where I'm getting confused - so the home page will have h2 + content block sections for each secondary category, and then it will also have h2 + content block sections for every service listed under the primary cat?
    
    So if the primary category has 10 services under it + 3 secondary categories, there would be 13 H2s + content blocks?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) no not all 13 as H2s, thats where the distinction matters. secondary categories all get H2s on the homepage cause each one becomes a hub page. but for primary category services you only make H2s for your top 2-3 highest value moneymaker services that link directly from homepage, the rest of the primary cat services still get their own dedicated pages but they get linked contextually within the homepage content or in a services grid section rather than each having their own prominent H2 section. so realistically your homepage might have 3 secondary category H2s plus 2-3 moneymaker service H2s, keeping it clean and focused rather than 13 equal sections
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Now I'm understanding!!! I'm a slow learner but by golly I think I got it now haha Follow-up Question: For chiropractors, their money maker service is typically like "chiropractic adjustments" which is what most people would expect from a chiropractor. Can you help me think through that?
    
    I can think of making something like Sciatica treatment or knee pain treatment or something like that; some of them will have special equipment for certain conditions \(herniated disc, for example\)
    
    But for a chiro without special equipment, would some of the main services be condition + treatment to make it a service?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) yes condition plus treatment is exactly the right approach for chiros without specialized equipment, so pages like sciatica treatment city, back pain relief city, etc are all legitimate service pages that match real search intent cause people search their condition not the procedure. chiropractic adjustments itself can be a service page too as the primary moneymaker H2 since thats what people expect, then the condition based pages build topical depth around it and capture all the different ways people search for chiropractic help
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) niiice thank you as always!

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 160. GBP optimization and management
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/gbp-optimization-and-management
Autor: anil-agrawal-2726  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  4 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Trying to get a feel for how much to charge for GBP audit, optimization, and ongoing management for a service based client, say, chiropractors, dentists etc. This is assuming if for some reason, the client doesn’t want me to touch their website or unable to add me to their website backend. So only GBP. Or that’s not a good in terms of showing ROI for the client?

Assume clients are in California.

Or maybe I am asking the wrong question? 😆

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: hey [@Anil Agrawal](obj://user/cedd2eb6ed1446bc9dcf33515c7cca9b) check out the [https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=7ecaadb4be6a4ab19a54f38a0ee616a1](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=7ecaadb4be6a4ab19a54f38a0ee616a1) module in the classroom cause Caleb walks through exactly how to calculate pricing based on deliverables and time. for GBP only without website access your options are limited and honestly harder to show ROI cause the biggest ranking gains come from the website core 30 pages not just GBP optimization alone. id price GBP only work at $300-500 setup and $200-300 monthly but strongly recommend positioning it as a starting point to get your foot in the door, then use the rank map results to upsell them into the full core 30 website work cause thats where the real movement happens :\)
  • anil-agrawal-2726: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Got it. Makes sense and that's what I was thinking... this website is in such a bad shape and I can't believe they have left it like that for years... I actually know this chiropractor \(actually a team of them in the office\)... my guess is some family member or some bad agency designed the website and they have repeat or even new clients coming in simply due to nearby high-tech companies with great benefits for employees who can afford their services... not sure how much they are struggling, if any.. looking at their website and profile, I would be shocked if they are getting any traffic from Google...
    
    Thanks again for your comments and help.
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Anil Agrawal](obj://user/cedd2eb6ed1446bc9dcf33515c7cca9b) that's actually a perfect warm lead situation, you already know them personally so show up with their rank map and website audit and let the data do the talking, most business owners have no idea how bad their onlin presence is until you put the red dots in front of them :\)
  • anil-agrawal-2726: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) love it 😍😁

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 161. Does the Core 30 AI agent produce the content for .net websites
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/does-the-core-30-ai-agent-produce-the-content-for-net-websites
Autor: waseem-ahsan-4922  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Wondering if it only works for word press.

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: the core 30 AI agent currently only connects and publishes directly to wordpress sites it doesnt support .net or other platforms for automated publishing. if your on a .net site you can still use the agent to generate all the content and page structure, you just have to manually copy and paste the output into your .net site rather than having it publish automatically. the content generation part works regardless of platform, its only the auto publishing feature that requires wordpress
• waseem-ahsan-4922: Great, thank you Miles.

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 162. Best ways to estimate traffic?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/best-ways-to-estimate-traffic
Autor: chiara-burns-7791  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  8 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Still in the early days here, but already seeing significant SEO jumps for clients. I'm curious — since search volume isn't really a metric in local SEO the way it is with organic, is there a formula or methodology for estimating the kind of traffic lift someone can expect once they break into the top 3?

Obviously it's contingent on factors like population density and industry, but I'd love to find a reliable way to ballpark this so I can give clients a realistic sense of outcomes.

RESPUESTAS:
• mark-farrow-6630: I've heard Caleb break this down, or Miles so will let them get it right. It had to do with national SV divided into the local population numbers vs national population. The formula is a best guess at the math.
• caleb-ulku-6571 ★: yeah best way we've done is to scale base don population another one of your clients in the same space
  
  assuming that isn't possible, scale search volume based on the demographics of the area your'e trying to rank in - this will be miserably wrong but I don't have a better way if you don't already have a ranking business to compare to
  • chiara-burns-7791: [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) Ah...does that mean taking on competing clients in the same industry/city? How do you navigate that fairly? [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d)
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c) yes the benchmark idea doesn’t mean taking competing clients in the same city. It’s better to compare similar businesses in different cities, like a plumber in Austin and a plumber in Dallas, so you get useful data without any conflict of interest
  • chiara-burns-7791: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) makes way more sense, thank you!
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: hey [@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c) yes Caleb is correct best way to estimate traffic lift is to scale based on population compared to another client you already have in the same industry, if you ranked a plumber in a city of 50k and they get X calls per month you can reasonably scale that up or down for a city of 100k or 200k. if you dont have a comparable client to reference then use SV data scaled to the demographics of the target area as a rough ballpark, just know its going to be imprecise without a real comparison point. having a ranking client in the same niche is genuinely the most reliable benchmark you can use
• ted-tibbetts-5295: This is an interesting conversation. It's such a powerful sales tool to be able to clearly show value to potential customers. I've used tools in the past that calculate increased profits from conversions...and it's really powerful for upselling when you can show systems based growth..."If we increase traffic by x and conversions by y, you'll see sales increase by $7,247 a month. Does that sound like it would be worth $497 a month to you?"
  
  I wonder if we could create a shared resource here...like a shared google sheet organized by niche and geography where folks could post data like, "I've got an electrician in a city of 76,000 ranking #5 and gets 15 calls a month."
  
  If we had a decent amount of data points, we could show businesses the value of moving from #5 to #3.
  • chiara-burns-7791: [@Ted Tibbetts](obj://user/b2238bbb591249e48db6fd6a63dc0d7c) Fantastic idea ^

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 163. GBP Audit for potential clients
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/gbp-audit-for-potential-client
Autor: anil-agrawal-2726  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  5 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
We have GBP audit questionnaire as part of Core 30. I was wondering how to use that for a potential client. Meaning, you wouldn’t do all the work or detailed audit, right? What and how much coverage you would do for getting a potential client interested?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: you dont need to do the full core 30 gap analysis, just pull a quick rank map in leadsnap showing their current rankings with all the red dots, check their GBP categories using GMB everywhere to spot obvious gaps, and do a audit on their site and use screaming frog for technical issues checking. it gives you enough to show the prospect exactly where they are losing calls to competitors right now and the rank map visual does most of the selling for you. and you can also do audit through the core 30 agent which does the comprehensive gap analysis the prospect audit is just enough to make the problem visible :\)
  • anil-agrawal-2726: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Nice! Thanks a bunch.
  • anil-agrawal-2726: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) I just realized I have EmbedMyReviews \(EMR\) white-labeled that has a simple "local search map" feature that does the grid/heat map, insights, and competitor analysis using DataForSEO API. It prolly is not as comprehensive as Leadsnap but ok for quick basic audit. So, that part is done... BTW, I am surprised they ranking #3 for 5x5 1mile grid for their niche keyword for their local area... granted the grid is rather small... top 3 is 71% but their competitors ranking 1 & 2 have a ton more reviews...
    
    Next is screaming frog audit ;-\)
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Anil Agrawal](obj://user/cedd2eb6ed1446bc9dcf33515c7cca9b) that actually means theres solid potential here, if you can close those review velocity gaps and implement proper core 30 structure you could realistically push them to number 1. the screaming frog audit will probably reveal the technical gaps holding them back and that data combined with the rank map showing where competitors are beating them is a compelling sales presentation. this sounds like a strong prospect :\)
  • anil-agrawal-2726: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) fantastic! Let me see how I can get them on board… 👍🏼

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 164. A Newby needs guidance
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/a-newby-needs-guidance
Autor: roy-scarboro-8167  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  16 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I'm new at this guys. I have a few beginners questions for anyone who cares to answer them.

I've invested in the Pro classroom as I believe this is exactly what I'll need to help me get across the finish line. I'm helping a friend who owns a tree service company with his site and even though I've never done any of this before, I've had 4 months in quarantiine with our son who has just underwent a bone marrow transplant and I've been able to teach myself Wordpress enough to get him a site up and functional. I've been binge-watching Caleb on youtube, joined last weeks VIP class and here we are.

I've opened the core 30 beta program, began filling it out and this is where I have seemed to hit a wall. These API keys are somethig I've never dealth with so I'm rather lost. I honestly don't know which way to go. There appears to be so many options that I have to choose from and knowing nothing about any of this, I'm wondering what I should do at this point? I'm reading everything I have time to read, and maybe one day all of it will start to make since. I just don't want it to take me forever to get where I can start to see real results for my friend. I could go on, but I need help just getting started. I'm not at all lazy, I'm just not wanting to waste mine or anyones elses time getting up and running.

BTW, the site I have built that is a work in progress is [www.millsexperttreeservice.net](http://www.millsexperttreeservice.net) if anyone has any suggestions for me. I'm willing to listen and learn. I've began to implement many of Calebs suggestions to the landing page but with this core 30 program,, I may be abkle to get everything where it needs to be without much of anything else.

RESPUESTAS:
• mark-farrow-6630: Hey [@Woody Scarboro](obj://user/ddeee472a10741c399a3db14d9e8e785) this is a nice site imo. One thing I noticed on the homepage - in your map embed its an address but not the GBP. You want to embed the businesses GBP there to make the connection. The homepage seems to be linking down into main services and I think the GBP CAT pages so good start sir.
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Mark Farrow](obj://user/5d7f726c110a43dc973a719104a5e4b4) agree! it's a good site, don't forget to swap out the map embed to actual GBP \(this is super common btw\)
    
    There's a separate module that shows you how to setup all the APIs. It's a little tedious but it really just involves going to the website, signing up for the API key, plug it in, and then let it rip. Here's the link to it: [https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/492fd5bc?md=a9ed9a90201a4299bd08202effdf9a0d](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/492fd5bc?md=a9ed9a90201a4299bd08202effdf9a0d)
  • roy-scarboro-8167: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) Thanks for your input. i went to our GBP and copied the url link to share the page and it has not workerd for me. I must be doing something wrong- but I may have it working now see if it look right now - [www.millsxperttreeservice.net](http://www.millsxperttreeservice.net) thank you all!!😀-
    
    UPDATE. I got it fixed I had to setup a HTML block on that page to place the html code from the GBP.
  • roy-scarboro-8167: [@Mark Farrow](obj://user/5d7f726c110a43dc973a719104a5e4b4) Thanks Mark. I've definitely had the time sitting around in the hospital to learn Wordpress. I just wish I had the extra funds to make a serious effort at this. I got the GPB map fixed but should it be up higher on my page?
  • roy-scarboro-8167: [@Mark Farrow](obj://user/5d7f726c110a43dc973a719104a5e4b4) Mark, you mentioned to me today on our call the need for topical relevance on each of our pages. Would that be in the form of frequently asked questions at the bottom of each page, linking back to a page per question answered, such that it would give a more in-depth explanation? If so, I can see where that would be a lot of extra work and a ton of pages if that's something that's necessary on every page that I have on every city page and ever service page, as well as home page. please advice. thank you
  • mark-farrow-6630: [@Woody Scarboro](obj://user/ddeee472a10741c399a3db14d9e8e785) Hi Woody, if you have a services pages that are key services you are trying to rank for the answer is yes, they need supporting content in the form of PAA questions/frequently ask questions. It can be a lot of pages, but that’s what drives the topical authority. You can apply supporting content to category pages, homepages, service pages. Hope this helps.
  • roy-scarboro-8167: [@Mark Farrow](obj://user/5d7f726c110a43dc973a719104a5e4b4) thanks Mark! 🙂
  • roy-scarboro-8167: [@Mark Farrow](obj://user/5d7f726c110a43dc973a719104a5e4b4)
    
    Hi Mark,
    I want to make sure I’m on the right track before I move forward.
    
    First, I currently only have ChatGPT to work with. Is that sufficient, or do I really need to be using Claude? My understanding is that I should take each of my service pages \(I have 6 now, going to 7\) and create a PAA/FAQ section with several questions specific to each service, like tree trimming. How many questions should I be aiming for per page?
    
    Second, when placing links inside the answers, should I link a specific keyword or phrase within the answer, or link the entire answer itself? Also, how long should each answer be?
    
    All of my pages are built from a WordPress template I’ve created, and each one has a contact form in the upper-right, above-the-fold section. For the pages I link to from the FAQs, should those follow the same template, or should they just be simple pages with an H2 or H3 title based on the question and a more detailed answer?
    
    Lastly, is there a specific prompt you recommend using in ChatGPT or Claude to generate strong, relevant FAQ questions for each service page?
    Let me know if I’m off anywhere here, or if anything needs clarification.
  • mark-farrow-6630: [@Woody Scarboro](obj://user/ddeee472a10741c399a3db14d9e8e785) Aim for 6 FAq's. I like the one sentence direct answer in the FAQ followed by 35 words.
    I link from a phrase in the 35 words as you can craft that \(have more words to work with\).
    They can be simple pages but the more you put into them the better they will index, use the form, build in images every 300/400 words, use callouts etc.
    The prompts are in the classroom content:
    [https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=a92c962b3fc24a76972646e306bb6b32](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=a92c962b3fc24a76972646e306bb6b32)
    At the bottom of this lesson.
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: hey [@Woody Scarboro](obj://user/ddeee472a10741c399a3db14d9e8e785) honestly the site looks really clean for someone who taught themselves wordpress, great job, one thing mark flagged is correct, your map embed is showing a generic address not the actual GBP listing, you want to embed the businesses specific GBP map so google sees that connection. for the API setup watch this module in the classroom [https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/492fd5bc?md=d4a3ad105d38432d960287fe28748add](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/492fd5bc?md=d4a3ad105d38432d960287fe28748add) it walks through everything step by step. also add 2-3 more CTA buttons throughout the page where it makes sense like after the services section and near the testimonials cause one button at the top isnt enough to capture leads. and swap the testimonials section for a google reviews widget so visitors see real verified google reviews which builds way more trust than manual testimonials :\)
  • roy-scarboro-8167: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Hi Miles, many of the testimonials you see there are from a period when the previous owner of the business had a yelp account, so maybe 80% of them are from back then, but they are still on the yelp page under their name. 7 of them are from recently google reviews that I have helped them get by setting up a GBP and hounding them to send out the link in a text to new customers, etc...
    
    I have been listening to Caleb discuss city pages, which I setup for all those in close proximity to High Point, NC. Last Monday in the VIP class I believe I understood him to say that city pages like I have, don't work, but to rather create pages for local landmarks and places of interest, etc. Whats the best way to approach that? Thanks for your help!!
  • roy-scarboro-8167: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Actually I had two buttons and a request form they could fill out to get in touch wity us 24/7. At the bottom of the first section there is a button labeled "call now to schedule an appointment."\(should I rename that something else?\) Plus there are the two links at the top right to text or call...
  • roy-scarboro-8167: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) which google review linking apparatus would you recommend for linking new google reviews direcely into our page? Ive looked at one such program earlier but can't recall what it was?'
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Woody Scarboro](obj://user/ddeee472a10741c399a3db14d9e8e785) i was referring to the testimonial section on the homepage specifically, adding a google reviews widget there so visitors see live verified google reviews right on the homepage rather than manually added testimonials. for the city pages question Caleb means low effort thin city pages dont work, what does work is proper location pages with unique local content mentioning specific landmarks neighborhoods and local context for each city, so your high point area pages are fine as long as each has genuinely unique content not just the city name swapped in a template :\)
  • roy-scarboro-8167: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Gotcha, so which google review widget would you recommend? Have you looked at my city pages for the 6-7 others we service? I'm curious if they are appearing to google to be so similar that they won't index them? I'd love nothig more than to have green dots all over the "piedmont triad" which is comprised over over a dozen large and small cities all cramped together.
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Woody Scarboro](obj://user/ddeee472a10741c399a3db14d9e8e785) for the google reviews widget use Smash Balloon plugin. for your city pages yes the concern about duplicate content is valid if they all have the same structure with just the city name swapped, each one needs genuinely unique content mentioning specific local landmarks neighborhoods and local context for that city to get google to index and rank them separately. go into GSC right now and manually request indexing for each city page that isnt showing as indexed, and while you wait for those to process start rewriting each page with more unique local content specific to each piedmont triad city :\)

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 165. Question about h1 + services index route
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/question-about-h1-services-index-route
Autor: marina-n-7893  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
meow hope everyone is havin a good weekend
i recently inherited an ugly wordpress site from a client. they are already ranking for their primary category \(pilates studio\), and so i'm giving it a light stylistic remodel as opposed to demolishing it and redesigning it. i have some questions:

- the business is a pilates studio in indy, but they are also a yoga studio and wellness center. the site has no h1, so i gotta add one but i'm indecisive on some things. the primary category is "pilates studio", so i'd go with "pilates studio in indianapolis, in" because this landing page is \(technically\) the "pilates studio" page. however, i think that it might look weird to the user to see just this as the h1 since the studio equally does pilates, yoga, and wellness. i'm thinking of making the h1 be "pilates studio in indianapolis, in" and not as stylistically prominent, maybe smaller, and then below it just a span or p element with "Pilates, yoga, wellness in North Indy" or something like that styled to look more prominent than the h1. would that work ?

- their services/content about their services are listed in a singular page, under /our-offerings/. can i keep this url as the services index page, or should i migrate it over to /services/ ?

tysm ^-^

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Marina N](obj://user/a540f5ba691e491ea3ad7464a1f352a0) for the H1 question keep it as "pilates studio in indianapolis in" and make it prominent not smaller, the SEO signal needs to be clear and google doesnt care if it looks weird to users as much as you think. you can add a visually styled subheadline beneath it saying "pilates yoga and wellness in north indy" as a paragraph that captures the other services naturally without competing with the H1 signal. for the URL keep /our-offerings/ as is rather than migrating to /services/ since changing URLs on a site that already ranks risks losing that existing authority, just make sure the page content and internal linking are properly optimized rather than changing the slug :\)
• ted-tibbetts-5295: I wonder \(and please someone tell me if I'm wrong\) if a good strategy is to create a home page for businesses like this that incorporate all the different branches, but then have a page like "pilates studio in Indianapolis" and dictate that as the landing page in your GBP. That way, you still have the home page for humans to see, "Oh, this place does Pilates, Yoga, and wellness" but google sees the Yoga in Indianapolis as a focus.

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 166. Did everyone move from WordPress to Claude code ?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/did-everyone-move-from-wordpress-to-claude-code
Autor: pedro-soares-6825  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  12 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hey !

Just wondering if everyone moved from WordPress to Claude code lately ?

We've been doing both and I can't seem to find a winner.

Claude code is great and getting to 90% of the job done fast, but then every tweak can quickly become a pain.

And WordPress is the exact opposite, slow to build, but once it's there, easy to manage and refine content if needed.

What are you guys thoughts ?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: honestly it really depends on the client, if they’re comfortable with code, Claude workflow can be great. But for most clients, WordPress is still easier to manage, edit, and hand off long-term 👍
• roy-scarboro-8167: I'm new at all this. I've managed to teach myself wordpress over the past 4 months and from what I have seen claude code seems to be much like the old DOS prompt requiring a lot of typing and etc., but its does seem to be coming right along. There are so many updates and different versions to it that I can't keep up.
• pedro-soares-6825: Thanks for your inputs, I feel less lonely right now 😎
• chiara-burns-7791: Replit; similar to Lovable but I find it way more efficient.
  • pedro-soares-6825: [@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c) Thanks, do you know if it renders as client side or server side ?
  • chiara-burns-7791: [@Pedro Soares](obj://user/001d82eed8614dc1886aaaeb6120cb9c) It will default to SPA unless you tell it otherwise. You have to instruct it to build with your designated stack. I do astro + SSG
  • pedro-soares-6825: [@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c) oh, you can ask it to do astro right away ? That's pretty cool. Does it build a CMS behind it with something like Sanity ?
  • chiara-burns-7791: [@Pedro Soares](obj://user/001d82eed8614dc1886aaaeb6120cb9c) Yes! and yes, you can plug in a headless CMS like sanity. If you message their support email, their AI \(Quinn\) is immensely helpful and can give you specific recommendations on what they easily integrate with
  • pedro-soares-6825: [@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c) whaaaaat ? Okay, too many new tools... Thanks, I'll definitely try it
• reece-rainer-3922: By using Claude Code do you mean using MCPs/CLIs/APIs to develop on Wordpress with Claude Code, or just vibe coding websites straight from Claude Code + IDE?
  
  I've been doing Claude Code vibe coding into Wordpress and have been really surprised by the results. That way you get the Wordpress platform for reliability/indexing but still vibe code with it. You just need to use the MCPs and then deploy with an agent friendly tool like Cloudways.
  
  Really shocked how my website came out as I thought you couldn't make a gorgeous website in Wordpress , and I also thought I couldn't vibe code with Wordpress but I was wrong on both accounts. Claude code is next level
  [https://www.localleader.au](https://www.localleader.au)
  • pedro-soares-6825: [@Reece Rainer](obj://user/dd7f5e98c32541d8b25db016fb63fd38) nice ! Which WordPress theme is it using ? The default twenty twenty something ? Or Hello Elementor ?
    Are you then able to do some changes without having to ask Claude code everytime?
  • reece-rainer-3922: [@Pedro Soares](obj://user/001d82eed8614dc1886aaaeb6120cb9c) @Pedro Soares no elementor bro just free template Ollie
    
    What I did was I asked Claude code how can I make my website look like a reference website , got it to suggest to me 5 free WordPress themes, I picked the one I liked most
    
    Then I got it to analyse the reference website and build my WordPress theme to look exactly like it
    
    It just SSH'd into cloudways and built the whole thing
    
    Any edits take like 5-20 mins depending on the scope
    
    I haven't logged into WordPress yet
    
    I imagine you can just login and modify it in WordPress but I cbf to do that

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 167. Do you typically include review embeds on your content pages or only certain ones?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/do-you-typically-include-review-embeds-on-your-content-pages-or-only-certain-ones
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Wondering how to decide which ones I should and which ones don't really need the review embed.

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: review embeds are most valuable on your homepage and main category hub pages where conversion decisions are made, those are the pages prospects most likely land on and trust signals matter most there. for individual service pages you can add them but its lower priority, focus on getting the review widget on homepage and your top 2-3 category pages first and only add to service pages if they are high traffic conversion pages, supporting content pages generally dont need review embeds unless they are driving significant traffic
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) got it, thank you!

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 168. GBP Categories and Services (again!)
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/gbp-categories-and-services-again
Autor: anil-agrawal-2726  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  10 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
It's more likely my short attention span and poor memory including scattered brain that I can't seem to get my arms around GBP category and services strategy.... so here comes my questions..

1\) How many categories \(min, typical, max\) one should have? I think it would vary with niche and what categories Google gives us to choose from, but still... Side Note: I felt like engineer in me with that "min, typ,, max\) there ;-\)

2\) How many services per category? I understand we shouldn't have duplication of services over various categories. I also heard \(somewhere\) that slight overlap is ok as long as THAT service is actually offered, correct?

3\) For my situation, we have 13 services listed on the website that customers can book appointments for. Should I list them all under primary category? And, with Price and description? The description should be exact replica from the website or a description blurb we have on the booking site \(subdomain, noindex\)...

4\) The GBP landing page is the website Home page. Only a few services out of 13 are listed there with link to individual service page and at the bottom of those few services listed is a link to /services/ page that lists all 13 services with links to booking page for THAT individual service. Is this setup ok?

I don't know what I don't know. So all comments and suggestions are appreciated. It will only make me "better" hopefully ;-\)

Thanks!

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: for categories use as many as genuinely apply to your business, typically 3-7 with max 10, each category needs its own dedicated page on the website to back it up. for services max 10 per category, slight overlap is fine if you actually offer it but keep each service unique to its most relevant category
  
  for your homepage setup its not quite core 30, homepage should mention ALL categories and few key services with internal links not just a few leading to a generic services page. create the services hub and link directly from homepage to each individual service page, that flat linking structure is what google needs to understand your full service offering :\)
  • anil-agrawal-2726: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Thanks for such a quick comment and help.. by the time I saw it, I had put all 13 services under the primary category... have 2 more secondary categories but haven't listed any services yet...let me think a bit more about you have said...my brain is able to absorb a bit atm.. ;-\)
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Anil Agrawal](obj://user/cedd2eb6ed1446bc9dcf33515c7cca9b) H no worries take your time, having 13 services under primary is not ideal but not a disaster either, just redistribute them so each secondary category gets the services that are most relevant to it
    
    think about it this way, if a service matches a secondary category better than primary then move it there. max 10 per category and each service should have its own dedicated page on the website to back it up, thats the core 30 standard!
  • anil-agrawal-2726: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) ok cool… I worked frantically past several days to swing all 13 services pages…
    
    Home Page:
    1\) A few services listed with link to their service page
    
    2\) A button with “All Services” > /services/ page
    
    Services Page \(/services/\)
    Has all 13 services listed > individual service page
    
    Service areas are like based on nearby neighborhoods \(10 of them\)… those pages are pending… argh! lol 😆
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Anil Agrawal](obj://user/cedd2eb6ed1446bc9dcf33515c7cca9b) Great progress man, for homepage structure id suggest link directly to each service page from homepage not just the services hub. so homepage mentions all categories plus has direct links to your top services, then services page can have the complete list. google needs to see that flat linking structure from homepage to understand your full service offering
    
    and yeah service area pages for neighborhoods are next step after core services are dialed in :\)
  • bobbee: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Thanks for adding more and more granular detail - this is super helpful.
  • anil-agrawal-2726: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) hey Miles, I remember you had mentioned something like "embed GBP on "some" pages..." but I wasn't sure what that meant and never actually did that other than Google maps embed for the location on the landing page \(Home Page\)... I can't find that comment or question thread...
    
    Can you please let me know what that could have been "embed GBP" on which pages? TIA
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Anil Agrawal](obj://user/cedd2eb6ed1446bc9dcf33515c7cca9b) the GBP map embed goes on your homepage and contact page at minimum, those are the most important pages for the local signal. you can also add it to main category hub pages and location pages if they have multiple service areas. the embed is just the google maps iframe showing your specific GBP listing not just a generic map, you get it by going to google maps searching your business clicking share then embed a map and copying the iframe code. having it on homepage and contact page is the standard core 30 recommendation, adding it to every single page is overkill but key landing pages definitely benefit from it or otherwise you can embed into your footer
  • anil-agrawal-2726: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) ok got it. So I am covered as I already had GBP map embed on Home page, contact page and even on booking page \(which is not that critical from SEO perspective but still helpful for people\)… thanks you so much.
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Anil Agrawal](obj://user/cedd2eb6ed1446bc9dcf33515c7cca9b) great 🙌

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 169. GBP: Adding a secondary phone number before making it the main number
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/gbp-adding-a-secondary-phone-number-before-making-it-the-main-number
Autor: anil-agrawal-2726  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  4 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
A GBP profile has a phone number for about 4 months now. It's the original number since the GBP verification. It's a Google Voice number which the client says has some issues... issues not defined or clarified... Claude suspects it's due to some carriers "think" it's a spammy number or something along those lines... I really don't understand this...

but anyways, decided to get a Twilio number and now thinking of adding it as a secondary number and also add on the GBP landing page \(entire website and schemas\) as secondary number... wait for 2-4 weeks and then switch over to Twilio as the main number and leave the Google Voice number as secondary for a while.

Is that the right process to follow to ensure Google doesn't get mad? ;-\)

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: Yes, that’s the safe way to do it. add Twilio as the secondary first, let it settle for a couple of weeks, then switch everything over at once so GBP, the site, schema, and citations all match :\)
  • anil-agrawal-2726: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) thanks much!
• michael-thiel-7089: Don't take my word as gospel, but I've set up a few GBP's using a cell number then within a week or two got my Twilio number live for tracking and then just switched it out as the main number on GBP. I've never had an issue.
  • anil-agrawal-2726: [@Michael Thiel](obj://user/ff028837e1ac426189e51a05a185591c) thanks for that data point

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 170. If category pages don't have all the services from the GBP, would you recommend adding them in there?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/if-category-pages-dont-have-all-the-services-from-the-gbp-would-you-recommend-adding-them-in-there
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  6 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I've always known I learn best by making mistakes and correcting them, unfortunately. 😅

I've been making category pages but a lot of times they don't include the services I put into the GBP. Is this crucial? Should I go back and put them in there or redo the category page to put them in there?

RESPUESTAS:
• chiara-burns-7791: Yeah, that's the defining trait of Core30 imho.
  
  The structure of homepage/parent/child pages is essentially the same as topic clusters that have been a best practice in organic SEO since ~2017 \(thanks HubSpot\), but what makes this methodology uniquely powerful for local SEO is how the clusters perfectly mirror the GBP.
  
  \(and the sequencing of material after completing the foundation/Core30, but as far as the foundation, the mirroring is the key\)
  
  To answer your other question, you don't need to redo the category pages, just weave the correct services in
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c) got it, okay! When you say to weave them in, does it matter if I put them in as H2s or can I weave them into the paragraph text of the content?
  • chiara-burns-7791: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) H2s carry more weight, technically. So ideally the service name would be H2, and then you've got a li'l body text describing the service that links off to the actual page. So H2 with the service name, link in the subsequent copy.
• mark-farrow-6630: Add to the CAT page as you add additional services.
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: yeah add mentions of all the services from that GBP category onto the category hub page, every service listed under a category in GBP should be mentioned by name on the corresponding category page and ideally linked to its individual service page. this is exactly what creates the topical authority, the category page acts as a hub that covers all the services under it comprehensively :\)
• stephan-franklyn-4962: Thank you all SO much for this - I think I got it. Sounds like my current marching orders will be:
  [ol:1][li]Go back and add in all the services from the GBP under that category to the existing content as H2s with body text[li]Put a link in the body text to the specific service page \(parent>child page\)[li]The service page should have a link back up to the parent page[li]The service page will have a link down to a geographical page once they are created.[li]Moving forward, category pages will start with all services from GBP cat as H2

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 173. Order of operations; Core30 --> topical --> Geo
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/order-of-operations-core30-topical-geo
Autor: chiara-burns-7791  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  7 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Looking to clarify and/or correct my understanding of the process:

1. Size heatmap in such a way that the map pack leader is between 60-75% green \(video said more than 30%, less than 100%; so not sure if there's ideal parameters within that\)

2. Until you match the map pack leader, focus on topical relevance \(FAQs first, blog second\)

3. Once you match topical relevance \(or if you've done everything and still aren't\), start building out geo content

Also I'm a bit confused on this: [https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=a92c962b3fc24a76972646e306bb6b32](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=a92c962b3fc24a76972646e306bb6b32)

^ Am I correct in understanding that the FAQ section the Core30 agent ouputs goes onto the Core30 pages as an overview...but if we need more topical relevance, we create longer articles/blogs \(1500 words\) each FAQ links out to?

If I know that we're going to need geo for a couple of adjacent neighborhoods that are highly competitive, can I build those out in tandem? Or is it necessary to write through all the FAQ articles first?

Summary
[ol:1][li]Is order of ops I have up top correct? [li]Am I understanding the FAQ <--> longform article relationship correctly? [li]If I know we're going to need geo pages for a couple of neighborhoods, can I build that in tandem?

EDIT: Nvm, live video from December 2025 said "Aim for roughly half the top competitor's market share before switching to geographic content creation." And we're currently there so I guess I do hop to geo now.

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: hey [@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c) your understanding is spot on and you basically answered your own question with the december video reference. the order is core 30 service pages first then topical supporting content to build depth, and geo expansion only after you hit roughly half the top competitors market share cause thats when topical relevance stops being the bottleneck and geographic coverage becomes the next limiting factor. the FAQ on core 30 pages is the overview and the 1500 word supporting articles are the deep dive those FAQ questions link out to. once your at that market share threshold you can start geo pages but topical depth should be the priority until you get there :\)
  • chiara-burns-7791: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Thank you! We're about there and I don't think we can crack the bottom quadrant without geo anyway
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c) yeah exactly, once you hit that threshold the green stops spreading from topical work alone and geo pages are what push it into those harder to reach areas. sounds like your right at the inflection point where geo expansion is the logical next move
• mark-farrow-6630: In high comp add in a hub page also to allow for even more supp content. Instead of building 20 faq’s…
  • chiara-burns-7791: [@Mark Farrow](obj://user/5d7f726c110a43dc973a719104a5e4b4) I already did all the FAQs at launch at the bottom of each page where I thought we'd need it, so no trouble there. In the content, the instructions are to hyperlink to a 1500-word article that goes deeper into the answer. Are you saying build a top-level FAQ page?
  • mark-farrow-6630: [@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c) what I’m saying is if you have say six FAQs and each FAQ links down to its long form answer 1500 to 2500 words and that’s not enough to push the rankings for that page, you can add a hub page off of that service page and add more supporting content that way instead of creating another 4 to 10 FAQs that start to look stupid on the page.
  • chiara-burns-7791: [@Mark Farrow](obj://user/5d7f726c110a43dc973a719104a5e4b4) Still not following. The FAQs are supposed to be brief blurbs so that they fit withing FAQ schema/have the potential \(even if not realistic in 2026\) to be pulled as a snippet. So the Q/A is brief on the actual page, but the answer links off to the long-form supporting content.
    
    Aren't we saying the same thing? I'm not putting 1500 word answers on a page lmao.

## 174. FAQ answer length
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/faq-answer-length
Autor: kevin-tully-4285  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I know that the training modules talk about keeping FAQ answers on services pages to something like 75-100 words, and then linking to a blog post.

Today I asked Claude to write me an FAQ reply and it spit out a much longer answer. That led me to ask it what the best practice is for FAQ reply length. It said the following:

-----

Best practice today is "answer the question fully in the shortest space that does it justice" — which usually means 40–60 words for the direct answer, followed by structured supporting content if the question warrants it. The total length depends on the question.

The actual rules that matter
Rule 1: The first 40–60 words should answer the question directly.
Rule 2: Length should match question complexity.
Rule 3: The "link to a longer post" model only works if the longer post exists and is genuinely better.

-----

If the question warrants a longer reply, should the reply do it justice regardless of length? Or keep it short and link out for the full explanation?

Asking for a Personal Injury site in a very competitive market. THANKS!

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: for local SEO purposes the 50-75 word direct answer format Caleb teaches is still the right approach for FAQ sections on service pages because the goal is to signal to google you answered the question concisely which is what gets featured in AI overviews and PAA results. the longer supporting blog post linked from the FAQ is where you go deep and that combination of short answer plus link to comprehensive article is what builds both topical authority and AI search visibility. for a competitive personal injury market especially stick to the short answer plus link model cause google rewards that structure for YMYL niches
  • kevin-tully-4285: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) thanks Miles!

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 175. Changing Clients GBP address ?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/changing-clients-gbp-address
Autor: brian-kiselstein-6058  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  3 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
[@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) Thank you for the advice about the GBP. I watched the tutorial on "video verification". If my client were to use a "friends" address, I read somewhere online google MIGHT ask for further documentation \(mail, business license address change\). Will Google go this far to verify the address change, or does that video typically cover all angles? Im only asking because before I tell my client lets change address, contact a friend, setup this whole arrangement to get it approved, I just want to make sure there isn't anything missing. Thank you so much guys

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: yeah google can and sometimes does ask for additional documentation beyond just the video verification, especially for address changes on established profiles. they might request utility bills business license showing the business name at that address. if the friends address cant provide any of those documents if google asks then there is risk of the verification failing or getting flagged. make sure the friend is willing to receive mail there and has something with the business name tied to that address before making the change, coworking space is honestly safer cause they handle this all the time and have proper documentation ready
• brian-kiselstein-6058: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) appreciate the help \(also totally missed this response\). Can a coworking space work for a video verification? If you ask AI, they say you'll fail miserably lol. Im just curious how much experience you or any of you guys have had with getting verified at a coworking space
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Brian Kiselstein](obj://user/04629cf34b4446269d04a62afd7d6350) yes coworking spaces work for video verification as long as the space has clear signage visible in the video and also make sure there are not alot if businesses are verified by using the same location address and you can show the address on any business documentation

## 176. Blog Posts
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/blog-posts
Autor: gary-magill-8831  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  1 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Your new Youtube video is all about how blog posts are not how you rank local businesses and that instead you need to do the Core 30. Does that mean that you don't do anymore SEO for clients after the Core 30 is built?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: hey [@Gary Magill](obj://user/084c0577e8dc41128d2ca7de13adc9eb) core 30 is the foundation not the finish line, after the core 30 is built you shift to topical supporting content like PAA based FAQ articles and supporting blog posts to build depth, then geographic expansion pages for surrounding cities, then ongoing GBP posts reviews and citation maintenance. blog posts for local SEO arent about ranking the blog posts themselves like traditional SEO, theyre supporting content that builds topical authority around your core service and category pages. so its not that blog posts dont matter, its that they serve a different purpose in local SEO and only come after the core 30 foundation is properly built :\)

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 177. When Re-theming, should we use the exact category on the home page or target kw?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/when-re-theming-should-we-use-the-exact-category-on-the-home-page-or-target-kw
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I understand on the cat page we use the exact match slug and H1, but Title and the rest of the content uses what we're re-theming to.

But on the home page where we put secondary cats as H2s, do we put the GBP Category or the Target KW?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: you can use the retheming target keyword not the exact GBP category name, since the H2s are what users actually read and google understands the semantic relationship between the category and the retheming. the category hub page slug and H1 should match the GBP category more closely for the ranking signal, but the homepage H2s can use the more natural customer facing language that matches how people actually search
• stephan-franklyn-4962: got it thank you!

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 178. Core 30 Gold Standards: Anyone Include PAA Supporting Content in Theirs?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/core-30-gold-standards-anyone-include-paa-supporting-content-in-theirs
Autor: pedro-febles-bula-8692  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hey everyone! I have a question about the Core 30 architecture. Does anyone have a website they built or are inspired by that you'd consider a gold standard for Core 30 execution? Especially curious if anyone has seen a site that also includes city-specific, PAA-targeting supporting content pages layered beneath individual GBP service pages in the hierarchy.

I'm trying to understand how that supporting content tier fits into the overall Core 30 structure. Thanks!

RESPUESTAS:
• mark-farrow-6630: Hi [@Pedro Febles Bula](obj://user/225c3da3cd484f64a51fbebf717df800), after you build a core 30 or as you’re building out the core 30 keep an eye on rankings. In some non-competitive niches the core 30 will be enough to push very good rankings. In other more competitive niches you’re going to need to build out more supplementary content to build topical authority. That is how it fits in.
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: the supporting content tier sits alongside the service pages not beneath them in a deeper hierarchy, think of it as a parallel branch rather than a deeper layer. so you have homepage to category pages to service pages as your core structure, and then PAA supporting blog posts link to and from the relevant service pages contextually but dont sit underneath them in the URL hierarchy. the supporting content builds topical authority around the service pages rather than creating more URL depth, and Caleb shows real examples of this in the topical relevance module in the classroom which is worth rewatching to see how the linking relationship works in practice

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 179. Location Hub Layout
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/location-hub-layout
Autor: kevin-tully-4285  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hey everyone! Newbie here.

Wondering which of these two layouts for a location hub would perform better for a personal injury site. The one with all service pages listed out \(more internal links\) or the one with the images that just links to the location page? Or do you have a third suggestion? Thanks.

RESPUESTAS:
• chiara-burns-7791: The classic tug of war between SEO and UX, Speaking Google vs. Speaking Human lol
  
  Regarding link architecture...option 1 \(visual cards\) would put links a layer deeper vs option 2 \(link wall\) has all the pages. If i were in your shoes I might go with a hybrid approach to satisfy both users and bots
  
  Link wall is great for technical SEO but it's a higher cognitive load for users; the visual/image version has a better brand feel and reduces choice paralysis by taking users to primary city page
  
  More and more, Google prioritizes what's best for users even as we SEOs try to game the system haha -- a hybrid approach would be having three location cards up top \(which will be what most of the users prefer to navigate\), and then a secondary section below-the-fold that has the link wall
  
  If you go the hybrid route just make sure your schema is airtight w breadcrummbing and the like
  
  EDIT: Morgan & Morgan has an example of a hybrid approach. The search bar above-the-fold is the user-friendly option visitors will actually use, and link wall underneath optimal for the non-humans. [https://www.forthepeople.com/office-locations/](https://www.forthepeople.com/office-locations/)
  
  Sweet James does the images that link deeper: [https://sweetjames.com/locations/](https://sweetjames.com/locations/)
  
  John Foy as example of a link wall \(hideous\): [https://www.johnfoy.com/areas-we-serve/](https://www.johnfoy.com/areas-we-serve/)
  
  My 2cents would be M&M's approach \(user-friendly navigation up top + link wall below\), but swap their search bar for visual cards since a search bar would be overkill for a handful of locations
  
  hope that helps!
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: the second one with all service pages listed out is way better cause it creates proper internal linking structure from your locations hub to individual service pages
  
  the first one with just images linking to location pages is cleaner visually but youre missing tons of internal linking opportunities. core 30 is all about building that silo structure so locations hub should link to all relevant location + service combinations not just generic location pages

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 180. Added Schema to website - Validator doesn't see it, but it's on the code of the page.
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/added-schema-to-website-validator-doesnt-see-it-but-its-on-the-code-of-the-page
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  6 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
So if I fetch the URL directly, it shows me hentry and Product schema. if I paste the full website code it shows me hentry and WebPage. Neither of these seem to be showing the Organization schema I've added.

Is this a sign of a problem?

RESPUESTAS:
• andrew-lin-7557: follow. I had the same issue. Claude told me as long as it shows in the full website code its okay. But would like to hear this too
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: ask claude to help debug this, paste your the schema you added and ask it to identify why the organization schema and other isnt being detected by the validator. the issue is likely that your schema is being injected via javascript after page load rather than being in the initial HTML, which means the validator cant see it even though it shows in the page code, claude can pinpoint exactly whats wrong and give you the fix :\)
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) will do thank you!
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) and jsut to clarify - if the schema is in the page code but Validator can't see it, that's fine because Google will still see it, correct?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) if validator can’t see it, I’d assume it may not be in the rendered HTML or may be injected too late. Google can still sometimes render it, but I’d verify with the validator before and inspect the rendered HTML
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) thank you - I did figure it out after asking this but will make sure validator can see it in the future!

## 182. First 4 weeks of progress for real estate client in LA :-)
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/first-4-weeks-of-progress-for-real-estate-client-in-la
Autor: chiara-burns-7791  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Client is a real estate agent in a *very* competitve part of Los Angeles County. First heatmap was run by client on March 3rd but our engagement/all SEO work began March 19th.

INPUTS:

[ol:1][li]Content only \(no backlinks so far\)[li]50% of Core30 published[li]37.5% of Core30 indexed

RESULTS TO DATE \(March 19th - April 14th\):

primary keyword:
[ul][li]"real estate agent": 2.37% → 10.06% of top-3 \(+153% visibility\)

secondary keywords tracking just out of curiosity:
[ul][li]"divorce real estate agent": +663% visibility[li]"probate real estate agent": +553% visibility

Two observations/next steps:

[ol:1][li]Judging by the map we're going to need a hefty geo cluster to unlock Westlake Village \(bottom right quadrant\). The Thousand Oaks/Westlake Village boundary is confusing; the 'Westlake Village' in the client's mailing address is actually inside Thousand Oaks city limits \(the LA County side of Westlake Village is the incorporated city\). So the pin sits over Thousand Oaks even though the address says Westlake Village. 

2.Getting those backlinks before we lose our content halo lol. Real estate agents tend to have pretty robust pre-existing partner networks, so I'm getting creative with the backlinks; e.g., infographic resources for divorce and probate attorneys.

Wish I saw more people sharing results in this group -- so here's my latest :-\)

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: hey [@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c) crushing it💪, 153% visibility increase on primary keyword in under 4 weeks with only half the core 30 published is solid progress especially in competitive LA market
  
  for westlake village geo expansion yeah build dedicated location pages for that area once you finish core 30 topical pages, and the backlink strategy targeting divorce/probate attorneys is smart niche-specific approach. keep pushing the rest of core 30 to index and those numbers will keep climbing, nice work sharing real results :\)
  • chiara-burns-7791: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Yessir, thanks so much! 🫡

Autor: tomas-affranchino-9734  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  4 comentarios

I noticed there is a new section in the off page category that mentions about using a PR service for Google AI and overall rankings. Historically I've been using Fatjoe for PRs. Is this strategy effective for Australia as they are mainly US media outlets. Do you recommend leveraging on a monthly basis? I am just trying to figure out if this should be part of the monthly workflow in terms of backlink efforts. Thx!

## 185. Branded Anchor Text for External Links - can we use urls for pages?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/branded-anchor-text-for-external-links-can-we-use-urls-for-pages
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  4 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Trying to think of how I can keep from repeating anchor text. Is it fine to use urls for pages rather than just home page?

For brand variations I'm assuming if it was for ABC Chiropractic I could do brand variations like ABC Chiro, ABC Family Chiro, ABC Chiropractic and Wellness....would those be fine even though the added words aren't actually part of the name?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: yes you can use naked URLs as anchor text for service pages for validation links alongside branded anchors. for brand variations yes those are totally fine all work as branded anchor variations even if the full words arent in the official name cause google understands brand associations and partial name mentions. the key is keeping anchors natural and varied, never use exact match keyword anchors like "chiropractor lakewood co" for validation links cause that looks manipulative
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) awesome thank you
• mark-farrow-6630: Go to claude and ask it for some good SEO optimized external anchor text options :\)\) mention the 30-20-30-5-15 distribution. This is in the skool material.
  
  30% — Exact Match The anchor text is the precise target keyword. Example: "custom kitchen cabinets + CITY" Sends the strongest relevance signal to Google for that keyword.
  20% — Phrase Match The target keyword embedded within a slightly longer phrase. Example: "custom kitchen cabinets in +CITY" Reinforces the topic while adding natural variation.
  30% — Partial Match Only part of the keyword is used, often combined with related terms. Example: "kitchen cabinet installation in +CITY" Keeps the linking profile natural and avoids over-optimization penalties.
  5% — Branded Company name or branded terms as the anchor. Example: " NAME OF BUSINESS" Builds brand authority and mirrors how real third parties naturally link.
  15% — Generic / Naked URL Non-descriptive anchors like "click here," "learn more," or the raw URL itself. Example: "see our work" or "[thebusinessURL.com](http://orlandomcustomcabinets.com)" Further naturalizes the profile — Google expects these from real sites.
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Mark Farrow](obj://user/5d7f726c110a43dc973a719104a5e4b4) thank you yep I'll do this.

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 186. Do you go back and add internal links to content?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/do-you-go-back-and-add-internal-links-to-content
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  7 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
For example, when I create a category page, I haven't created the service pages for the services that's mentioned in the cat page yet. Do you guys suggest keeping track of that and then going back and adding it in once the new service page is created? \(same with Geo pages, etc?\)

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: yes you should add internal links once the service pages are created, that is completely normal and expected in the core 30 build process since you cant link to pages that dont exist yet. you can make a google sheet tracking which pages mention each service without a link yet, then as each new service or geo page goes live do a quick pass through the relevant category pages and homepage to add the contextual links. google recrawls pages regularly so adding links after the fact works just as well as having them from the start :\)
• caleb-ulku-6571 ★: yes
• stephan-franklyn-4962: Thanks guys!
• chiara-burns-7791: I launch with them already linked to their sister/parent/child pages, and launch the interlinked topic clusters in batches. It's more efficient for me then linking retroactively. A URL doesn't have to be published/public for you to designate it. As long as you don't set live any links that would just 404 because the correlating pages aren't live yet.
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c) To clarify, are you saying you'll launch articles in batches rather than 1 by 1?
    
    So rather than launching a category page for Massage Therapist, then launching one for a service page like therapeutic massage, then deep tissue massage, you will draft all of the articles so you can link them and put them on the site altogether roughly at the same time \(Launch the cat for Massage therapist + all the service pages of different massages\)?
  • chiara-burns-7791: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) Yes. Either like that OR depending on the site content, I'd do all the category pages first and then launch the pre-linked clusters of services together in batches. In the case of the latter, the only retroactive interlinking is category --> service \(so those 3-8 links all in the same module on cat page\)
    
    bc I think I'd lose my mind going back and trying to keep track of the web of interlinking between the service <--> service pages + service <--> category pages lol
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c)

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 187. Does text spacing/formatting impact Google ranking?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/does-text-spacingformatting-impact-google-ranking
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  5 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
2 images attached - 1 has gaps between the heading and the final sentence. The other shows the section before it being more bold.

I don't usually have this problem but I'm curious if Google will see that and possibly penalize or not give it the same positive impact?

I do plan to fix it, I just don't know if it's a high priority to fix immediately or not.

RESPUESTAS:
• chiara-burns-7791: virtually zero impact and very low priority. Google doesn't care about awkward whitespace. That's pure UX polish
  
  Is this one of the chiros you mentioned dipping recently? Just based on what I've seen you share, my intuition would be backlinks + verify the schema markup [validator.schema.org](http://validator.schema.org)
  
  hope that helps!
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c) Thank you, and yes it is! I just got the schema working \(Yoast was interfering\) and I have ordered more backlinks. Also, Caleb pointed out that the H1 was not exact match \(it was Personalized Chiropractic Care in St Petersburg - now it uses Chiropractor in St. Petersburg\)
  • chiara-burns-7791: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) Haha I just saw your post on schema 2mins ago, so looks like you were already working on that. Glad you figured it out!
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c)
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: nah text spacing and formatting quirks like that wont impact rankings directly, google reads the content not the visual layout
  
  fix it for user experience cause weird spacing looks unprofessional and might hurt conversions but its not a high priority seo issue :\)

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 188. Lovable HTML
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/lovable-html-2
Autor: abhishek-gupta-8624  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  6 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hey folks,

Just curious if [lovablehtml.com](http://lovablehtml.com) is still needed to rank Lovable sites? I was researching on ChatGPT and even Lovable tool today. Lovable says that my site is now hosted on the TanStack Start, which is a full stack framework that contains fully rendered HTML with all the content. This means the crawlers should be able to read HTML immediately.

For context, this is what Lovable tells me:

**"Lovable switched to TanStack Start SSR in April 2026, and your project is on that stack — so lovablehtml.com \(which prerenders the old CSR template\) is unnecessary. Verify with curl after publishing: you'll see real HTML, not an empty shell."**

Does this mean that we don't need [lovablehtml.com](http://lovablehtml.com) anymore? cc: [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c)

RESPUESTAS:
• austin-delaney-1000: As far as I can tell it's stil Client Side Rendering \(CSR\), not Server Side Rendering \(SSR\). So lovablehtml would still be needed. I use preferendering on Netlify instead. It's free.
  • abhishek-gupta-8624: [@Austin Delaney](obj://user/c61e510a6883428db5bc4d4d3d1c212e) try a new project on lovable. It’s SSR
  • austin-delaney-1000: [@Abhishek Gupta](obj://user/ec6810c2b3504accbcd870f46888e1e0) yup, I did - still getting the blank <div id="root"></div>
    
    maybe it hasn't fully rolled out yet.
  • abhishek-gupta-8624: [@Austin Delaney](obj://user/c61e510a6883428db5bc4d4d3d1c212e) strange. Fully possible. I’ll create a new site today or tomorrow and let you know if I see the same
  • abhishek-gupta-8624: UPDATE: I built one more site today, and I asked Lovable what stack it is on, and below is the output.
    
    The technology stack:
    [ul][li]Framework: TanStack Start v1 \(React 19, full-stack with SSR\)[li]Build tool: Vite 7[li]Styling: Tailwind CSS v4 with shadcn/ui components[li]Language: TypeScript[li]Routing: TanStack Router \(file-based\)[li]Deployment target: Cloudflare Workers \(edge\)
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: if your Lovable project is actually on the new TanStack Start SSR stack, [lovablehtml.com](http://lovablehtml.com) is probably no longer needed because Google gets real server-rendered HTML on first crawl
