## 190. Service based website adding on eComm shop
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/service-based-website-adding-on-ecomm-shop
Autor: andrew-drury-8632  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  1 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hi guys - wondering if I can get some help, please!

I have an Interior Design website which has just launched in the last couple of weeks that is aimed initially at their Interior Design service. However, the site will go into phase 2 in the coming months and become an eComm shop selling furniture as well.

No Core30 for the site has been performed yet. I want to know how to perform the Core30 and which page I should use as the GBP landing page?

I believe if it's a service based company then the home page would be the GBP landing page, and an eComm site you would potentially use the 'About' page. Is this right?

If so, what is the best strategy to perform the Core30 in this instance where the site is currently a service based website but will turn into an eComm store as well?

Thank you

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: Your homepage should still be your GBP landing page targeting your primary GBP category not the about page. build the core 30 service structure first since thats what drives local map pack rankings, then when the ecomm phase launches add the shop section as a separate branch of the site that doesnt interfere with the service page hierarchy. the service pages and ecomm pages can coexist on the same domain, just make sure the homepage H1 and title tag stay focused on the primary service category and dont get diluted by the product side of the business

## 193. GBP LP and Keywords
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/gbp-lp-and-keywords
Autor: jeff-kelsheimer-8919  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  6 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Good morning, I had a quick question over mapping out a site. So a client of mine is going to do kitchen remodels and bathroom remodels so i am planning to have those as Secondary Categories. When i map out his services they are vague for each of those and when i look at search intent for those "services" they are "0/month". Any advice on moving forward with GBP optimziing and core 30. Here is the layout currently based off the prompt zero in Claude.

This is a brand new GBP and was originally set up as General Contractor, which has tons of search but all over the place as to intent or why they are searching so I am trying to narrow it down for what they actually do. As long as the below is what they actually plan to do, regardless of search intent, this is a solid layout to build to align with their GBP?

Thanks in advance for the feedback.

Primary: Remodeler
Category Page URL: /bathroom-remodeler-city-xx
Child Services:
[ul][li]Full Bathroom Remodeling → /full-bathroom-remodeling-city-xx[li]Shower Remodeling and Walk-In Showers → /shower-remodeling-walk-in-showers-city-xx[li]Bathtub Installation and Replacement → /bathtub-installation-replacement-city-xx[li]Tile Installation \(Bathroom\) → /bathroom-tile-installation-city-xx[li]Vanity Installation → /vanity-installation-city-xx[li]Bathroom Lighting and Fixtures → /bathroom-lighting-fixtures-city-xx

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: hey [@Jeff Kelsheimer](obj://user/bd8ea739028b4f35a79d26b0d9d268a8) the structure looks solid and yes build it even if individual service keywords show zero SV because google doesnt rank pages based on volume alone, it ranks based on topical relevance and GBP alignment. those child service pages exist to build topical authority around the category hub pages and signal to google this business comprehensively covers bathroom and kitchen remodeling, not to individually rank for each child service term. the primary category remodeler with kitchen remodeler and bathroom remodeler as secondaries is exactly the right core 30 structure :\)
• jeff-kelsheimer-8919: awesome! thanks Miles. And if this if the services are the same in other cities/service areas do the same?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Jeff Kelsheimer](obj://user/bd8ea739028b4f35a79d26b0d9d268a8) yes exactly the same structure for each city, just create location specific versions, the page structure and service hierarchy stays identical across all locations, unique local content per city is what prevents cannibalization and tells google each page targets a distinct geographic area
  • jeff-kelsheimer-8919: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) and is there a best practice on how many supporting services for those secondary categories?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Jeff Kelsheimer](obj://user/bd8ea739028b4f35a79d26b0d9d268a8) we usually add 10 services for each category but it's up to your business about how many services you do actually you can add
  • jeff-kelsheimer-8919: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Miles this was super helpful and connected the dots from my previous SEO plan. Love it. Again, thank you 🙏

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 194. Should I change GBP LP original headings to h3/h4 or paragraph text?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/should-i-change-gbp-lp-original-headings-to-h3h4-or-paragraph-text
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  16 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
The GBP LP I'm working on has multiple H1s, H2s, etc. I've changed so the only H1 matches the Title tag, The only H2 are categories and important services.

The other headings are for: Testimonials; serving Jefferson, Washington, and Smith Counties; Benefits of Chiropractic; Common Conditions; How it Works \(3-step process to book, exam, and discuss care plans\); Unlock Your Full Potential with ABC Chiropractic; and then the Dr's name for an About us blurb.

This is all on the GBP Landing Page \(home page\)

RESPUESTAS:
• douglas-page-2404: Many novices use the header tags for formatting purposes and not what they were actually intended for. I find many local sites like what you describe although it is getting better. You just need to understand the hierarchy of the information on the page and use the appropriate header tag. Only one H1 to match the title tag, the groups of info below that should start with an H2 and then if info is broken down into further sections within a group, then add the next header tag in order.
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: yeah you can change those to H3s or paragraph text, you only want H2s for your main GBP secondary categories and main services on the homepage. testimonials about us benefits and how it works sections dont need H2 treatment cause those arent ranking signals google needs to see, H3s work fine for those or just bold paragraph headings. keep your H2s reserved for the topically important stuff like your secondary category names and top services cause that hierarchy signals to google what the page is actually about :\)
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) perfect, thank you! With that said, would I exclude using the primary category in the h2 section where I would list the secondary categories?
    
    If so, I may have misunderstood the training where the main category is in Title and H1, and then I've been putting primary category as just "chiropractor" in H2 with the secondary ones as well.
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) yeah exclude the primary category from H2 since its already covered in your H1 and title tag, no need to repeat it as H2 cause that dilutes the H2 signals. H2s should be for your secondary GBP categories and your most important services. the primary category owns the H1 and title, secondaries own the H2s, thats the proper hierarchy that signals to google exactly what each level of your page is targeting :\)
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Got it - so with this Chiro they do chiro for families as well as animals...should we do h2 for those or at least the animal chiro since it is like a subcategory of Chiropractor? \(Screenshot attached for better context\)
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) yeah absolutely make animal chiropractor its own H2 since its a distinct secondary categry that opens up completely different ranking opportunities, same with wellness center and holistic medicine practitoner if those are actual GBP categories. family chiropractor and animal chiropractor are different enough that google treats them as separate entities so each its own H2 and dedicated category page. massage therapist should also be an H2 if its a secondary GBP category
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) just to clarify - Should I keep family chiropractor as well because Google would treat that as a separate entity from Chiropractor? Or get rid of that one and just keep Animal Chiro?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) keep family chiropractor as an H2 too cause it IS a separate entity from just chiropractor in googles eyes, family chiro targets a different searcher with different intent than general chiropractor. so you want H2s for all of them that are actual GBP secondary categories, family chiropractor, animal chiropractor, wellness center, holistic medicine practitioner. each one is a ranking opportunity for a different search term and gets its own category hub page on the website. the more relevant secondary categories with proper H2s and dedicated pages the wider your topical coverage
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Okay I understand now, awesome and thank you sir!
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Would you recommend having a content block about Chiropractor like I would for the h2s?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) yes you should if that is your secondary category
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Chiropractor is the primary category.
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) then you should make it h1 in your homepage cause primary category we put as a h1 on homepage
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) I have done that. I'm assuming I don't need any sort of content block like I would with the secondary categories then? I'm assuming that since "Chiropractor in CITY" is the Title and H1, Google sees the entire page as context towards that \(which is the same relationship the content blocks are towards the h2s\)
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) exactly right, the entire homepage IS the content block for the primary category so no separate section needed, everything on the page supports that H1 the same way content blocks support the H2s :\)
• stephan-franklyn-4962: Awesome man, thank you so much again. I really appreciate all the support!

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 195. Prompts for Google's AI Overview.
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/prompts-for-googles-ai-overview
Autor: jaime-ruiz-8971  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  19 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Dear community, do we have access to the prompts you use in this video [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=piS8h6LpNVo](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=piS8h6LpNVo) in AI SEO Mastery Pro for Google's AI Overview?

RESPUESTAS:
• joshua-albanese-3149: I copied it from the video. Hope this helps.
  
  You are an SEO strategist specializing in AI Overview optimization.
  
  ----- CONTEXT -----
  AI Overviews appear for queries where Google detects informational intent and can synthesize comprehensive answers from multiple sources. Position matters less than content quality and structure.
  
  ----- TASK -----
  Analyze the Google Search Console CSV to identify queries with the highest potential for AI Overview features, regardless of current ranking position.
  
  ----- FILTERING CRITERIA -----
  Include ALL queries that match ANY of these patterns:
  [ol:1][li]Question queries: how, what, why, when, where, who, which, can, does, is, are, should, will, could, would[li]Informational triggers: guide, tips, best, ways to, steps, process, tutorial, definition, meaning, difference, versus, vs, compare, examples, types of, benefits, pros and cons[li]Problem-solving: fix, solve, troubleshoot, prevent, avoid, improve[li]Educational: learn, understand, explain
  
  ----- AI OVERVIEW OPPORTUNITY SCORING -----
  Calculate opportunity score based on AI Overview likelihood:
  Intent Score \(0–3 points\)
  [ul][li]3 points: Direct question or clear informational intent[li]2 points: Comparison or list-based query[li]1 point: Contains informational keywords[li]0 points: Navigational or transactional
  Impression Volume Score \(0–3 points\)
  [ul][li]3 points: 500+ impressions[li]2 points: 100–499 impressions[li]1 point: 50–99 impressions[li]0 points: <50 impressions
  Competition Opportunity \(0–3 points\)
  [ul][li]3 points: Position 11–50 \(high potential, low competition\)[li]2 points: Position 4–10 \(already visible, needs optimization\)[li]1 point: Position 1–3 \(may already trigger AI Overview\)[li]0 points: Position 50+ \(too far to optimize quickly\)
  Query Complexity Bonus \(+1 point\)
  +1 if query has 4+ words \(indicates detailed intent\)
  Total AI Score = Intent + Volume + Competition + Complexity \(max 10\)
  
  ----- OUTPUT -----
  Return TWO MARKDOWN tables:
  Table 1: Top 20 AI Overview Opportunities
  Columns: Rank | Query | Position | Impressions | CTR | AI Score | Intent Type | Strategy
  
  Table 2: Hidden Gems \(Position 11–30\)
  Queries currently on pages 2–3 with high AI Overview potential
  Intent Types:
  [ul][li]Question \(who/what/when/where/why/how\)[li]Comparison \(X vs Y, difference between\)[li]Process \(how to, steps, guide\)[li]Definition \(what is, meaning of\)[li]List \(best, top, types of\)[li]Problem-solving \(fix, troubleshoot\)
  Strategy:
  [ul][li]"Create comprehensive guide" \(for new content\)[li]"Enhance with structured data" \(for positions 1–3\)[li]"Expand and restructure" \(for positions 4–10\)[li]"Build authoritative resource" \(for positions 11+\)[li]"Focus on list-type queries like games" \(for informational type content\)
  
  ----- INSIGHTS -----
  Provide THREE strategic recommendations:
  [ol:1][li]Immediate Opportunities \(High impressions + positions 4–20\)[li]Queries where better structure could trigger AI Overview[li]Include specific content recommendations[li]Content Investment Targets \(High impressions + positions 20–50\)[li]Queries worth creating comprehensive new content for[li]Estimate effort vs. potential impact[li]AI Overview Gaps \(Low CTR despite good position\)[li]Queries where current content isn’t meeting AI Overview criteria[li]Suggest specific improvements \(FAQ schema, bullet points, definitions\)
  
  ----- REMEMBER -----
  [ol:10][li]AI Overviews favor comprehensive, well-structured content over ranking position[li]Multiple pages can be featured in one AI Overview[li]Don’t include topics that are likely to be very competitive with high trust domains currently answering: football, video games, movies, etc.[li]Focus on answering user intent completely rather than just ranking higher
  
  • caleb-ulku-6571 ★: [@Joshua Albanese](obj://user/3295972c704348298cd99a31fdd6eea0) Thank you for taking the time to do this. =\)
    
    To everyone below: I am aware of the prompt access issue. I will work on it as soon as I can.
  • jaime-ruiz-8971: [@Joshua Albanese](obj://user/3295972c704348298cd99a31fdd6eea0) thank you very much!!!!
• ros-trujillo-7738: I was about to ask the same question. Thank you Joshua for taking the time grap that from the video. [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) please let us know when that lesseon will be in Skool.

## 196. Is it worthwhile/has anyone tested ensuring that hours on NAP listings are correct - even if NAP is correct?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/is-it-worthwhilehas-anyone-tested-ensuring-that-hours-on-nap-listings-are-correct-even-if-nap-is-correct
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I've seen on some directories that have exact match NAP where the hours are different.

I'm not sure if that's a rank factor in Google but I feel like it could be for AI.

Wondering if anyone has tested this?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: yes keeping hours consistent across directories is worth doing, google cross references business information from multiple sources and inconsistent hours can create trust signal conflicts especially as AI search systems like perplexity and chatgpt pull structured business data from multiple directories. its probably a minor ranking factor for traditional google maps but for AI search consistency matters more cause these systems are looking for reliable consistent entity data to confidently recommend a business. not the highest priority fix but worth updating when you're already in a directory correcting NAP :\)
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) nice okay that was exactly my hunch. Thank you.

## 198. Selecting services in Google puts them in all categories, even if I add it as a custom service.
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/selecting-services-in-google-puts-them-in-all-categories-even-if-i-add-it-as-a-custom-service
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  1 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
When I choose Oil Change as a service, it puts that service in all my categories on the GBP. I tried to add it as a custom service but "Oil Change" forces the selection, which then puts it under all categories. I changed it to "Oil Changes" to bypass it but wondering if this is new, if there's any other solutions, and if it will affect things very much by making it Oil Changes instead of Oil Change?

Mostly concerned for if this happens again but if there's not a simply way to get around it.

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: yeah google forces certain popular service names into all categories automatically and there isnt really a way around it, its a known GBP limitation. using "oil changes" instead of "oil change" is a totally fine and the slight variation wont meaningfully impact rankings cause google understands they are the same thing

## 199. Would you recommend creating a category or service page if they're already ranking well?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/would-you-recommend-creating-a-category-or-service-page-if-theyre-already-ranking-well
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  5 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I put Animal Chiropractor on the Home Page as an H2 and was thinking of making a service page for it, but they are already ranking #1 across the board throughout the entire area \(they are the only licensed animal chiro\)

Should I still make the page?

RESPUESTAS:
• stephan-franklyn-4962: Adding on to this - my Chiro clients search volume breakdown shows they're typically found because someone searched "chiropractor" keywords. Would you recommend focusing mostly on Chiropractor keywords then or the other related services which may be "sciatica treatment" "Back pain treatment" etc?
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: yes build the animal chiropractor dedicated page, core 30 teaches that your main GBP categories and services should each have their own dedicated page to properly mirror the GBP structure and topical relevance with google. since animal chiropractor is a secondary GBP category it absolutely should its own hub page, and having that page behind the ranking makes the position more authoritative and harder to displace over time. for keyword focus keep chiropractor as your primary money keyword cluster and build supporting service pages for sciatica back pain and neck pain as secondary topical pages that build relevance around the main chiropractic category and strengthen the overall entity signal :\)
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Animal chiro is not a secondary cat in the GBP, but a main service. So when you say hub page it's a little different than like a 2nd cat like Massage Therapist where I would then add the different MT services in the Massage Therapist hub page. However, I'm assuming I still make the page as if it were a category since it's a main service? \(Can you clarify that?\)
    
    For the Chiropractor primary cat, I'm assuming I use the home page as the category page then would I make a service page for chiropractic that I use as the hub for all the different conditions \(sciatica, back pain, neck pain, etc\)? That's what I'm a little confused about because I didn't want to have another page that's "Chiropractor in ABC Town."
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) ok since animal chiropractor is a main service not a secondary category it gets a regular service page not a full hub page, so just build it like any other service page with proper H1 title tag and content targeting animal chiropractor + city. for the primary category confusion you are right that the homepage IS the chiropractor category page so you dont make a separate chiropractor hub page. the conditions like sciatica back pain and neck pain each get their own individual service pages that link back up to the homepage directly since they fall under the primary category, not under a secondary category hub. so structure is homepage targets chiropractor, then sciatica page, back pain page, neck pain page etc page all link back to homepage as primary category services
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d)

═══════════════════════════════════════

Autor: jean-almonte-2448  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  5 comentarios

Looking for a tool recommendation for heat/ranking maps. Up to now have been using search atlas tool but it’s not great so looking for an alternative. Also for those of you who use lead snap, how does citations work? Can you pay onetime $20 on top of your GBP management membership and get all your citations or do you have to keep paying $20 a month to keep them? I’ve used bright local before and your just pay once. I think I asked about this here, but can’t find where the thread is.

RESPUESTAS:

• miles-hayat-9206 ★: hey [@Jean Almonte](obj://user/dbbc42147ce141d8ac752fb1194a835f) leadsnap is the recommended heatmap tool in this course, the citation building in leadsnap is typically a one time build per location not an ongoing monthly fee on top of your subscription, but check their current pricing directly cause it can vary by plan. that permanence is the main reason Caleb recommends leadsnap for long term client work :\)
• miles-harding-9169: [@Jean Almonte](obj://user/dbbc42147ce141d8ac752fb1194a835f) Same - I know lead snap is the go-to for this community, but I'm also new here and also using search atlas. 😅
• austin-delaney-1000: [https://seoutils.app/](https://seoutils.app/) works with your own Dataforseo API key.

## 205. Core 30 Structure Question for Multi-location in one metro-area.
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/core-30-structure-question-for-multi-location-in-one-metro-area
Autor: neal-hopkin-3283  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  3 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
The client has two locations in Chicago, which makes it a bit tricky. I think I have this correct, but I'd love some feedback before I create 50 pages. Do I have this structure correct?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: yeah your structure is correct, but for two real offices in same metro id use one homepage, one unique gbp/location page per office 'northbrook + westmont' and one shared service architecture on the main domain
  
  so homepage links to both location pages, each location page has unique nap map and local content, then your service pages are shared site-wide. those service pages link to the most relevant location page or your locations hub where those make sense. that keeps structure clean without duplicating pages unnecessarily :\)
• neal-hopkin-3283: Hu, that is interesting. The two offices are 35 miles apart, do you think that is too close to have separate service pages? Should the service pages be more like "Hospice in Chicago" and "Home Health in Chicago?"
  
  And what about on a larger scale when the client is in 20+ states. Should I have "Hospice in Dallas", Hospice in "Portland" and so on? I already have a general service page on the main site for each service listed. \(not metro/city specific\).
  
  Thanks [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d)
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Neal Hopkin](obj://user/b66f5c01d835485ba9ce7bd85d3904f5) 35 miles apart is fine for separate location pages, use the actual city names for each office location rather than trying to combine them. for the larger scale multi state situation yes absolutely build dedicated location pages for each major city you serve, those city specific pages are what help you rank in each individual market. keep your general service pages as the parent hub and let the city specific location pages sit underneath them linking back up, so hospice care main page links down to all the city variations and each city page links back up to the main hospice service page :\)

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 206. Lead Snap Rank Type
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/lead-snap-rank-type
Autor: stefan-girndt-6973  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  3 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I noticed that Lead Snap now offers a new type of rank map, similar as Local Dominator has done before.
Before, you got a rank map based on the Maps algorithm Now you can also get on based on Search.
What is recommended, [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) ?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: id suggest stick with google maps as your primary tracking metric since thats what Caleb teaches and what core 30 decisions are based on, specifically the 30-40% green threshold for shifting from topical to geographic expansion is measured on the maps heatmap. the local pack search type is useful secondary data cause it shows how you appear in regular google search results but the maps ranking is your north star for core 30 strategy. run both if you want the full picture but always make your strategic decisions based on the google maps heatmap :\)
  • stefan-girndt-6973: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) I was just wondering if Maps will still be the better algorithm to track when most people start their search on, well Search and get to see the rankings on the search map pack.
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Stefan Girndt](obj://user/39239ed694d8453090a39fc867a9a754) yeah still track google maps cause leadsnap geo tags simulate exactly how google determines map pack rankings regardless of whether the searcher started on search or maps, the algorithm is the same :\)

## 207. Should I add a brand new page for Massage Therapist GBP cat or add on to existing?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/should-i-add-a-brand-new-page-for-massage-therapist-gbp-cat-or-add-on-to-existing
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  5 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
GBP Secondary Cat is Massage Therapist and they also have a page for Massage as a service.

Should I go ahead and make a category page for Massage Therapist in City or just edit the existing page for Massage?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: id suggest create new dedicated category page for massage therapist in city, dont just edit the existing massage service page cause they serve different purposes in the core 30 hierarchy. the massage therapist category page sits above the massage service page and links down to it, so you need both. category page targets the GBP secondary category keyword, service page targets the specific service, and the category page internally links to the service page below it. two separate pages with clear parent child relationship is the proper core 30 structure :\)
• stephan-franklyn-4962: NIIICE OKAY I understand that completely!! Thank you for the detailed explanation! Now does it matter if I put the category pages in the Navigation Bar and then nest the massage service page under that?
  
  Also, for the massage service page, am I correct in thinking that since they are not ranking well at all for massage \(19.89 avg\), that it's safe to rewrite a lot of the page itself? Or I should still only add to it similar to a GBP LP that is already ranking?
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) bumping this - curious if it's important to put category pages and/or service pages in the nav bar?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) yes you should put the categories pages in nav and some important services only in nav :\)
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) got it!

## 208. Domain redirect question
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/domain-redirect-question
Autor: todd-shields-6982  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  3 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I am getting rid of our old domain \(name change\) , new one has been running a couple years now. but the old still has traffic. should i forward each page individually to the corresponding page on the new domain, or just do a full domain wildcard forward. ?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: in my understanding you do individual page to page 301 redirects not a wildcard domain redirect, page level redirects pass authority much more precisely to the matching content on the new domain which preserves your rankings better. a wildcard redirect just dumps everything to the homepage which wastes all that page level authority youve built up on the old domain. map each old URL to its corresponding new URL and set up individual 301s, especially for your highest traffic and most linked pages cause those are the ones worth preserving the most :\)
• todd-shields-6982: thats what i was thinking as well, but there was alot of competing info out there. cheers!
• miles-harding-9169: Hey [@Todd Shields](obj://user/9e7938fd6af5416592bc4d15dede1cd8)
  
  I'm pretty new here. This is actually my first post to be honest.
  
  I'm in an AI community, and one of the members answered this question for me a few months back with a detailed overview. Here's the 21 slides that will get you to a safe migration from start to finish.
  
  I kept this in my toolkit in case I needed to implement it for any clients in the future.

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 209. Simplified content workflow — one pipeline, better results
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/simplified-content-workflow-one-pipeline-better-results
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles  ·  4 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Quick update on the Core 30 AI Agent

We consolidated the content creation workflow. Instead of two separate flows \(Content Production for single articles and Bulk for multiple\), everything now runs through one unified tab called Content Generation

Why the change:
The single article flow and the bulk flow were running on different code paths behind the scenes. That meant fixes and improvements to one didn't always carry over to the other. Some of you ran into issues with the Content Production tab over the past week — outlines not advancing, articles regenerating the wrong content, steps not completing. Those bugs existed because the two pipelines had drifted apart

Now there's one pipeline. Whether you're generating 1 page or 100, it all goes through the same process with the same quality

What changed:
[ul][li]The "Content Production" tab is gone[li]The "Bulk" tab is now called "Content Generation"[li]To generate a single article, just add one item to the queue and run it[li]The Content Wizard still works the same way — it creates your content through the same pipeline automatically

What didn't change:
[ul][li]The generation process itself[li]Your client settings, prompts, and configurations[li]The Wizard flow[li]Publishing to WordPress[li]Everything on the Review & Export tab

If you were in the middle of something on the old Content Production tab, just recreate it as a single item in Content Generation. Same result, more reliable

Let me know if you have any questions

RESPUESTAS:
• mark-farrow-6630: Thank you Caleb, we will be trying it.
• reece-rainer-3922: Thank you Caleb!!!
• edwin-jean-gilles-7626: Thanks! I still see the content production tab and no content generation. I hard reset, logged off/on, and created another client from scratch but still it doesn't show up for me.
• john-hunt-7134: This is mind-blowing! Long way from my first attempts using Weebly. 🙂

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 210. Lovable or Not?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/lovable-or-not
Autor: william-schultz-1499  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  3 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Give me one good reason to NOT build a local home services business on lovable and host it at [https://lovablehtml.com/](https://lovablehtml.com/). Or better yet, tell me what to do instead, or what I should watch out for from an SEO/ranking perspective. This vibecoding solution at lovable is just too dang easy, and even with Core 30 content being published in html or in a word document and prompted into custome URL lovable project, I can stand up this site so much faster and it looks very professional. I don't mind WordPress with Elementor Pro editor, but this is just so much faster in my opinion.

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: the main reason to be cautious with lovable for competitive markets is the client side rendering issue, google has to execute javascript to see your full content which can cause indexing delays and lower core web vitals scores in the 60-70 range compared to wordpress server side rendering. for less competitive local markets lovable works fine and the speed advantage is real, but if your client is in a competitive market like a major city fighting against established businesses that technical disadvantage can be the difference between ranking and not. use lovable for less competitive markets and wordpress for the tough ones, knowing when to use each is the real skill :\)
• austin-delaney-1000: I use Netlify for hosting Lovable \(via Github\) which has prerendering built in. That means Googlebot etc see the full html page and content.
• caleb-ulku-6571 ★: there's some evidence of websites built with tools like lovable not ranking as well - even if you're using lovable html, it's very obvious to google that ai built the site

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 211. New to the community - building in Wordpress
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/new-to-the-community-building-in-wordpress
Autor: matthew-clark-6696  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hello, all. Really appreciate the opportunity to participate in this community! I'm just building my first Wordpress site \(or site of any kind\) since 2012. I see from the Lovable course how prompts can be used to produce a finished Core 30 product. Lovable is lower quality content than Claude, but still a good looking contractor site.

I have not yet been able to distill, though, how we do the same in Wordpress. I see the Core 30 tool will output Wordpress pages. If I'm building a Wordpress site from an old HTML version of the site, how do the the design elements and structure seen in the Lovable site take shape in a WordPress site, in Astra or Gutenberg, for example. Not through a prompt clearly. Using a builder like Breakdance, as I understand.

Still working through all the resources - just thought I'd ask for any guidance you all can offer on this gap in my know how. Thank you!

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: hey [@Matthew Clark](obj://user/3cd2c18ba5654c6fa5d2d96eee7c0dae) welcome to the community! 🙌 for wordpress the design and layout is handled through a page builder like elementor rather than through prompts, so you build the visual structure using the drag and drop builder then use the claude prompts to generate the actual content that goes into each page. think of it as two separate steps, page builder handles how it looks and claude handles what it says. the core 30 agent then connects directly to your wordpress site via wp application password and can publish pages automatically once you have the site structure set up, check the core 30 agent modules in the classroom cause caleb walks through exactly how to set this up
• matthew-clark-6696: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) thank you! That’s the confirmation that I needed. My research within and beyond the community has led me to Breakdance Pro as a lightweight alternative to Elementor. I’ll definitely be working my way through the Core 30 agent modules - looking forward to it!

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 212. Categories
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/categories
Autor: mary-bevard-4780  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  7 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I’m trying to understand how to properly handle secondary and tertiary GBP categories on my website. Is it necessary to explicitly mention and link to those categories \(like “portrait studio” or “photography service”\) on the homepage to support rankings?

From a user experience standpoint, that feels a bit unnatural and potentially confusing. Right now, my homepage links out to my core services like headshots, personal branding, and modern portraits, which are much clearer for clients.

What’s the best way to balance SEO signals with a clean, client-focused experience? Should secondary categories be worked in subtly, or is focusing on primary services enough?

RESPUESTAS:
• chiara-burns-7791: Yeah, for interlinking, the homepage should link to your secondary categories. You can also include a few of your key service pages \(what you're calling "tertiary categories"; also called child pages\).
  
  I agree that it can feel like a bit of a tug-of-war between speaking Google and speaking human. What's optimal for SEO does't always = what's optimal for the user experience on the website. Might have to get a bit creative with it.
  
  Example: Just bumped into this with a real estate client, where the industry standard is to have "buyers" and "sellers" as the two big hubs on real estate agent websites -- but those aren't equivalent to any Google categories. So what we've done with the homepage is put "buyers" and "sellers" as icon boxes towards the top of the homepage \(even though those are both service pages\), knowing that most people are going to click on one of those two options. With a clear CTA, most people are only going to scroll 25% of a homepage. Then we put the secondary categories as a grid of icon boxes further down the page.
  
  Something similar might work for you depending on how your site is structured. If you're able to share a visual of your sitemap, could chime in a bit better on that.
  
  Also worth nothing: In addition to interlinking, the hierarchy of homepage \(primary category\) --> parent page \(secondary category\) --> child pages \(services\) is also visible to Google through breadcrumbing and the schema.
  • mary-bevard-4780: [@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c) hi Chiara! Wow thank you for this. This is great. I appreciate you taking the time to write this all out. It really is hard for balance Google math and human being lol. 😝
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: yes you do need to mention and link to your secondary categories on the homepage but you dont have to make it feel forced or unnatural. just use them as H2 section headers with a brief natural sentence underneath linking to the category hub page, it can flow naturally as part of your services overview section. the key is google needs to see those secondary cat phrases on the homepage with internal links to their dedicated pages, but you control how naturally and elegantly you present them so it doesnt have to feel like keyword stuffing at all :\)
  • mary-bevard-4780: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) gotcha! Thank you so much for the response Miles. I really appreciate it
• caleb-ulku-6571 ★: yeah give claude or some other ai your actual secondary categories, tell it your'e building out a gbp landing page to establish more authority and relevance with google, and then tell it about your awkwardness "human language" concern - usually it does a pretty good job at balancing out the competing priorities
  • mary-bevard-4780: [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) thank you Caleb! Question does the h2 have to be the exact keyword. For example, if my secondary category is portrait studio, am I asking Claude to replace that term or just the layout and description to be more user friendly? I hope that makes sense. Tyia
  • mary-bevard-4780: [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) on a different note, besides having core 30 service pages, is it recommended to have location pages as well. For instance, ‘headshot photographer Huntington” and have dedicated pages just for this? I’m sure you answer this in your class but I’m still going through all the amazing info you have

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 213. Name change on a brand new GBP, risky or worth it?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/name-change-on-a-brand-new-gbp-risky-or-worth-it
Autor: karan-holland-4673  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  7 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Just got a cleaning client's GBP verified as a storefront after a painful process \(signage, multiple calls with Google, rebuilt from scratch\). Brand new profile, about 3 months in. Rank map is all 20s which is expected. Core 30 is 67% done but not many pages have been indexed by Google yet so still early days.

Client wants to change from "Velouré Property Collective" to "Velouré Cleaning Co." I'm not sure about it and wanted advice. I think long term it helps because it puts "Cleaning" in the business name which matches what people actually search for and targets the right audience.

Dont really want to keep triggering a re-verification or suspension on a profile we just fought to get verified.

Thoughts?

[@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c)

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: the name change makes sense long term since having cleaning in the business name does help with relevance signals, but timing is risky on a brand new profile still building trust. id wait until the rank map starts showing some green and the profile has established more authority before touching the name cause any changes now could trigger reverification and set you back further on an already all 20s map. give it another few weeks of stability with consistent posts reviews and core 30 pages getting indexed, then make the name change from a position of strength rather than risking the profile you fought so hard to verify
  • karan-holland-4673: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) appreciate it mate, that makes sense. Just checking though, if she really pushes for the name change would it actually be that hard to deal with? Or is it genuinely risky enough that I should hold firm on waiting
  • karan-holland-4673: Here’s the heatmap
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Karan Holland](obj://user/98eb442ef7f44e1bae025edeee895522) yeah exactly right, change the website citations socials and signage first so google sees the new name everywhere else BEFORE you touch the gbp name, that consistency helps prevent suspension flags
    
    and honestly looking at that heatmap being all 20s yeah theres real risk here cause google is more likely to scrutinize changes on new unestablished profiles vs ones that already have authority built up. if she really pushes for it make sure you got business documentation ready showing veloure cleaning co as a dba under hdtm group ltd just in case google asks for proof during reverification. but id still recommend waiting until you see some green on that map cause changing now means potentially losing weeks of progress if it triggers reverification, not worth it when your still building foundation
  • karan-holland-4673: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) legend, that's exactly what I needed to hear. Will hold firm on waiting, get the Core 30 finished and indexed, wait for some green on the map, then do the website, socials, citations and signage first before touching the GBP last. Will get the business docs sorted in the meantime showing Velouré Cleaning Co. as a trading name under HDTM Group Limited so we're ready if Google asks. Not worth risking weeks of progress for a name change we can do from a stronger position later. Appreciate the help mate, cheers
• caleb-ulku-6571 ★: what's the actual business name? legal / dba name? if the new name matches the actual legal name then you'll usually be able to submit an appeal if you have an issue with the name change
  
  that being said, as Miles suggested, I'd wait a month or so before making the change and use that time to bbuild up a trail supporting the new name: social profiles, citations, website, DBA, signage, etc.
  
  make the name change and only the name change
  
  make sure the client is ready for a re-verification or a suspension and have everything ready to deal with them
  • karan-holland-4673: [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) appreciate the advice mate! The legal name is actually HDTM Group Limited on the Companies Register, Velouré Property Collective is just the trading name. So the new name Velouré Cleaning Co. wouldn't match the legal name either way. So just to make sure I've got the order right, I finish installing all the Core 30 pages, update the website, socials, citations and signage to the new name first, and then the GBP name change is the very last thing I touch? That way Google sees the new name everywhere else before I change it on the profile? [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d)

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 214. GBP Primary Category & Title Tag Twist
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/gbp-primary-category-title-tag-twist
Autor: don-roberts-5493  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
[@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) / [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) - I know one of the basic Core 30 requirements is to have the GBP primary category name placed at the beginning of the landing page's title tag.

Most of my clients' competitors use the same primary category in their GBP listings, but it's one of those long, complicated-sounding names that almost no one would search for or even think of.

None of the competitors uses this complicated name in their title tags.

However, several do have variations of the actual services offered in the title.

I'm thinking of still keeping the long primary category name in the title tag, but prepending some of the main keywords that some competitors use at the beginning of their title tags.

Your thoughts?

Best, Don

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: the core 30 rule is to align title tag with primary GBP cat but you dont have to use it verbatim if it sounds unnatural the retheming approach works well here, take the essence of the primary category and combine it with the actual search terms people use which captures both the GBP alignment and the real search terms. the goal is google sees the connection between your GBP category and title tag while users see something they actually searched for :\)
  • don-roberts-5493: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Thank you for your helpful advice. I appreciate it.

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 215. GBP Images- Does geotagging actually do anything?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/gbp-images-does-geotagging-actually-do-anything
Autor: colm-fitzgerald-6027  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  3 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I read a lot of conflicting info on this. We encourage clients to turn on location on their devices and take photos \(why not?\). We also optimize images we use in posts with Adobe Bridge \(this is how I was taught to do Local SEO awhile back\). Does any of this matter? I know Google strips the data but does it read and interpret before stripping?

What about screenshots from a Yelp page for example? I've experimented with just uploading screenshots with no optimized filename, metadata, etc. and saw no difference in rankings.

I would love to get some overall feedback on this.

RESPUESTAS:
• caleb-ulku-6571 ★: we split testing geo tagging and saw improvements from doing it - it also takes < 5 minutes so we always do it
  
  yes, google strips the meta data but as you said, there's a lot of evidence that it reads it before stripping
  • colm-fitzgerald-6027: [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) what tools do you use for this?
  • mark-farrow-6630: [@Colm Fitzgerald](obj://user/6ccdc587cb4a426cbb014eec0dd805ed) Leadsnap

## 216. Flat URLs vs nested URLs under hub pages?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/flat-urls-vs-nested-urls-under-hub-pages
Autor: karan-holland-4673  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  3 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Got a question on URL structure.

Right now my service\(Core 30\) pages are flat like /commercial-cleaning-christchurch and the hub page is /cleaners-christchurch. So the service pages aren't nested under the hub \(Category Page\) in the URL, just connected through nav and internal links.

Should I be nesting them like /cleaners-christchurch/commercial-cleaning-christchurch so the hub comes first and the service page sits under it? Or does it not matter as long as the nav and linking is right?

[@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c)

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: flat URLs are totally fine and actually preferred in core 30, because it keeps pages closer to the root domain which passes more authority. the hierarchy is communicated through internal linking and navigation not through URL nesting, dont change what you have, the flat structure is correct :\)
  • karan-holland-4673: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Mateee your a legend! Always so clear appreciate it alot. Will start ripping into the pages just wanted to double check
  • anne-gerszewski-7814: [@Karan Holland](obj://user/98eb442ef7f44e1bae025edeee895522) I always had that question too. Thanks for the answer Miles.

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 217. Page Optimizer Pro
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/page-optimizer-pro
Autor: andrew-lin-7557  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  1 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I saw in one of the weekly huddle meetings Caleb mentioned he doesn't use Page Optimizer Pro \(POP\) to optimize the content anymore. I wonder why that is since in the training video he mentioned it would help the pages rank better.

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: hey [@Andrew Lin](obj://user/a049778615d640539f8149e823998da7) yeah Calebs methodology has evolved over time and the core 30 AI agent now handles content optimization in a way that makes POP less necessary for most local SEO work. the agent generates content that already follows the right keyword density and structure so adding POP on top becomes redundant for standard core 30 implementation. also Caleb keeps refining the course as he tests what works across hundreds of clients so tools and approaches get updated regularly, just apply what the current training teaches and dont overthink tools that may have been removed or replaced. the core fundamentals of GBP alignment topical authority and proper page structure are what consistently move rankings regardless of which specific tools are being used :\)

## 219. How?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/how
Autor: neal-hopkin-3283  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  5 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Does anyone have ideas on how a location like this \(Unity Hospice\) can rank so high on Google Maps? They have 55 reviews, but others have as much or more. Their rating is only 3.8, which is lower than many others. They only have 6 reviews this year and 3 of them are 1-star.

They for sure are not doing Core30. Their site gets little traffic. They are not doing any Facebook or Google ads. They show up 1-2 all over Google maps for "Chicago Hospice" and "Hospice Care in Chicago".

I'd really like to figure out what they are doing. Either they have some insider knowledge, or the data is just all wrong, but the LeadSnap support assures me the data is correct.

RESPUESTAS:
• chiara-burns-7791: These guys? [https://www.unityhospice.com/chicagoland/](https://www.unityhospice.com/chicagoland/)
  
  They're a big company. Between 200-500 employees. LinkedIn following and a legacy. I'd peek their backlink profile and that will probably answer your question ;\)
  
  And they may not have followed Core30 but they're not SEO dummies either. They did a good job structuring their site for a user experience + took the right approach to multiple locations as far as SEO is concerned. Keywords appear very naturally bc they aren't trying hard to optimize
  
  Hope that helps!
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: hey [@Neal Hopkin](obj://user/b66f5c01d835485ba9ce7bd85d3904f5) check their domain age citations and backlink profile cause sometimes an old established brand with years of citation buildup and strong domain authority can dominate rankings even without core 30 or good reviews
  
  unity hospice might have been around for 15-20 years with hundreds of citations built up over time plus legacy backlinks that newer competitors dont have, that historical trust signal can outweigh recent review velocity especially in healthcare niches where google prioritizes established medical entities. also check if theyre part of a larger hospital network cause corporate healthcare entities get massive domain authority boosts from their parent org
• neal-hopkin-3283: SEMrush show them with only 2.3K traffic and only 437 referring domains. Apollo shows they have 280 employees. Domain age 26 years.
  
  Meanwhile, my client has 22.2k traffic, 1.1k referring domains, and 1,200 employees. Domain age is fewer at 14 years, but still a long time.
  
  They are even beating VITAS, which has 153k traffic and 4k referring domains.
  Of course, Unity is not winning on all the keywords. But still, I just want to know how they are winning so I can win too. 😭 [@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c)
• caleb-ulku-6571 ★: this exact question is why we don't generally bother with a lot of competitive analysis
  
  keep in mind that one of the most important signals to google is how users engage with the website / business. when people call, do they answer? do people then call other businesses or move on with their life?
  
  we don't have access to any of that data
  
  it's not uncommon to see a gbp that's been ranking for 15 years because they provide solid user engagement signals - even gbp's not even linked to a website in compeittive areas
  • neal-hopkin-3283: [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) thanks for the insights. Sounds like that is what's happening here.

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 220. Does it matter if Google AI Overview is wrong when you're fact checking?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/does-it-matter-if-google-ai-overview-is-wrong-when-youre-fact-checking
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I was fact-checking an article that said the landmark was "located on the east flank of XYZ mountain." I asked in Google, and it said the landmark was on the west flank of the mountain. I looked on the maps and got directions \(ABC Landmark to XYZ Mountain\) and it took me to the trailhead, which the landmark is west of, but the landmark is actually on the east of XYZ mountain.

Maybe I'm overthinking it but would you make the content match what Google's AI says? or keep it 100% factual? Or how would you recommend handling that?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: yes sometimes it happens cause you know its google AI overviews are known to make mistakes and you dont want to publish wrong information on site just because google got it wrong. accurate content builds real E-E-A-T trust signals and if google AI is wrong it will eventually correct itself :\)
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) okay thank you!

## 222. Who here has had Good success with acquiring local sponsorship links?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/who-here-has-had-good-success-with-acquiring-local-sponsorship-links
Autor: brian-kiselstein-6058  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  5 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hi, I seem to be struggling with claude finding these opportunities. Local chambers are easy, but for local sponsorships, it definitely seems like its a challenge. If anyone is able to give a little more insight as to what their acquisition methodology was to get those, that would be awesome and much appreciated.

RESPUESTAS:
• chiara-burns-7791: I think Perplexity is a bit better suited for this type of task. It's a native RAG \(retrieval augmented generation\) engine, whereas Claude/Gemini/ChatGPT are LLMs that have RAG as a feature. So it's particularly powerful for research. I have it pull me a list of local publications and kids' sports teams. Then I review the magazines/blogs/papers to see if they do native "ads" with dofollow or opportunities to pitch to the editor. For the sports teams, it often lists sponsors or will include a pdf of their packages.
• brian-kiselstein-6058: Thank you Chiara
• jeremy-bengtson-6101: Use SEMrush to do a backlink analysis for your competitors. this will show you what links are valuble and which links are non follow. Not all sponsorships are created equal
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: yes local sponsorships work best when you find community events sports teams etc in your target city then reach out directly offering to sponsor in exchange for a website link!
  claude cant do professionally theres no public database of sponsorship opportunities, you gotta do manual outreach to little league teams local 5ks community festivals school events etc and ask if they need sponsors. also use ahrefs or semrush to check your competitors backlink profiles and see what local orgs they sponsor, then reach out to those same orgs or similar ones in your area
• colm-fitzgerald-6027: I've been researching this as well and found United Way to be one of these authoritative backlinks you can pay to be a donor/sponsor

═══════════════════════════════════════

Will that be a problem? It goes to his cell phone.

If that is a problem, but he wants those calls to go to his cell phone, what should I do?

• douglas-page-2404: Why is there a different number on the site? Is it a tracking number? If it's a number in GHL, you can forward calls to his cell phone.

## 224. Prospect with WP site > Move to GHL?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/prospect-with-wp-site-move-to-ghl
Autor: ashley-brown-1503  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  7 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I have a prospect that has a bare bones WP site that's been up for 2 years and isn't ranking for their keyword \(see map below\). I've read the threads about the impact on rankings when moving a site and was hoping for some input on moving to GHL for 2 reasons...

1: Cost. I am not very familiar with WP and would need to outsource the original dev and continued SEO content development for the site

2: No back-end lead tracking/CRM - with GHL the client would be able to login and see all website leads and track them. I would like to upsell on reviews or AI once completed and I think it will be easier to have everything under GHL to begin with.

Thoughts? How are most of you handling this?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Ashley Brown](obj://user/574a472af50745dba02be9b4bd45f3b2) looking at that rank map the site is ranking position 20 average which means fundamentals are broken, moving platforms wont fix that and might actually make it worse during migration
  
  the real issue is they got zero topical relevance built, platform doesnt matter for local seo rankings the content structure does. if your not comfortable with wordpress just optimize what they got, add the core 30 pages to their existing wp site and keep the domain authority they already. migrating to ghl means starting domain trust from zero + risk of losing any existing rankings during the move. the crm features are nice but dont sacrifice 2 years of domain age for that, you can always add separate crm tools that integrate with wordpress
  • brandon-bradford-7248: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Just a question to follow on with this as I'm facing a similar thing...
    
    If I can access their domain \(hosted on godaddy\) can I just transfer their wesbite from WP to GHL, keep the same domain and not have an issue with their ranking?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Brandon Bradford](obj://user/03bea7064a5e4ff6963f2dbfbc2314da) Yes, you can move the site from wp to ghl and keep the same domain
    Just expect a temporary ranking drop at first while Google re-crawls and the new site structure. As long as you keep the same URLs, content, titles, and redirects in place, it usually recovers
• douglas-page-2404: And to add to what Miles said, you can also incorporate the GHL features into a WP site giving you the best of both worlds.
  • ashley-brown-1503: [@Doug Page](obj://user/2667d9a03c934d32ad8bed6e5f7041e9) thank you, that’s exactly what I was thinking. If you have contacts or even someone on upwork that can help me design and update the site that’s reasonable $$ would appreciate a reccomendation!
  • douglas-page-2404: [@Ashley Brown](obj://user/574a472af50745dba02be9b4bd45f3b2) I do these types of things myself so have no one to refer. But this is a very easy job in most cases. It's just a simple embed like putting a google map on a page. You still need to create the asset like a chatbot, form, etc. There are very good instructions in the GHL support portal showing how to make the embed.
  • colm-fitzgerald-6027: [@Doug Page](obj://user/2667d9a03c934d32ad8bed6e5f7041e9) I was just about to comment the same. Beat me to it!

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 225. I made a mistake a couple months with categories - should I fix it or move on?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/i-made-a-mistake-a-couple-months-with-categories-should-i-fix-it-or-move-on
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  3 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I realized I probably put too many secondary categories for a client, some of which weren't really needed.

Now that I'm a little better at this, I would've not used some at all, and possibly different categories \(they currently have 7 secondary cats, only 2-3 would've been better and could add 1 more based on Category audit\)

They have improved in their ranking for the most part although the last couple weeks has been a little more stagnant or ranked a little worse - not majorly worse but noticeable.

Should I change those categories again? I haven't put them all on the GBP LP but a couple of them are. The one I would add is not on the GBP LP. Or should I just keep making content for the primary category \(Chiropractor, and they don't offer any other services besides Chiropractic\)

RESPUESTAS:
• stephan-franklyn-4962: Speaking of mistakes, I created their services using titles with locations \(Service 1 in Chicago, IL\) should I correct those as well?
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: given that rankings have been improving overall id be cautious about making big category changes right now, removing categories that have been live for months can cause a ranking drop while google reprocesses. if the stagnation is minor just keep building content for the primary chiropractor category and let it stabilize before touching categories. for the service titles with locations in them yes fix those cause google doesnt want location keywords stuffed into service names, just use plain service names and make those edits gradually one at a time not all at once
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) excellent thank you

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 228. Is it worth paying to claim or correct a directory listing?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/is-it-worth-paying-to-claim-or-correct-a-directory-listing
Autor: stephan-franklyn-4962  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  10 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Some of the directories I've seen like CitySquares, Tupalo, EZlocal and others will say we need to pay between $4.99 - $10.99 to claim the listing or even make an edit to the listing.

Is it worth paying for to edit or claim?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: id say only pay to claim if the directory has decent authority and the listing has wrong NAP information that could hurt you, also before paying check the authority/traffic and low DA random directories arent worth paying for, focus your money on the ones that actually matter for local trust signals
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) what would you consider for DR \(ahrefs\) to be for it to be worth it or not? For example, my client has a wrong address on CitySquares \(DR 74\), Tupalo \(72\), which are pretty high authority, but also BDIR.in \(30\) & findachiropractor.com/ \(8\). Also a non-english one \(katalog-firm.cybo.com\) but DR is 74.
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) yeah DR 74 is definitely worth paying to fix, wrong NAP on high authority directories actively hurts you and $5-10 to correct it is a no brainer at that domain rating. for BDIR at DR 30 its borderline, id fix it if its cheap or free, findachiropractor at DR 8 skip it not worth paying. for the non english cybo one at DR 74 yes fix it cause domain rating is domain rating regardless of language. just keep in mind DR is ahrefs metric not a google metric so use it as a rough guide not gospel, google cares more about relevance and trust than raw DR numbers but high DR local directories are generally worth maintaining
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d)
• stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Another question on this: If we need to create an account to edit/claim a listing, would you do that? And if so, do you use the client's email or one of your own emails?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) id suggest use the clients email to claim and own their listings, never use your own email cause if the client relationship ends you want them to retain full ownership of their profile without any complications
  • stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) got it - for this client specifically, they changed names & location \(ABC Tire Shop to ABC Auto Repair\), same phone. Would you recommend still getting in and claiming and changing or start a new one completely?
    
    I'm assuming claim & change but both the name & address being different is throwing me off
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Stephan Franklyn](obj://user/ca15cc9e219b405d85358caf9a76bcae) claim and update the existing ones rather than creating new ones, duplicate listings across directories hurt NAP consistency and can confuse google. since the phone number stayed the same use that to find and claim existing listings, then update the business name and address to match the new GBP details
• stephan-franklyn-4962: 
• stephan-franklyn-4962: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) and then should I do anything about a previous business who was in the location before my client but still has directories showing them there \(MapQuest, YP.com, etc\) ?

## 229. Primary services display
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/primary-services-display
Autor: andrew-lin-7557  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
On my homepage: I have 3 subcategories and 1 of the services from my primary category displayed.

I have 5 services in my primary category. Where should I put the other 4 services according to the core30 structure?

Do I display and link them from the nav bar?

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: the other 4 services from your primary category should be linked contextually within the homepage content, not necessarily all in the nav bar cause that gets cluttered fast. the nav bar should show your main secondary categories, then within each category page you list and link to all the child services underneath it. keep nav clean and let internal linking within page content do the heavy lifting for the remaining services :\)
• andrew-lin-7557: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) got it. thank you!

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 230. SEO Small town
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/seo-small-town
Autor: luca-budgen-4222  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  1 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hey, I have a client thats based in Thousand palms, CA. It's a VERY small town. He targets: Palm Desert, Palm Springs, La Quinta, Indio, Indian Wells, Rancho Mirage, Cathedral City, Coachella, Thermal, Bermuda Dunes, Beaumont, Banning.

For the GBP landing page / main area, should we target thousand palms? Or isnt that too small and should we go for a county or area like palm desert or coachella valley? I struggle to understand this

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: your homepage should match your actual gbp location, so homepage title needs to be "primary gbp category + thousand palms ca" matching what your gbp primary category is, the homepage h1 and first paragraph should also include thousand palms cause thats where the business is actually located and core 30 is all about that gbp to website alignment. then build separate dedicated location pages for all those other cities to rank in those bigger markets. own thousand palms as your base then expand geographically with city pages for the surrounding targets :\)

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 231. Warm up campaing
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/warm-up-campaing
Autor: lalita-sundari-2382  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  6 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hi everyone, we’re currently running a warm-up campaign for my company using ActiveCampaign. I’d love to get your recommendations on the best way to approach the warm-up process.
We have a database of around 6,000 contacts, and I’m wondering how many emails I should start sending per day, and how much I should increase that number daily.
I’d really appreciate any suggestions or insights you can share.

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: id suggest start slow at 50-100 emails per day in week 1 targeting your most engaged contacts first cause high open rates in the early days signal to email providers that your a legitimate sender. increase by 25-50% each week so week 2 do 150-200, week 3 do 300-400 and so on until you reach your full volume over 4-6 weeks. always prioritize sending to contacts who are most likely to open and engage first cause inbox placement in the warmup phase depends heavily on those early engagement signals
  • lalita-sundari-2382: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Thank you so much Miles!
  • chiara-burns-7791: [@Lalita Sundari](obj://user/6685057634644cd5b45935b0e6b6a045) Just to confirm -- this is NOT cold email right? [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) is bang on if you're talking about a warm database of people who already know you :-\)
  • lalita-sundari-2382: [@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c) yes! warm database
• terri-sassone-7507: [@Lalita Sundari](obj://user/6685057634644cd5b45935b0e6b6a045) Even before you start warming you need to validate that you domain is authenticated properly and verify the email addresses. If you've had your list for awhile, the addresses may not be valid. I use EmailListVerify. It's pretty inexpensive & does a good job of identifying the safe to send to emails. Don't start warming until you have cleaned your list & verified domain authentication with a domain authentication tool. Also, do you have a sending domain that is different than your primary domain? It's best to do that so you insulate your primary domain from any complaints or issues you have. I'd also highly recommend that you monitor your sending domain with Google Postmaster tools.
  • lalita-sundari-2382: [@Terri Sassone](obj://user/0463d578198544dcb46bdf70f063a321) awww thank you!!! I’ll take this in consideration!!

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 232. Error While publishing page on Wordpress from the Core 30 AI tool
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/error-while-publishing-page-on-wordpress-from-the-core-30-ai-tool
Autor: hemant-kumar-4980  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  12 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hi Caleb and team. I am getting this error while trying to publish the page on wordpress from the tool. I have disabled all the plugins to test. But still getting the same error.

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: that error means your hosting security settings are blocking the rest api connection from the core 30 agent, also can you tell me is this happening only for this specific domain or are you getting the same error across multiple sites? and when did this start happening, was it working before or first time trying to publish from the agent? that info will help troubleshoot if its a hosting issue or something else
  • hemant-kumar-4980: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) I have tried posting on another website on another hosting and it worked. I have also tried on site sites hosted on siteground and got same error. Looks like a siteground firewall issue. How do I resolve this??
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Hemant Kumar](obj://user/b25bcd50e36d46d08d274f46157fb901) you should reach out to your hosting support
  • hemant-kumar-4980: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) The support is asking for the IP the core 30 tool uses ?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Hemant Kumar](obj://user/b25bcd50e36d46d08d274f46157fb901) i think you should ask support for the user agent allow from their side cause core 30 agent is not based on ip address
  • hemant-kumar-4980: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) this is what he says
  • hemant-kumar-4980: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) This is what I got from siteground technical team " I can confirm that the issue was caused by a mod_security rule being triggered on the server. According to the logs, there were login attempts from an outdated user agent: Chrome version Chrome/95.0.4638.69.
    
    Please note that Chrome/95.0.4638.69 is not inherently malicious. However, because it is an older version \(released in October 28, 2021\), it can be flagged as potentially suspicious by security rules.
    
    We have now removed the block on the affected IP address. To avoid this issue in the future, we recommend reinstalling Google Chrome and ensuring it is updated to the latest available version. If you use any local automations, make sure they also work with an up-to-date user agent."
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Hemant Kumar](obj://user/b25bcd50e36d46d08d274f46157fb901) so what its working now by their end?
  • hemant-kumar-4980: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) its still not working
  • hemant-kumar-4980: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) Here is my conversation with support. I used chatgpt to help write my response to siteground.
    I have 50 plus websites on siteground and want to publish content on them but stuck with this issue.
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Hemant Kumar](obj://user/b25bcd50e36d46d08d274f46157fb901) ok for that you need to DM Caleb directly
• hemant-kumar-4980: Its the first time i am using the agent. havent tried on other websites. I have tried finding the security settings on siteground hosting account but cant find it.
