## 233. Wordpress vs html
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/wordpress-vs-html
Autor: vladislav-shklyar-6514  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  10 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hey I have a prospect client that has 8 dental office locations with a main website and 2 other stand alone websites for their locations that go by a different name. Their main website is directly competing with the stand alone ones and creating a ghost gbp completely suppressing their rankings to a soLV of 0. The local business schema is incorrect for 7 out of 8 locations. \(E.g. wrong geo coordinates, inconsistent NAP, multiple addresses for same location etc\). It’s a Wordpress website and I’m wondering would it be detrimental if I just scrap the Wordpress and create a clean html site for them with 301 redirects? Or is it worth just cleaning up the mess they have and keeping it on Wordpress?

RESPUESTAS:
• anne-gerszewski-7814: [@Vladislav Shklyar](obj://user/dbacdabea1d348e49a521030718ded4c) I am a big fan of Wordpress, specially with Elementor. Make sure they have a managed hosting with proper CDN and security to avoid getting hacked. If the website is ranking well in SERP and the primary GBP is ranking I would consider rebuilding it. There is a part in the course that talks about multiple locations and how to address it with internal landing pages. WP will give you the flexibility for it. Sounds like they didn't know what they were doing and they created a lot of cannibalization for themselves.
• vladislav-shklyar-6514: I will take a look at that, thank you for your input!
• chiara-burns-7791: You're saying the main website isn't ranking at all? If they aren't ranking, you've got nothing to lose by migrating. But if the main location IS ranking, and their satellite locations aren't, migrating may be more trouble than it's worth. Worth checking out the lesson on structuring for multiple locations if you haven't already: [https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=8c266b3b783246908270d88672365071](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=8c266b3b783246908270d88672365071)
• vladislav-shklyar-6514: Thank you for the link, I’ll check it out. Only 2 of the 8 locations rank.
  • chiara-burns-7791: [@Vladislav Shklyar](obj://user/dbacdabea1d348e49a521030718ded4c) Is one of 'em the big daddy? the mothership? the flagship/main location?
    
    the point in that video is that all the GBPs link to one website, but have their own designated landing page on that singular website. so if the main site is already ranking well, migrating may be more trouble than it's worth.
  • vladislav-shklyar-6514: [@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c) surprisingly it’s not. The one that ranks best is just their busiest location it’s open Saturdays and Sundays so they get a lot of patients on those days. And the local business schema for that location has 3 different addresses attached to it. The 2nd location that ranks doesn’t really have any competition close by and has no schema at all.
  • chiara-burns-7791: [@Vladislav Shklyar](obj://user/dbacdabea1d348e49a521030718ded4c) In that case, migrating the main website could make sense. Just to clarify: when you say it isn't ranking, do you mean at all, or do you mean in the top 3?
  • vladislav-shklyar-6514: [@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c) they rank right over the address. So one is a 2 right over their address, the other a 4 right over the address. The rest of the map is 20+ position.
  • chiara-burns-7791: [@Vladislav Shklyar](obj://user/dbacdabea1d348e49a521030718ded4c) Yeah, so you're starting from zero in either case. Meaning migrating won't much affect local SEO or traffic. So the question becomes whether Wordpress or html is best for the client. Wordpress is something the client can edit. If you build them an html site they're going to be dependent on you. I agree with Anne that Wordpress + Elementor is a good move.
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: id say dont scrap and rebuild, fix the existing wordpress site cause you have domain authority and existing rankings to preserve and starting fresh with a new HTML site means losing all of that plus managing 301 redirects for every URL. the issues you described like wrong schema coordinates inconsistent NAP and ghost GBP suppression are all fixable within wordpress without rebuilding, use rank math or whatever uyou using to fix the LocalBusiness schema for each location, clean up NAP consistency sitewide, and address the ghost GBP suppression through GBP management. rebuilding would take longer cause way more harm than good when the problems are fixable in the existing setup

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 234. Recategorizing an integrative clinic...is it worth the risk?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/recategorizing-an-integrative-clinicis-it-worth-the-risk
Autor: chiara-burns-7791  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  2 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
[ul][li]Client: Established integrative clinic, 20+ years in business; 400+ reviews

[ul][li]Location: Mid-size metro \(~100K city population, ~315K metro\) anchored by a dominant regional health system and a newer medical school / research institute; Healthcare is the #1 employer in the region by a wide margin.

CONTEXT

Prospect's current primary category is Medical Clinic. Problem is, they're not really a medical clinic in the way Google \(or searchers\) think of one, especially since we have a huge medicine scene here.

Client is integrative: chiropractic, physical therapy, MDs who lean naturopathic, hormone testing, bloodwork, whole body wellness, etc.

My first gut reaction was that the primary category should be Wellness Center, and the heatmaps are aligned with that \(see attached\). However, they're also lighting up entirely yellow for Alternative Medicine Practitioner without that even being a category on their GBP. To be fair, I don't know if "alternative medicine" really does them justice because it kind of carries a woo-woo connotation for a lot of people \(not judging, i appreciate alt med, it's just facts lol\).

And more importantly, I'm not sure what's going to bring them their highest LTV clients. e.g., I wouldn't put their primary category as chiro because it has a much lower LTV than other services \(which they typically sell as high-ticket, custom care packages\).

\(1\) Is recategorizing in this situation risky?
\(2\) Is recategorizing even worth it -- >>>***meaning is their significant potential for that change to actually bring them more clients?***<<<

I think the next steps might be exploring their GSC to see what people are actually coming to them for and if they're running ads, which services/hooks have had the best LTV:CAC ratio.

But wanted to run this one by the group bc it's tricky and would love insight from those who have successfully navigated similar situations :-\)

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: hey [@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c) recategorizing from medical clinic to wellness center makes sense based on what they actually do but with 400+ reviews and 20 years established yeah theres risk of temporary ranking drop during the transition
  
  the question is are they getting the wrong leads right now with medical clinic category, if people are showing up expecting traditional urgent care and leaving disappointed then wellness center is worth the switch even with temporary dip. check their gsc and ad data like you said to see what services actually convert to high ltv clients, if most conversions come from integrative wellness searches not medical clinic searches then switch makes business sense. alternative medicine practitioner might be too niche/woo woo like you said, wellness center is broader and more premium sounding which fits their high ticket packages better :\)
  • chiara-burns-7791: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) I don't think people are finding them at all for medical clinic -- the map is entirely 20+, but great point about asking them if folks every show up confused. I'll ask. thank you! And duly noted on the rest.

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 235. Improving click rate
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/improving-click-rate
Autor: aiden-hardy-8619  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  12 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
My impression numbers have been improving every week around 1k every 7 days

I want to learn how to convert impressions to clicks more often so we can start turning them into calls

I was thinking maybe adding the 10% off first time customers deal to the meta description to catch eye when people see it.

Anyone have any trips on improving click rate and driving more calls through the website

RESPUESTAS:
• miles-hayat-9206 ★: yes improving the title tag is the biggest lever for CTR, make sure it leads with the primary keyword and includes a compelling CTA cause those power words in the title tag catch the eye before anyone even reads the meta description. the 10% off idea in meta description is solid too, adding a specific offer or urgency like "available today" or "free quote" in the meta description gives people a reason to click you over the competitor ranked right next to you
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) title tag or meta description?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) yes meta title and descriptions
• aiden-hardy-8619: What would a compelling CTA look like?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) you can do research for compotators look what they are using
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) how would I know they actually work or not also if I change a CTA on the title tag should I rerun a site map ?
  • miles-hayat-9206 ★: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) honestly we cant guarantee what actaully works we keep testing and looking what user actually clicks and no you dont need to rerun sitemap you should only submit that url in GSC
• chiara-burns-7791: Another consideration would be which pages are getting most of those impressions. I have a client with low CTR across his site, but it's because almost all of the impressions are for blog pages where people are looking for a quick answer that they can glean from SERPs page, OR a specific address listing \(bc he's a real estate agent\) and they're probably looking for the Zillow. \(i.e., low intent searches\)
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c) so you’d say tune up on design? Or add FAQs perhaps
  • chiara-burns-7791: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) UI/UX and FAQ won’t make a difference for CTR; they can affect dwell/bounce rate. But no, I’m saying take a look in GSC to see which pages are getting the most impressions + evaluate those queries for search intent. Does that make sense?
  • aiden-hardy-8619: [@Chiara Burns](obj://user/ab2fa261ee364c63a6210081e91f077c) ah so look at the pages doing best and see what pages bring the most clicks and see why? And what the descriptions and tags are doing better on those pages
  • chiara-burns-7791: [@Aiden Hardy](obj://user/5dd21256b6c04d7fb2eccd7ef921b611) Yes ;\) Look at what's getting the most impressions. Where there's low CTR, consider the search intent behind the query. Sometimes there's nothing to be done, other times you'll get a clear idea of how to optimize the meta info to better draw people in.

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 238. Should we focus on what the client wants, or what would be most helpful?

I feel like we should get "Tire Shop" ranking well first as it's the lowest hanging fruit that will start bringing in more vehicles \(the goal at the end of the day\) and then start to work on the other keywords.

Am I right in this or would you recommend doing what the client wants? For added context, I'm a white label for another agency on this account so the end-client will most likely do whatever the agency recommends \(which is most likely whatever I would recommend\)

## 242. New Lesson: How to Size Your Rank Maps
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/new-lesson-how-to-size-your-rank-maps
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Just dropped a new lesson on one of the most common questions I receive - running rank maps that are either too big or too small

Here's the quick version:
Look at the number one player's top 3% score. That tells you everything

If they're at 100% top three, your map is too small or you need to drop the city name from the keyword

If they're down at 30-something percent, your map is too large and you need to tighten it up or add the city name back in

You're looking for that middle ground where the top player dominates their core area but naturally drops off toward the edges. That's when you know you've got a properly sized map

The lesson also covers how the city name in your keyword affects everything and how to decide whether to include it or not based on search intent

Go check it out and let me know if you have any questions below.

[https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=4d5790fa04a3435e858fe008467ab921](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=4d5790fa04a3435e858fe008467ab921)

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 247. Core 30 AI Agent Update: New Login System
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/core-30-ai-agent-update-new-login-system
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles + comentó  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Quick update on Core 30 AI Agent

We just rolled out a new login system. Instead of the old device-based activation, you'll now log in with a simple email code

How it works:
[ol:1][li]Enter the email associated with your Skool account[li]You'll receive a 6-digit code at that email[li]Enter the code and you're in

Your session stays active for 2 weeks, so you won't be entering codes every day. After that, you just verify again with a new code

Why the change?
The old system tied your login to a specific device/browser, which meant if you switched computers, cleared your cache, or used a different browser, you'd run into activation issues. This was the #1 support request we were getting. The new system lets you log in from anywhere — your desktop, laptop, phone, whatever — with zero friction

What you need to do:
Next time you open Core 30 AI Agent, you'll see the new login screen. Just enter your Skool email and check your inbox for the code. That's it

One heads up — check your spam/junk folder the first time. The emails come from [noreply@core30aiagent.com](mailto:noreply@core30aiagent.com). Once you mark it as "not junk," future codes will hit your inbox normally

If you run into any issues, drop a comment below and we'll get you sorted

═══════════════════════════════════════

"Electrician Canterbury" - Avg rank 19.83, Top 3% is 0%, sea of red 20s across the board

## 255. Big Win
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/big-win
Autor: hipster-marketing-3708  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Landed a New Client on a 15% Profit Deal \(Core 30 + GBP in Play\)
Just locked in a new client on a 15% profit share deal instead of a flat monthly… and honestly, this is the kind of setup I want more of.
No chasing retainers. “No more what did you do this month?” conversations. Just: we win when they win.

We’re going all in on:
[ul][li]GBP ranking \(Core 30 style\), expanding across multiple states. Building out 3 websites based on three seperate businesses[li]Tight service + location page structure[li]Cleaning up citations and reinforcing NAP consistency[li]And making sure the traffic actually converts once it hits
What I like about this model is it forces you to think differently. You’re not just trying to rank — you’re trying to drive real revenue.
It also makes the client way more bought in. When they know you’re tied to results, the relationship changes fast.
Curious if anyone else here is doing profit share deals vs flat monthly?
Feels like this could be a cheat code if you’re confident in your process.

═══════════════════════════════════════

• Primary: Dentist | Secondary: Cosmetic Dentist

Rank map data:
• "Dentist \[city name\]" \(vol: 4,400\) → Avg rank 14.19, 7th overall, 10.71% market share
• "Cosmetic Dentist near me" \(vol: 49,500, KD: 13\) → Avg rank 17.21, 3rd overall, 58.31% market share
• "Cosmetic Dentist \[city name\]" → Ranking 1st

The caveat: "Dentist near me" has 2.3M search volume, but they're buried on page 4, realistically not a winnable keyword anytime soon, need to master dentist "city name" first.

Given that they're dominating on the cosmetic side and the local cosmetic dentist volume is significantly higher \(~49k\) vs local dentist \[city name\] \(4.4k\), I'm considering flipping Cosmetic Dentist to primary.

Does this make since or should we just continue with dentist as the primary category?

PREGUNTA:
Topics covered:

[ul][li]Handling a GBP address change when moving locations \(citations, press releases, verification timing\)[li]When to shift from topical to geographical relevance[li]Using Google Trends to diagnose market-wide search volume drops[li]GBP category setup — choosing primary vs. secondary, organizing services[li]Why adding an address to a service-area business can dramatically improve rankings[li]Google's Ask Maps feature and what AI means for local search[li]Picking the right primary category when big-box competitors dominate your keyword

Next call is Tuesday April 14th.

Link: [https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/52e05dc7?md=3843104add784715a8238c0ac32b48e0](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/52e05dc7?md=3843104add784715a8238c0ac32b48e0)

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 269. Regus Offices
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/regus-offices
Autor: scott-desalvo-1096  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hello, all:

I understand that creating additional GBP's unless you have a physical permanent office that is staffed with permanent signage and a locked office violates Google's rules.

I'm also aware that there are businesses who do it, evidently without penalty.

I'm brainstorming here. I'm attempting to expand my geographic reach to areas slightly further away from my office. Multiple listed office locations on a webpage tend to impress people and show that the business is larger and more established.

I also am aware that there's something called "service area business".

With a $69 a month Regus co-work membership, I can list in my footer and about page and other prominent positions that I have satellite offices in all of the areas I'm going after. In fact, I can list half a dozen or a dozen essentially surrounding me.

I have to believe that this would increase my credibility with potential customers.

The membership allows you to use regional shared coworker spaces, but you can reserve an office or a conference room and pay for it if you choose. My thought is, I would keep meeting people at my legitimate office, but if somebody insisted that they meet me closer to home, we could meet at the Regus.

I wouldn't add any of this to my Google business profile, nor would I create new business profiles, listing these addresses.

Would I be creating a problem for myself when I'm listening additional addresses on my website but they don't appear in my address information for citations and webpage schema?

Or should I add the locations on my webpage and in my schema?

Please share your thoughts.

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 273. 3 New Lessons Dropped: AI Visibility Stack for Your Clients
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/3-new-lessons-dropped-ai-visibility-stack-for-your-clients
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Just added three new lessons that work together as a complete system for getting your clients recommended by AI systems — Google AI Overviews, Ask Maps, ChatGPT, all of it

Here's what's inside:

Lesson 1: Monthly Google News Strategy for Clients How we get every client mentioned in Google News every single month for $75 using "silly day" press releases. I'm including the AI prompts we use to generate the silly day list and write the releases. This one is dead simple to systematize and hand off to a VA
[https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=a1d424a207c741f0a34f495bcb82f902](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=a1d424a207c741f0a34f495bcb82f902)

Lesson 2: Leveraging Quality Mentions for AI System Recommendations How to use [PRASAP.com](http://PRASAP.com) to buy affordable mentions in quality regional and topical publications. These are the authority signals that AI systems use when deciding who to recommend. I walk through exactly how to search for placements by city and how to think about budget vs. premium tiers
[https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=a4c76d98c78b4f3b9c4eb87ce21b6b4c](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=a4c76d98c78b4f3b9c4eb87ce21b6b4c)

Lesson 3: Maximizing Local Business Reviews for AI Recommendations Two shifts that most people aren't making yet. First — Yelp reviews matter more than GBP reviews for AI systems \(yes, even Google's own AI\). Second — generic 5-star reviews are useless for AI recommendations. I show you exactly how to change your review request message so customers leave the kind of detail-rich reviews that AI systems actually match to queries
[https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=21dcf3ad0683465e8890496f6e8152b8](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=21dcf3ad0683465e8890496f6e8152b8)

How these connect: Press releases build entity recognition. Quality mentions build authority signals. Specific, diversified reviews build the recommendation layer. Run all three and you're building an AI visibility moat that compounds over time

Go watch them and let me know what questions you have.

═══════════════════════════════════════

URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/core-30-ai-agent-tool-update
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles + comentó  ·  0 comentarios

Finished a significant update of the Core 30 AI Agent Tool. I'd still say it's in beta, but it's working better now.

Here's the location: [https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/492fd5bc?md=a9ed9a90201a4299bd08202effdf9a0d](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/492fd5bc?md=a9ed9a90201a4299bd08202effdf9a0d)

Everyone in the Pro group as of 3/2 morning should have access. If you don't, let me know.

Biggest update is that it is now a web app. Login credentials are still stored locally, and they use a tokenized system to share with the browser for security.

Beyond that, the content production system is completely revamped. It now uses a 9 step process and the average AI scores are coming in at 20-30% after one shot.

YouTube uploads, image generation, etc all still work.

We're using this in our team to produce content for clients starting today.

Let me know what you guys think!

Next update will be a significant improvement to the geo relevance planning and production.

client does sewer line repair and drain cleaning. Not licensed plumbers, so we can’t use “Plumber” as primary. Problem is, every top-ranking competitor in their area for “sewer line repair” and “drain cleaning” has “Plumber” as their primary category.

[@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d)
I have a friend who works at a large roofing company that's interested in me doing some local SEO for them, and I wanted to get your input on a few things.

1. If their map grid were to turn mostly green, what kind of results could they realistically expect in terms of leads? How is this calculated? \(Market is Raleigh, ~500k population.\)

The top 3 competitors are ranking green everywhere so I know this is a good size rank map. It's the standard, 1 mile apart pins \(13x13\).

2. Roughly how long would you estimate it takes to turn a map grid mostly green in a market like this?

3. At your agency, what kind of timeline would you expect and do you offer any type of performance guarantee? How much would you charge? They are looking for some kind of guarantee, so I am looking for some advice.

4. For roofing companies, is it typically better to focus on ranking for "roofing contractor" or "roofing company"?

5. If you guys have any more advice or tips let me know, ive attached their current map vs competitors map.

I know there are a lot of variables and you don't have all the details, but I’d really appreciate your best estimates based on your experience.

## 286. Site Destruction Update
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/site-destruction-update
Autor: scott-desalvo-1096  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Some of you may recall that I had a mess of a site with bad content, bad structure yet ranking for certain valued terms, but mainly garbage.

I made the risky decision of blowing it up and rebuilding, since I needed to standardize on a different front end plug-in \(I chose Breakdance\) and completely different site structure.

My site now ranks for some pretty "money" keywords that I honestly didn't think I could rank for ever comparing back link profiles.

Here are some conclusions. I know that most of you here are professionals. So please let me know if some of my conclusions are incorrect or partially incorrect.

[ol:1][li]Your back link profile doesn't matter nearly as much as it did a year ago or two.  I have focused exclusively on very high-end and/or geographically significant back links.  My back link count is nowhere near as high as many other websites, but it hasn't stopped me from outranking them.[li]You can rank organically for a keyword you once coveted, and once you do, you realize that, even though you're in the top three, there are about 12 non organic listings above you, making the value of the keyword pretty low. SERP congestion is the term, I guess?[li]Long tail keywords that are very relevant, I have searched traffic, and indicate a purchase focus can still be worthwhile organically.[li]I'm very glad I rebuilt with a local focus and an AI focus, but I need more improvement.[li]I may need to open a new office for local, though I do not want the expense of staffing and rent. Competitiors list office share setups as their 'offices' and seem not to be penalized, but I will not run the risk of black hat.[li]I need to review the course again and improve my content for AI.  Other than a manual review, is there any commerical site or software which can do it for me?  Is there a Claude prompt in the course that I missed or should i subscribe toa s scraper to automate it? [li] Messing with the various AI's set me on a road to using AI for quite a bit.  I'm very grateful for that. I've got some pretty slick skills in Claude set up.

Thanks for reading and please correct me where my assumptions mmay be wrong or incomplete.

Lot of thanks and gratitude to Caleb for shepherding me through this.

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 296. Client Acquiring A Shop In A New Area - Website Question
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/client-acquiring-a-shop-in-a-new-area-website-question
Autor: mark-volzer-5910  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I have a client who is acquiring another shop in an area that my client does not currently serve. It's pretty much perfectly located between the two shops my client has now, and if we get it ranking it will cover the area between the two shops.
What is the best way to handle the competitor's existing website after the acquisition?
[ol:1][li]Should we keep it running under its existing domain \(ex. [competitor.com](http://compeitor.com)\)? [li]Should we get a new domain branded to the client's business name, but referencing the new location \(ex. clientsbusinessname-newlocation.com\) and add links from the client's website to this new location from the menu bar, footer ect?[li]Or should we add a new subsite for the new location to the client's existing site \(ex. clientsbusinessname.com/newlocation\)?
If we choose 2 or 3, how long should we wait to redirect everything to the new site/subsite?

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 297. My client's domain is being held hostage (sort of)

Autor: marla-bainbridge-5295  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

They are using Wix for the website , it isn't ranking, I hate Wix, so I am moving it to WordPress \(sorry Caleb :\).

I have created a new GoDaddy account in their name, there is domain available that is their business name with .co \(not .com\). I hope I can build a redirect from Wix to this new domain. But this means building all of the pages on the .co domain.

But I am not 100% convinced this is the right move. They have paid and it has been a month and I have not been able to make progress so I am looking for creative solutions.

Thoughts, suggestions, etc...

Autor: frank-apuzzo-6489  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:

## 326. Think I'm Lost
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/think-im-lost
Autor: joe-brown-3698  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
So, the Core 30 agent is making more sense to me now, but I'm still a little lost. Let me tell you what I experienced and you guys/girls can tell me what I'm missing:

1- I started watching videos and using Claude prompts to build on loveable. The site looked almost exactly like the Gary Plumber example. It had service landing pages, location landing pages, the GBP primary as the main page, etc. I defintely saw the value of wordpress, but seemed like Loveable was working

2- I started with WP and after some tinkering, got it working with the Core 30 agent. However, I'm seeing now that the core30 agent working on a Brand new Site, is not making the homepage based on teh primary category... it's not making a list of location pages based on info I key into Core 30 \(I actually didn't see a spot to put locations\), it's also not nesting the services under the categories like I saw on the Gary-Plumber example.

is the core 30 agent more of a content writing helper than a true website builder? and if so, do I just build the nested pages like we were trained to do like the loveable training video, but now I do it manually?

Sorry, not complaining :\). There's a ton of helpful stuff with the core 30 agent and the content is AMAZING! SO awesome. BUT, as someone with little to no website design, i'm struggling to know when I build part of the site and when the agent does. Maybe I got the cart before the horse duing teh core 30 agent without building out the structure of the GBP landing page?

\(EDIT: when i say there's not a location hub, I mean that building it from scratch its not nesting anything. I was able to make the hub per the tab in the agent app, but it's not doing what it was doing with claude and loveable.\)

## 335. where did "don't publish too many pages at once" come from?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/where-did-dont-publish-too-many-pages-at-once-come-from
Autor: chiara-burns-7791  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I went looking for the original Google source on this and honestly couldn't find one, so curious if anyone here has more context.

Background: I came up doing big enterprise builds and migrations where we'd publish hundreds of pages at a time without issue. Organic/traditional SEO. So since jumping to local SEO through a few Skool groups, I've been a little fuzzy on where the publishing volume concern comes from.

What I found in Google's actual docs is that crawl budget is really only a consideration for sites with 1M+ pages updating weekly, or 10K+ updating daily. Below that threshold they basically say keep your sitemap updated and you're fine.

The one volume-adjacent flag they do mention is low-value URLs \(duplicate content, thin pages, nav bloat\) but that's a quality issue, not a cadence one.

Google's crawl budget docs if helpful: [https://developers.google.com/search/docs/crawling-indexing/large-site-managing-crawl-budget](https://developers.google.com/search/docs/crawling-indexing/large-site-managing-crawl-budget)

Maybe there's a practical reason for pacing that I'm not accounting for...would love to hear how others are thinking about this?

═══════════════════════════════════════

Autor: andrew-drury-8632  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

Hi - I want to know if performing this local SEO work for a UK based \(drainage and service maintenance\) business that is targeting work in it's main city \(30 miles away\) and broader "county" areas \(approx 2,000 sq miles rather than where they are located is worth doing or not for them?

The business is located in a small village \(where their head office and vehicles are kept\) which is outside their closest town \(10 miles away\) and city \(30 miles away\).

The town \(10 miles away\) & village they are located would not be somewhere they are targeting but the city \(30 miles away\) and surrounding county areas would be.

Can I get some advice on whether it's still worth performing this SEO work for them?

I understand getting a separate office location for another GBP in the city could be a good option but I know the level of competition in the city would be high.

## 344. LeadSnap Heat Maps

Autor: gary-magill-8831  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I have been going back and forth trying to figure this out. when using leadsnap for heatmaps \(rankmaps\) i used to use the keyword + \[city\]. i have tried also just using keyword and it obviously gives different results. I have asked Claude and done some research and just wanted to clarify my findings. Findings state that you should NOT be using the city modifier when doing heatmaps in LeadSnap because people searching for said keyword are not typing in the location they are just typing in the keyword/service. I just wanted to get some clarification on this because i have asked before and i was told to use the city modifier but I have been doing some digging and it seems I should NOT be using it anymore... ## 353. GBP Hours as a Ranking Factor
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/gbp-hours-as-a-ranking-factor
Autor: joseph-joubert-5384  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Been seeing and hearing a lot about open hours affecting search rankings when someone searches outside your posted business hours and want to hear how others are advising clients on this.

The scenario: client has a physical office staffed 8:30–5:30, a live call center running 6 AM–10 PM, and a website with 24/7 self-service, account management, and AI chat.

The question is where to draw the line on main GBP hours without getting into territory a competitor or user edit could walk back.

My instinct is to set main hours to the call center window \(6–10\) since a live human is reachable, and layer the rest into More Hours — online service hours at 24/7, any access hours separately. Covers the bases without the exposure of claiming 24/7 when overnight is purely automated.

Has anyone actually tested hour configurations against local pack rankings? And for businesses with this kind of tiered availability model, how are you framing “open” to clients — staffed hours only, or any meaningful customer interaction counts?

═══════════════════════════════════════

These clients do not have an office locally as they operate service-based businesses \(e.g. painting\).

## 364. New Lesson: The First 60 Days — Our Exact Client Onboarding System
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/new-lesson-the-first-60-days-our-exact-client-onboarding-system
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Just dropped a new lesson in the classroom

This is the exact system we run at the agency for every new client. Not a framework, not a theory — the actual internal tracker we use, with all 66 tasks, timing, scripts, and notes

It covers everything from the minute the sales call ends to the Day 60 retention check-in:
→ How we front-load communication in Week 1 to kill buyer's remorse before it starts
→ How we run GBP audits, landing page audits, and content gap analysis in parallel \(not sequentially\) → The Meeting 1 structure that gets approval on all changes in a single call
→ The tech fix + content sprint running simultaneously in Days 7–30
→ Our full Loom and email cadence so you never go dark on a client
→ What we do at Day 60 if results aren't where they should be

There's a video walkthrough plus the downloadable tracker itself you can grab and adapt for your agency

This is the single biggest reason we retain clients past month 3. Go check it out and let me know if you have questions — happy to go deeper on any piece of it.

Link ->[https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=ffcdad1e7132415db7ae05c55f92b4e2](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=ffcdad1e7132415db7ae05c55f92b4e2)

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 365. Joining Multiple Chamber of Commerce
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/joining-multiple-chamber-of-commerce
Autor: andrew-lin-7557  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
My business is a single-location acupuncture clinic serving a local client base. I’m trying to decide whether it’s worth joining multiple Chambers of Commerce, or if I should just focus on the one in my city.
In the course, Caleb mentioned that joining multiple Chambers within a 70–80 mile radius can be powerful for backlinks and local authority.
However, most of my patients are local, and it’s unlikely that people would regularly travel ~30 km from another city for ongoing treatments.
So my question is:
[ul][li]Should I just only join my  local Chamber of Commerce for relevance?[li]Or is there still a strategic SEO or networking advantage to joining multiple Chambers within that radius, even if those areas aren’t part of my core patient base?

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 366. Two Different Companies, Same GBP Part 2
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/two-different-companies-same-gbp-part-2
Autor: brian-kiselstein-6058  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
So, further update. We're about 3 months into this campaign, and there's no real movement in the heat maps \(looks the same as 3 months ago\) Sourced PBNs, finished the core 30, Chamber of commerce liink. I'm leaning heavily towards the 'Possum Filter suppression' theory. Even though his Uncles GBP isn't visible in search, I still think its dragging his rankings down. I spoke to my client and he said it would be an "Uncomfortable" conversation to have with his stubborn uncle \(89 years old\). I attempted the "moved location" approach \(they have a second ACTIVE GBP just a few towns over\), and my edit was denied. Trying to come up with another way to get his uncle's inactive GBP off that address \(one 8-year-old photo of a dog on a beach and 1 bad review\).

Just trying to figure out how to get this nearly 'dead' profile off that address. My concern is it's a residential home, and I know Google doesn't like it when businesses operate out of their home. So its manually reviewed by a real person at google, they might suspend both profiles? Any thoughts on this, I know its a weird one. [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d)

## 381. Question for GBP Structure
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/question-for-gbp-structure
Autor: andrew-lin-7557  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
[@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) I’m currently optimizing my acupuncture clinic, which operates as a general acupuncture practice with three practitioners. In addition, I offer a specialized practice in treating eye-related conditions \(e.g., macular degeneration, glaucoma\).
Prior to joining this group, I created a separate website for the eye-focused practice to improve user experience, maintain distinct branding, and provide clearer marketing positioning. However, I have not yet created a Google Business Profile \(GBP\) for this eye practice.

My current plan is:
[ul][li]Main clinic website → Primary category:  Acupuncture Clinic[li]Eye practice website → Primary category: Eye Care Center[li]Both operating from the same address, I can use a callrail service for the second GBP

From what I understand, using different primary categories may allow both listings to coexist at the same location, particularly in healthcare.

My question is:

Would it be better to:
[ul][li]Include “Eye Care Center” as a secondary category on the main clinic’s GBP and integrate eye services into the main entity for more exposure
or
[ul][li]Keep the eye practice completely separate \(separate website, future GBP\) and avoid including any eye-related categories on the main clinic’s GBP?

I’m trying to determine which approach is stronger from a local SEO and ranking perspective, without risking dilution or conflicts between listings.

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 384. Heatmap oddity I'm trying to figure out
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/heatmap-oddity-im-trying-to-figure-out
Autor: chiara-burns-7791  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
[ol:1][li]Running heatmaps on a new client and seeing something counterintuitive: "realtor" = heatmap green as a Christmas tree, while "real estate agent" is barely registering. Despite real estate agent being in the H1/title tag of homepage and throughout the whole architecture.

My best guess: their blog heavily uses "realtor."

Which leads to a second question:

2.Their blogs aren't housed under a /blog/ subfolder — they live at the root level, same as the service pages:
[ul][li][domain.com/article-title](http://domain.com/article-title) ← blog post[li][domain.com/service-page](http://domain.com/service-page) ← service page

Structurally the content is different, but the URL gives Google no clear signal that one is editorial and one is transactional.

Two concerns with this:
[ol:1][li]Does the flat URL structure affect how Google weights topical authority across the site?[li]Is there any risk that blog content \(which skews toward "realtor"\) is diluting the keyword focus of the service pages?

## 392. GBP Services Auto-Duplicating Across Categories
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/gbp-services-auto-duplicating-across-categories
Autor: tommy-west-9370  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Setting up a GBP for a painting company. Primary category is Painter with three secondaries: Drywall Contractor, Pressure Washing Service, and Contractor.

I originally had Painting as a secondary category, too, but ran into an issue. When I added services under Painter \(primary\) and Painting \(secondary\), Google kept merging them together. If I tried to delete a service from just the Painting category, it would delete it from all categories. No way to separate them.

So I dropped Painting as a secondary entirely and moved all those services under Painter. That fixed the duplication issue since Drywall Contractor, Pressure Washing, and Contractor are semantically distinct enough that Google isn't cross-pollinating services between them.

For categories that are semantically close \(like Painter and Painting\), is it better to just pick one and consolidate all services under it?

And does having the same service show up under two categories actually dilute your ranking signals, or does Google just treat it as one signal regardless?

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 403. Two different companies, Same GBP?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/two-different-companies-same-gbp
Autor: brian-kiselstein-6058  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
OK, so I just found something interesting with one of my clients. It looks like there's an old associate of his who left the business \(good term, bad terms 🤷‍♂️\), but they're still using the same address on their GBP, just with a different phone number.

Now, my client IS showing up in the map pack, he's sitting around #5-#6 via incognito search \(still waiting on cached data from leadsnap/local dominator\) on the days he's open, but the old associate's listing doesn't show up at all.

MY CONCERN is whether that shared address is quietly dragging on my client's rankings in the background. Like, should I be worried about a dormant GBP pointing to the same address, even if it's not actively showing up in results? And should I be pushing to get them to change their address?

Because I'm pretty sure it's my client's home, I'll confirm with him next time we talk, but this feels like something that should be addressed. Thanks guys!

Autor: andrew-lin-7557  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
[@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c)

I'm currently trying to implement the Core30 strategy for my own business, which is an acupuncture and Chinese medicine clinic.
Using the categories suggested in Google Business Profile, I've identified the following as the most relevant:

Primary:
[ul][li]Acupuncture Clinic
Secondary:
[ul][li]Acupuncturist[li]Chinese Medicine Clinic[li]Pain Control Clinic[li]Ophthalmology Clinic[li]
I included Ophthalmology Clinic because part of my specialty is treating degenerative eye conditions, which is not common among most acupuncture clinics.

My Questions:

[ol:1][li]"Acupuncture Clinic" vs. "Acupuncturist" - These two categories seem very similar to me, and I'm not sure how Google differentiates them. When building the Core30 structure, would it make sense to treat them as separate categories, or should I focus only on “Acupuncture Clinic” and disregard “Acupuncturist”?[li]Services: When gathering services from GMB everywhere under these categories, many of them appear to be conditions treated rather than actual services offered. For example:
[ul][li]Condition-based: back pain acupuncture, anxiety acupuncture, insomnia acupuncture, chronic fatigue acupuncture[li]Actual services: IMS, cupping, electroacupuncture, cosmetic acupuncture, trigger point acupuncture

How would you recommend organizing these in the core30 strategy?

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 424. Core30 Question: Are Blogs Needed or Can AI Builders + Service Pages Work?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/core30-question-are-blogs-needed-or-can-ai-builders-service-pages-work
Autor: jean-almonte-2448  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Quick question for those getting good results with the Core30 method.

My understanding is that for local service businesses the main ranking drivers are:
• well-structured services and service area pages
• strong GBP optimization
• citations / local links
• good internal linking

And that blogs aren’t really necessary to rank if those pieces are in place.

I’m asking because I’m considering moving away from Wordpress and using faster AI site builders \(Claude-generated sites, etc.\) to speed up fulfillment.

For those already ranking sites with Core30:

Are you consistently getting results without blogs?
And has anyone here successfully done this outside of WordPress or any other CMS?

## 426. New quality of life updates to the Core30 AI Agent tool
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/new-quality-of-life-updates-to-the-core30-ai-agent-tool
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Just pushed a few quality-of-life improvements based on your feedback:

1. Auto-Fill Client Settings: Enter your client's website URL, hit the new Auto-Fill button, and the tool will crawl the site and populate your client settings for you \(business name, address, phone, about section, USPs, credentials, tone, and more\). You can also paste About page content for even better results. Review everything before saving - it's a starting point, not a final draft.

2. Export HTML from the Publish Tab: You can now select one or multiple pages in the Publish tab and export them as HTML files directly. Single page downloads as an .html file, multiple pages download as a .zip. Same HTML output you could get from the Bulk tab, now available right where you're reviewing your content.

3. Gemini Image Watermark Removal: Images generated through Gemini no longer have the visible watermark. The tool now automatically cleans this up before the image is saved, so your content is publish-ready without any extra editing.

All three are live now. Let me know if you run into anything.

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 439. GBP Support Forms Every Local SEO Should Know
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/gbp-support-forms-every-local-seo-should-know
Autor: miles-hayat-9206  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
For anyone dealing with Google Business Profile \(GBP\) issues, here are the main working support forms 👇
1️⃣ Reviews \(report or manage reviews\)[https://support.google.com/business/workflow/9945796](https://support.google.com/business/workflow/9945796)

2️⃣ Verification issues[https://](https://support.google.com/business/workflow/12825603)[support.google.com/business/workflow/12825603](http://support.google.com/business/workflow/12825603)

3️⃣ Suspensions & appeals[https://](https://support.google.com/business/workflow/13569690)[support.google.com/business/workflow/13569690](http://support.google.com/business/workflow/13569690)

4️⃣ Edit publishing issues \(name, category, address, etc.\)[https://support.google.com/business/workflow/15989794](https://support.google.com/business/workflow/15989794)

5️⃣ GetHelp \(general support – use this when none of the above apply\)[https://support.google.com/business/gethelp](https://support.google.com/business/gethelp)

⚠️ Important: If your issue falls under reviews, verification, or suspension, GetHelp will redirect you to the correct tool automatically. That’s how Google controls the support flow.

Other useful forms \(less known but very practical\):

• Business Redressal \(spam, fake listings, keyword stuffing\)[https://support.google.com/business/contact/business_redressal_form](https://support.google.com/business/contact/business_redressal_form)

• Report review manipulation or incentives[https://](https://support.google.com/maps/contact/14718793)[support.google.com/maps/contact/14718793](http://support.google.com/maps/contact/14718793)

• Merchant extortion \(review blackmail\)[https://support.google.com/business/contact/merchant_extortion](https://support.google.com/business/contact/merchant_extortion)

• Third-party abuse \(agencies, fake guarantees, unauthorized access\)[https://support.google.com/business/contact/gmb_3p_complaints](https://support.google.com/business/contact/gmb_3p_complaints)

Save these you’ll probably need them sooner or later when working with GBPs.

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 452. GBP Profile Completeness changed after adding Services
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/gbp-profile-completeness-changed-after-adding-services
Autor: anil-agrawal-2726  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Yesterday I added \(under primary category\) a few remaining services out of the total 13 services that the business offers and made a few edits in the services under the 2nd secondary category. Today, I noticed that the profile completeness icon changed from all closed green color to ~90% closed orange/yellow. What! I clicked on customize profile link and went through the steps and there was nothing it prompted for me to do except sign up for ad credits and then another step to sign up for Google Business account blah blah which of course I skipped both.

Any ideas what might have caused it and how to get it "fixed"? The profile had been showing closed green for several weeks since we initially set up the GBP.

Note: We are missing the Cover Photo as we don't have that yet but that had been the case all along.

I have a prospect with 3 roofing locations currently under a major franchise. They are breaking away to go independent and rebrand entirely.

The secretary mentioned their biggest fear is losing their digital equity, specifically their GBPs and years of customer reviews.

I’m in the process tryna get a call with the owners and I want to make sure I’m not missing any 'landmines.' I offer Local SEO and GBP management, but I’m looking for the group's perspective on the overall approach:

[ol:1][li]The Pitch: If you were sitting across from these owners, how would you frame the 'Franchise-to-Independent' transition? Is it a 'Risk Mitigation' play, a 'Fresh Start' play, or something else?

2.The Technical Strategy: For 3 locations, are you leaning toward a subfolder structure on a new domain, or something else to ensure they don't lose their current rankings?

3.The GBP Rebrand : How are you guys handling the rebrand of the listings to ensure they don't get suspended or lose their review history?

4.Pricing: How would you structure this deal? A heavy setup fee for the migration + a bundled retainer for 3 locations? Or do you keep it simple?

I’m keeping my strategy open right now to make sure I build the best possible solution for them. If this was your prospect, how would you approach the 'Big Picture' to close the deal?

[@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) Would love to get your input on this.

## 457. Claude Code saves the day ...
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/claude-code-saves-the-day
Autor: james-hurst  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Google sent me one of those emails today. "New reason preventing your pages from being indexed." Something about "duplicate without user-selected canonical."

I used to dread these. Not because I can't figure it out, but because it means an hour of Stack Overflow tabs, cross-referencing docs, and hoping I edit the right file.

So instead I screenshotted the email and pasted it into Claude Code.

"Fix this."

Five minutes later: canonical tags fixed, sitemap.xml created, robots.txt created, everything deployed to production. Google was already crawling the new sitemap before I knew it.

Then I asked it to connect to the Google Search Console API so I could check indexing status without logging into the web UI. Ten minutes later that was done too.

Old way: get the email, panic, open 12 tabs, read contradicting advice, make a change, hope it works, check back in two weeks.

New way: paste a screenshot. Done.

═══════════════════════════════════════

A few questions on the correct implementation:

1. Landmark keyword format: Is the right approach to target something like "glazier near Newport Station car park" \(see attached image — that's the landmark I found for Newport\)? Or is the format different?
2. City modifier: Do we still include the main city name \(in this case, Melbourne\) in the keyword, or does the landmark replace it entirely? In this example, is this the primary keyword: Glazier near newport station car park in Melbourne?
3. GBP driving directions. For the landmark itself — do we add driving directions from that landmark to our GBP listing, or just list the address of the landmark?
4. Any other caveats? Happy to take any tips on getting this right before deploying at scale. I'm following the Claude prompts from the academy, so I just want to confirm the approach is correct before rolling it out across multiple suburbs.
Thx!

## 471. Google Reviews, ranking factor or conversion factor?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/google-reviews-ranking-factor-or-conversion-factor
Autor: karan-holland-4673  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Genuine question for the group. I hear a lot of people say Google reviews directly help your rankings in the map pack. But is it actually that simple or is it more that once you're in the top 3, reviews become the deciding factor for the customer?
Like if two businesses are sitting side by side in the map pack, one has 7 reviews and the other has 80+, the customer is going with the one that has the reputation every time. So is the real power of reviews less about climbing the rankings and more about winning the click once you're there?
Or does it genuinely move the needle on rankings too?
I'm building out a review system for my clients at the moment because most of them are struggling to consistently get reviews after jobs. Want to make sure I'm positioning it the right way, whether that's "this will help you rank higher" or "this is what wins the job once people find you" or both.
Keen to hear what you guys have seen. [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c)

## 485. Intro

Autor: brandon-bradford-7248  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hey all, thought I'd do a quick intro post. Im from the UK and in 2024 I scaled a content agency to around $11k/month things were great, had some pretty hefty clients and we even scaled my business partners page to around 70k followers on IG...

Stupidly we decided to pivot from agency to coaching program and it wrecked the business... so here we go again haha

I'm great at ads & organic \(used to script, ideate, shot design for some of the biggest pages on IG\) but this is a bit of a different ball park for me.

Spent January researching by spamming cold calls to local businesses to work out what their pain points are, got sick in February and now we're here.

If anyone wants to connect drop me a message.

I was in Caleb's lower ticket skool but upgraded.

Also just want to say this skool has been super valuable so far, thank you Caleb for creating something truly incredible!

## 491. Core 30 AI Agent New Feature: Supp Planner — Automated Supporting Content Research
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/core-30-ai-agent-new-feature-supp-planner-automated-supporting-content-research
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Excited for this one! Added a new tab for "Supp Planner" \(sorry for the horrible name\)

The Workflow
Step 1: Enter a service or category. Something like "water heater repair" or "sewer line replacement" or "frozen pipes." That's your only input

Step 2: The tool goes hunting. It pulls real questions people are actually asking from three sources:
[ul][li]People Also Ask [li]Reddit threads[li]Local forums and Q&A sites 

All three sources run through the DataForSEO API you're already connected to. No new accounts or API keys needed.

Step 3: Deduplication and rephrasing. The tool removes near-duplicates across all three sources, then rephrases every question. The rephrased version keeps the same search intent and meaning but isn't a word-for-word copy of what Google shows. You're targeting the topic, not copying the exact PAA string.

Step 4: Auto-scoring. Every question gets scored 1-100 based on three factors:
[ul][li]Relevance to your target service — "should I repair or replace my water heater" scores higher than "what temperature should my thermostat be"[li]Search intent — questions that imply someone needs professional help score higher than pure curiosity[li]Uniqueness from content you've already generated or published — no duplicating topics you've already covered

Step 5: You pick the winners. Results show up as a ranked list with checkboxes. Each question shows the score, the rephrased version \(which you can edit inline\), the original question for reference, and a tag showing where it came from \(PAA, Reddit, or Forum\). Check the ones you want. Ignore the rest.

Step 6: One click to the bulk queue. Hit "Send to Bulk Queue" and your selected questions drop straight into the bulk content generator as supporting content items. Switch to the Bulk Content Queue tab and run them through the pipeline like any other batch.

That's it. Service term in, scored question list out, selected topics pushed to generation. The research that used to take an afternoon now takes about two minutes.

A Few Details
[ul][li]20 questions per run — enough to fill a solid supporting content cluster without overwhelming you[li]Run history saves for 60 days — you can come back to previous research runs without re-running them[li]Rephrased questions are editable — if you want to tweak the angle before sending to the queue, just click and edit inline[li]Source tags on every question — so you can see whether a topic came from Google PAA, Reddit, or a forum. Useful for understanding where your audience hangs out

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 498. Has anyone dealt with duplicate franchise business names causing citation/GBP conflicts?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/has-anyone-dealt-with-duplicate-franchise-business-names-causing-citationgbp-conflicts
Autor: mason-ross-6015  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Working through a situation I'd love some input on.

I have a client operating under a franchise name \(think "ABC-Plumbing \[Town Name\]" with a dash in the name\). There's another franchisee in the same market under a nearly identical name, "ABC Plumbing \[Town Name\]" without the dash, that appears to no longer be in business. The problem is they still have an active Google Business Profile and existing citations out there. Worth noting: the defunct listing only has a couple of Google reviews from two years ago and no website attached to it.

So now we've got two very similar business names pointing to different addresses, different phone numbers, and slightly different NAP formatting across the web. Not ideal.

A few things I'm working through:

1. Can the inactive GBP be removed? I know you can suggest an edit to mark it as "permanently closed," but given the listing has no website, minimal reviews, and no recent activity, I'm wondering if a full removal request is realistic.
2. What do you do about the stale citations for the defunct business? Do you try to claim and suppress them, or just leave them alone since they're pointing to a different address anyway?
3. Am I overthinking this? Since the addresses and phone numbers are different, Google may already be treating these as two distinct entities.

Happy to hear if anyone has dealt with something similar, found a clean resolution, or has a take on whether this is actually worth chasing down. Thanks in advance.

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 500. Core 30 AI Agent — Major Update Shipped 🚀
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/core-30-ai-agent-major-update-shipped
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles + comentó  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Just pushed a big update to the Core 30 AI Agent. This one touches the bulk content generator, image handling, and overall content quality. Here's what changed and why it matters for your client campaigns.

Bulk Content Generator — Complete Overhaul
The 8-pass content engine got a full rework. If you've been running bulk location pages, you're going to notice a significant jump in output quality and consistency.

What's new:
Smart word budgets per section. The tool now calculates a dynamic word budget for each H2 section based on your total page target. Instead of each section guessing how long it should be, the model gets an exact word range — and the token limits are calibrated to enforce it. Result: every section pulls its weight, and your total page length lands in the 1,200–1,700 word sweet zone consistently.

Refinement passes preserve content. Previously, the style editing passes \(burstiness, perplexity, human bookends, conversion optimization\) could accidentally cut your article in half. That's fixed. Passes 4–7 now carry a refinement guardrail that locks in your content length while still improving sentence rhythm, vocabulary variety, and conversion elements. You'll see 97–100% content retention through the full pipeline.

Clean 3-section outlines every time. The outline generator no longer produces FAQ questions disguised as section headings, or "Introduction" and "CTA" as separate sections. You get exactly 3 content-rich H2 sections — the intro and CTA are handled by the page template where they belong.

External authority links. Published pages now include external links to high-authority domains. These are generated automatically and inserted into your content for topical relevance signals.

FAQ sections — exactly one. Previously, some pages were getting duplicate FAQ blocks or zero FAQs. Now you get exactly one FAQ section with 3 relevant, locally-contextualized questions and answers on every page.

Retry logic for API overload. When Anthropic's API returns a 529 \(overloaded\), the tool now retries up to 4 times with exponential backoff instead of failing immediately. Fewer failed items in your queue, less babysitting.

Crash protection. Resolved a process crash that was silently killing the pipeline after the final quality check pass. The bulk generator now completes reliably through all 8 passes, external link insertion, FAQ generation, and WordPress publish — without dropping items.

Image Handling
Metadata stripping. All AI-generated images now have their EXIF, IPTC, XMP, and C2PA metadata stripped before uploading to WordPress. Google can't identify them as AI-generated through metadata inspection.

Cleaner alt text. Image alt attributes no longer contain raw DALL-E generation prompts. Alt text is now cleaned to short, descriptive, SEO-friendly text automatically.

Content Quality Improvements
The published pages coming out of this update are consistently strong. Real neighborhood details, practical service context, specific street names, building types, infrastructure references — the kind of content that reads like a local expert wrote it, not a template.

A few specifics the engine handles well now:
[ul][li]Local infrastructure context \(combined sewer systems, municipal water sources, street tree root intrusion\)[li]Driving directions that reference actual landmarks, intersections, and traffic patterns[li]Seasonal plumbing patterns tied to local climate[li]Multi-family building dynamics \(shared stacks, cascading water damage between units\)

What's Coming Next
Supp Planner — a new tab that automates supporting content research. Enter a service category, and the tool pulls real questions people are asking from Google PAA, Reddit, and local forums. It scores them by relevance and search intent, rephrases them for SEO, and pushes your picks straight into the bulk content generator. This turns topical authority building from a manual research task into a one-click workflow.

Internal linking — automated cross-linking between your location pages and supporting content. Coming soon after Supp Planner ships.

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 503. Multi Location Phone Nightmare
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/multi-location-phone-nightmare
Autor: benjamin-shannon-7351  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
[@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) I'll try and keep this short as possible.

We're implementing a Lead Connector IVR system for a three-location service business \(third location is brand new, still need to verify\). Their existing primary number is used on their first two GBP's and embedded across all their trucks, signage, and marketing materials, so we can't discontinue it entirely. Our strategy is to make one tracked LC number the primary contact on all three GBPs with the IVR routing calls to each specific location- press 1 for location A, etc... While keeping their original number as a secondary number on each profile. The question is whether having one universal Lead Connector number listed as primary across three separate GBPs compromises their local rankings compared to giving each location its own dedicated number? You've said separate number for each GBP, the first two were set up prior to us taking them as clients. Not really thrilled to handle this mess. Would rather be working on their sites authority and reviews but I'm also concerned this strategy could compromise past or future gains. They want IVR to manage spam calls and have the call tracking / recording benefits.

Since they only have 1 person handle all inbound calls to all three locations it seems like overkill and a potential IVR tree mess to get 3 separate LC numbers. Sorry for the long question, I've avoided attacking this for a reason.

## 514. Tomorrow... core 30 ai agent update
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/tomorrow-core-30-ai-agent-update
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles + comentó  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
So finally got it working well enough to push it from the staging server to the live server

But!

In my experience working with claude code, it almost always makes mistakes

So I'm not going to push it to the live server tonight

\(since I need to wake up a 5am to do a tempo run and pushing it to the live server and then going to bed seems... unusually cruel...\)

I'll push it in the morning.

Biggest update:
New Geo Relevance Tab: Upload your leadsnap csv \(will later expand to include other formats\) and the tool will recommend content to produce based on the map

You can then export those recs to the bulk content generator and hit go

And then you're basically done \(except for review of the content before publishing and external link sourcing\).

Other major improvement is in the 9 prompt content generation process - I broke it out to use a different series of prompts for each content type. Customization was also added so you can customize each step for each of the content types if you so desire =\)

In testing over 200 articles generated, the highest AI score was 59, the second highest was 41. The lowest was 11 \(which I've not actually seen before\) with an average of 22

Autor: tomas-affranchino-9734  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

Hi Team,

I have a query for an existing client in Sydney which we are ranking in top positions organically and quite high in AI models but are really not performing well in the local map pack. She offers two main core services which makes it hard to focus \(executive coaching and keynote speaking\) under which she then has sub services.

If we focus on the main keyword, executive coaching Sydney, most top performing competitors have the 'coaching centre' as the primary keyword and then some other sub-categories. I have the following queries:
[ol:1][li]Home page: She wants to keep it broad and definitely doesn't want to include coaching centre there \(none of the top competitors has that in the H1, title, meta, etc., as well\). Can we leave it like that eventhough the gbp is pointing to the home page?[li]Attached is how our map pack is looking for 'executive coaching sydney'. Now we are ranking #1/2 for main executive coaching keywords in traditional seo and the campaign has been delivering exceptional results. How do you recommend tackling this? Focusing more on topical relevance based on the pages we have?[li]I've attached a screenshot of their current services, shall we map them out all under coaching center and delete the other sub category?[li]We may be able to build out maybe 4-5 more services but after that is it a matter of changing the faq on the gbp landing page eventhough we are ranking for a service + location page for executive coaching?

I am mainly trying to get some guidance on the best path forward considering the traditional seo has been working so well but the main gap seems to be local. We are also based in bondi junction which is slightly 6km away from sydney cbd where most top competitors are based. Is that prominance factor affecting as well? Thx all!

## 528. 🚀 Announcing: The Core 30 AI Agent — Beta Access Now Open
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/announcing-the-core-30-ai-agent-beta-access-now-open
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles + comentó  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I've been building something behind the scenes for months, and it's ready for you guys to start testing.
The Core 30 AI Agent takes the entire Core 30 methodology and turns it into a step-by-step tool that walks you through the full local SEO workflow — from audit to published pages.

Here's what it actually does:
GBP Profile Parsing — Paste in your Google Business Profile data and the tool automatically extracts your primary category, secondary categories, and all services. No manual sorting.
Service Entity Overlap Research — Uses DataForSEO to analyze which of your services share SERP overlap with your primary category and which are distinct. This tells you exactly which services need their own dedicated pages vs. which can live under a broader category page. It's the Core 30 content strategy on autopilot.
Site Crawl & Content Gap Analysis — Crawls your existing site, auto-categorizes each page to a GBP category or service, and shows you what's missing. Instantly see "you have pages for 6 of 15 services" and know exactly what to build next.
Full Content Generation Pipeline — A multi-step stepper that takes you from Research → Outline → Article → FAQ → Schema → Images → Video → Review & Publish. Each step feeds into the next. Content is generated with related keywords injected, FAQ sections built from real People Also Ask data, and schema markup generated automatically.
AI Detection & Humanization — Built-in AI detection scoring \(GPTZero integration\) and a humanize button to rework content before publishing.
WordPress Publishing — When you're happy with the content, publish directly to a WordPress site with styled pages — hero sections, CTAs, trust badges, proper formatting. Not just a raw text dump.
Bulk Content Mode — Toggle from single page to bulk queue. Select multiple services, hit generate, and let it work through the queue while you do something else. Schedule publishing on a drip so your site grows naturally.

This tool is the culmination of everything I teach in the Core 30 methodology — but instead of doing it manually across 10 different tabs, it's one workflow.

This is a beta — it works, but it's still evolving. Your feedback will directly shape what gets built next. You're getting in before this becomes a standalone product.

Who wants in? Here's the link: [https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/492fd5bc?md=a9ed9a90201a4299bd08202effdf9a0d](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/492fd5bc?md=e7d75255384a492a865ca01132509fe9)

When you have issues with it, either send me a note on Skool or comment below and we'll get it fixed =\)

Thank you to everyone who wants to test the tool

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 543. Google Antigravity and other AI tools for building websites
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/google-antigravity-and-other-ai-tools-for-building-websites
Autor: fenwick-snowdon-5296  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hi everyone - sorry long post. I hope this is on topic but it does relate to Local SEO and client management overall. I've just been down this youtube rabbit hole last couple of weeks looking at AI website builders. The market is evolving so rapidly.

Currently, for our local SEO clients - we're finding that adding new pages to Wordpress via traditional block editor or page builder methods is taking a long time per page \(up to 1 hour per page\). We're working in the restaurant space and for better or worse, we aren't charging our clients anything like $3 or $5k per month for our services. But we also need to get our clients a result. I feel there is a middle ground.

So I was wondering if there is a quicker way. I did experiment with Claud cowork recently to develop two new pages for one client then pasted the new page content as HTML into an existing wordpress site. That was quick.

Lovable is great as well, and we used your Lovable Core 30 website building prompts to build a 30 page website in about 3 hours \(excluding a lot of the content which can be added later\). It was impressive and looks great!

But I'm concerned about being trapped on a platform that looks unnecessarily expensive on a project by project basis.

And I have just discovered Google Antigravity \(with potentially free hosting?\).

My goal: a platform that is inexpensive on a per project basis for my new Local SEO clients \(thank you Caleb!!\). Websites need to be super quick to spin up and easy to update with cheap hosting. Just as a reference, we are currently hosting wordpress sites on Flywheel hosting for around $5USD per site.

So my question is, what platforms are you using for your websites and what are your experiences \(pros and cons\) with them?

Which technology stack is best for Local SEO integration and getting the results our clients are demanding?

Any help greatly appreciated.

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 556. GBP Issue I've Never Seen Before
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/gbp-issue-ive-never-seen-before
Autor: mark-volzer-5910  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I had a call with a prospect today and his GBP has an issue I've never seen before. He's set up as an SAB and google sees and displays his Service Area. It even shows a pin in the middle of his service area, but when you search for his business on maps, Google pulls out to show the entire state and displays as if the business is located 3 hours away. When I run scans with Plepper and LeadSnap they both show his business as being 3 hours away on their maps. I've never seen this before. I told him to display his address and make sure he doesn't accidentally have some location on the other side of the state listed, but has anyone seen this before?
I recorded a video to show what is happening.

[https://www.loom.com/share/9d1932a5f3874e35ac68c4b40146c99c](https://www.loom.com/share/9d1932a5f3874e35ac68c4b40146c99c)
