Autor: jerry-newman-3595  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Has anyone had a situation where rankings suddenly started dropping and you can't figure out why? I have begun implementing the content created through Caleb's system on a clients site over the past couple of weeks. However, since I've started doing that their rankings are steadily dropping every couple of days. When they came to me they were completely at the bottom, rank 20+ across the board. I've been heavily focused on their rankings for deck building terms and had them up to an average rank of around 10, but over the past 5 days they dropped from rank 10 to rank 15. I've purchased two backlinks this month \(both just chamber of commerce\) and they have all the citations from LeadSnap. The only thing I started doing different is generating content with Claude rather than manually writing the bulk of everything myself. The rank map also looks kind of weird. Right at their location where they used to be 1st/2nd, they are now showing 20+. But out past that they are 5-7. Completely lost on what to do, any ideas are appreciated.

## 576. NEW LESSON: Complete Copywriting System (Internal Training)
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/new-lesson-complete-copywriting-system-internal-training
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles + comentó  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hey everyone,

Just dropped a new lesson in the Content section that I'm pretty excited about.

What it is:
This is the exact training I built for my internal copywriting team - nothing held back. It walks through every single step of producing publication-ready content, from keyword research to final quality checks.

Results we're seeing:

My team went from 60 minutes per article down to 20-25 minutes, with better quality output. Every article comes out with:

[ul][li]Internal links placed contextually[li]External authority links to credible sources[li]Fact-checking completed[li]ZeroGPT scores that pass \(typically under 40%\)[li]Ready to publish with minimal editing

Fair warning:
This lesson isn't fully polished yet. I wanted to get it out to you as soon as it was functional rather than making you wait another week while I perfect every detail. You might catch a rough edge or two, but all the important stuff is there.

Link to lesson: [https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=0f0406fc4a0d46b197263cce3896ee9e](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=0f0406fc4a0d46b197263cce3896ee9e)

What's next:
Over the next couple days, I'll be updating the other content lessons in the Pro group to align with this methodology. This will become the standard approach we use going forward.

Try it out and let me know what you think. Questions, issues, or wins - drop them in the comments below. Let me know any roadblocks you hit and I'll make updates to address them =\)

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 579. Should a Booking page on a subdomain be "noindex" and how?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/should-a-booking-page-on-a-subdomain-be-noindex-and-how
Autor: anil-agrawal-2726  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
The booking page for all the services is on a subdomain via custom domain setup via the booking software called Trafft. The site including the subdomain is behind/via Cloudflare CDN. Trafft app does not provide access to specific <head>, <body>, or <footer> tag sections and require DNS Only setup on Cloudflare.

Trafft does have a Custom Java Script section but that's about it.

All suggestions by Copilot ended up stuck with a wall spending a few hours on this and finally had to give up.

[ol:1][li]Should the subdomain be even a "noindex"?[li]If yes, any ideas how one would go about implementing it?
Thanks,
Anil

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 581. 🎉 Call Recording Now Available! (Happy Mardis Gras!)
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/call-recording-now-available-happy-mardis-gras
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
This week's call is up in the Classroom under 2026-02-17 Rank Maps, Tool Demo, LLMs.

Here's what we covered:

🗺️ Near Me vs. City Name Rank Maps – Why your near me map should be smaller and how to size it correctly using the top competitors' top-three percentages.

🛠️ Core 30 Tool Demo – Got a sneak peek at the new automation tool I've been building in Claude Code. Site crawling, gap analysis, content generation, WordPress publishing, and more. It'll be free for everyone here when it's ready.

📊 The 50% Rule – How to know when you've built enough topical relevance and it's time to switch to geographic content.

🏠 Rebranding Risks – Why even a simple WordPress theme swap can tank your rankings, and how to handle domain and name changes without losing everything.

🍽️ Restaurant SEO – A huge untapped opportunity. Most restaurants aren't doing any of this, and the ones that are \(even bad ones\) are dominating searches like "Gumbo New Orleans" with 10K monthly searches.

🤖 Getting Mentioned by ChatGPT – How digital PR and brand mentions across publications, Reddit, and LinkedIn can get your clients recommended by AI chatbots.

We also covered chiropractor site structure, personal injury attoney re-theming, multi-location phone numbers, GBP reverification, replying to old reviews, and more.

Link: [https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/52e05dc7?md=fc63cbab8c074c06bf3b074ceceb13db](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/52e05dc7?md=fc63cbab8c074c06bf3b074ceceb13db)

⚠️ Heads up: No call next Tuesday the 24th. I'll be in Portland hunting for truffles. Post your questions here or message me or Miles and we'll get back to you!

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 603. Content Creation Rate

Autor: jerry-newman-3595  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hey everyone!
Just joined a couple of days ago and I'm loving the course so far. I'm curious if anyone here would be willing to share how many new pages/articles they create for a client each month? I was chatting with a fellow agency owner who told me that he's been publishing 100-150 new pages to a site per month through various uses of AI. While I really enjoy using AI and its definitely made me move a lot faster, I still feel like that's an insane number of pages to publish in a 30 day period, especially when you multiply that by the number of clients you have. However, he states its been helping him rank local service businesses pretty quick \(a couple of weeks to 30 days, similar to what Caleb talks about\).

Even with the use of AI I still double check copy, edit it a bit cause sometimes AI is kinda dumb, build out a page in Wordpress, etc. It takes me at least an hour or two to build out a page, maybe my process is an issue and I'm taking too long?

Hey everyone,

I’ve built a website using GoHighLevel and connected it to my custom domain. Currently, the homepage is accessible through three different URLs:

[ol:1][li]The root path: /[li]A page-specific slug: /home-plumber[li]A path defined in the website settings: /website

My question is: Should I set up redirects so that only one of these URLs is accessible, or would it be better to allow them but prevent the duplicates from being indexed by search engines \(e.g., via robots.txt or meta noindex\)? Or should I just do nothing 😅

I've added the canonical url but is this enough?

From an SEO best-practice standpoint, what would you recommend?
Thank you

## 612. Just making sure Im tackling this GBP Correctly
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/just-making-sure-im-tackling-this-correctly-gbp-correctly
Autor: brian-kiselstein-6058  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
FYI.....I've read the GBP Changes/Warnings training, which is WHY I'm asking this question - it explains the risks but not the exact sequencing for this scenario, so....Current Situation:

[ul][li]Wrong primary category selected \(furnace repair instead of AC repair\)[li]23% heat map coverage on 9x9 grid[li]Most Core 30 pages already built out[li]GBP has redundant/messy service listings[li]Citations and chamber links in the works

My Plan:
Step 1: Change primary category to the correct one and wait to see how the map pack reacts
Step 2: If coverage jumps to 40%+, move into geographical relevance pages

Where I Get Confused:
If the goal is to rank for MULTIPLE services \(AC repair, furnace repair, furnace installation, plumber, water heater installation, etc.\), what's the proper sequence?

[ul][li]Do we leave the GBP alone \(even with redundant services\) as long as the primary is hitting 40%+ coverage?[li]When trying to boost those secondary services, do we clean up the GBP first, or just keep adding topical + geographical content and accept the primary will always perform best?[li]Is there a point where cleaning up redundant GBP services becomes necessary, or do we prioritize content creation over GBP optimization?
What's the best practice for tackling multi-service rankings when the GBP is messy but showing some momentum?

Thanks guys, sorry for the long one here.

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 613. How do you handle it when a GBP category and its main service are basically the same thing?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/how-do-you-handle-it-when-a-gbp-category-and-its-main-service-are-basically-the-same-thing
Autor: connor-doyle-8725  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Take "Bathroom Remodeler" \(GBP category\) and "Bathroom Remodeling" \(the core service within that category\). A few things we're trying to figure out:
[ol:1][li]Homepage services section — do you label it "Bathroom Remodeler" or "Bathroom Remodeling"?[li]Page structure — do you create both a "Bathroom Remodeler" category page AND a separate "Bathroom Remodeling" service page, or just one? If one, which?[li]Homepage link — whichever you label it on the homepage, which page does it link to?
It feels redundant having both, but we don't want to miss an SEO opportunity either. How are others handling this?

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 614. 📹 Feb 10th Call Recording Is Live
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/feb-10th-call-recording-is-live
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Good call this week. We got into a lot of practical stuff.

Here's what we covered:
[ul][li]Adding a physical address to service area businesses and the ranking impact we're seeing[li]Sizing rank maps correctly for "near me" keywords[li]Seasonal GBP category switching for HVAC clients — when to do it and when not to[li]How to build enough brand mentions for AI systems to start recommending a business[li]Content audit process for clients who are stuck and not ranking despite having pages up[li]Live site audit of a Swiss e-commerce store with Ahrefs SERP analysis[li]Google removing the direct Call button from SERPs — what it means and what to do about it[li]Quick hits on GBP map embeds, tracking numbers, service limits, and the LLMs.txt file

Also shared some takeaways from the SEO conference I attended and some updates on our agency onboarding process.

📂 Recording is in the Classroom under 2025-02-10 GBP Address, Near Me, AIO

[https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/52e05dc7?md=513b3c9a75b04ac6b5b704d8224c0636](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/52e05dc7?md=513b3c9a75b04ac6b5b704d8224c0636)

## 629. Title Tag Length
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/title-tag-length
Autor: joseph-sorensen-crivello-1445  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I noticed in the website that Caleb built in the AI SEO Mastery Pro course [https://ai-seo-pro-gary.lovable.app/plumber-gary-in](https://ai-seo-pro-gary.lovable.app/plumber-gary-in) several pages have title tags that are more than 60 characters. I've always used 60 or less. Will having more than 60 affect my SEO? This is from Caleb's class: 🤖 Prompt: Generating a Title Tag 🤖
I want you to help me create compelling blog title that are optimized for a specific keyword phrase. I will provide you with the target SEO keyword phrase, and your task is to generate 10 unique, enticing titles that incorporate this phrase. Each title should:
Include the exact keyword phrase or a very close variation to maintain SEO relevance
Be concise and attention-grabbing, with a character count between 50 and 60 characters per title.
After generating the titles, assign each one a score from 1 to 10 based on two factors: One - How likely the title is to rank well for the target keyword phrase \(SEO Score\) and Two - How likely viewers are to click on the title due to its engaging and compelling nature \(Engagement Score\)
Additionally, provide an overall score that combines both the SEO score and engagement score, giving equal weight to each. The overall score should also be on a scale from 1 to 10.
Please format your response as follows:
\[Title\] - SEO Score: \[X\]/10, Engagement Score: \[Y\]/10, Overall Score: \[Z\]/10
Please generate these for the list of keywords I’m going to give you.

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 640. Question on Primary Category Strategy for New GBPs
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/question-on-primary-category-strategy-for-new-gbps
Autor: connor-doyle-8725  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Setting up a new GBP for a remodeling contractor who does kitchen and bathroom remodeling equally—no specialty in either.

Logically, "Remodeler" is the most accurate primary category since it covers both services. But "Remodeler" has way lower search volume and is MUCH less competitive compared to "Kitchen Remodeler" or "Bathroom Remodeler."

Since this is a brand new GBP with no existing rankings to protect, would you:
A\) Use "Remodeler" because it's the most accurate representation of the business
B\) Pick whichever service category \(kitchen or bathroom\) has more search demand in their market, even though it only represents half their business

How do you weigh category accuracy vs. search volume potential when starting fresh?

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 649. Getting to 10,000 pages for a real estate site
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/getting-to-10000-pages-for-a-real-estate-site
Autor: jon-burden-1856  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hey [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) thanks again for the really helpful feedback yesterday on my website. I really appreciate you taking the time to review it!

I wanted to run a content scaling plan by you and get your take on whether this feels Google-safe. All content would come from my existing 95+ long-form YouTube videos about living in Durango, Colorado — not AI-generated information.

Plan \(very high level\)

[ul][li]Transcribe and tag my YouTube videos into a local knowledge vault[li]Break transcripts into small, intent-specific answer blocks[li]Assemble pages from those blocks using fixed page types \(not writing from scratch\)

Examples of pages

[ul][li]Street & road pages \(e.g. Living on Florida Road, CR 250 tradeoffs\)[li]Neighborhood / area pages \(elevation, winter access, lifestyle\)[li]Buyer scenario pages \(remote workers, families, out-of-state buyers\)[li]Constraint pages \(HOA vs no HOA, internet, snow, elevation\)[li]Comparison pages \(Florida Road vs Animas Valley, in-town vs acreage\)

How this scales to ~8,000 pages

[ul][li]Each video produces dozens of reusable answer blocks[li]Blocks are reused across different page types, never verbatim on the same page[li]Pages differ by intent, structure, framing, and internal links \(ideas reused, not pages\)

Safeguards

[ul][li]No raw transcript pages[li]No AI-invented facts[li]Human QA before publishing[li]Each URL targets a distinct search intent

The goal is to start around ~2,000 pages and scale responsibly while building a true local knowledge graph, not mass SEO pages. I’d love your honest take on whether this looks like a solid approach and anything you’d flag as a risk.
Thank you!!

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 650. Multi-Service Artisan – Which GBP Category?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/multi-service-artisan-which-gbp-category
Autor: hubert-langlois-1058  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hey everyone, I need some insight for a new customer. He doesn’t have a website or a Google Business Profile yet, so I’m starting completely from scratch. I’m having trouble figuring out the best fit for his primary GBP category and which secondary categories to use.

He’s a solo artisan offering a wide range of services:

Interior Renovation:
[ul][li]Painting[li]Tiling[li]Parquet flooring[li]Wall repairs[li]Bathroom renovation

Structural & Outdoor Work:
[ul][li]Wall construction[li]Rendered façades[li]Earthworks[li]Concrete terraces[li]Fence installation[li]Pillars and gate installation[li]Outdoor tiling[li]Exposed aggregate concrete[li]Paving[li]Foundations

He’s very versatile and not highly specialized, so I’m unsure how to categorize him to maximize visibility on Google.

I also made a search of the categories and services in the city, and they all range in the same monthly volume. So I believe maybe something general for the primary category could fit.

Any advice on primary and secondary categories for a multi-skilled artisan like this would be greatly appreciated! Thanks in advance!

## 653. NEW PRO TRAINING: Building a Core 30 Website in Lovable
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/new-pro-training-building-a-core-30-website-in-lovable
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Just dropped a full lesson series walking you through exactly how to build a complete local business website using Lovable + Claude.

What's Included:
[ul][li]Overview – The full framework and what we're building[li]Prompt 0: Planning – Site architecture setup with Claude[li]Lovable HTML: NEEDED to Get Ranked – Why this matters for indexing[li]Prompt 1: Skeleton – Getting the foundation built[li]Prompt 2: GBP Landing Page Content – Writing high-converting homepage copy[li]Prompt 3: GBP Landing Page Build Out – Implementation in Lovable[li]Prompt 4: Secondary Category Page Build Out – Scaling your service structure[li]Prompt 5: Core Service Page Build Out – Your money pages[li]Prompt 6: Service Page Build Out – Filling out the full site[li]Prompt 7: About and Contact Page Build Out – Trust-building pages[li]Prompt 8: Technical SEO Implementation – Schema, internal links, the works[li]Walkthrough of Caleb's Build – Watch me build an 80-page site in about 90 minutes

The walkthrough shows the exact prompt sequence between Claude and Lovable, including how I handled issues that came up \(schema fixes, footer cleanup, location pages, etc.\).

If you're doing this for real clients, I'd recommend using the content writing prompts from the copywriting section for your core pages—Claude writes better than Lovable. But for structure and scale, this workflow gets you a full local site fast.

[https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=8027ed1871ed49f08392555e53edb6fa](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=8027ed1871ed49f08392555e53edb6fa)

## 656. Homepage vs. Service Page Keyword Cannibalization
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/homepage-vs-service-page-keyword-cannibalization
Autor: daniel-greer-1044  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
For a personal injury attorney that we're working with, we have the GBP going to the homepage which is [domain.com](http://domain.com)

then they have the services for Personal Injury, Employment and Workers' Comp nested under services...so its [domain.com/services/listed-service](http://domain.com/services/listed-service)
specifically for personal injury it's [domain.com/services/personal-injury-lawyers-in-pasadena-ca/](http://domain.com/services/personal-injury-lawyers-in-pasadena-ca/)

Right now, the keyword targeted for each is:
home: personal injury lawyer
/Services/: personal injury lawyers in pasadena ca

Is this going to cause cannibalization and how should we handle that if so?

His rank map is solid for personal injury lawyer pasadena \(taken 2 days ago\) , but the types of cases he wants to rank for are abysmal so we're working on creating category pages to match GBP which we'll continue to nest under the services folder, as well as missing services pages.

Any advice is appreciated :\)
[@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c)

## 658. Link acquisition strategy
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/question-on-link-acquisition-strategy
Autor: connor-doyle-8725  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Beyond obvious local links \(like chamber of commerce, local associations, sponsorships, etc.\), how are you all thinking about link acquisition as part of your SEO strategy?
Specifically:
[ul][li]Do you actively pursue links at all, or focus almost entirely on relevance/content first?[li]If you do build links, are you leaning more toward outreach, paid placements, niche edits, PBN-style links, or a mix?[li]At what point in a campaign do you feel links actually start to matter?
Curious how others are approaching this, especially for local SEO, where relevance and GBP optimization seem to do most of the heavy lifting.

## 662. Question about targeting townships vs larger areas in NJ (and similar markets)
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/question-about-targeting-townships-vs-larger-areas-in-nj-and-similar-markets
Autor: connor-doyle-8725  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Quick question for anyone working with clients in New Jersey or similar markets with small townships:
How do you handle keyword strategy when your client services a larger region, but the actual cities are tiny townships?

For example, we have NJ clients pushing back saying "I don't just work in \[Township Name\], I cover the whole county" or "I service a 30-mile radius." But the townships themselves are so small that targeting them individually feels like we're limiting their reach.

What keywords are you actually chasing in these situations? Are you:
[ul][li]Still optimizing GMB and website for the specific township despite the small size?[li]Going broader with county-level keywords?[li]Using "\[Service\] near me" type strategies?[li]Doing something else entirely?

I get their concern - they don't want to turn away leads from nearby areas. But we also need to rank somewhere specific for Map Pack.

How are you balancing client expectations with the reality that Google needs us to pick a specific location to dominate?

Am I missing something?

PREGUNTA:
January 27th Call is Now in the Classroom

This week's call was packed with real client consultations and some insights that might change how you think about a few things...

Here's a taste of what we covered:
🔥 Why a client ranking 38% top 3 with 200+ five-star reviews is barely getting any calls \(and the counterintuitive reason perfect ratings might be hurting you\)
🏠 Live audit of a real estate agent's rank map — and why having 1,700 pages might still not be enough in some markets
⚖️ How to use re-theming on secondary categories to rank for the cases you actually want \(not just your primary category\)
📍 The exact moment to stop building service pages and switch to geographical content
🔗 Why we never publish a single page without this one thing
💰 Real talk on which clients from the 90-Day Challenge are actually worth upselling \(and which ones never will be\)

Plus answers to questions like:
[ul][li]How fast should you expect rank map improvements?[li]Is there such a thing as too many GBP services?[li]What do you do when video verification requires signage you don't have?

Check it out here: [https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/52e05dc7?md=1f3c0614cac1465eada024e80594ae70](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/52e05dc7?md=1f3c0614cac1465eada024e80594ae70)

## 669. Local News For Local Signals?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/local-news-for-local-signals
Autor: scott-desalvo-1096  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
OK, so we know that just creating pages and throwing them on your site isn't the way to expand content.

It has to be strategic.

For background, I am a personal injury lawyer.

I was solicited by a guy who scrapes the Internet for car crashes in particular geographic areas. His pitch in involves posting a news story that is ranked immediately somehow and then he sells you a space next to his story about the crash. This sounds like an extremely low percentage chance of someone involved in the crash finding that page and calling me.

The upsell is the news pages get posted on my website

But a secondary benefit of doing something like that might be massive for local relevance signals to Google for maps and also for AI.

The downside is once the crash is a year old, nobody's ever gonna search or go to that page. I'm afraid of just loading my site down with non-evergreen content.

The smarter play I think is Evergreen content, but there are a lot of vendors out there trying to sell local timely events posted on your website.

I'm also concerned about confusing AI in Google about whether I'm a personal injury lawyer or a news aggregator site or like basically an online local newspaper reporting about local crashes. I don't want to confuse anybody.

Anybody test any of this or have any thoughts? Response is much appreciated.

═══════════════════════════════════════

When embedding Google Business Profile \(GBP\) review widgets on a website:
1. Full reviews vs review summary
Some plugins display:
[ul][li]All \(or many\) full review texts, or[li]Just a summary \(star rating + total number of reviews\)

2. Crawlability & content interpretation

[ul][li]Does Google actually crawl and treat the embedded review text as page content?[li]Could large blocks of repeated review content:
3. Best-practice guidanceFor local service pages \(e.g. physio, legal, allied health\):
[ul][li]Is it safer to show rating + review count only?[li]Or have you seen benefits from surfacing a limited number of full reviews?
Keen to hear testing outcomes, edge cases, or confirmed best practices, not just plugin marketing claims. Thanks 🙏

## 678. Coworking address + video verification for former SAB — best next step?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/gbp-video-verification
Autor: tomas-affranchino-9734  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hi everyone,

Looking for advice on a GBP verification scenario.

I have a client who has historically been a Service Area Business \(no public address\). We added a coworking office address to test having a location, and Google has now triggered video verification.
Context: the client doesn’t have staff onsite at the coworking space and doesn’t really receive customers there. We can potentially arrange signage and record the video showing the building/location and the branded vehicle/equipment, but it’s not a traditional staffed storefront.

A few questions I’d love your guidance on:

[ol:1][li]Best practices to pass video verificationWhat are your top tips / “must include” items in the video to maximise approval \(especially for coworking or hybrid setups\)?[li]Edits pending until verifiedGoogle is showing “edits won’t be visible until verified.” In your experience, does Google generally hold back all edits until verification, or only address/location-related changes?[li]If verification is rejected, can we revert to SAB safely?If we attempt verification for the coworking address and it gets rejected, can we then simply remove/hide the address and revert back to SAB \(service areas only\)?Any known risks of suspension / extra scrutiny after a rejected verification attempt?[li]Citations + PR before verifying — worth it?Would you recommend:
[ul][li]Updating citations \(Apple Maps / key directories\), and/or[li]Running a PR mentioning the new address,then waiting ~30 days before trying verification?Or is that unnecessary \(or even risky\) if the address setup may not be fully compliant?

Any real-world experiences, do’s/don’ts, or recommended sequence would be hugely appreciated. Thanks so much.

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 681. Local SEO vs Map Pack
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/local-seo-vs-map-pack
Autor: nathan-gabriele-2557  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I’m hearing/ reading stuff like

Website content, backlinks etc are primarily used to rank in SERP but has no significant correlation with map pack ranking/ getting more calls.

Essentially for the goal of dominating local map pack your website/ business only needs to hit a certain trust level with Google \(citations, NAP consistency, core 30\).

Once Google ticks you off as a legit business adding more content, blogs, pages etc are irrelevant because it becomes more about GBP competitiveness and user trust signals \(reviews\).

Hence why some plumbers for example with terrible “outdated” sites still rank highly.

I’m new to all of this and I’m just trying to understand what I need to do to dominate local map pack, what are your thoughts?

## 691. PBN Strategy
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/pbn-strategy
Autor: connor-doyle-8725  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Quick question about the PBN tier 1 strategy from the course - this goes against every piece of SEO advice I’ve received and I need more clarity.

Everything I’ve learned outside this course says pointing PBNs directly at money pages as tier 1 links is extremely risky and most SEOs would strongly advise against it. The standard advice is to only use PBNs as tier 2 links \(if at all\) to create a buffer and protect the money site. But the coaching calls recommend using them as tier 1 links directly to service pages and homepages.

My understanding from this course is the main risk is just losing the link juice if the PBN gets deindexed, not actual penalties since Google knows about negative SEO. But PBNs are notoriously considered high-risk links, so I’m trying to understand the real risk profile here in 2026.

Also - the strategy says PBNs aren’t for ranking but to help Google not classify pages as AI slop and get them indexed. But if our pages are already getting indexed organically without PBNs and aren’t getting hit as AI content, what’s the actual purpose of using them? Are we just adding significant risk to client sites / spending additional budget for no real benefit at that point?

For those implementing this - have any of you actually seen negative consequences beyond just losing the links? And have you tested publishing without PBNs first to see if it even makes a difference? Just want to make sure I understand what I’m getting into before putting client sites at risk with this approach.

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 692. Website redirects
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/website-redirects
Autor: andy-fladung-5555  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

My question is, how do I get the other two redirecting websites to rank in the top 3 like I have his main location?
I did create landing pages for the other two locations and linked the GBP's to them. Each of the websites are optimized with primary categories pages, secondary category pages, supporting service pages and location pages. Internal links are completed to create topical silos for the category pages.

## 695. Thoughts on the 2026 AI-Driven Local Pack? 🫣
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/thoughts-on-the-2026-ai-driven-local-pack
Autor: axel-hilland-8573  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hi community! I’d love to hear your thoughts on the new 2026 map pack rollout. To be honest, it’s posing some serious challenges for the optimization strategies we’re currently focused on.

A few major pain points:
Reduced Visibility: The new AI-driven edition only displays two businesses, making the competition for the top spots even more cutthroat.
Lower Conversion: There are no call buttons anymore! 😫 This adds significant friction to the user journey.
Algorithmic Shifts: The new algorithm is surfacing completely different businesses than what we’re used to seeing in standard rankings.
Tracking Issues: Most rank trackers are currently unable to accurately report positions since the update rolled out.

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 700. Strategy For Painter in Buffalo NY
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/strategy-for-painter-in-buffalo-ny
Autor: michael-page-8400  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hey everyone, quick question and looking for some strategic input here.

I’m currently working with a painting company in Buffalo, New York called Paint Now WNY. They’re set up as a service area business on their Google Business Profile and are ranking decently overall.

From the heat map data:
[ul][li]“Painter in Buffalo, NY” looks fairly solid. Coverage isn’t perfect, but it’s respectable.[li]“Painter near me” is where things really fall apart. Visibility drops off hard, especially outside tighter proximity zones.
The owner doesn’t necessarily want Buffalo to be the primary focus. His ideal market is Western New York as a whole, which makes proximity-based searches a bigger concern.
We’ve discussed potentially adding a physical address to the Google Business Profile to strengthen proximity signals and improve rankings for “near me” searches. Before making that move, I wanted to get feedback from others who’ve dealt with similar situations.
Based on these two heat maps side by side:
[ul][li]Would you recommend transitioning from a pure SAB to a listed address?[li]Or would you focus more on service-area optimization, supporting locations, and off-GBP signals to improve proximity coverage across Western New York?
Appreciate any insight on what direction you’d take here and why.

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 704. and again - keywords for rank-tracking

Autor: heinrich-taitl-6301  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Although this topic has been discussed many times before, I would like to return to the subject of keyword research for rank tracking. For a city with a larger population \(in our case, Vienna\), everything is clear: category + city and maybe service + city.

But what about rural areas? Three small towns within a 20 km radius, all interesting and important. The company is located in town A, where category + city works very well. But we should also be found in locations B and C.

In this case, does it make more sense to track only by category \(without city\), or would you also prefer category + city B / C in order to best represent the progress?

## 706. Sequence of work
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/sequence-of-work
Autor: heinrich-taitl-6301  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
This is a new customer with no services in the GBP yet. We have defined four categories, each containing 8–15 services.
Now, I have some questions about the order of the work that needs to be done.
Should we update the GBP with all categories and services at once? Should we then gradually create the service pages on the website?
Or would it be better to only create as many services in the GBP as we can create pages for at the same time?
The same applies to creating pages. Should we first create basic content for all pages and then add the FAQs later, or should we create one page at a time, but complete each one immediately with all the necessary content?
Which approach is better and faster?

## 708. New Section Added: GBP Audit & Setup Process
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/new-section-added-gbp-audit-setup-process
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles + comentó  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Just added a new section to the Pro classroom walking you through our complete GBP audit and setup process—the exact workflow we use for every new client at the agency.

What's included:
[ul][li]GBP Audit – How to identify the right categories using GMB Everywhere, organize services with AI, and systematically check every field \(special hours, review responses, photos, etc.\)[li]GBP Landing Page Audit – NAP checks, schema validation, title/H1 optimization, and the biggest mistake local businesses make \(writing about themselves instead of the searcher\). Plus a breakdown of why Zell & Associates ranks #1 for "personal injury lawyer Houston"[li]GBP Management Software Setup – Full Leadsnap walkthrough covering category/service setup, photo scheduling with geotags and drip posting, and automated recurring posts

This is the foundation we build everything else on. Get the audit right, and the rest of the optimization has a clear path forward.

Here's the direct link: [https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=772272ad42334023968e5f3ff6f45ff6](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=772272ad42334023968e5f3ff6f45ff6)

Let me know if you have any questions 👇

## 721. Client wants global brand but needs to rank in NZ first - strategy advice?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/client-wants-global-brand-but-needs-to-rank-in-nz-first-strategy-advice
Autor: karan-holland-4673  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Quick question on ecommerce SEO strategy:

Client is a NZ-based nail strengthening brand with aggressive expansion timeline:
[ul][li]Now: NZ market[li]April/May: Australia \(3-4 months away!\)[li]Oct/Nov: UK[li]2027: Europe

He's concerned that optimizing for "nail strengthening NZ" will make the brand look too local when he launches in Australia in just a few months, potentially turning off AU customers who prefer local brands.
His preference: Build one global brand identity with NZ origins in the background.

My question:

Option A: Start with global positioning NOW
[ul][li]Title: "Nail Strengthening | Doctor-Formulated | Brand Name"[li]Still rank in NZ through Google Search Console geo-targeting, schema markup, NZ backlinks[li]Brand ready for AU launch in 3 months

Option B: Start NZ-focused, pivot in 3 months
[ul][li]Title: "Nail Strengthening NZ | Brand Name"[li]Rank in NZ with location keywords[li]Rebrand/re-optimize when expanding to AU in April

Given he's launching in Australia in 3-4 months, Option A \(global from day one\) seems smarter than rebranding so quickly. Will Option A still rank well in NZ without "NZ" in title tag and meta?
His main differentiators work globally: doctor-founded story + lab-proven 30% results.

Thoughts? [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) [@Miles Hayat](obj://user/8942b93b3b2a4ec4879f876a2b27455d) [@Mark Farrow](obj://user/5d7f726c110a43dc973a719104a5e4b4)

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 722. New Call Recording Available: January 13th, 2026
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/new-call-recording-available-january-13th-2026
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hey everyone! 👋
The recording from today's call is now up in the Classroom \(and the year is even correct\)

Here's what we covered:
📍 Working with existing client websites – When to make changes vs. keeping what's already working \(hint: if they're ranking 4-5% top three near their location, don't burn it down!\)
📍 Service area business game-changer – Adding an address to a SAB can boost your average rank position by 2-3 points in just a week. Sharif dropped an awesome before/after in the chat showing exactly this.
📍 Rank map sizing – How to know if your rank map is too big \(spoiler: if even the top competitors are only showing 3% top three, zoom in!\)
📍 The Google Dance for GBPs – Google tests your business profile the same way it tests webpages. If users bounce or you're not answering the phone, you won't hold those top rankings.
📍 Content publishing velocity – Why pushing 30 pages at once can hurt you and what pace actually works
📍 Re-theming secondary categories – How to rank for keywords that don't match your GBP categories
📍 E-commerce SEO structure – Quick breakdown for those working with non-local clients
Tools mentioned: GMB Everywhere, Index Meow, Lead Snap, Gemini 3 Pro \(surprisingly good for finding local sponsorship opportunities!\)

🔗 Find the recording here: Classroom → 2026-01-13 Rank Maps, GBP Address Tips: [https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/52e05dc7?md=180d6771359d49fbbd3540f2225f8b0f](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/52e05dc7?md=180d6771359d49fbbd3540f2225f8b0f)

Big thanks to Billy, Mike, Dylan, Jorge, Daniel, Jack, Karin, and everyone else who jumped in with questions today!

Also – the Core 30 website with all the prompts is getting unlocked for everyone next week. Stay tuned! 🚀

See you on the next call!
– Caleb

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 723. Any Insight on what could be going wrong with this clients rankings
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/any-insight-on-what-could-be-going-wrong-with-this-clients-rankings
Autor: blake-sanders-2839  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I have been running my agency for over a year now and have ranked several clients in more competitive fields in the map pack and normally 2 months into a local seo campaign I see at least some rank movement.

This scenario is a head scratcher for me:
We took on this client who originally had pressure washing as their primary category but also listed on their online presence that they did windows, roofing, and siding as well. they already had around 70-80 reviews.

We took them on 2 months ago with a primary focus on windows, even though they still offer the other services, we re optimized the profile, got them more reviews with window keywords, built a new website \(their old one was bad and no authority\), got them to join two local chamber of commerce, and also built several backlinks and citations, and nothing has happened in the rankings for the window keywords, it is like they are invisible. there is not 10+ window companies locally, so not being in the top 10-15 is strange for the ranking, I thought it may be due to NAP consistency so I am working on a citation clean up service, but just wanted external insight.

Thank you for any insight!

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 725. Feedback Requested on Targeting regions in Australia
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/feedback-requested-on-targeting-regions-in-australia
Autor: justin-bryce-8305  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
[@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) - something I've struggled with is the City / Region targetting based on how Australia works v's the US.

With the examples you provide you talk about City's & Neighborhoods.
In Australia we think about Suburbs. Our Postal Address has Street, Suburb, State.
However, doing some work with Paid Search I've discovered Google does have a City setup in Australia - albeit sometimes hard to figure out the name of it.

My question is what should we be targeting as the location on the website?
[ul][li]especially when the City does not appear on the clients business address.

Can someone help clarify my understanding?

Assumption: from a rebuild perspective, it is easier for Google and AI to recommend a site locally which also is structured properly for traditional SEO. And as a result, on a major site demolition and rebuild, it makes sense to get fundamental SEO right, as well as Local and SEO, subject to the Pareto Principle, and subject to the Local and AI optimization being most important for a local service business.

Foundational links are necessary. These would be the type of links Caleb suggests we create for every money page we create -- every service + location page.

Local links are necessary for local authority. Chambers. Associations. Sponsorships. Scholarships.

Traditional high quality SEO links. Expensive. Hard to get. Only to be obtained strategically on target pages.

Thoughts?

## 755. Indexing Lovable Website
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/indexing-lovable-website
Autor: shaun-gambardella-1718  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hi Everyone,
On one of the weekly calls it was stated that Google has a hard time finding websites built with Lovable . I found out that for the most part on marketing websites it uses React SPA \(Single Page Application\) technology which is great visually but it does not natively render the HTML for Google, Bing, etc. to index your pages. I figured out a prompt to fix the issue. Paste the prompt below into your lovable project and after re-index your website on Google Search Console and Bing Webmaster Tools. After that all your pages should be indexed.

##Prompt##
I need to implement Static Site Generation \(SSG\) to fix SEO indexing issues. My React SPA currently renders an empty <div id="root"/> which Google cannot index. I need to pre-render all pages as static HTML at build time.
Please implement SSG using the vite-react-ssg plugin with the following architecture:
## 1. Install the dependency
Add `vite-react-ssg` to dependencies.
## 2. Create SSG entry point: src/main.ssg.tsx
```tsx
import { ViteReactSSG } from 'vite-react-ssg';
import { routes } from './routes.ssg';
import './index.css';
export const createRoot = ViteReactSSG\(
{ routes },
\(\) => {
// Optional setup for both client and server
}
\);
3. Create SSG routes file: src/routes.ssg.tsx
Create a file that exports all routes using this pattern:
import { RouteRecord } from 'vite-react-ssg';
import { lazy, Suspense } from 'react';
import RootLayout from './components/RootLayout';
import LoadingFallback from './components/LoadingFallback';
// Helper to adapt default exports for vite-react-ssg
const lazyPage = \(importFn: \(\) => Promise<{ default: React.ComponentType }>\) => {
const Component = lazy\(importFn\);
return \(\) => \(
<Suspense fallback={<LoadingFallback />}>
<Component />
</Suspense>
\);
};
// Define all your page routes here
const pageRoutes: RouteRecord\[\] = \[
{ index: true, Component: lazyPage\(\(\) => import\('./pages/Home'\)\) },
{ path: 'about', Component: lazyPage\(\(\) => import\('./pages/About'\)\) },
// Add ALL your routes here...
{ path: '*', Component: lazyPage\(\(\) => import\('./pages/NotFound'\)\) },
\];
export const routes: RouteRecord\[\] = \[
{
path: '/',
Component: RootLayout,
children: pageRoutes,
},
\];
4. Create RootLayout component: src/components/RootLayout.tsx
import { Toaster } from "@/components/ui/toaster";
import { Toaster as Sonner } from "@/components/ui/sonner";
import { TooltipProvider } from "@/components/ui/tooltip";
import { QueryClient, QueryClientProvider } from "@tanstack/react-query";
import { HelmetProvider } from 'react-helmet-async';
import { Outlet } from 'react-router-dom';
import { Suspense } from 'react';
import LoadingFallback from './LoadingFallback';
const queryClient = new QueryClient\(\);
const RootLayout = \(\) => {
return \(
<QueryClientProvider client={queryClient}>
<HelmetProvider>
<TooltipProvider>
<Suspense fallback={<LoadingFallback />}>
<Outlet />
</Suspense>
<Toaster />
<Sonner />
</TooltipProvider>
</HelmetProvider>
</QueryClientProvider>
\);
};
export default RootLayout;
5. Update vite.config.ts
Add these configurations:
export default defineConfig\(\({ mode, isSsrBuild }\) => \({
// ... existing config
ssgOptions: {
entry: 'src/main.ssg.tsx',
script: 'async',
formatting: 'minify',
},
ssr: {
noExternal: \['react-helmet-async'\],
},
build: {
rollupOptions: {
output: isSsrBuild ? {} : {
manualChunks: {
'react-vendor': \['react', 'react-dom', 'react-router-dom'\],
'ui-vendor': \['@radix-ui/react-dialog', '@radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu'\],
},
},
},
},
}\)\);
6. Update vercel.json
CRITICAL: Remove any catch-all rewrite rule. Use this configuration:
{
"buildCommand": "npx vite-react-ssg build",
"outputDirectory": "dist",
"rewrites": \[
{ "source": "/sitemap.xml", "destination": "/sitemap.xml" },
{ "source": "/robots.txt", "destination": "/robots.txt" }
\],
"headers": \[
{
"source": "/\(.*\)",
"headers": \[
{ "key": "X-Robots-Tag", "value": "index, follow" },
{ "key": "X-Content-Type-Options", "value": "nosniff" }
\]
}
\],
"trailingSlash": false,
"cleanUrls": true
}
7. Keep src/main.tsx for development
The existing main.tsx should remain for local development with hot reloading. Use standard createBrowserRouter pattern there.
CRITICAL RULES:
Do NOT create separate entry-server.tsx and entry-client.tsx files - use single main.ssg.tsx
Do NOT add catch-all rewrites in vercel.json - they break SSG
EVERY route in your app MUST be listed in routes.ssg.tsx
For dynamic routes \(like blog posts\), use getStaticPaths:
{
path: 'blog/:slug',
Component: lazyPage\(\(\) => import\('./pages/BlogPost'\)\),
getStaticPaths: \(\) => [blogSlugs.map](http://blogSlugs.map)\(slug => `/blog/${slug}`\)
}
After deployment, verify SSG is working by viewing page source - you should see actual HTML content with data-server-rendered="true" attribute, NOT an empty <div id="root"/>.

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 756. Growing Mobile IV Therapy - Pure IV
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/growing-mobile-iv-therapy-pure-iv
Autor: joseph-lopez-4869  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hey everyone! I’m really liking this group so far, even in the cheaper tier. I’ve been in SEO a long time, and I love how Caleb keeps it direct and no-nonsense.

I wanted to get your opinions on something:
We run mobile IV companies in 7 states, and we plan to expand into another 12–13 states in the next 12 months. Everything is on one website: [www.pureiv.com](http://www.pureiv.com).

For a multi-state business like this:
[ul][li]Should we keep doing the same SEO strategy for every city/state page, or should we send most links and directories to the main homepage?[li]For Google Business Profiles \(GBP\), do we need one for every city, or should we only have profiles for major cities?[li]When we get backlinks \(like chamber of commerce links and local directories\), is it better to point them to [pureiv.com](http://pureiv.com) or to specific location pages?

Pure IV started as 6 websites, but we condensed to 1 website literally this week.

What’s the best practice for a mobile IV company with one website covering multiple states?

## 757. retheming the category homepage
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/retheming-the-category-homepage
Autor: marjohn-oviedo-9934  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Quick question on homepage H1 + retheming.
I understand retheming when the GBP category isn’t a perfect match \(ex: using Concrete Contractor while the business mainly does Foundation Repair / Waterproofing\).
What I’m stuck on is how that applies to the homepage H1.
URL + title tag including the category = makes sense.Body content/contextual mentions = makes sense.
But putting the GBP category directly in the homepage H1 feels off from a UX standpoint if customers don’t actually think of the business that way.
So just want to clarify before locking in the homepage:
Is the intent to:• Use the category mainly in supporting context \(subheadings, body, schema, internal links\),or• Literally include the category in the H1 even if it’s not the primary customer-facing term?
Want to make sure I structure the homepage correctly before building out Core 30.

My traffic value has gone from $1,200 to $15,000 in about the last month.

Most importantly, I am getting some phone calls.

Recovery is going great, Thanks to Caleb's advice and a lot of time and work.

Autor: karan-holland-4673  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

Hey team! Working on an ecommerce site \(nail care products, NZ-based\) and want to sanity-check my SEO plan. No local SEO needed since it's pure online sales.

My Plan:
Week 1-2: Foundation
[ul][li]Title tags & meta descriptions \(front-load target keyword "nail strengthening"\)[li]Image optimization + alt text[li]Schema markup \(Organization, Product, FAQ\)[li]Internal linking structure in Shopify

Week 3-4: Content
[ul][li]FAQ page targeting People Also Ask questions[li]3 initial blog posts around main keywords[li]Article schema on all posts

Ongoing:
[ul][li]2-3 blog posts/month[li]Backlink outreach \(have some solid connections lined up\)[li]Monitor GSC rankings

Do I actually need Ahrefs \($130/month\)? I've got Claude and ChatGPT for content/research, can I get away with cheaper tools or even free options

What am I missing in this plan? Any tools you couldn't live without?

## 765. New client's in the map pack but receives no calls from his GMB
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/new-clients-in-the-map-pack-but-receives-no-calls-from-his-gmb
Autor: christine-del-castillo-3298  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hi everyone! I'm currently working with a painting contractor in Cranberry Township, PA that's ranking #1-#3 for painter keywords in his immediate area but according to him, he's not getting any calls from Google.

I haven't done any optimizations yet since I just got access to his GMB this week and I'm a bit stuck on how to approach this 😅

Here are his GMB stats from November:

Phone click - 1
Website clicks - 23

He did get 2 leads \(form fills\) from his website.

I'm thinking one or more of these might be the reasons why he's not getting calls/leads:
[ul][li]One of his competitors on the map pack has 270 reviews while he has 45[li]His website's pretty dated and is pretty much just a portfolio site \(no title tags & meta descriptions, thin content, only 1 service page for all services, his homepage only has 1 section other than the hero\) so it might be affecting the conversion rate too[li]Possibly low search volume on the keywords[li]Maybe I made a mistake with choosing the keywords to use in the rank map? 😅

My questions:

[ol:1][li]Am I missing something else that could be the issue? If you were in my shoes, how would you approach fixing this problem? [li]Is there a way to verify if these keywords actually have decent search volume to make sure it's not the reason why he's not getting calls?[li]Did I use the correct keywords in setting up my rank maps?[li]Would adding new sections \(review, services, etc\) to his homepage, creating new service pages & adding title tags and meta descriptions to all of his pages count as dangerous changes to his site that could affect his rankings? I'm thinking of using the same builder and just adding to the live pages incrementally, instead of doing a complete overhaul.

Here are the links to his rank maps along with the keywords I've used:

Painters cranberry township
[https://app.leadsnap.com/rank-tracking/share/742358/8148d721-b738-41c8-a0c2-e8a8bb0f19c0](https://app.leadsnap.com/rank-tracking/share/742358/8148d721-b738-41c8-a0c2-e8a8bb0f19c0)

Painters near me
[https://app.leadsnap.com/rank-tracking/share/742352/06deb200-a7fb-41a0-9e8a-a4ae908d535e](https://app.leadsnap.com/rank-tracking/share/742352/06deb200-a7fb-41a0-9e8a-a4ae908d535e)

Exterior painting
[https://app.leadsnap.com/rank-tracking/share/742356/cd5607ec-00a9-4b55-97ec-cbe38a9a5ef6](https://app.leadsnap.com/rank-tracking/share/742356/cd5607ec-00a9-4b55-97ec-cbe38a9a5ef6)

Interior painting
[https://app.leadsnap.com/rank-tracking/share/742355/ef88b808-07d6-40e4-b2d0-b72aacfd4527](https://app.leadsnap.com/rank-tracking/share/742355/ef88b808-07d6-40e4-b2d0-b72aacfd4527)

Painting companies
[https://app.leadsnap.com/rank-tracking/share/742354/88838bdd-57bf-4f9b-8aac-4c599cf0c53f](https://app.leadsnap.com/rank-tracking/share/742354/88838bdd-57bf-4f9b-8aac-4c599cf0c53f)

Sorry this got a bit long, guys! I'm kinda new to SEO and I feel like I might be overthinking this a bit 😶‍🌫️ but I would really appreciate any guidance on how to best approach this. Thank you so much in advance!

## 780. New Call Recording in the Classroom
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/new-call-recording-in-the-classroom
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles + comentó  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hey everyone! The recording from Tuesday's call \(Dec 16th\) is now up in the Classroom.

What we covered:
→ Live audit of a brand new chiropractor site in Chicago \(2 months old, already breaking into top 5!\)
→ Schema 101 - what goes where and why ChatGPT actually cares more about it than Google does
→ The 4 links every page needs \(and why most people skip the hardest one\)
→ Multi-location site structure - how to build Core 30 without creating 150 pages
→ Alaska fishing business case study - sometimes your nav bar is working against you
→ Why that competitor with no website and 4 reviews is outranking you \(hint: it's not something you can copy\)

Quick wins from this call:

✅ Your domain doesn't need to match your business name - just keep your branding consistent
✅ Stop using exact-match PAA questions - have AI rewrite them first
✅ Always run rank maps during business hours \(Google tanks your rankings when you're closed\)
✅ Gemini 3 Pro actually recommends real GBP categories - Claude and ChatGPT make them up

Head to the Classroom to watch:
[https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/52e05dc7?md=a2fc775d268342049265d15041c2e0e4](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/52e05dc7?md=a2fc775d268342049265d15041c2e0e4)

Drop any follow-up questions below 👇

## 786. What Next?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/what-next
Autor: ashley-brown-1503  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I have been working with a great client but I don't know what to tackle next so that I can keep our engagement going. Within 2 weeks of doing their initial GBP clean up and website alignment for a website I built for them in October, they have already reached top 3 in map pack for the keyword "Insulation Contractor Cumberland RI". First rank map was from 11/21/25 - Average Rank was 6.87. Second rank map is from 12/13/25 - Average Rank is 2.72. I'm wondering where I should go from here so that I can keep charging them. Should I work on increasing their average rank by turning the 4's and 5's into 3's with geographical content? ## 794. Images order on the google business profile. - Todays zoom meeting
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/images-order-on-the-google-business-profile-todays-zoom-meeting
Autor: jeremy-bengtson-6101  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
To whoever asked about the ordering of images and if you could arrange the images for your company profile.

Here is the breakdown of how Google Maps decides which images to show you and where "relevancy" actually kicks in.
1. The "Cover Photo" \(Standardized\)
When you click on a business, the large main image at the top is generally the same for everyone.
[ul][li]How it works: Google’s algorithm picks the "best" photo based on objective metrics: resolution, focus, brightness, and—most importantly—user engagement.1 If 1,000 people clicked on a specific photo of a burger, Google assumes that is the most relevant photo for everyone and will make it the cover photo.[li]User Personalization: None. Google does not currently change this main image based on your demographics or history.
2. Search Results Previews \(Contextually Dynamic\)
This is where "relevancy" happens. If you are scrolling through a list of businesses after a search, the thumbnail image you see next to the business name can change based on your specific search terms.
[ul][li]Example:[li]Why: Google's AI \(Vision AI\) scans images to understand what is in them \(food, drink, seating, wheelchair ramps\). It then attempts to match the image to the intent of your search keyword.
3. The "Highlights" or "Vibe" Section \(Emerging Personalization\)
Google has recently rolled out AI-powered features like "Vibe Check" and "Photo Highlights."
[ul][li]How it works: These sections use machine learning to summarize the "feel" of a place. While currently mostly static \(showing what is generally popular\), Google has announced plans to make these more personalized using Gemini AI.[li]Future State: In the near future, if your Google account history shows you frequently review vegan restaurants, the "Highlights" section might prioritize photos of menus or vegetable dishes, but this is not yet the standard behavior for the main photo gallery.

Google Maps personalizes which businesses it ranks for you \(based on your history\), and it personalizes images based on what you just typed \(context\), but it does not yet radically change the visual presentation of a business profile based on your personal identity.

═══════════════════════════════════════

Autor: brian-kiselstein-6058  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Got a GBP category situation with my landscape client and I'm second-guessing myself.
Where we're at:
[ul][li]Primary: Landscape Designer[li]Secondary: Landscaper, Landscape Architect

So here's the thing - dude does environmental consulting \(shoreline restoration, wetland stuff, native plant consulting\) and I'm rebuilding his site around three main silos: \(Landscape Design, Landscape Installation, and Environmental Consulting.\)

Here's where I'm torn:
I want to add "Environmental Consulting" as a secondary category but I'm nervous about touching the GBP at all because I don't want to fuck up his rankings. He doesn't have a TON of local visibility yet \(like 2 green dots on his heat map\)- so is NOW actually the better time to make changes while he's still building momentum? Or do I leave it alone?

Also, "Landscape Architect" feels redundant as hell with "Landscape Designer" already there. Should I ditch it? Or keep it for keyword coverage and just accept the overlap?

My thinking:
With Landscape Designer, Landscaper, and Environmental Consulting, we've got more than enough to tackle the core 30 services. The Landscape Architect category feels like unnecessary, but I don't know if removing it will hurt him.

So TLDR....what Im actually debating:

[ol:1][li]Add Environmental Consulting NOW \(while rankings aren't established\) or wait until the site's fully done?[li]Remove Landscape Architect now, later, or never?

Thanks, guys and apologies for the novel here lol

Autor: colton-hibbert-1693  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
[@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) I just got a massive client that operates over 60 GBPs throughout Michigan, and I'm working on doing the Core 30 for all of them 🥳. The issue I'm running into is that many of the locations reside in the same city, like Ann Arbor, MI.

For the GBP naming structure, I'm using \[Client Name\] - \[City\] \(\[Location Name\]\), since they have a master spreadsheet that usually differentiates specific locations by neighborhood or street. However, I'm trying to figure out the best way to structure the relevant Core 30's, since I assume we don't want 2 structures built around /car-wash-ann-arbor-mi for cannibalization or duplication purposes.

I see 3 options - which would you pick, or would you do something different entirely?

[ol:1][li]Keep standard /category-city URL structure \(/car-wash-ann-arbor-mi\), doesn't matter as long as LocalBusiness schema is unique on the relevant GBP landing pages[li]Go with the location name only, based around the neighborhood or street, i.e. /car-wash-jackson-rd[li]Use the city AND location name, i.e. /car-wash-jackson-rd-ann-arbor-mi

Appreciate the help and any insight!

## 809. Core30 Works
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/core30-works
Autor: christian-wert-2241  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Implemented core30 with a client and 30 days later my client said, "There is so much work we can barely keep up. This is almost ridiculous. You're a genius!" All in the Portland Metro area, for a Plumber, where competition is VERY strong. Housecall Pro shows 53K booked appointments over the next 3 weeks. That's more money booked in the past 30 days then they actually made in the prior 3 months.

I just got off a Zoom with the client who wanted to double his retainer if I would agree to not help any other plumbers in Portland. 🤷

Oh yeah, 100% AI. No I don't mean I used AI to write content and then built pages from that content. I used Page Generator Pro to dynamically build out all the category and services pages and do all the SEO, internal linking, etc., using directory structures. So 3-4 hours of research and setup and then click a button and the Core30 site pages are all built out automagically in WordPress. Not only that but I can produce hundreds more services pages in minutes and drip them to the site over time. I feel like a mad scientist over here!

Now that I have proven my method for Core30 works I can implement a Core30/60/100/1000 on clients sites in just a few hours from start to finish 😉

Merry Christmas me 😁

Thanks [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) 👈

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 811. How Do You Handle Multiple Website Builders as an Agency
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/how-do-you-handle-multiple-website-builders-as-an-agency
Autor: daniel-tenney-7382  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Our current preferred website stack is Wordpress with Bricks Builder, hosted on a LiteSpeed server.

When we bring on clients, some of them are hosted already with Wix, Wordpress \(Built with Elementor, Divi, etc\), Webflow, raw HTML/CSS, PHP, etc.

What is your process with these clients? Do you just work in whatever stack they currently have or do you do a complete site rebuild \(only maintaining links/content already ranking\)?

For a lot of the clients we bring on, they already are not ranking well, so a rebuild doesn't hurt their current seo rankings \(because there are none to begin with\). At the same time, rebuilding the website takes time to clone in a different stack.

We've had some \(not the majority\) that are ranking decently and have just built within their existing stack, but usually it's tough trying to build on someone else's implementation that is slow, outdated, or not setup properly.

Another concern here is trying to find a dev that's jack of all trades and knows all these different systems/platforms/plugins.

How are you guys handling this?

═══════════════════════════════════════

Additionally, Search Console’s day-to-day data refresh seems significantly slower than usual. There’s also news about an upcoming update: [https://www.seroundtable.com/google-core-updates-not-done-40574.html](https://www.seroundtable.com/google-core-updates-not-done-40574.html)
Has anyone else experienced sudden drops like this, where you’ve been holding a steady 3.5 average position for months and then suddenly find yourself at positions 8 or 9? The most significant change we made last week was to fix trailing slashes. We were doing very well so far.

## 815. Business has a physical address but does not accept walk ins - online only?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/business-has-a-physical-address-but-does-not-accept-walk-ins-online-only
Autor: conor-smart-1294  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hi everyone, I am new here and had a question. My business sells online only but we do have a physical address in the United States - specifically Daleware. We want to take advantage of Local SEO but we also do not want to infringe on Google policies for GMB \(GBP\) business listings.

My question is: Can I create a Daleware GMB listing even if I do not accept walk ins? I have a physical address in this state but it's a mailing address only and none of my staff work there. We are fully remote.

If no, would the only option be to create a GBP profile with the physical location listed then hide the address and ensure I am listed as a "Service Area Business" only. I know this is more work but I'd rather do the SEO work over time than get suspended due to inaccurate GMB listing.

To note - I also will not be able to complete the video verification as I am not in the country.

I have done some research on this and the answer was below but i'm hoping someone else can provide some guidance on this if they are in the same situation.

Your best option \(summary answer\)

"Yes, you can create a Google Business Profile only as a Service Area Business \(SAB\) for Delaware — using your Delaware address for verification but hiding it from public display.
That keeps you fully compliant and safe from suspension.
Your setup should look like this:
[ul][li]Business type: Service Area Business[li]Service areas: Delaware \(optionally expand nationwide later\)[li]Address: Verified privately \(hidden publicly\)[li]Category: Custom T-shirt Store / Uniform Supplier / Screen Printing Service[li]Verification: Postcard sent to Delaware address[li]Profile display: “Serving customers in Delaware and across the United States”

I welcome your help,
Thanks

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 817. December 9th Call Recording Now Available!
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/december-9th-call-recording-now-available
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Just uploaded this week's call to the Classroom.

Big topics we covered:
→ Hiring developers on Upwork \(and why $12/hour + QC beats $50/hour "experts"\)
→ What happens when you change your GBP primary category \(spoiler: expect a ranking hit\)
→ How to rank in cities 40+ miles away from your actual location
→ The simple Google Sheet system we use to manage all client content
→ Which AI platforms care about which review sources \(ChatGPT loves Reddit, Google AI Overview loves Yelp\)
→ Building your agency team in the right order

One thing that came up multiple times: Proximity is still the #1 GBP ranking factor. If you're struggling to rank in a distant city, sometimes the answer is just getting a location there rather than fighting an uphill battle with content alone.

Also showed a live example of competitor websites in Biloxi, Mississippi where 2 out of 3 top results had broken websites. Sometimes your competition makes it easy. 😂

Recording is in the Classroom → Look for "2024-12-09 Hiring Devs, GBP Categories"
Here's the link: [https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/52e05dc7?md=2187524be47e473b82c3b47f8231989b](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/52e05dc7?md=2187524be47e473b82c3b47f8231989b)

## 818. Link prioritization
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/link-prioritization-2
Autor: stefan-girndt-6973  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I'm helping someone rank their GMB.
They don't have a lot of budget and would have to save up to join the local chamber of commerce.
And there is the cost for the LeadSnap citations and PBNs to every page I build.

So my question is, in which order should they invest in those links?
I assume LeadSnap citations \(because it's just $60\) > Chamber > PBNs
Or is the chamber link so powerful that it makes sense to wait with the citations until they got that taken care of?

Would like to hear what you think, [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) [@Elliot Berard](obj://user/b3625451747b472ebed209d60b70f050)

## 824. Big Multi Location SEO
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/big-multi-location-seo
Autor: daniel-tenney-7382  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I know the multi-location site structure should follow something like this \(Image attached\). Unless they are big cities that are not within close proximity, then the GBP1 page should be NAP agnostic and general content and then build location Core 30 from there.

I was trying to find some bigger multi location sites to see how they do it and one I found that ranks very well organically in the map pack is [https://a1garage.com/](https://a1garage.com/).

I noticed they left their version of the core 30 NAP agnostic \(non location specific\) for the initial category and service pages, then built out location pages. In each location page, it internally links to the general service page and there is not one that is specific to that location \(Screenshot showing Georgia location page internal linking service to non location based service page\).

This way seems more scalable for larger locations with a lot less work than building Core 30 for each location. What are some cons you see about this approach vs building out core 30 for all separate GBP location pages?

I ask this because I have a plumber with locations in Florida and in California looking to share the same site, just pointing to separate GBP location pages. They are looking at opening a third location in Texas. Just weighing pros and cons here on amount of work vs reward and the best way to structure their site.

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 827. SAB with or without address
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/sab-with-or-without-address
Autor: shareef-huddle-7505  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Okay, I know that everything says, gotta have an address. I have been going back and forth for two months for a company that is brand new this year. He did not want to use his address. I went through the GBP Optimization and Core 30 and let him know that rankings would definitely change if we could use his address. He finally agreed and here is the results. Friday ran scan, Sunday added address, this morning, Monday, ran scan. I never really realized how much of a difference until now. The proof is in the pudding ;\)

## 828. December 2nd Call Recording Now Available
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/december-2nd-call-recording-now-available
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Just dropped the latest weekly call in the Classroom.

This one covered a lot of ground — from what to do when you're feeling overwhelmed and second-guessing everything, to why I think most competitive research is a complete waste of time \(and what to focus on instead\).

Some highlights from this week:

→ The real reason service area businesses struggle to rank \(and how much extra work they actually require\)
→ Why a perfect 5-star rating might be hurting your client's conversions
→ Live site review of an Alaska fishing charter — they were in way better shape than expected
→ How to think about new sites and why sprinting to publish everything on day one doesn't make sense
→ The one type of client I almost always avoid taking on

Also got into service page depth, schema for every page, platform migrations, and how to unlock GBP services when your category doesn't seem to allow them.

\[Watch the full recording here\]
[https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/52e05dc7?md=427651b160d84cbc942d423664c9417a](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/52e05dc7?md=427651b160d84cbc942d423664c9417a)

## 838. Adding dedicated "Locations" Page
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/adding-dedicated-locations-page
Autor: scott-desalvo-1096  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
In my complete site, demolition and manual rebuild, I followed Caleb's excellent advice as to site structure. However, I did not build a dedicated "locations" page.

Instead, my locations are directly navigable from the homepage. Then the topic plus location specific pages are linked from the main location page for each location. I hope this makes sense.

I figured this would give me some leeway, and I would eventually create a location's page once I spread to multiple locations.

For a little background, I added hundreds of location pages very poorly years ago and so ended up with ranked pages here and there for service plus location. Nothing spectacular nothing generating a ton of traffic but when you add it all up, it amounts to something. And I used to have a poorly ranked locations page.

I redirected or recreated a lot of those pages, but abandoned more than I created or redirected because they just didn't have any juice. But I am noticing more and more locations popping up here and there and being ranked or I'm getting impressions for two or three different service plus same location.

I think it would make sense for me to move forward more expeditiously and creating new location page and then building out sub location pages

Would the new structure be: Home - Locations - Location 1 Hub, Location 2 Hub \(etc\) and then Location 1 Hub - service plus location 1, Service plus location 2 and so on?

The site is recovering slowly as expected but I'd like to set myself up for success.

Thoughts you can share? Thanks in advance.

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 839. This Week's Call Recording Is Live!
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/this-weeks-call-recording-is-live
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Smaller group this week \(Thanksgiving timing!\), but packed with solid Q&A.

A few things we covered:

📍 When two businesses CAN share the same address for separate GBPs \(and what makes it legit vs. risky\)
📍 The case for keeping multiple services under ONE domain instead of splitting them up—even when your client really wants separate websites
📍 A real-time hotel SEO breakdown—why this member is crushing organic but struggling in the map pack, and what to do about it
📍 The "50% rule" for deciding when to shift from topical content to geographic content
📍 Why I hate website redesigns \(and what to do if your client insists on one anyway\)

Also talked through dental SEO site structure, franchise GBP challenges, schema for multi-location businesses, and how to handle clients with multiple phone numbers on their site.
One thing that came up multiple times: Stop worrying about duplicate content. With AI, you have infinite unique content available. Similar page structures across geo pages are fine—it's the actual content that needs to be different.

Link to recording in the Classroom 👇
[https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/52e05dc7?md=52da444f27f24ec18b5bf5b19c709549](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/52e05dc7?md=52da444f27f24ec18b5bf5b19c709549)

See you next Tuesday—December 2nd!

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 840. Question on Address setup

Autor: lokesh-chand-8826  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

When setting up a Multi Location, Google Business profile setup.

What address would suffice for Google business.

I have seen in some cases that a dedicated office suite with a unique suite number works.

But I also see shared co-working spaces, where a business will "share" a suite number, can work, but can also get flagged and suspended.

What would the "safe" address type to get, and ensure that profile does not get suspended, when asked to verify by Google.

I know PO Boxes and virtual addresses are a hard no from Google, and I also know Home Addresses can only qualify as SABs, and not Map Pins.
