## 853. Categories and Services for GBP
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/categories-and-services-for-gbp
Autor: chris-white-1808  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hey [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c)! As you might recall I'm working on my first local seo client which is a roofing business. He just approved the list of categories and services below. He also told me his critical/high margin services are:

[ol:1][li]Residential and Commercial Insurance [li]Residential and Commercial Storm Damage

Based on his critical services feedback I'm thinking I take the following 3 services from the master list below and link those from the home page per your training.

[ol:1][li]Residential insurance roof claims assistance[li]Commercial insurance roof claims assistance[li]Residential storm damage roof repair

MAIN QUESTION: Before I start writing content can you look at how this is organized and let me know if you see anything that you would consider doing differently to include those critical services above?

Primary Category #1
Roofing contractor

Services
Residential roof inspection
Residential roof replacement
Residential insurance roof claims assistance
Residential emergency roof repair
Residential roof restoration
Residential roof coating
Residential roof estimates
Residential storm damage roof repair
Residential storm damage roof replacement
Residential hail damage roof repair
Residential hail damage roof replacement
Residential wind damage roof repair
Residential wind damage roof replacement

Secondary Category #2
Construction company \(*using this for commercial roof positioning\)

Services
Commercial roof inspection
Commercial roof replacement
Commercial roof installation
Commercial new construction roofs
Commercial insurance roof claims assistance
Commercial flat roof repair
Commercial flat roof installation
Commercial TPO roofing installation
Commercial EPDM roofing installation
Commercial emergency roof repair
Commercial storm damage roof replacement
Commercial hail damage roof repair
Commercial hail damage roof replacement
Commercial wind damage roof repair
Commercial wind damage roof replacement

Secondary Category #3
Solar energy company \(*using this for solar\)

Services
Commercial solar panel design and installation
Commercial solar shingles design and installation
Residential solar panel design and installation
Residential solar shingles design and installation

Secondary Category #4
Waterproofing service

Services
Commercial flat roof waterproofing
Commercial roof leak repair
Commercial storm damage roof repair
Residential roof waterproofing
Residential roof leak detection

Secondary Category #5
Gutter service \(jobs that could lead to roof replacements\)

Services
Residential gutter installation
Commercial gutter installation

## 854. Is this possible?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/to-many-categories
Autor: christian-wert-2241  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
[@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c)

I have a client that want's to combine 4 businesses into a single website.

Their businesses are:

Electrician \(Simpson Electric\)
HVAC \(Simpson HVAC\)
Fireplace Services \(Your Fireplace Pros\)
Chore Services \(Chore Boy's\)

Why?

They don't want:

Multiple GBPs
Multiple HouseCallPro Accounts
Multiple Sites to manage
Multiple Review capture/monitor services
etc...

Instead, they are asking to put all 4 services \(businesses\) under a single parent company:

Simpson Property Services

I am having a hard time envisioning how this is going to work since I can't find a good category to encompass all of these business? And then secondary categories for each of the 4 services listed above.

So I guess I'm looking for some feedback.

Does anyone know a good category I could use as primary in a situation like this?

would a set up like this actually work with core30?

Any other advice?

## 855. This Week's Call: Content Strategy, Local Authority & GBP Optimization
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/this-weeks-call-content-strategy-local-authority-gbp-optimization
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles + comentó  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hey everyone!

This week's call was packed with practical local SEO strategies you can use right away. We spent quality time diving deep into real client scenarios and troubleshooting actual ranking challenges.

🔥 Top Highlights:
Internal Linking & Topical Authority - We covered proper internal linking structure \(stop linking your headings!\), building topical authority through connected content, and how to improve rankings without messing up what's already working.

Content Strategy That Works - Quality beats quantity every time. We talked about the right pace for content creation \(hint: it's not hundreds of articles\), how to build content clusters that actually boost rankings, and why you need to give content time to work before panicking.

Spa Business Deep Dive - Extended consultation on building local authority for a spa in Athens, Georgia. Covered everything from local citations to chamber of commerce memberships, content velocity, and using rank maps to track real progress.

GBP Ranking Reality Check - Reminder that proximity is still king. Service areas don't directly impact rankings the way most people think. Your primary category matters way more than your secondary categories.

💡 Key Insight:
The spa consultation really drove home an important point: you can't just keep creating content without stopping to measure results. Publish in batches \(5-10 articles max per month\), run your rank maps every two weeks, and adjust based on what you're actually seeing. Stop guessing and start measuring.

🛠 Tools Mentioned:
[ul][li]Claude AI for content \(still beating ChatGPT for SEO quality\)[li]Perplexity or Gemini for finding local link opportunities[li]Lead Snap for rank mapping[li]Schema markup implementation

📚 Find It All Here:
The full recording and detailed summary are now in the Classroom under "2025-11-18 Content Strategy & Local SEO"

Whether you're working on service business rankings, building local authority, or just trying to figure out the right content strategy, there's something valuable in this one.

Direct Link: [https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/52e05dc7?md=ff67e37c7ed346daaccf2a34be2ef77a](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/52e05dc7?md=ff67e37c7ed346daaccf2a34be2ef77a)

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 856. 90-DAY CHALLENGE UPDATE | WEEK 1-2 | LOCAL SEO AGENCY
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/90-day-challenge-update-week-1-2-local-seo-agency
Autor: raphael-rechberger-4961  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
What's up SEO Masters - Quick update on my challenge:

WHAT I'VE ACTUALLY BUILT THE LAST 9 DAYS:

This isn't just a landing page. This is a full-stack automated acquisition & delivery system:

🔥 FRONTEND CONVERSION FUNNEL:

✅ 15-Question Assessment with smart scoring algorithm \(0-100 points\)
[ul][li]Q1-Q10: Scoring questions \(max 100 points\)[li]Q11: Lead capture form \(business name, email, city\)[li]Q12-Q15: Qualification \(industry, desired outcome, main obstacle, budget indicator\)

✅ Dynamic Result Pages \(personalized by score + business info\)
[ul][li]Score visualization with performance category[li]Personalized video strategy section \(VSL placeholder ready\)[li]Score-based package recommendations[li]Real-time social proof notifications[li]Exit-intent recovery modals

✅ Package Selection System
[ul][li]STARTER: €284/month[li]GROWTH: €599/month \(Most Popular - 61% choose this\)[li]PREMIUM: €1,299/month[li]ENTERPRISE: €1,999/month[li]Monthly + Yearly billing \(10 months pricing = 2 free months\)[li]Mobile-optimized sticky CTAs

✅ Multi-language support \(German/English/Spanish throughout\)

⚡ BACKEND INFRASTRUCTURE \(32 Edge Functions\):

Payment Processing \(8 functions\):
[ul][li]Stripe + PayPal integration with webhooks[li]Subscription management \(create/cancel/upgrade\)[li]One-time service payments[li]Automated payment validation + error handling

Email Automation \(8 functions\):
[ul][li]Welcome emails \(personalized by package + referral status\)[li]Onboarding sequences[li]Support ticket system[li]Referral notifications[li]Automated email queue processing

Referral System V2 \(4 functions\):
[ul][li]Unique codes per customer[li]Automated tracking + commission calculation \(10%\)[li]Referee discount \(20% off first payment\)[li]Referrer rewards \(10% commission OR free months\)[li]Admin approval workflow

Service Automation \(7 functions\):
[ul][li]Citation building automation[li]GBP audit automation[li]Monthly task generation \(based on package tier\)[li]Monthly report generation[li]Task assignment + tracking

Churn Prevention \(2 functions\):
[ul][li]Engagement score tracking[li]Automated onboarding reminders

Database Architecture:
[ul][li]assessments: Every lead captured with UTM tracking, score, recommendation[li]profiles: Customer data + onboarding progress[li]subscriptions: Stripe/PayPal subscription tracking[li]referral_sales: Commission tracking + approval workflow[li]analytics_events: Every click, every conversion tracked[li]customer_metrics: Monthly performance tracking \(GBP views, clicks, rankings\)[li]customer_monthly_reports: Automated PDF report generation[li]admin_tasks: Task management for service delivery[li]And 15+ more tables...

Customer Portal:
[ul][li]Onboarding wizard \(collects all info I need for delivery\)[li]Progress dashboard \(shows monthly deliverables by package\)[li]Monthly report viewer \(automated SEO performance reports\)[li]Referral dashboard \(track commissions, generate codes\)[li]Analytics integration placeholders \(GA4, GSC, GMB\)

🎨 CONVERSION OPTIMIZATION \(THIS WEEK\):

Big shoutout to [@Elliot Berard](https://www.skool.com/@b3625451747b472ebed209d60b70f050?g=ai-seo-mastery-pro) for the benefit-messaging tip! 🙏

Rewrote EVERYTHING from feature-focused → outcome-focused:
[ul][li]✅ Email templates \(was talking tech, now talking results + timeline\)[li]✅ Exit-intent modals \(was pushing discounts, now showing lost opportunity + position estimate\)[li]✅ Package descriptions \(was listing features, now showing ROI timelines + ideal customer\)[li]✅ Hero CTAs \(4 variants tested: pain/competition/urgency/outcome\)

WHERE I'M AT NOW:
Platform = LIVE ✅ Funnel = WORKS ✅Payment = INTEGRATED \(Stripe + PayPal\) ✅ Backend = RUNNING \(32 automated functions\) ✅

BUT NOT READY FOR PAID TRAFFIC YET ⚠️

WHAT'S MISSING \(NEXT 1-2 WEEKS\):
[ol:1][li]Produce VSL Videos \(scripts done, just need to film\) → Without VSL = 50-70% conversion loss according to tests[li]Manual Fulfillment Playbook → Starting manual for first 5-10 customers → Automation infrastructure is READY, but margins don't work without some manual QA first[li]Facebook Ads Setup → Pixel installed, campaigns drafted → Starting with €50-100/day test budget

TIMELINE:
[ul][li]Next Week: VSL production + fulfillment process finalized[li]Week 4: A/B Testing and Launch first Facebook campaign & Working the Onboarding and Fullfillment Processes[li]Week 5-6: Scale to profitable \(target: €3-5k MRR = 5-8 customers\) 

LESSONS LEARNED:
❌ I over-engineered Week 1-2 a little bit I guess 😁→ could've started with simpler MVP ✅ BUT now foundation is SOLID → can scale fast when traffic hits ✅ Having referral system + analytics baked in from Day 1 = smart move

NEXT MILESTONE: First 3 paying customers by end of Week 4. 🎯

Question for the group: Who's got tips for Local SEO + Service Business ads on Facebook? What angle converts best for you? Pain? Competition? Urgency? Drop below!

Appreciate your inputs a lot!

Hot lead landing page: [https://local-seo-audit.auranex.ai/](https://local-seo-audit.auranex.ai/)
Cold Lead Landing Page \(Suckers that geat eaten by my facebook lead filter :D\) : [https://local-seo-audit.auranex.ai/lead-filter](https://local-seo-audit.auranex.ai/lead-filter)

Pictures added: Client Dashboard and Admin Dashboard for delivery: - Let me know please if I am obviously missing something crucial here...

Final Question to my challange sparrings partner and SEO guru :\) [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) - U think that thing can convert or am I missing something?

You know something… for years I struggled to figure out what actually moves the needle with a Google Business Profile. I tried different strategies, tested a bunch of ideas, and slowly started realizing that the website connected to the listing plays a huge role in how well that listing ranks.

Once I got into this training, it confirmed everything I’d already been seeing on my own. Caleb's breakdowns just connected the dots even more. So thank you, Caleb. The training has been solid. I’m still learning, implementing, and dialing things in.

But now, after all these years of messing with this stuff, I finally feel 100 percent confident that I can rank a Google Business Profile for a local business. That confidence shows when I’m talking to business owners, and it feels good to know I’m on the right track.

## 862. Custom Google Map Creation?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/custom-google-map-creation
Autor: belynda-scarsbrook-1344  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I came across this custom made google map whilst doing competitor research for a HVAC company in Melbourne.

When you click on every single one of those dots, it has a different latitude, longitude, and keyword. The keywords are repeated around the circles: heating and cooling Melbourne, air conditioner, hot water services, evaporative cooling, split systems, ducted heating. Repeat.

There's also some points dropped in there too. If you look closely, you can see the letters A-H. Each letter has a keyword. He has 38 location pages on his site, most of which have this map embedded \(see 2nd pic\).

Has anyone ever seen this?

When I compared his GBP to my clients, they have the same primary categories, we have more services filled out with descriptions, the same number of reviews, and his last update posted was Jan 2025 with a stock photo. Could this strategy be something to consider? Heating and Cooling in Melbourne \(hot summers, cold winters\) is VERY competitive.

## 869. Just Joined, Thanks for Having Me
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/just-joined-thanks-for-having-me
Autor: john-brockway-7092  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Just officially joined after Caleb's zoom call! I've been watching your YouTube videos on repeat, taking diligent notes, learning something new every time I watch them, and of course implementing.

My concern at this point is getting information overwhelm, as I now have access to even more material than before... I really need to focus on action and accountability \(a big part of why I joined this community\)

Optimized most of my GBP \(still need to add a few "products" and likely need to remove the stock
photos and replace with actual photos or AI photos, based on what I'm learning from Caleb\).

Started Leadsnap citations about 2 weeks ago, 17 live, 18 submitted, 13 updating

I'm still working on my "Core 30", or in my case, more like Core 60. I'm about 6 more pages away from having those pages done, optimized with your local SEO prompt for the content, filtered through AI detector, and optimized with Page Optimizer Pro for local SEO.

I think I wore Claude out at the $20/mo rate, as I was pumping out content faster than it wanted, so I upgraded to the $100/mo plan.

In total, it takes me about 1 hour per page to do this, as I'm also scrutinizing the content for accuracy and making edits and probably not as AI proficient as I could be. The plan is to be done with my Core 30 this weekend, then plan on doing the Screaming Frog audit for internal links. I've got a close to baseline local maps report, that I was doing in [LocalDominator.co](http://LocalDominator.co), but it seems you're leaning towards leadsnap now to monitor this. I've got about 20 keywords I'm keeping tabs on \(all pulled from my GBP\), and running the report bi-weekly. I'd like to run it more often, but it seems cost prohibitive to do so for 20 keywords.

Happy to be here. Looking forward to sharing my success stories with you.

I'll jump in and help as much as I can.

═══════════════════════════════════════

Autor: pat-friedl-9948  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

OK, I understand needing the main category as the H1 on the page, but this is an odd niche. It's legacy media to digital conversion, and Google simply doesn't have categories that work for it. You can pretty much pick these:

Video Production Service \(main category\)
Video Editing Service
Film Production Company
Photo Restoration Service
Video Duplication Service

So the services, like "8mm film to digital", "VHS to DVD Transfer" are much more important.

## 877. Local vs National Strategy
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/local-vs-national-strategy
Autor: iman-diaz-8339  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hey everyone - I had a conversation last night with a doctor and his wife. They are a aesthetics & regenerative medicine clinic. Doing pretty cool stuff for patients.

The do 80% of their business right now from local customers in their area. Given the philosphy taught here - I am relatively comfortable with what to do, check, etc.

The question comes to the other 20% of their business - they get from national / international patients.
They have three domains.

I am trying to wrap my head around how to suggest a plan for them.

right now I am thinking:

Domain 1. local focus that ties to their GBP and optimize, structure their content to help their GBP etc.
Domain 2. national focus on non local terms / keywords so it's not completeing with local doctors for services so their domain isn't fighting for real estate with GBP listing for those other practices.

wanted to bouce this thought process with the more seasoned folks here - thank you for your input

## 879. Designer Debates
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/designer-debates
Autor: mark-farrow-6630  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I've been hyperlinking to service pages from homepage with contextual links but my designer wants to put a "learn more" or "View Service" link under the 50-75 words to assist with user experience. I get that BUT...If I only do it that way we lose the contextual value, and if I do it both ways that just adds additional links diluting each links power into the service page? Overthinking this, what are your thoughts?

## 888. NEW CLASSROOM: YouTube Reality - My Complete Process for Building a Channel
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/new-classroom-youtube-reality-my-complete-process-for-building-a-channel
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Alright, quick announcement.

A lot of you have been asking me about YouTube - how I built my channel, how it drives clients to my agency, whether it's worth the effort.
So I made a full course breaking down my exact process.

Important disclaimer upfront: I'm not claiming to be a YouTube expert. I've only done this once - for my own channel. This is just me laying out how I do it, what's worked, and what I've learned. Take it for what it is.

Here's what's inside:

Module 1: Foundation & Mindset
[ul][li]The reality of building a channel \(and why most quit at month 6\)[li]How to define your lane so every video actually compounds[li]The metrics that actually matter \(hint: not just views\)

Module 2: Setup & Systems
[ul][li]My studio setup \(and why starting beats perfection\)[li]The consistency systems that keep you publishing when results are slow

Module 3: Content Creation Process
[ul][li]How I find topics using outlier research \(stealing what already works\)[li]My title formulas that balance search + clicks[li]The brain dump process \(getting your expertise out of your head\)[li]My exact AI prompts for outlining and scripting[li]How I finish scripts without overthinking

Module 4: Production
[ul][li]My filming process start to finish

Who this is for: If you're thinking about starting a YouTube channel to attract clients \(not to become a YouTuber\), this is your roadmap.

Who this isn't for: If you want viral growth hacks or shortcuts, you'll be disappointed. This is about building a sustainable client acquisition channel.

No extra charge or anything for this. Maybe you'll find it valuable. Maybe you won't. Either way is okay with me - but let me know any constructive feedback or additions that would make it better for you.

The classroom is live now in the course section.

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 902. GBP Major Changes - no trigger for re-verification
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/gbp-major-changes-no-trigger-for-re-verification
Autor: mark-farrow-6630  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I took a client that needed a new website rebrand as he was leaving a franchise, so perfect timing to create a fresh website with Core 30. On the GBP we changed the primary category and removed one and added two additional. We changed the name, logo, the website URL, product card Url's, the booking link, removed some recent posts. AND all this triggered NO VERIFICATION. 🤷‍♂️ I made the changes on the front end of the GBP not thru the Lead Snap API.

This was a service based GBP, and many competitors also don't have physical addresses.

I was super surprised as I was sure we would have to go thru Verification. Its been 24 hours, I'll update the post if that changes. Will also share some results as they improve.

## 910. Core 30 Notes from Caleb's Video
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/core-30-notes-from-caleb-video
Autor: anand-maheshwari-2888  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
This one video from [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) opened my eyes
[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xMNaUDv5Nlg](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xMNaUDv5Nlg)

This one was I believe one of the best Local SEO video I have ever watched already asked my team to watch this 7-10 times and prepare notes and re-watch as many times as you want to. As Caleb have been explaining every dimension of that in this 20 min.

Here are the notes of me from this:

A. Google Business Profile \(GBP\) Workflow
---
This is your foundation. Complete these steps first, as your website structure will be built to mirror this setup.
A.1\] What to make sure: You have one Primary Category, 2-3 Secondary Categories, and 20-30 specific Services listed in your GBP.
Why to make sure: This structure is the blueprint for your website. Every category and service listed here will become a dedicated page on your site, creating a 1:1 match that Google loves.

A.2\] Set Your Website Link
What to make sure: The "website" field in your GBP profile links directly to your website's homepage.
Why to make sure: This homepage is now your "GBP Landing Page." This is the most important page on your site, and its relevance passes directly to your GBP, boosting its ranking.

A.3\] Ensure Operational Readiness \(Ongoing\)
[ol:1][li]What to make sure: You answer every single phone call that comes from your GBP.[li]Why to make sure: This is a critical "goal completion" signal. Missed calls tell Google you failed to help the user, which is a massive negative signal that will lower your rank.

B. Website Workflow
---
Follow these steps in order to build a site that perfectly supports your GBP.

B.1\] Stop and Audit \(Content Cleanup\)
What to make sure: You stop writing generic weekly blog posts and audit your existing content. Delete irrelevant posts, combine similar ones, and rewrite any "how-to" guides to target local keywords \(e.g., "Water Heater Repair in \[Your City\]"\).
Why to make sure: Generic blogs attract low-value traffic, not local buyers. This dilutes your site's local focus and actively hurts your map ranking by targeting the wrong audience.

B.2\] Optimize the Homepage \(Your GBP Landing Page\)
What to make sure \(Title Tag\): The page's Title Tag includes your \[Primary Category\] and \[City Name\]. \(e.g., "Expert Plumber in Chicago - Brand Name"\).
Why to make sure \(Title Tag\): The transcript states these are "the most important words on your entire website" for establishing your primary relevance.

What to make sure \(H1\): The main H1 \(Headline\) also includes your \[Primary Category\] and \[City Name\].
Why to make sure \(H1\): This reinforces the Title Tag and tells users and Google what the page is about in a single headline.

What to make sure \(First Paragraph\): The very first paragraph is focused on "goal completion," giving users the best reasons to call you immediately.
Why to make sure \(First Paragraph\): You have 30-45 seconds to convert a user. This paragraph is your "elevator pitch" to get the call before they bounce.

What to make sure \(H2s\): You have H2 \(Subheadings\) on the homepage that list your Secondary Categories \(e.g., "Water Heater Repair in Chicago"\).
Why to make sure \(H2s\): These H2s act as signposts, introducing your other main services and providing a place to internally link down to your Category Pages.

B.3\] Build Your Category Pages
What to make sure: For each Secondary Category on your GBP, create a new, dedicated page.
Why to make sure: This page will act as a "mini-hub" for all the specific services that fall under that category.

What to make sure \(Title Tag & H1\): The Title Tag and H1 for this page must include the \[Category Name\] and \[City Name\] \(e.g., "Water Heater Repair Service in Chicago"\).
Why to make sure \(Title Tag & H1\): This establishes relevance for this entire group of services in your location.

What to make sure \(H2s\): The H2 \(Subheadings\) on this page will be the specific services from your GBP that fall under this category \(e.g., "Water Heater Replacement," "Water Heater Installation"\).
Why to make sure \(H2s\): This organizes your content and provides the perfect place to link down to your individual Service Pages.

B.4\] Build Your Service Pages \(The "Core 30"\)
What to make sure: For every single service listed on your GBP, create its own dedicated page.
Why to make sure: This is how you build deep, specific relevance for everything you do, targeting users searching for that exact job.

What to make sure \(Title Tag & H1\): The Title Tag and H1 must include the \[Service Name\] and \[City Name\] \(e.g., "Sewer Line Repair in Chicago"\).
Why to make sure \(Title Tag & H1\): This targets the "long-tail" local searcher who knows exactly what they need.

What to make sure \(First Paragraph\): The first paragraph is focused on conversion for that specific service.
Why to make sure \(First Paragraph\): You are building a service page to get a call, not a blog post to educate. Convince them you are the expert for this service.

What to make sure \(H2s\): The H2 \(Subheadings\) on this page should answer questions users have about that specific service \(using ideas from Reddit, forums, or "People Also Ask"\).
Why to make sure \(H2s\): This builds authority and relevance by directly addressing user concerns, positioning you as an expert.

B.5\] Connect with Internal Links
What to make sure: You link down from the Homepage \(H2s\) -> Category Pages. Then link down from the Category Pages \(H2s\) -> Service Pages.
Why to make sure: Google understands your site's structure through links, not URLs. This creates a clear hierarchy and flows relevance from your most important page \(homepage\) down to your most specific pages.

C. Implement Final Technical Signals
---
What to make sure: Your Name, Address, and Phone Number \(NAP\) are an exact match on your website and your GBP. Also, embed your GBP map on your homepage or contact page.
Why to make sure: These are direct, technical signals that connect your website to your GBP, proving to Google they are the same entity.

What to make sure: You add Schema Markup: LocalBusiness schema on the homepage, and Service schema on all category and service pages.
Why to make sure: Schema is like a label on a box. It speaks directly to Google, telling it exactly what your business is and what your pages are about, removing all guesswork.

D. Build Trust \(Ongoing Task\)
---
What to make sure: You acquire local, high-authority backlinks from places like the Chamber of Commerce or local event sponsorships.
Why to make sure: This is the "Trust" factor. Links from other trusted local sites are a powerful signal to Google that you are a legitimate, respected local business.

Right now, my goal set is to build up something that can AI Automate this entire process say like one click we get all these content datasets + images etc. Will keep you posted my journey with the same...

I hope this summary helps.

Hey everyone!

Just wrapped up our extended 2.5-hour session since we missed last week - and WOW, what a call!
🎯 Major Wins Shared Today:
[ul][li]Member hit AI overview ranking + multiple service rankings in just 14 days using Core 30[li]Window cleaning biz jumped from 20+ average to 8.7 after fixing site structure[li]Multiple 2-3 week ranking improvements reported across the community

Key Breakthroughs Discussed:
Website Structure Reality Check:
[ul][li]Your homepage should link to MAX 7 pages \(most of you are way over this!\)[li]Match your site structure to your GBP categories EXACTLY[li]Stop cross-linking between different service types - create clean silos

The "Wall of Text" Problem:
[ul][li]It's about LAYOUT, not word count[li]Break up content with images, callouts, formatting[li]Google knows users skip big blocks of text

Service Area vs Physical Address:
[ul][li]Service area businesses are getting crushed[li]Physical addresses = major ranking advantage[li]Multiple locations need separate GBPs with real addresses

💡 Hot Takes from Today:
[ul][li]"Reviews aren't a major ranking factor" - saw plumbers with 10K reviews ranking 15th[li]AI detection tools are garbage - aim for 60% and move on[li]Schema markup is becoming critical for AI crawlers

🛠️ Tools Members Are Using:
[ul][li]Page Optimizer Pro \(target 80%, not 100%\)[li]Lead Snap for super citations[li]QGP for quality backlinks[li]GHL snapshots \(DM me if you want the plumbing template\)

🎥 Coming Soon:
Working on that demo site everyone's asking for - my developer's hard drive crashed last week but we're back on track!

Thanks for hanging in there during the marathon session today!

Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles + comentó  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
This week's call recording is now available in the Classroom: [https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/52e05dc7?md=0ebede398d12466fa618df5f60aa1503](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/52e05dc7?md=0ebede398d12466fa618df5f60aa1503)

We covered some really practical stuff this week:

✅ How to handle demanding clients who eat up all your time with questions \(the monthly call batching strategy is a game-changer\)
✅ Why you should focus on primary GBP categories instead of chasing secondary rankings
✅ Using Lead Snap to set realistic client expectations about local rankings
✅ The truth about "clean" websites vs. content-rich sites that actually rank
✅ Where to put supporting content and why internal linking matters more than user navigation

Key insight from the call: If your clients want to rank for non-branded terms, they NEED content on their site. Period. Apple doesn't rank for "smartphone" - they only rank for "iPhone." Don't let clients use Apple as an example unless they only want to rank for their brand name.

This was a great Q&A session with real agency problems and practical solutions. Definitely worth the watch if you're dealing with client management challenges or trying to explain SEO basics to resistant clients.

Hi everyone, I am getting ready to do my first core 30. I am so psyched! I fully understand the process. Here's how I did it:

I watched a ton of Master Caleb's YouTube videos and got hooked.
I plowed through SEO Mastery, the $27 course.
I plowed through SEO Mastery Pro.

I went back through both courses, and took notes.

Still confused!

I had pages and pages of notes & decided to write out a step by step process. As I watched and read more, I slowly organized it, wrote down my questions, and hounded Caleb and Elliot. As my questions got answered, my notes got clearer and clearer.

Things became clearer and clearer as I continued. Especially helpful were the clear step by step overviews Caleb gives in some of his videos. Makes the process easy to understand.

I went through the sections of the course many times as I organized my notes.

Ignore the $27 course material
Disregard any SEO material that is not local. It is a separate animal and will confuse you.

## 935. OpenAI's Atlas Announcement (More Details)
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/openais-atlas-announcement-more-details
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles + comentó  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I posted something similar in the non-Pro group, but wanted to give more details for the people in here. I'm sure this is going to be a big topic in the coming days.

TL;DR The Good News: The SEO Process Still Works

Atlas doesn't show you page 1 of Google results. It shows you what ChatGPT recommends.

Your Google ranking doesn't matter if ChatGPT doesn't pick you.

Here's what matters: ChatGPT is usually live crawling the web when it makes recommendations. It's not using pre-trained data. It's checking your GBP, your website, your reviews, your license - in real time.

And your Google Maps rank position matters for ChatGPT recommendations. High Google Maps rankings correlate strongly with ChatGPT recommendations. It's not 1:1 but ranking on Google influences ChatGPT's recomendations

That means the foundational work you're already doing - building topical and geographic relevance, optimizing GBP, earning quality citations - still matters. You're not starting from zero.

But the signals ChatGPT prioritizes are different. And if you understand those differences, you can get clients recommended by ChatGPT right now while your competitors are still figuring out what Atlas even is.

Google cares about links. ChatGPT cares about mentions.

Someone linking to your site tells Google you're trusted. Someone mentioning your site tells ChatGPT you're relevant.

What ChatGPT Actually Prioritizes
Here's the hierarchy of what ChatGPT checks when deciding who to recommend:

Legitimacy and Risk Removal
ChatGPT won't recommend a business it can't verify is legitimate:
[ul][li]License and insurance everywhere - License number in header/footer, contact page, and GBP. Link "Verify our license" to the state lookup page. Upload insurance certificate PDF.[li]Google Local Services Ads \(Google Guaranteed\) - Pass the checks even if you spend $1/day. The badge is a trust signal ChatGPT recognizes.[li]NAP you can verify - Canonical phone number in HTML and schema. No fake suites or virtual offices. High-quality citations: Apple Business Connect, Bing Places, Yelp, BBB, local chamber, manufacturer dealer locators.

1. Demand Capture \(The GBP + Reviews Foundation\)
This is your existing process - just tightened:
[ul][li]Google Business Profile 100% complete - Primary category correct, all services filled with plain-English job names, messaging on, holiday hours current, 25-50 original photos \(trucks, techs, jobs in progress\).[li]Review quality and velocity - Target 4.7+ rating, Owner replies to every review within 48 hours, especially negatives. No review gating, no incentives.[li]Phone experience - Click-to-call above fold

2. Conversion Footprint \(Your Method + Key Additions\)
Your current local SEO method works for ChatGPT - here's how to optimize it:
[ul][li]Service and category URLs - You're already doing this with the Core 30. Each GBP service gets its own page: problem → diagnosis → parts/brands → time estimate → warranty → what to do before arrival →  proof \(before/after photos + testimonials\)[li]Geographic and topical relevance - You're doing this with the local rank map. Replace thin city pages with 3-6 strong regional pages with unique case studies, real job photos, permit numbers, and neighborhood mentions. Add driving-directions pages from major landmarks to your location.[li]Trust content that proves experience - Permit numbers on completed jobs, brand certifications, before/after gallery, video of actual work \(like sewer camera footage with narration\). Financing and warranty details.
3. Technical Wins \(These Move the Needle\)
[ul][li]Schema markup - Use LocalBusiness or niche-specific \(Plumber, HVACBusiness\). Include: name, URL, telephone \(canonical\), image, address, geo coordinates, areaServed \(cities/regions\), openingHoursSpecification, sameAs \(social + citations\), hasOfferCatalog \(services\), knowsAbout \(job types\).
Service pages: add Service schema with serviceType and areaServed. FAQPage schema for each service page's FAQs. VideoObject for explainer videos. AggregateRating only for first-party reviews you collect yourself.
[ul][li]GBP embed - You're doing this. Keep it on contact page with "Get Directions" button.[li]Review embeds - Keep them on home and service pages, but don't mark up third-party reviews with Review schema.[li]NAP matching - You're doing this with high-quality citations. Keep focusing on Apple Maps, not spam directories.

4. What Makes ChatGPT Pick You Over Competitors
ChatGPT doesn't prioritize proximity the way Google Maps does. For ChatGPT recommendations, proximity matters less than:
[ul][li]Response time proof - Screenshot your average response time. "Average 37 minutes to on-site in Logan Square last week."[li]Technician credentials - Bios with certifications, background checks, brand training.[li]Local presence proof - Chamber membership \(you're doing this\), sponsorships \(you're doing this\), photos at local events.[li]Knowledge demonstration - Weekly GBP posts about actual jobs. "We fixed 3 frozen pipes in Bucktown this week - here's what caused them."

What to Add Specifically for ChatGPT
Your current process is 80% of the way there. Add these for ChatGPT optimization:
[ol:1][li]License verification link - Header, footer, and GBP description. Make it one click to verify.[li]Emergency playbook on site - "Shut off this valve → take 2 photos → call/text this number." Practical, helpful content ChatGPT can reference.[li]First-party reviews page - Collect reviews via your own survey \(email/SMS\), display them on your site with AggregateRating schema. Separately show Google reviews without schema markup[li]Driving directions included in geographic relevance pages[li]Missed-call handling - Auto text-back system, weekend on-call banner.[li]Bing for Business copy of GBP \(very important as ChatGPT was trained on Bing's index\)

The Critical Difference: Mentions vs Links
Your link building strategy for Google still works. But for ChatGPT, add mention building:
[ul][li]Industry publications that name your company[li]Podcast appearances where you're introduced by company name[li]Press releases mentioning your business[li]Expert quotes in news articles[li]Speaking events with transcripts[li]YouTube videos about your niche mentioning your company[li]Reddit and forum discussions where people recommend you

ChatGPT was trained on these mentions. The more places your company name appears in relevant contexts, the more likely ChatGPT recommends you.

The Reality
Right now, Atlas is new. Chrome has 70% market share.

But your Google Maps ranking matters for ChatGPT. Your GBP optimization matters. Your citation building matters. Your service pages matter.

The foundation you're already building for Google works for ChatGPT. You just need to add the verification layer, the mention strategy, and the trust signals ChatGPT specifically checks.

Detailed training on getting recommended by ChatGPT is in the Pro classroom

The game just changed. Again. But you're already closer than you think.

Start adding the ChatGPT optimization layer now. Your competitors won't figure this out for months.

URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/feature-image-in-google
Autor: ron-kwok-4884  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
When I am looking at the organic search results from Google, some pages have feature images that show up on the post \(like Aveda in #1 on the attached image\) while other pages have no feature images \(like World of Beauty #2 on the attached image\).

How do I embed an image on my website that Google can use to put in a feature image in the organic search results?

My hunch is that this is done through schema? I see an IMAGE placeholder here:

<script type="application/ld+json">
{
"@context": "[https://schema.org](https://schema.org)",
"@type": "LocalBusiness",
"name": "Hyphen Beauty School",
"image": "[https://hbsorlando.com/images/hyphen-campus.jpg](https://hbsorlando.com/images/hyphen-campus.jpg)",
"@id": "[https://hbsorlando.com/esthetician-school-alafaya](https://hbsorlando.com/esthetician-school-alafaya)",
"url": "[https://hbsorlando.com/esthetician-school-alafaya](https://hbsorlando.com/esthetician-school-alafaya)",
"telephone": "+1-321-323-3216",
"priceRange": "$$",
},

And if this is where I specify the image, are these the dimensions:

Primary Image \(for Schema & Open Graph\):
Recommended Size: 1200 x 630 pixels
Minimum Size: 600 x 314 pixels
Aspect Ratio: 1.91:1
Format: JPG or PNG

URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/new-training-multi-location-site-structure-strategy
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hey everyone!

In a recording mood today =\) Fourth lesson I've added so far today

Just added a comprehensive new training video that's essential if you're working with multi-location clients:

🏢 Multi-Location Site Structure Strategy
This is one of the most important lessons for agencies scaling local SEO across multiple markets. I walk through the exact structure we use for clients with multiple GBPs, including:

✅ The #1 Rule: Never move a GBP landing page if it's already ranking \(I share a real 8-location example where we protected 6 ranking locations while optimizing 2 non-performers\)

✅ Core Principles: Unique landing pages, distinct phone numbers for each location, and how to leverage homepage authority for your most competitive market

✅ Advanced Strategies: When to create city-agnostic pages for navigation, how to treat each GBP landing page as a mini-homepage with full Core 30 structure, and why 90% of traffic goes to GBP landing pages \(not your actual homepage\)

✅ Scaling Content: How to go from 12 pages for a single location to 40+ pages across multiple locations using AI tools efficiently

This structured approach makes it easy for search engines to understand geographical relevancy and helps potential customers find services in their local area.

Critical for anyone managing multi-location clients or scaling across multiple markets!

Link: [https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=8c266b3b783246908270d88672365071](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=8c266b3b783246908270d88672365071)

## 948. UPDATE: Scope-Based Pricing Calculator Lesson
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/update-scope-based-pricing-calculator-lesson
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles + comentó  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hey everyone!

I just completely overhauled one of our most important lessons with a full video walkthrough of the actual pricing calculator we use at my agency:

💰 Scope-Based Pricing Calculator Walkthrough

This isn't theory—this is the exact spreadsheet and methodology we use to price every client engagement with complete transparency.

What's New in This Update:

✅ Complete Cost Breakdown: See exactly how to calculate costs for every role \(SEO expert, copywriter manager, copywriters, schema/image specialists, developers\) and every step in the process
✅ Month-One Setup Strategy: Learn how we handle setup costs \(Claude projects, GBP audits, landing page optimization\) WITHOUT charging setup fees—just reduce the first month's article count
✅ Real Numbers: Actual per-article costs including link building, time allocations for each task, and how AI has reduced content costs by 75% while maintaining quality
✅ Margin Strategy: Why we use 50-70% gross margins and what that actually covers \(revisions, client feedback, overhead\)
✅ Downloadable Calculator: The exact spreadsheet structure you can customize for your team \(all editable fields highlighted in green\)

Why This Matters:

When you show clients exactly what they're getting—specific article counts, word counts, link commitments, and deliverables—they trust the process and stay patient while results develop. This is the opposite of "black box" pricing where clients just hope rankings improve.

The formula is simple: Transparent scope = Client trust = Better retention

Link: [https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=7ecaadb4be6a4ab19a54f38a0ee616a1](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=7ecaadb4be6a4ab19a54f38a0ee616a1)

## 957. Main location currently in suburb
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/main-location-currently-in-suburb
Autor: kent-macwilliam-7586  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I have a potential client and I'm considering a strategy for him, he's a general contractor. Currently his only GBP location is his home address. He's located in the suburb right next to the main city in the region. I know we could get in the top 3 in the suburb without too much difficulty BUT he prefers business in the big city next door.

I'm wondering if it makes sense to start a new GBP in the main city, call it the headquarters and compete there \(which would be a lot of effort because every contractor is based there, some sites have 250+ pages\), or just stick to his suburb and own it in less time \(and without the hassle of setting up a new GPB\).

Option A\) stick with home address in suburb for an quicker win

Option B\) set up new GBP in regional centre city next door and compete much harder for map rank \(keep current location as a secondary location\)

He's ambitious and wants to grow, but I think this one will come down to timelines.

Thoughts on how I should approach this with him?

## 962. General vs specific category
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/general-vs-specific-category
Autor: connor-doyle-8725  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Quick GBP + homepage strategy question. Client is a general contractor with multiple services \(kitchens, basements, bathrooms, etc.\), but we really want to rank for bathroom + kitchen remodeling. Should the GBP primary category stay General Contractor with the homepage covering all services, or should we niche the homepage harder toward Kitchen Remodeling?

Also — when it comes to GBP categories and homepage content, do we only include categories/services that are closely tied to the primary one \(eg cabinets, countertops, flooring etc\)? For example, if they also do roofing, should we leave roofing off the GBP/homepage and just have a dedicated page for it \(maybe not even linked in the main nav\)?”

Ad went live at 7am yesterday. Less than 24 hours later I got 4 leads. At $8.90 CPL. 😳

Question is now: What did you offer them? I believe the ad read "I can contractually guarantee..." How do we go about in doing that? FB Ads for them?

I'm also wondering if I should use 'conditional answers' in the form for those that replied "no" on the very first form question: "Are you or an employee of yours a licensed plumber?" \(2 out of 4\). That way the form submission ends right there. Thoughts?

BTW, I'm floored on how accurate ChatGPT is in generating images \(see below\). I basically took a screenshot of Caleb's ad on the vid, and had attached it as an example. Just. Wow. Then I simply had ChatGPT add the text. So it saved me the Canva step 😜

I selected the primary category "beauty school + Location" as well as the secondary categories "esthetics school + Location", "Hair school + location", etc.  I don't think there is much else I can do here except for waiting and buying more ice cream if I see it is working. Google has indexed almost all of the pages and I've already added schema in most of the pages on my site, and over time, I will add more images and links as content to beef up the articles[li]I've already setup Citations within Leadsnap. ## 973. RankMath Schema Duplicating issues
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/rankmath-schema-duplicating-issues
Autor: muhammad-abdullah-9132  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Been dealing with schema markup for our 3-office roofing company and ran into something that's been driving me nuts. Google's Rich Results Test showed 4/5 separate business entities on one page when there should only be 2.

I had an Organization schema set up site-wide with all 3 office addresses in a location array. But then each office page also has its own LocalBusiness schema with full details.

I did a funny thing. I did add the local business and organization schema as global with conditional logic, and then re-added them by going into the pages by taking them from the templates.

So it was a whole mess.

RankMath was generating the Organization schema from 3 places:

[ol:1][li]Local SEO settings \(auto-generated\)[li]Schema global Templates \(my custom one\)[li]Manual schema entry
These were outputting on pages, creating actual duplicates.

Also, schemas reference each other using @id - like Article schema referencing Organization. Here’s my solution:

For Organization schema \(site-wide\):
[ul][li]Thinking about disabling RankMath's Local SEO auto-generation[li]Custom schema template applied globally. Just HQ address, not all locations. No location array with multiple offices[li]included Basic company info: name, logo, founding date, social profiles, service area

For LocalBusiness schema \(per office page\):
[ul][li]Full office details: address, hours, coordinates, ratings, services[li]Unique @id for each office \(#schuylkill-haven, #york, #selinsgrove\)[li]parentOrganization reference pointing to Organization schema[li]Only on that specific office page

Questions:
[ol:1][li]For multi-location businesses, should you always disable RankMath's Local SEO auto-generation and go fully custom organization schema?[li]What do you recommend to solve the same issues?

Thanks in Advance. It would be really helpful if [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) could give us templates for schemas or maybe the contact of the guy who writes schemas for clients.

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 974. “I” vs “we”
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/i-vs-we
Autor: richie-cronin-7990  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
We’re noticing that the prompts for service and category pages have this story component to them and the content frequently starts out with a personal First Person thing like “As a fitness enthusiast in Nashville I understand about … “ And then usually followed up with something like “just last week I helped Sarah <get this result >”. \(Nearly always Sarah!\)

We’re finding this content doesn’t fit with the voice of the websites we’re working on and clients don’t like it.

Are we losing a lot of power if we corporatize this content a bit. Talk about “we” \(as the business\) rather than “I”

Or is the power of this technique all in the “story” part rather than who is narrating it?

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 995. Day 20 Update: Major Mobile Landing Page Fix & Key Learnings
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/day-20-update-major-mobile-landing-page-fix-key-learnings
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles + comentó  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Just posted the Day 20 recording where I had to fix some embarrassing \(but educational\) mistakes with my Google Business Profile management offer

The Big Problem: My mobile landing page was absolutely terrible - people were clicking Facebook ads and getting dumped straight into a checkout form with no context, no trust-building, no headline, nothing. Since 90%+ of Facebook traffic is mobile, this was killing my conversion rates

What I Fixed:
[ul][li]Completely redesigned the mobile landing page experience[li]Added proper headline: "Get more qualified calls from Google and AI"[li]Included trust elements and 30-day money-back guarantee upfront[li]Simplified the checkout process \(removed shipping/coupon fields\)[li]Set up email bounce automation to stop sending to bad addresses[li]Lowered price from $147 to $97/month \(big psychological difference\)[li]Created retargeting campaign for people who submitted lead forms

Current Metrics:
[ul][li]Cost per lead: $10-13 \(acceptable for B2B\)[li]Conversion rate: Under 1% \(hence the fixes above\)[li]Email deliverability: Only 40% \(too many fake emails from Facebook leads\)

Key Takeaways:
[ol:1][li]Always check mobile first when your traffic source is Facebook[li]Don't assume your funnel works without testing the actual user experience[li]Retargeting is crucial when email deliverability is poor[li]Pricing psychology matters - $97 feels much different than $147

The retargeting campaign targets people who opened/submitted the lead form, and I'm optimistic this will help since I can't rely on email follow-up when 60% of emails aren't being delivered

Next Steps:
[ul][li]Monitor new conversion rates with improved mobile experience[li]Test if returning to VSL funnel performs better[li]Possibly implement call tracking for fulfillment

Sometimes you need to spend $200 on a terrible landing page to learn what not to do. The important thing is catching mistakes quickly and fixing them

Link: [https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/0e95ceb2?md=de8706c16b694f68a6f45567664d3035](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/0e95ceb2?md=de8706c16b694f68a6f45567664d3035)

GBP links to franchise location page.

I create a new domain, call it plumberjoe .com

I still create external backlinks to my water heater and other service pages.

Bonus fun. My homepage is a branded page for PlumberJoe, which link to locations page that goes to plumber joe detroit page, plumber joe ann arbor page, etc. I can put the whole franchise on one domain and build up each one.

## 1007. Which should I trust: Trends.google.com or Lead Snap
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/which-should-i-trust-trendsgooglecom-or-lead-snap
Autor: ron-kwok-4884  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
When I look at Leadsnap, it tells me that "Nail Tech School" has higher search volume than "Nail School". However, [Trends.Google](http://Trends.Google) tells me the opposite. My hunch is to trust [Trends.Google](http://Trends.Google) more since it can filter down to my specific region.

Can you take a look at the screenshots and let me know what you think? In both tools, should I add location in my search terms \(like "Nail School Orlando"\)? Whenever I add location, the search volume drops to zero in Trends.Google.

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 1008. Help me Navigate Core 30 Pages for Multi-Location Business Serving a Whole State and Beyond
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/help-me-navigate-core-30-pages-for-multi-location-business-serving-a-whole-state-and-beyond
Autor: muhammad-abdullah-9132  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Let’s say a company has 4 offices, serves almost the whole big state, and also covers parts of 3 neighboring states.

Question and scenario for the main location:

[ul][li]Homepage is the main GBP landing page \(tied to the HQ location\). Although, its not a local page that refers to the same county. the office location in almost in the center and southern part of the county.[li]We already have service pages targeting the full serving regions. Because they cover wide areas, we can’t just localize those main core service/category pages by county. Now here’s where I’m stuck: Should we still create additional Core 30 pages for the HQ location itself? I did the core pages for them. But, it seems most of them aren't indexed after this google update.

Question and scenario for the 3rd location gmb:

Looking at data for the 3rd location. Its a small city near to river and located at the border of its county.

[ul][li]25-mile radius \(service + city\) → avg rank ~6, most rankings at 3–4, with 51% in the top 3.[li]“Near me” searches → avg rank ~11, only 5% in the top 3.

This makes me think:
[ul][li]When looking at “near me,” rankings, Core 30 seems necessary.[li]But based on the service + city performance, maybe Core 30 isn’t necessary for this location. So, we're still navigating through the leadsnap citations and waiting for them to go live all. What do you prefer in this case.

I am helping mostly home service contractors. Most of the cases when dealing with big clients, it appears to be the same confusions and worry of mine.

Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
The recording and summary from yesterday's call is now live in the classroom under "2025-09-23 Internal Linking & GBP Optimization"

\([https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/52e05dc7?md=a8a7a5aa929b4ff2b74b30800708fc96](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/52e05dc7?md=a8a7a5aa929b4ff2b74b30800708fc96)\)

What We Covered:

Internal Linking Deep Dive
[ul][li]Proper editorial link structure for the core 30[li]Two-thirds branded, one-third keyword anchor text ratios[li]How categories, services, and supporting content should connect

GBP Optimization Strategies
[ul][li]Why primary category selection often determines success[li]How to organize services without duplication[li]Using GMB Everywhere and competitor analysis for category research

New AI Copywriting Workflow
[ul][li]Complete system that cuts article creation time from 45+ minutes to 15-20 minutes[li]Separate outline and writing processes using Claude projects[li]Built-in fact-checking that catches errors humans miss[li]Client-specific prompts with business context

Schema Implementation Guide
[ul][li]Exact schema requirements for each page type[li]When and how to nest service schema properly[li]Structured data testing best practices

Current SEO Landscape
[ul][li]Google's August spam update impact on lead gen sites[li]How AI overview recommendations differ from map results[li]Why Yelp profiles matter more than you think

Key Decisions Made
[ul][li]Only run rank maps for primary category plus 2-3 critical services[li]Create location pages only when heat maps show 4s, 5s, and 6s[li]Match website structure exactly to GBP organization[li]Use different content creation approaches based on ranking status

The full summary breaks down all questions, answers, and actionable tips from the 90-minute session.

This Week's Topics:

AI-First SEO Strategy
[ul][li]Why ChatGPT recommendations are becoming more valuable than Google Maps rankings[li]How to optimize for AI systems vs traditional search

Schema Markup Mastery
[ul][li]NEW: Nested schema techniques that get ChatGPT to repeat your exact messaging[li]Real case study: Attorney settlement mentioned word-for-word by ChatGPT

Citation Game-Changer
[ul][li]Why perfect citation consistency is now CRITICAL \(after years of not mattering\)[li]How one character difference can kill your AI recommendations

Website Redesign Reality Check
[ul][li]The harsh truth about ranking drops and client expectations[li]When to say "no" to save the relationship

Advanced Internal Linking
[ul][li]Why URL structure doesn't matter \(but this does\)[li]The silo strategy that actually works

Client Acquisition Insights:

## 1025. SCHEMA
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/schema
Autor: lalita-sundari-2382  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Please help, I know this is the second time I'm asking about schema, but I feel I need to get it right. Is this the structure of how I should prompt chat gpt for to give the schema ?

Home page: Organization + LocalBusiness + WebSite + WebPage + BreadcrumbList + FAQ

Category page: WebPage with subtype CollectionPage + BreadcrumbList + an OfferCatalog/ItemList of Service objects that each reference your LocalBusiness @id. \(what I think we are calling nested schema right\)

Service page: WebPage with subtype ItemPage + BreadcrumbList + Service \(with provider → your LocalBusiness @id \(nested\)\) \(+ FAQ if present\).

Also, ok when talking about PBM links, do I need to buy them after my page is optimized with schema, or can I buy the PBM LINK and then keep optimizing my website pages?

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 1054. Zero GPT Scores
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/zero-gpt
Autor: mark-farrow-6630  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Following the wizard of Ai SEO \[Caleb\] :\)\) to a T the ZERO GPT scores seem to be getting lower for me using a few extra instructions in the files area of the project.
I loaded in:

GBP CAT and SERVICES
Contact Info Text, including website, Phone, address, email \(so you don't have to edit the text each time.
Customer AVATAR
USP's \(so you don't have to edit the prompt each time, you can just type there "provided in the instructions\)

I also tried to adjust the prompt and say please complete all of the tasks without stopping to save time, BUT it seems to create better content with the breaks. This is a gut feel but the scores seem lower with the break in the action.

My two cents. I am in the middle of creating three new client sites with the Core 30 from the very begining for them so very excited to see the results.

## 1059. Advice at this point in the process?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/advice-at-this-point-in-the-process
Autor: rebecca-degrossa  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hey [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) - For my first client that I'm implementing this for, we have done the following:
[ol:1][li]Changed GBP structure and mapped site to it \(involved re-theming\)[li]Localized the content on the site more[li]Added FAQs to home page[li]Modified our schema to how you do it[li]Geotagged all images in GBP[li]Started Leadsnap citations[li]Got Chamber of Commerce link[li]Started getting reviews \(a bit tricky for therapists as you cannot ethically ask clients for reviews\)

The organic rankings are still good - largely on page 1.

The rank maps are MUCH improved for the terms closely related to the Main Category \(in this case Marriage or Relationship Counseling\). Terms that have improved a lot are: main cat term, couples therapy, couples therapist, marriage counselor and relationship counselor.

However, we also had Sex Therapy as a service under the Main cat, and it hasn't budged. -- second rank map image. \(color diffs are Local Falcon vs Leadsnap\). This is also ranking well organically - #4.

Also, the terms we re-themed are not moving at all. For instance, we tried to use the category of Mental Health Clinic to re-theme the service Ketamine Assisted Psychotherapy. It's was all red and still is. The secondary category page is here: [https://www.couplestherapistboulder.com/mental-health-clinic](https://www.couplestherapistboulder.com/mental-health-clinic) -- with the h2 as Ketamine Assisted Psychotherapy. I think this needs some supporting content, anyway, but it's already ranking on page 1 organically, so I thought it already had topic relevance.

Anyway, given what's happening \(or not happening\) with the Sex therapy term and the Ketamine term, what would you suggest?

═══════════════════════════════════════

Autor: scott-desalvo-1096  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I am updating my site structure to follow Caleb's brilliant suggestions.

I am abandoning a lot of Blog posts, as they have no keywords, traffic or backlinks.

I DO, however, have a lot of Blog Posts and some Pages which have some combination of backlinks, traffic and/or keywords. But they do not neatly fit into the new structure.

Should I make them Blog posts? This would seem to lose me quite a bit of link juice these pages may have.

Most are pages which DO track Services in the new site structure. So, should I make them Pages and put them in the top sub Menu \(Home-Car Accident Lawyer Chicago-\[Blog post name here\]? OR make them sub sub pages? \(Home-Car Accident Lawyer Chicago-Exisiting Page- New Page\)? Just Link to them from the Category page for the topic? This may pass some power upwards to my Category pages.

If they do not exist anywhere in the new link structure, \(orphaned pages\), their utility is very low.

I hope this is clear and I hope it makes sense. Thanks for any advice you can share.

## 1073. Do I need hundreds of external links?!
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/do-i-need-hundreds-of-external-links
Autor: michelle-adamo-8591  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
[@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) Excellent training! I've learned some gems from you, some missing pieces, that have made a difference in results pretty quickly! I have a question about external links via Ice Cream Shop. I have hundreds of pages to get an external link for, if I'm following your recommendations correctly.

For instance, we just created a set of 15 exterior cleaning pages for one of my multi-location clients \(15 different locations\) x 6 different cleaning services = 90 pages. Are you saying each one of those 90 pages needs an external link? Are your links PBNs? And what should the timing be? Would adding 90 external links over a period of a few weeks be detrimental?

Each of these 15 locations has a set of 10+ service area pages = a minimum of 150 pages. Do each of those pages need an external link? This is for just one client. I have dozens. So, that's a lot of external links! :\).

I have a similar question about my single location clients: 15 service area pages + 30 service pages = 45 external links. Most of my clients rank for 'most' of their money keywords in the top 3 in maps and in ChatGPT, but I want them to be solid as things change and advance with Google and AI; I'm trying to be proactive. But there are trouble spots. In some cases, we're adding brand new services, some are new businesses/websites \(less than 12 months old\), and some are expanding into new locations.

I'm seeking some clarity and reassurance about how to proceed. I'm a little weary, as these are my beloved clients, most of them for 10+ years, and their businesses are their family's livelihoods. I don't want to do anything to jeopardize them and I've not gotten good results using a few link building services in the past. Let me know if we need to do a consultation if this is too meaty a question. Much appreciated, Caleb! Thank you so much!

## 1086. Day 11 Recording is LIVE - Major Funnel Pivot & Email Sequence Overhaul
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/day-11-recording-is-live-major-funnel-pivot-email-sequence-overhaul
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
The recording from today's session is now available in the classroom.

Key takeaways from this session:

The Problem: After spending ~$400 on ads and getting 80+ leads but only 1 confirmed sale, it became clear the funnel wasn't converting. The VSL was losing 50%+ of viewers in the first 30 seconds.

The Solution: Complete funnel restructure based on advice from someone running $1M+/month in Facebook ads:

[ul][li]Killed the sales conversion campaign \(Facebook needs more data to optimize\)[li]Removing the VSL page entirely - sending leads straight to checkout[li]Added $50 off coupon \(CALLS97\) to drop first month to $97[li]Recording new 30-second VSL for the checkout page focused on trust-building[li]Completely rewrote email sequence to address the 5 biggest objections

Critical Fix: Facebook lead form field mapping wasn't set up correctly, so leads weren't being added to HighLevel and weren't getting follow-up emails.

Next Steps:
[ul][li]Test this new funnel structure[li]Potentially create specific ads for each service professional type \(plumbers, electricians, roofers, etc.\) instead of broad targeting

The session shows the reality of testing and iterating when something isn't working - sometimes you need to make bigger changes rather than just tweaking.

Check out the recording to see the complete process and grab the new 30-second VSL script if you want to use it for your own offers.

[https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/0e95ceb2?md=5fb95da40b1e47cab4736db159a53e77](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/0e95ceb2?md=5fb95da40b1e47cab4736db159a53e77)

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 1099. Day 6 of the Challenge Recording Posted
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/day-6-of-the-challenge-recording-posted
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Just wrapped up Day 6 of the challenge with some funnel analysis and optimization work. Here's what went down:

The Numbers So Far
[ul][li]Total Ad Spend: ~$180 over the weekend[li]Lead Cost: $3.76 per lead \(aiming for closer to $2\)[li]Squeeze Page Conversion: 27% \(solid performance\)[li]VSL Retention: Only 39% after 30 seconds \(needs work\)

Key Issues Identified
[ol:1][li]VSL Hook Problem - Terrible retention in first 30 seconds. The "agencies charging $2-4k" opener isn't resonating[li]Checkout Page Conversion - Getting traffic but poor conversion rates[li]Tracking Confusion - 2 Stripe sales showed up but not in funnel analytics \(likely from people watching recordings\)

What We Fixed Today
New VSL Script: Complete rewrite with better hook - "If your phone isn't ringing with new jobs every week, it's because you're invisible online"
[ul][li]200 words shorter \(726 vs 978 words\)[li]Results moved up earlier in presentation[li]Recorded live on the call \(one take!\)[li]Off camera adjusted the URLs to stop messing up funnel data with direct visitors

Checkout Page Optimization:
[ul][li]Clarified shipping info purpose \("Where can I ship your bonus review cards?"\)[li]Added social proof testimonial[li]Changed CTA to "Yes, get my phone ringing"[li]Updated guarantee language

New Ad Creative: Added 4 new AI-generated images to test, including one intentionally "bad crop" to see if engagement mistakes boost performance

The Reality Check

This is exactly what real funnel optimization looks like - not pretty, lots of testing, and data-driven decisions over gut feelings. We're not making changes based on what "looks good" but on actual performance metrics.

Key Takeaways for Your Campaigns
[ol:1][li]30-second retention is everything - If people bounce in the first 30 seconds, your hook is broken[li]Track everything properly - Make sure your analytics match reality[li]Test counterintuitive things - Sometimes "mistakes" in ads perform better[li]Don't optimize what's working - 27% squeeze page conversion is good enough for now

What's Next
Running the new VSL and ads for a few days to gather data, then we'll analyze results and continue optimizing. Once we get consistent performance, I'll share the snapshot.

For those following along: You can either test with the current version or wait until we dial it in further - do what makes sense for your budget and risk tolerance.

Remember: Most offers take weeks of testing before they work consistently. This is the messy middle that nobody talks about but everyone goes through.

## 1107. Chamber of Commerce Scam
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/chamber-of-commerce-scam
Autor: matthew-furman-1681  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I believe I was scammed. Coincidentally, right around the time I was learning to join a Chamber of Commerce, I received an email from [https://www.essexchamber.org/](https://www.essexchamber.org/) to join. $220 seemed about right from what I learned. So impulsively, while driving, I paid it :\)

I setup a listing, there is a website, yet the same emails have not stopped, continuously. I emailed telling them I am already a member, please stop emailing. No response!

They did a lot of things right to seem legit, even fooled ChatGPT.

Yet Claude is no dummy:
Red Flags:
Very limited content and information
"Powered by Nex" connection
Confusion with legitimate Essex chambers
No clear geographic designation
Robots.txt blocking

Look at the "Member Services" girl in the pic \(lmao\).

Do you think I was scammed?

## 1113. GOING LIVE TODAY - Ad Troubleshooting Session (Pro Members Only)
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/going-live-today-ad-troubleshooting-session-pro-members-only
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles + comentó  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Going live this Wednesday \(9/3\) at 1 pm CST for an exclusive ad troubleshooting session - just for you guys today.

What's happening: I've done a quick scan of the numbers and spotted some clear issues that need fixing. The end-of-funnel stats are completely off - we had 2 actual signups with payments in Stripe, but Facebook is showing 0 conversions. Plus 7 unique views on the onboarding page since ads started running - this should be a URL that's only visible after signing up and should be counted a conversion on FB ads.

Here's the plan:
[ul][li]Start by walking through exactly what I'm seeing in the data[li]Make the necessary changes live that I can[li]Talk about the changes I'm going to make to the VSL and why[li]Wrap up with next steps

Quick note on tracking: FB tracking isn't perfect \(never is\), so we'll work with what it's giving us. The low onboarding page views might be skewed since I shared that URL during the launch recording - I may tweak the landing page URL slightly off-camera to clean up the data and protect the final URL.

Should be a great session with the smaller group - looking forward to diving deep with you all!

Here's the zoom link: [https://us02web.zoom.us/j/4651169348](https://us02web.zoom.us/j/4651169348)

As always, I'll post the recording later today or tomorrow if you can't make the live session

## 1114. To migrate or not to migrate?! Stuck.
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/to-migrate-or-not-to-migrate-stuck
Autor: victor-sammarco-1675  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I own an established Wix website that generates significant monthly revenue. For years, I've wanted to migrate it to WordPress to unlock better functionality and performance. However, I'm deeply concerned about one critical risk: will the migration destroy my top 1-2 Google search rankings?

This website means a lot to me, so I'm extremely cautious about making any move that could jeopardize its search visibility. I'm looking for real-world experiences from others who've made similar migrations. Did your rankings survive the transition intact, or did you see them crater with no recovery?

The potential upside of WordPress is huge, but not if it costs me the organic traffic that drives my business. Has anyone successfully navigated this type of migration while preserving their hard-earned search rankings?

[@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c)

## 1120. GBP Categories for Painting Contractors
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/gbp-categories-for-painting-contractors
Autor: michelle-adamo-8591  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
We've used Painter as primary, Painting as secondary for years. I've got numerous independent painter and franchise painter clients.

Do you suggest any other secondary categories?

Should we add categories such as pressure washing service, carpenter, dry wall contractor, siding contractor and stucco contractor \(only because they do minor repairs and paint siding\), fence contractor \(only because they paint or stain them\), and lighting contractor \(because they install holiday lighting\)?

Many also offer gutter installation & repair, but then we're competing with stand-alone gutter companies.

I've hesitated to use these categories because:

a\) My understanding is that Google doesn't like it when you use categories that aren't exactly relevant.

b\) These are not stand-alone services; they are add-ons. So we don't want prospects to contact us about painting a fence or drywall repair/installation or a large carpentry project without hiring us for a large painting project. I also don't want my clients complaining they are getting leads for small jobs. They want whole-house painting projects.

c\) Service page content is challenging. We could do a GBP secondary category hub page about fence painting, but what else would be relevant for sub-service pages about painting a wood fence?

Any thoughts?

## 1131. small town in rural area - location pages?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/small-town-in-rural-area-location-pages
Autor: heinrich-taitl-6301  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
as already mentioned, we have a location in a small town in a rural area. not surprisingly, we already rank 1-2 there with our categories for the search terms category-city.
now we want to rank in the neighbouring towns in the area as well, because that's where the companies that are of interest to us are based.
how should we proceed?
a separate location page with the title category-city for each larger town in the surrounding area? or extra location pages for the district?
there are about 4-5 towns within a radius of approx. 30km that are interesting for us, each with approx. 10-20,000 inhabitants, i.e. really rural.

## 1132. Day 1.5 is complete - ads are LIVE and running!
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/day-15-is-complete-ads-are-live-and-running
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Had to finish what we started from Day 1 since school pickup interrupted right when we were getting to the Facebook ads setup.

What we accomplished today:
[ul][li]Finished building both Facebook ad campaigns \(lead form + sales funnel\)[li]Set up 8 different ad variations with those impressively ugly images[li]Built the complete email sequence \(3 emails over 4 days\)[li]Double-checked the entire funnel flow[li]Got everything published and running

Smaller crowd today but we got the job done. The ads are processing and should be live soon.

All the resources are now uploaded:
[ul][li]Complete recording of Day 1.5[li]All AI prompts used

Next session: Wednesday 9/4 at 1pm CST

By then we'll either be troubleshooting why the ads aren't working... or walking through fulfillment because we have a bunch of signups to handle.

Either way, it's going to be interesting. I won't be checking the ads at all this weekend \(family wedding in Minneapolis\), so Wednesday morning will be our first real look at the data.

See you Wednesday for Day 2 - whatever that brings!
