## 1133. Landed My First (real) Upwork Client
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/landed-my-first-real-upwork-client
Autor: andrew-cunningham-2871  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
The Upwork strategies actually work! My profile is not very good. Just one completed job for $100, and one current ongoing job from a personal contact I brought onto the platform. But applying immediately with a full proposal to a post got them to message and I was able to send a lead magnet, set up the call and close for $1300/mo.

I set up Upwork job alerts so I could apply immediately. I had to use a service called Vollna \($20/mo\) because the service in the training \(Freelancer Feed\) is permanently down. It took a few attempts but applying immediately really makes up for all the difference in your profile.

Today, Friday 8/29 at 1pm CST

I forgot to create the classroom and make it all pretty ahead of time. So it's pretty basic for now, but wanted to get the recording and material to you guys ASAP. Next week or maybe the week after it will hopefully be beautified. Apologies for the mess

✅ Build a second campaign from scratch

✅ Get everything live and running

One comment from yesterday: "Today I learned just to do things and see what happens instead of making it perfect."

Perfect summary of what we're doing here.

## 1136. Multi location business closed the main location
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/multi-location-business-closed-the-main-location
Autor: brian-mcdaniel-1678  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I have this client for a few years in Wellington, FL. No problem, GBP Category Wellington FL. Then he opened another location Miami. No problem, location page GBP cat Miami.

Then he moves Wellington to West Palm Beach. OK, change Wellington to WPB, no problem.

Now he is dumping WPB and keeping Miami. Miami has been a battle, but we are seeing results at last. Miami GBP has always pointed to the Miami page.

What to do with the WPB home page? Just call it Florida? Leave it alone? Just build all pages in the future to link to the Miami page and use Miami for all of them?

Or just stick to one city name for the home page?

Alright Pro family, this is it. Today at 1:00 PM CDT, we're not just watching - we're BUILDING together.
While everyone else gets a taste of Day 1, you're getting the full 90-day transformation. Every strategy, every behind-the-scenes moment, every breakthrough \(and every challenge\) as we scale from $0 to $10K/month.

What you're getting as a Pro member:
🎯 Full access to ALL 90 days \(not just today\)
🎯 Every live Zoom session + recordings
🎯All the material I used \(snapshots, prompts, etc\)
🎯 Direct access to the exact playbooks I'm using

Today's mission: Get started with day one

I try to avoid theory - that's why I still have an active local SEO agency. I know what actually works. Someone suggested this challenge a few months ago and I've been putting it off because... well because it seems hard.

But today's the day

Pro Tip: Come prepared with questions! It's a Zoom call after all. I don't have a script. A handful of technical things will be done in advance, but most of it will be done live and on camera

Also I've never built a landing page on GHL before so that will be fun to do for the first time... live...

Lock in, stay focused, and let's build something incredible together.

See you at 1:00 PM sharp! 🚀

P.S. - Make sure you're ready to take action. This isn't a spectator sport anymore.

## 1139. Setting Up New Client's Core 30 Dilemma
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/setting-up-new-clients-core-30-dilemma
Autor: charles-rosseel-2722  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Caleb's Core 30 example uses Fort Wayne, Indiana \(pop 273,000\). His instruction is clear and produces excellent results. Here's the rub, I have a new client \(and several older clients\) who has a service business located in a town with a population of only 8,000. His GBP already ranks #1 in the Map Pack there. Of course, there are very few searches for his service in a town of 8,000. My dilemma, should I try to get his business ranked in top 3 of the County Map Pack \(the county he's in has a population of 650,000\), instead of building dozens of Core 30s for all the surrounding towns that have populations from 8,000 to 15,000? Note: there is a major city of 1.6 Million \(extremely competitive\) about 25 miles from his small town. Suggestions appreciated as I'd like to impress my new client with terrific results.

## 1147. Different results: Leadsnap vs Local Falcon
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/different-results-leadsnap-vs-local-falcon
Autor: rebecca-degrossa  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
hey [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) - Been working on this client for 3 weeks now. We were using LocalFalcon before. Just got Leadsnap due to the citation building. Today I ran all keywords for this client \(who was 20+ in Local Falcon for ALL terms to start, but had mostly page 1 organic rankings\) and we're definitely seeing improvements in the GBP rankings!

Haven't yet built PBN links or even pinged Google Search console to re-crawl GBP landing pages, category pages and services pages yet. We added local info to those pages as it didn't exist before.

Pretty exciting!

But question: Do you know why these two systems are so different, besides the fact that Local Falcon uses more red? \(i.e. they make the circles red for everything 8 and above\). But even accounting for that, Leadsnap gives a much more positive spin. Any idea why they're so different?

Thanks!

1\) My spa only has 12 services, and the category is only massage and spa. For the Core 30, could I separate the services by time to create 30 pages, and should I create a time-specific service Google profile?

Deep Tissue Massage - 60 Min
Deep Tissue Massage - 90 Min

and then create a page for each of those services with their specific time.

[ul][li]Spa[li]Day spa[li]Health spa[li]Massage therapist[li]Sports massage therapist

## 1164. Claude Service Page response being repetitive
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/claude-service-page-response-being-repetitive
Autor: justin-bryce-8305  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

Everytime I request a Service page, the H1 always starts with 'Professional' & the sub-heading starts with 'Transform'

Here is an example:
3. Page Content
3.1 Hero Section
H1: Professional Trim & Skirting Board Painting in Derby, WA
Sub-heading: Transform your home's finishing touches with climate-tested painting solutions designed specifically for the Kimberley region's challenging conditions.

have you experienced the same and do you have a way around this?

each time I let the ai know it is repeating itself and ask it to rewrite it and be creative, remembering it is a local seo expert.

Here is the section of the prompt I have for the Hero Section:
3. Page Content \(write section by section\):
3.1 Hero Section
- Write a h1 title for this page
- Include a sentence as a sub heading for this page.

Autor: bill-van-valer-9224  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Good morning all. We have been trying to get our new office location verified through google for the last couple of weeks. We have submitted copies of the lease, done a video verification showing the sign on the building, opening the door with the key and walking through the office, but it still says "in review". Now this morning I woke up to another location \(we have 3 currently\) getting suspended. Not sure where to go from here. From past experience it seems like if we appeal the suspension and loose we only get one shot and we out of luck at that point, so I haven't appealed it yet. If we had to move the suspended one to the new one that would be ok because we are closing that location anyway, and I would like to keep the reviews and the age of the GBP if i only get one of the 2 locations. I'm at a loss and would appreciate any advice you wizards have to offer! Thanks everyone!

## 1171. Question about high competition for very small business
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/question-about-competition
Autor: kent-macwilliam-7586  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I have a potential client, a small general contracting company in a mid sized city. The city is midsized, but it's one of those amalgamated cities where the municipality covers a huge area \(low density\).

Their map pack competition has 100-150 pages on their site, professional photos of their jobs, and 100+ reviews. The competition is also doing a good a pretty good job with service pages and location pages.

My potential client has 5 reviews, cell phone grade photos, and a bare-bones website.

I could probably rank this small business, but it's small and I'm not sure they have the budget to hire me to produce 100+ pages of quality content to compete with the top three in their city.

As I see it I have 2 options: 1\) pitch full service, $1000's/mo to get up to the size of the competitors or 2\) niche down, pick some more obscure service offerings and try to rank those first.

Or option X\) admit this is not an ideal client

How would you approach working with a very small business in a mid-sized market with strong competitors?

═══════════════════════════════════════

I am working on a General Contractor website.

Here are the categories:

General Contractor \(Primary\) \(Homepage\)
Remodeler \(Secondary\)
Home Builder \(Secondary\)

I will have about 6-7 service pages under each category.

I am wondering about the correct way to do the slug paths here. the secondary categories are easy because they come with an interior slug path.

Example secondary category paths:

domain.com/remodeler/home-remodeling/
domain.com/home-builder/custom-home-construction/

domain.com/general-contractor/deck-construction/ ????
or just...
domain.com/deck-construction/

domain.com/remodeler-city/home-remodeling-city/ ???

## 1175. Taking on new clients but terrible website/rebuild option?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/taking-on-new-clients-but-terrible-websiterebuild-option
Autor: victor-sammarco-1675  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
We all know a website and SEO go hand in hand. You can’t really seperate the two—if the site isn’t build right, the SEO will always fall flat.

That’s why I only take on new clients if they’re willing to get a new website done first. We build everything on WordPress with Elementor Pro and the Hello theme, fully optimized so it’s fast, clean, and setup the right way.

Doing it this way has honestly catapulted clients into way higher SEO success than if we were just patching up an old or poorly built site. The website lays the foundation, and the SEO pushes it over the top.

The tricky part is, I’ve had to turn away alot of people who don’t want to invest 1–5k upfront for a new build. I get it, but skipping that step usually means weaker results—and I don’t want to put my name on something that won’t work.

Now I’m trying to figure out how other agency's handle this. How do they take on clients without a full rebuild, but still keep that strong website + SEO connection in place? [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c)

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 1193. We have created the geographical relevance before doing the topical depth
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/we-have-created-the-geographical-relevance-before-doing-the-topical-depth
Autor: muhammad-abdullah-9132  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hi everyone [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c)
Our original plan was to develop the core 30 pages for topical relevance, publishing 2–3 pages per week. The goal was to build steadily so Google wouldn’t flag sudden surges in new content and to avoid triggering any algorithmic issues.
After completing the topical build-out, we planned to work on geographical relevance.
However, I didn’t establish the right approach for this client at the start and was a bit uncertain about keyword cannibalization, especially since their organic rankings were already performing quite well overall.
They were worried Google might see them as spammy or “doorway” pages, and didn’t understand the strategic role these pages play, and had a fear of splitting ranking power across too many URLs.

Here’s the current status:
[ul][li]I have created all the neighborhood pages for the primary county.[li]I still need to create a few more for two other counties to move them out of the “red zones.”[li]I have completed the core pages for our primary category, but the 3 subcategory pages remain unfinished.[li]For the second GBP location, I have already created several neighborhood pages.[li]I recently got the client to agree to create core pages for this GBP location, but now they’re hesitant and unsure.[li]I’ve shown them multiple references and examples, including 4–5 competitors in the same regional market following a similar strategy.

My questions are:

[ol:1][li]How can I re-present and reinforce this strategy so the client feels confident moving forward?[li]Was it a significant mistake to create the geo pages before completing the core topical pages?

Any suggestions or best practices would be appreciated.

Thanks

═══════════════════════════════════════

2- Stuff like press releases or blog comments would only need to be done every few months correct?

## 1197. GBP location not showing in branded search
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/gbp-location-not-showing-in-branded-search
Autor: saif-saab-2424  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hi again – following up now that I'm on AI SEO Mastery Pro, I'm hoping to get more in-depth help.

We had asked about a case where a newly launched branch of a restaurant \(~3 months old, 470+ reviews, 4.8 stars\) is completely overshadowed in branded searches by the original location \(4 years old, 3,300+ reviews\). The two branches are in two different cities \(1,000 miles apart\), and both Google Business Profiles are active and verified.

When users in the new city search for the brand name on Google Maps or Search, Google shows the older location directly, instead of the local branch that’s nearby. There’s no map pack and the older location opens by default.

We were advised to "merge the knowledge panels", but:

-There is no knowledge panel for the brand \(just individual GBPs\).
-Google already recognizes both branches under the same brand, grouping them together in Maps under the brand name.

Could you clarify what actions are available to help Google understand that the new branch should be locally dominant for branded searches in its own region? Note: the business is in the middle east.

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 1204. Taking on a test client with limited budget

Autor: andri-werren-4157  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hey Guys,
I wanted to share a quick thought and get your feedback on something:
I’ve got a client for whom I recently built a website. Now, I’ve offered to do local SEO for his business as well. His budget is limited — max. $1,000 per year — which is definitely on the low end.

However, I see this as a potential “test-client” opportunity. I’m considering using this project to put into practice everything I’ve learned in this course — from GMB optimization and citations to content and link-building strategies.
I’m wondering:
[ul][li]Do you think this is a smart move to gain hands-on experience, even if it’s not super profitable right now?[li]Or would you recommend proposing a slightly different offer — like $1,000 for 6 months — so that I can show results faster and reevaluate together after that?

Curious to hear your thoughts [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c). Anyone else tried a similar approach with a low-budget client to build their portfolio and skills?
Cheers,
Andri

## 1218. Progress Tracking
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/progress-tracking
Autor: robert-foerster-2343  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I'll just start a thread here for progress tracking, things I've tried, etc.

So far, I've seen some interesting things... Some good, some not so good.

Published core 30. Now, started adding some location pages to nearby towns as they're all commonly traversed by people. We are in Boardman, it's the largest shopping area, so folks from neighboring towns come here..

In the last few weeks, I cleaned up the services on GBP, we had way too many dups and small items for which we didn't have pages for. Now we have 1:1 alignment on GBP -> site.

Originally, we had spa as our primary cat. We switched a month ago to Facial Spa, since that's pre precise. However, we see all other top ranking competitors using Spa, so I switched this back last week.

Some of our lack of progress is probably me switching GBP, categories, etc?

At this point, I have it set, and I'll let it ride to see how things go.

We have local sports team sponsor listing, chamber listing, all main citations.... So far, non of these have moved the needle at all. In fact, our rankings have been trending slightly worse the last week or two.

This is last week vs today for 'keyword location'. In the red areas, we published \(and verified google indexed\) dedicated location page there, plus a supporting article going more into depth with local landmarks, etc.

Our 'near me' is also awful -- still monitoring. We ordered PBN links to 10 pages, none of landed yet. Curious if any of those move the needle.

Also, will be on live tv morning news segment next week -- not sure if anything will come of that \(maybe some brand searches\).

Spray tanning \(secondary for us\) is shown some improvement.

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 1219. Weekly AI SEO Call Recording Now Available!
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/weekly-ai-seo-call-recording-now-available
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hey everyone! The recording from todays' call is now live in the classroom.

📝 New Format Update
We implemented a new format this week to help manage time better and get to more questions:
New Rules:
[ul][li]One question per hand raise - after asking your question, put your hand down and raise it again if you have follow-ups[li]Written questions answered between verbal ones - I'll tackle all chat questions after each hand-raised question[li]Quickest response = chat questions - if you have a simple question, type it in chat for faster answers
This seemed to work well and let us cover way more ground. Let me know your thoughts!

🔥 What We Covered
[ul][li]AI/LLM landing pages - how to optimize specifically for ChatGPT and other AI search[li]Schema's growing importance - why structured data is becoming critical for AI visibility[li]Reddit strategy for local SEO - practical tips for building brand mentions[li]Website change warnings - why even small modifications can tank your rankings[li]Indexing troubleshooting - tools and techniques when pages won't index[li]
💡 Key Takeaway
The biggest revelation: Google's AI overview and map pack often recommend completely different businesses for the same local searches. This disconnect shows how important it is to optimize for both traditional and AI search.

Here's a link to the replay: [https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/52e05dc7?md=934d818383764f9d93fb70bf1a14649b](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/52e05dc7?md=934d818383764f9d93fb70bf1a14649b)

## 1224. GBP Suspension FINALLY Lifted
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/gbp-suspension-finally-lifted
Autor: jeff-garner-3218  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
It took from July 10 until August 6 to FIGHT tooth and nail to get my GBP for MY company reactivated. I only made a text update to my SEO Services description and my GBP was suddenly suspended with no explanation. I was only told my profile didn't comply with "Quality Guidelines". I appealed, got denied, and sent feedback, reported an issue etc. I tried to work with my two Google Ad reps considering I run client ads that are more than $10,000 A DAY!!! ...and you'd think they would help because I clearly make money for Google. I know - I'm a drop of water in the ocean to them...but you would think the reps would care - they don't, even after I generated millions in ad revenue for Google...

I have no lessons learned or details to share on how to solve this, because I didn't receive any answers or feedback from COUNTLESS communication threads to GBP support and my acct reps. Nothing, not one word on why it happened or how I could avoid this.

I even paid for the GBP Advisor session in hopes that would help get reactivated, but they said they can't help...money and time wasted. The only comment I got from the advisor rep was "Google is cracking down on GBPs and getting way more strict on home-based businesses showing their address - when they don't have PERMENANT SIGNAGE".

In today's world of remote work locations and so many freelancers/agencies working from a location other than an office building, Google is clearly trying to penalize companies like many of us run...

Just be extremely careful everyone, and be extremely deliberate about every word, character and detail you add in a GBP. \(Not like you don't\) But the risk factor seems to be at DEFCON 4 these days...
[@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c)

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 1225. Website Homepage Concerns
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/website-homepage-concerns
Autor: mark-earle-5141  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hi guys,
We have a pressure washing business in the UK and I’m following the training for optimising my new web pages as per the Core 30.
I’ve created a project in Claude, uploaded our Customer Avatar details and service areas etc, and asked Claude to start writing the new Homepage based on the new optimized GBP.

I’m concerned that the intro is fluffy and not really professional. A little contrived even.

Some feedback would be great before I proceed with this page. The intro content is below.

Pressure Washing Warrington: Restoring Your Property's Pride

I'll never forget the day I first drove through Grappenhall village and saw how the morning light caught the pristine driveways and spotless patios.

Each home told a story of pride and care, yet I knew from experience that beneath Warrington's unpredictable weather—those sudden downpours that seem to come from nowhere and the persistent Cheshire drizzle—even the most beautiful properties were fighting a constant battle against dirt, algae, and stubborn stains.

After fifteen years of transforming tired-looking exteriors across Warrington, from the bustling streets near the town centre to the peaceful lanes of Lymm, I've learned that your property's appearance isn't just about kerb appeal—it's about the satisfaction you feel every time you pull into your driveway after a long day at work.

Last month, I cleaned a block-paved driveway in Stockton Heath that hadn't seen proper attention in three years. The homeowner, a busy professional like so many of you reading this, had watched helplessly as oil stains from her car gradually turned her once-beautiful entrance into something she was embarrassed about.

When I finished that afternoon, she stood at her front door with the biggest smile, telling me she felt proud to invite friends over again.
That's exactly why I started my pressure washing service here in Warrington.

Whether you're dealing with green algae creeping across your patio in Birchwood, stubborn moss between your block paving in Culcheth, or simply want to restore that fresh, clean look to your property in Great Sankey, I understand the frustration of watching your home's exterior lose its lustre while you're juggling work deadlines and family commitments.

Professional pressure washing isn't just about blasting water at surfaces—it's about understanding which cleaning methods work best for different materials, knowing the right pressure settings to avoid damage, and having the experience to tackle everything from delicate render cleaning to heavy-duty concrete restoration across Warrington and the surrounding areas.

Q: do any of have an intro like this on your home pages?
Thanks

## 1230. Example Site
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/example-site
Autor: clovis-shropshire-8344  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Back in June Caleb mentioned an example site releasing for us to help visualize the Core 30, just wondering if there was an update on it?

Going through the course, while a lot of it makes sense it is very hard to visualize/imagine certain parts without seeing an example.

For example, the homepage GBP AI generator produces story content for the homepage which doesn't seem appropriate when the goal is lead gen. Very hard to wrap my head around how best to use that prompt for the homepage when it seems to produce blog-like content.

Another example is how to visually structure the core 30 pages. In the course it said to make a page that has every service on it, but in a live call it was said not to do that, so I'm just confused on how to proceed.

I've been getting good results with what I do understand, but without client or fake examples, it's a bit tough at times.

## 1242. I'm Not A Smart Man... I Need Your Help With My CORE 30 Structure
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/im-not-a-smart-man-i-need-your-help-with-my-core-30-structure
Autor: jason-leibler-1057  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Ok, I've been trying to get my wife's physical therapy business to rank for about 4 months now, and I'm making very little progress. Maybe even going backwards slightly.

I'm sure there is lots I've done wrong, but for the purposes of not making this post super long, I just want to go back to the basics and focus on my Core 30 structure.

We have a physical therapy business. We are are a running specific PT clinic. We are ranking number one for running related searches, but the problem is there aren't many of those searches each month.

So we want to rank for general PT searches \(physical therapy boise, physical therapy near me, etc.\) because those searches have a lot more volume each month, and we want to capture non runners with lower body injuries.

I'm not sure why I'm having such a hard time wrapping my head around this. The only GBP categories we're using are as follows:

Primary category: physical therapy clinic
Secondary category: physical therapist
Secondary category: sports medicine clinic

*** None of the other category options really make sense, they're all a stretch.

Originally, when I built out our CORE 30 on our website \(back in May\), I built it using the hierarchy in the spreadsheet screenshot:

One challenge is that I am very limited by the GBP category options. Right now I have every service in my GBP listed under our primary category of 'physical therapy clinic'.

I'm probably making this harder than it needs to be, so I'll stop my rambling.

How do you guys think I should structure my website \([fitandfunctiontherapy.com](http://fitandfunctiontherapy.com)\) and build out my Core 30 based on the info I've given you?

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 1256. Excited to Join!
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/excited-to-join
Autor: mars-lin-6842  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hi everyone! I’m thrilled to be part of the AI SEO Mastery Pro community and to connect with fellow professionals who are passionate about leveraging AI for business growth.

A little about my background: I’ve been specializing in Click-Through Rate \(CTR\) optimization for Google Business Profiles \(GBP\) since 2019. Over the years, I’ve completed 1,500+ orders on Legiit and helped more than 500 local businesses boost their GBP rankings through targeted CTR strategies.

I’m eager to explore how AI and data-driven approaches can help us attract high-value clients and deliver measurable, sustainable results. I look forward to learning from this community, sharing my own insights, and collaborating on innovative SEO strategies that drive success in today’s dynamic landscape.

Excited to connect and grow alongside all of you!

═══════════════════════════════════════

Autor: karl-burchette-5486  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

I'm working with a data center cleaning company with about 120 employees in 25 US cities. The headquarters is in the Atlanta metro and I wanted to help set up chamber of commerce links. There's a strong chamber of commerce for their Atlanta office, but as it says "Gwinnett Chamber General Membership pricing is based on the number of full-time employees an organization maintains". There are less than 20 employees here \($680 a year\) but all employees are substantially more per year \($2,030\). Now if they do this for all their cities I can imagine the price will be pretty high. Is the membership price for all 120 employees \(most are cleaners\)? Or should this be for just the employees here in the Atlanta area?

## 1279. Core 30 Structure for Business That Doesn't Have Services in GBP
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/core-30-structure-for-business-that-doesnt-have-services-in-gbp
Autor: rebecca-degrossa  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hey [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) - I'm drinking up your course to see what I can apply to my audience -- psychologists, psychotherapists, counselors -- and the occasional Psychiatrist or Coach.

I'd love to tell you what I'm seeing and then what I *think* I should test -- and get your feedback on it if you're game!

Our client's business don't have the option to list Services in GBPs. To get our client's "specialties" and "modalities" in the GBP, we've been using the Product feature in the GBP and then creating these pages on the site - optimized URL, Title, H1. I.e. The GBP product would be Anxiety Therapy \(see GBP image below\) and we'd have an Anxiety Therapy Denver page on the site. See image below.

Not sure what I should change in terms of site structure.

The GBP links to the home page optimize for main category - Psychotherapy Denver, for instance.

Right now, our navigation has these pages that correlate with the products just under the home page. I.e. sitename.com/anxiety-therapy-city.

The specialty pages \(that correlate with GBP products\) are the hubs of a hub and spoke with anxiety therapy blog posts pointing to the hub \(anxiety therapy page\). I'd see these blogs as supporting pages in your model?

I like your idea of adding external links to each page. We haven't done that. Could get expensive adding to each blog page. But maybe we don't need to blog anymore... wouldn't that be amazing.

Challenges with Secondary GBP Categories for this Niche
-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
There are some Categories that might work as Secondary Categories depending on what the practice treats, but many of them mean the same thing: Psychotherapist, Counselor, Mental Health Clinic, Mental Health Service. You can use Psychologist if you're licensed as one, but its against the terms of your license to use that if you're not.

The ones I'd use as secondaries, if they apply to the client, include:
[ul][li]Child Psychologist \(can only be used legally if client is a licensed psychologist\)[li]Marriage or Relationship Counselor[li]Family Counselor[li]EMDR Psychotherapist[li]Life Coach

If the client works with general stuff \(anxiety, depression, trauma, grief--none fit into a secondary category above\), I'd just have those page on the site under the home page. Would you agree? See image below of the specialty dropdown.

I think we have the GBPs pretty well optimized.

But here is the experiment I was thinking about running first.

[ol:1][li]Find a term that has less than 1/3 green in the rank map -- and for which the KW is on page 2 organically. Depression therapy Denver for this practice fits the bill. Ranked at 13 organically and the rank map is below.[li]Do the content analysis on it and make the content changes. [li]Get an external link to the page[li]See what happens to the map. If not fixed, then add supporting pages.

Do you agree that this is a good place to start? Please let me know if you would do something else!

Thanks.

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 1291. Content Needs to Be on 1 Domain, Correct?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/content-needs-to-be-on-1-domain-correct
Autor: kyle-huggins-7805  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
BLUF - can you please confirm whether or not a GBP would get "Relevance" credit from the GMaps algorithm if it points to a Landing Page \(LP\) one one domain but then hosts all of the GBP CATS/SERVS content pages on a second, distinct domain? \(I'm assuming the answer is "no"\)

CONTEXT:
I'm working with a new client who is opening a franchise. It looks like we will only be able to edit one page on the franchise-provide domain \(a dedicated page for the new location she's opening up\). So we will not be able to add/nest content pages in topical silos off of that page on that same domain. One option is that we could proceed and use that franchise domain's location-specific page as the GBP LP and have that LP point to content on pages that sit on a second, separate domain that we control. However, I'm guessing this will not get the "Relevance" credit since Google will see that as an external reference as opposed to an internal link.

═══════════════════════════════════════

URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/gbp-suspension
Autor: jesse-williams-2266  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hey everyone — looking for some advice.
I was making some basic edits to a client’s second Google Business Profile and it triggered a suspension. First location was fine, but this one got flagged quickly.
What’s the best way to walk the client through this without stressing them out? I want to keep things clear and calm, but also get the suspension lifted as quickly as possible.
Also just to confirm: is suspension different from reverification, or are they basically the same thing with different labels?
Appreciate any insights from anyone who’s handled this recently. These profiles are touchier than ever.

## 1303. Good idea to synchronize content between blog, social media, and GBP posts?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/good-idea-to-synchronize-content-between-blog-social-media-and-gbp-posts
Autor: hassan-chaaban-8242  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I've got a client, made him core30 a few days ago, now planning social media/GBP/blog posting for topical authority.

I have tools for generating both blog and social media posts, and I watched your video on generating GBP posts. Now, I'm definitely overthinking here, but would there be any value to having a system that synchronizes content between blogs, social, and GBP?

The idea was that, after a blog post would be created, I'd have something like Zapier trigger an alert for Claude, which summarizes that content, and then links to my social media tool and GBP Zapier plugin to post those summaries there too.

The reason I thought of this was after seeing the heavy emphasis for the GBP synchronized perfectly with clients' core30, where if something was changed on GBP it'd have to be changed in core30 to reflect that, it lead me to wonder if there'd be some benefit to having the content generated be synchronized too.

TL;DR: Just as it's crucial for the GBP to be synchronized with core30, and any changes on GBP be made in core30 too, would there be any benefit to having their blog/social media content be synchronized with GBP posts too?

Thanks again 👍

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 1304. Ok, big changes made for Core30, let's see what happens
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/ok-big-changes-made-for-core30-lets-see-what-happens
Autor: robert-foerster-2343  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Brightlocal showed we weren't doing terrible, but just tired local falcon and it says otherwise. So I guess this is the starting point.

We just revamped our GBP categories a bit, cleaned up the services, and then redid the website to align better per core30, etc.

One thing that's interesting a bit. People don't search for <category> <location> for these services. It's a spa, or facial spa, but people search predominantly for 'facials near me', so this is what we are tracking. Competitors DO NOT have optimized GBP or websites, but they have duration, popularity, high review counts, etc.

Will be interesting to see how this changes in coming weeks.

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 1307. <keyword> <location> vs <keyword> near me
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/keyword-location-vs-keyword-near-me
Autor: robert-foerster-2343  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
So, here's the conundrum.

Nobody searches for our keyword + location \(shown by keyword planner and our search console data\). It's always 'keyword near me'.

I had never run the keyword + location scan til today \(and we did a ton of core30 work recently\). This is actually pretty awesome, but it doesn't seem relevant since nobody searches this way.

How do we get the actual 'near me' searches to boost our relevance? Any ideas?

Do I need to put 'near me' on our pages? Bad one is 'near me', better one is 'location'

## 1308. Interesting Heatmap
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/interesting-heatmap
Autor: bruce-fleck-8929  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Attached is a heatmap for my client in Wichita. She runs a medical clinic and one of her four services is hair restoration. She's competing with some businesses who focus mainly on hair restoration.The arrow shows where her location is.

I found this map odd in that she is ranked 20+ at her exact location. However, she is top 3 or 5 in many locations further away. I'm guessing it's because we are generally doing things right for local SEO but can't compete in the exact places where specialty hair restoration practices are located.

Thoughts or comments?

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 1309. Plan for GBP Suspensions
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/plan-for-gbp-suspensions
Autor: jeff-garner-3218  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Some of you heard that I have been fighting Google to get my GBP reactivated after making a minor description update to my SEO services section. So I learned a hard lesson related to reviews.

I encourage everyone to periodically view their reviews and save them as a PDF or just screen shot the reviews in case you ever need to publish them outside of a GBP. Also be aware of the impact of using dynamic GBP Review feeds should your profile be suspended.
All the best!
Jeff

═══════════════════════════════════════

Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

The recording from today's AI SEO community call \(July 15th\) is now live in the Classroom section under:
2025-07-15 Platform Migration Risks & Lead Snap
\(link here: [https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/52e05dc7?md=70ad638e98b049839c167c88443346db](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/52e05dc7?md=70ad638e98b049839c167c88443346db)\)

What We Covered This Week:

Platform Migration Warning - Why moving from Wix to WordPress can cost you months of rankings \(and thousands in revenue\)

Google Business Profile Mastery - How to pick the right categories even when they all look the same

Smart Link Building - Why every single page needs at least one backlink + the exact strategy we use

Lead Snap Deep Dive - Multiple members shared their onboarding experiences with this game-changing tool

Ranking Strategies - From franchise limitations to content structure best practices

Key Takeaway:

Don't chase shiny new websites if your current one is already ranking! Sometimes "ugly" sites convert better than pretty ones.

Reminder:

No call next Tuesday \(July 22nd\) - I'll be in San Francisco visiting a client \(and maybe catching Lady Gaga 🎤\).

We'll be back to our regular schedule the following week!

## 1325. Clarification on GBP Categories + Services
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/clarification-on-gbp-categories-services
Autor: chris-white-1808  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
[@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) / anyone who can help...

I just got a roofing client. My first client. Woohoo!!

I'm using GMB Everywhere as a tool to help with Categories and Services.
My client focuses on \(1\) Residential Roofing, \(2\) Commercial Roofing, and \(3\) Solar Installation.

GMB Everywhere is suggesting the categories in the screenshot below. The only two I'm seeing in the screenshot below that seem feasible are: \(1\) Roofing Contractor and \(2\) Roofing Service.

The only category I see populating in the GBP is "Roofing Contractor". "Roofing Service" doesn't seem to be a Category.

I also ran this through AI and got the following and put AVAILABLE next to the categories showing up in GBP:

PRIMARY CATEGORY \(CRITICAL\):
• "Roofing Contractor" - Used by 94.3% of top-ranking roofing companies
TOP 9 ADDITIONAL CATEGORIES:
[ol:1][li]Service Establishment \(24.47% of roofers use this\)[li]General Contractor \(captures broader searches\) AVAILABLE[li]Siding Contractor \(for your soffit/fascia services\) AVAILABLE[li]Window Installation Service \(for skylight work\) AVAILABLE[li]Gutter Cleaning Service \(for your gutter services\) AVAILABLE[li]Construction Company \(for commercial positioning\) AVAILABLE[li]Solar Panel Installation Service \(growing market in CO\)[li]Waterproofing Service \(specialized, less competition\) AVAILABLE[li]Emergency Service \(critical for Highlands Ranch hail damage market\)

Can someone with experience working with Roofing Companies give me some insight on how to look at this issue with categories. I'm also seeing the same issue with Services where GMB Everywhere/AI is suggesting services that seem relevant but they are NOT populating as Service Options in the GBP.

Any insight for a newbie on how to think through this would be super helpful!!

## 1332. Question on home page content and how much to revamp
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/question-on-home-page-content-and-how-much-to-revamp
Autor: robert-foerster-2343  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hi All,
I have spent a LOT of time over the last few months TRYING to improve my wife's site following the YT videos and the original lower cost SEO course. I think I did a lot well, we have improved, but not breaking into the top yet.

Basically, the website domain just became a real site around December. I started trying to track and rank in April. So, definite progress \(it was a lot worse a week ago, this run shows a lot of improvement\). We had a location move 1 month ago, about a 1 mile move \(hence the marker not showing properly on the grid\).

I know, if things are starting to work, we probably don't want do do a major change...

My home page has the category + location in h1. Then I have h2 and h3 for each category. I listed almost every service there -- I think that's a mistake?

Where I got confused, was on the 'Generating GBP Landing Page' -- it basically spit out 2600 words of good text, but is that really what would go on the home page?

I found some other items I need to clean up: we had overlapping services, and claude recommend I rename some using sub-notation for search intent, ex: Rezenerate NanoFacial \(Anti-aging facial\)...

Links: only thing we have so far, is an expensive chamber link \(ours is $500/yr!!!\), about 30 of the bigger citation cites.

I just pitched/convinced a local digital newspaper to run an article, and they usually provide a link to the domain which is awesome - this will likely hit in the next week or so as they just called for quotes today. We have local news coming in a few weeks for morning TV segment \(no linking sadly, but visibility\).

Sorry for the ramble. Things have felt helpless after all this time, but the latest map shows some signs of hope. Anyone want to chime in? Happy to share the site also.

## 1335. Rank Without an Address?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/rank-without-an-address
Autor: joe-parker-9166  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:

PREGUNTA:
Hi [@Caleb Ulku](obj://user/e7baaba6ac1b456f85089b72541a190c) , I'm sorry you've probably answered this question many times before but I still can't wrap my head around the concept.

To give context, Sydney, Australia and Houston, Texas are cities about the same size \(640 square miles\).
Sydney is made up of 658 'neighbourhoods' about 1 square mile each in size.
Only 1 of those neighbourhoods, the Sydney CBD, have 'Sydney' as the name you would mail a postcard to.
Therefore lets say if I live in a neighbourhood called Bondi, that means my mailing address would be Bondi and not Sydney.

hey all, holding myself accountable here.

I've dipped my toes into SEO in the past but never got serious enough to have a 'real' business around it. My wife has a couple of friends with small businesses in need of some help, so we're stepping in and hopefully using it as our launching pad. She'll handle the social media aspect and I'm handling the SEO.

I have 2 main goals:

[ol:1][li]Freedom for my wife and myself to live where we want, when we want[li]It's incredibly easy for small business owners to get completely screwed out of a lot of money by "companies" that make unrealistic promises just to make a quick buck. I'd like to be a part of stopping that. I manage a website for an electrician in my area who spent $20k on SEO over 10 months, and got 0 leads. All because they promised him "you'll have so many phone calls you won't know what to do with yourself."

## 1363. Google Business new company Verification without Video
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/google-business-new-company-verification-without-video
Autor: jeremy-bengtson-6101  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Has anyone received this "text" option lately?
Feel free to try this method
Step-by-Step: GBP Setup with Text Verification

Add NAP to the Website
[ul][li]Hardcode the Name, Address, Phone into the footer of every page \(not just the contact page\).[li]Don’t rely on plugins alone. Create a Facebook Business Page
[ul][li]Use the exact business name, address, phone, and website URL.[li]Connect it to the existing Instagram account \(must be a business/creator account\).
Set up a Yelp, Bing Places, or BBB listing, twitter, reddit USE THE GOOGLE DOMAIN EMAIL.
[ul][li]Use the same NAP and link to the website.[li]This adds trust and legitimacy, especially for new businesses.
Update Existing Listings
[ul][li]Find any existing online citations/directories \(even if sparse\).[li]Make sure the address, phone, and business details are consistent.[li]Prioritize niche/industry-specific directories.

Connect Website to Google Properties
[ul][li]Use the business’s domain-based email \(e.g., [info@businessname.com](mailto:info@businessname.com) via Google Workspace\).[li]Connect the site to Google Search Console and Google Analytics.
Get a Local Guide to Add the Business on Google Maps
[ul][li]Find someone with Local Guide Level 4+ \(ideally higher\).[li]Have them add the business manually in Google Maps using the real NAP.
Seed the Listing with Real Reviews
[ul][li]Ask a few real clients to leave honest reviews on the listing added by the Local Guide.[li]Keep it natural—no stuffing.
Claim the Existing GBP via Workspace Account
[ul][li]Use the same Google account that was connected to GSC + GA.[li]Search for the business in Google Maps, click "Claim this business."[li]Google offered us two options: Text or Video verification.[li]Chose Text. Entered the code. Done.
💡 Why This Works
Google is more likely to offer non-video verification options when:
[ul][li]The account claiming the profile is connected to the business \(Workspace + GSC + GA\)[li]The business already exists in Google’s ecosystem \(citations, reviews, map activity\)[li]There are consistent signals across the web \(website, Facebook, directories\)
Skip the guesswork. Build trust first. Claim later. Hope this helps someone dodge video hell. Let me know if you test this and it works for you

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 1377. Key Insights from Today's Call: Nav Structure, Call Tracking & GBP Optimization
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/key-insights-from-todays-call-nav-structure-call-tracking-gbp-optimization
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Key Topics Covered:

✅ Navigation structure - Why it's priority #86 for local SEO
✅ Call tracking - How missed calls directly impact rankings
✅ GBP optimization - Homepage structure that works
✅ What NOT to focus on - Testing results from 90+ plumbers

Biggest Takeaways:
[ul][li]Don't fight clients over nav bars \(focus on GBP ranking instead\)[li]Missed call rates = ranking factor \(use Go High Level for tracking\)[li]New websites take 3-6 months minimum to rank[li]GBP products/service areas have zero ranking impact

📚 Find the recording + detailed summary in the Classroom section, or here: [https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/52e05dc7?md=64bed73887e0481583fdb06b2dcc9167](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/52e05dc7?md=64bed73887e0481583fdb06b2dcc9167)

## 1381. How to tag Website Section Pre-Headlines?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/how-to-tag-website-section-pre-headlines
Autor: bruce-fleck-8929  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I'm a big fan of Wes McDowell's web design formulas. He teaches a storytelling webpage flow. Each section has a pre-headline announcing what the section is about. The copywritten headline that's H2 follows. The pre-headline primes the reader with additional context for quickly scanning the page section by section.

My question is, what H-tag should I use for the pre-headline? H3, H4, or paragraph?

I don't want to use H2 because that will compete with the headline that follows. I'm currently using H4 for the pre-headline and H2 for the headline. However, I wonder if that is confusing the search engine crawlers. I could use simple paragraph text for the pre-headline.

Here's an example of one my client's pages with the pre-headlines highlighted.

Thoughts?

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 1382. One Thing I’ll Share That Might Help Someone Else
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/one-thing-ill-share-that-might-help-someone-else
Autor: jason-leibler-1057  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
A couple months ago I was messing with my GBP secondary categories too much and triggered account re-verification.

I ended up doing a live Google Meet with a Google rep in order to re-verify. She told me everything looked great for the live video verification, but before they would officially re-verify my profile she wanted me to go in and delete all the 20 service areas off of my profile while we were on the phone \(since we have a commercial location\).

I think in Google’s eyes you either have a physical commercial location or you are a service area business. I don’t think they like if you act like you are both.

Just my own anecdotal experience, but thought I’d share.

═══════════════════════════════════════

## 1386. HVAC/Air Conditioning in Australia
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/hvacair-conditioning-in-australia
Autor: belynda-scarsbrook-1344  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Hi all, new member here. I'm based in Australia and have just started working with an air conditioning contractor \(primary GBP category\). Their secondary categories are HVAC contractor, Central heating service, furnace repair service, heating equipment supplier, air conditioning repair shop, and air conditioning system supplier.

In Australia, we don't really use terms like HVAC, furnace, or even "contractor". Central heating and cooling is more commonly referred to as "ducted". The last person to work on their GBP has put in an enormous list of custom services, some duplicated under the categories \(see below\).

They are based in Melbourne, and want to service a 20km radius from their business address, which includes at least 20 suburb locations. Their primary services are:

[ul][li]air conditioning installations, service and repairs \(split system and ducted electric systems\)[li]evaporative cooling installations, service and repairs \(sometimes called evaporative air conditioning\)[li]ducted gas heating maintenance, service and repairs[li]changeovers from ducted gas heating to electric heating and cooling \(new regulations in Victoria mean new gas systems can no longer be installed, but ~2 million homes across the state already have this\)

I'm in a bit of overwhelm trying to figure out the core 30. Their rankings are terrible across everything right now. Competitors also have air conditioning contractor as their primary category, so this I'm okay with.

I'm thinking the other 3 secondary categories to be:
[ul][li]air conditioning system supplier \(then stack split system, ducted, and evaporative installations under this + add on cooling systems that can be paired with gas heating\)[li]central heating service \(put both gas servicing/repairs/maintenance and electric heating installations here\)[li]air conditioning repair shop \(put all repairs, maintenance, and servicing here\)

They also do not offer commercial, duct repair, or duct cleaning. They have a physical location, along with their desired service areas.

Am I on the right track here? Any advice please?

Current GBP Categories & Services:

Air conditioning contractor - Primary category
A/C system maintenance
A/C system repair
Heating system installation
Heating system maintenance
Heating system repair
HVAC repair
HVAC system maintenance

## 1399. Location Priorities
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/location-priorities
Autor: matt-hirsch-2389  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I have a scenario I’d be curious to hear how the community would approach.

Let’s say there’s a plumber located in Carmel, Indiana. This plumber is interested in ranking for Indianapolis due to the larger search. Their whole site is targeted to Indy, but obviously they’re not physically there.

They’re not ranking super well for Indy, but they’re 2-3 in Carmel.

[ol:1][li]Do you redo all the content to focus on Carmel since they’re there?[li]Do you use a location page to go after the larger city?[li]Do you do something else I can’t think of?

═══════════════════════════════════════
## 1400. What Did I Do Wrong? / What Do I Do Next?
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/what-did-i-do-wrong-what-do-i-do-next
Autor: jason-leibler-1057  ·  Match: comentó Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
I'll try to keep this brief.

I've learned a lot about Local SEO from Caleb, and as I've been learning I've been implementing these teachings on my wife's physical therapy business. In other words, her business has been my guinea pig for Local SEO.

My problem is that so far all the changes I've made haven't moved the needle at all in terms of rank map tracking \(Local Falcon\). Here's what I've done so far:

-fully filled out GBP including categories, services, products, and weekly update posts / pictures
-about a month ago I did a huge revamp on her website where I built out her "Core 30" service pages and internal linking like Caleb teaches \(Note: I have NOT purchased links for these pages yet, but they are indexed in GSC\)
-we've been getting consistent reviews the whole time
-signed up for Local Chamber of Commerce about 2-3 weeks ago

As I mentioned, all those changes haven't moved the needle at all on the Rank Map \(she was ranking 13th for her main keyword before all these changes, still ranking 13th\).

Questions:

-Is it the fact that I didn't purchase any PBN links for my Core 30 pages yet?
-How long does it normally take to see movement after implementing major changes?

I'm trying to understand where I'm coming up short, or what I need to do next?

I want to start start a SEO agency, but my struggles on her local SEO are killing my confidence :\(

Her site is [fitandfunctiontherapy.com](http://fitandfunctiontherapy.com) in Boise Idaho if that helps. Thanks!

## 1420. New Lesson: Technical Audit
URL: https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/new-lesson-technical-audit
Autor: caleb-ulku-6571  ·  Match: AUTOR es Caleb/Miles  ·  0 comentarios

PREGUNTA:
Okay so the launch was a couple days ago on Thursday...

Already added a new lesson. I've had a few people specifically ask after this so I put a lesson in that breaks down how we do technical audit's with AI.

Here's the lesson: [https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=1535f6ca78dd46398c841e0bdad4529a](https://www.skool.com/ai-seo-mastery-pro/classroom/63a81f62?md=1535f6ca78dd46398c841e0bdad4529a)

Keep in mind this isn't something we do for every client. Generally, if the client has a large website and is struggling ranking, then we do this. By large, I mean above the average size of the top 3 ranking competitors.

If the top 3 had 100 URLs indexed and you're looking at a website with 400 URLs indexed but they aren't ranking, then run this audit.

If the website you're looking at is over 500 URLs in general, this would be a solid option also.
